Two books in particular made deep impressions on her: Catherine Maurice’s Let Me Hear Your Voice: A Family’s Triumph over Autism, and Understanding the Mystery of Autism and Pervasive Developmental Disorder: A Mother’s Story of Research and Recovery by Karyn Seroussi. Both promoted the notion that autistic children could be brought to the point of recovery while offering different road maps for getting there. “These books told me the only news I wanted to hear,” Shannon says. “That my son could be made normal again.”

有两本书给她留下了深刻印象：凯瑟琳·莫里斯的《让我听见你的声音：一个家庭战胜孤独谱系的故事》，以及卡琳·塞鲁西的《理解孤独谱系与广泛性发育障碍之谜：一位母亲的研究与康复之路》。这两本书都宣扬孤独谱系儿童能够达到康复状态的理念，同时提供了不同的实现路径。"这些书告诉了我唯一想听到的消息，"香农说，"那就是我的儿子可以重新变得正常。"



Maurice’s book focused on applied behavior analysis (ABA), a form of behavior modification based on the animal-learning theories of B. F. Skinner and pioneered as an early intervention for autism in the 1960s by psychologist Ivar Lovaas at the University of California in Los Angeles. In the first chapters of the book, the author’s two-year-old daughter, Anne-Marie, is trapped in a swirling vortex of regression, like a time lapse of development in reverse. After losing the few words she had learned to speak months earlier, Anne-Marie seemed to retreat into a hermetic existence, solitary and unreachable.

莫里斯所著的书籍聚焦于应用行为分析（ABA），这是一种基于 B.F.斯金纳动物学习理论的行为矫正方法，由加州大学洛杉矶分校的心理学家伊瓦尔·洛瓦斯于 20 世纪 60 年代率先将其作为孤独症的早期干预手段。在书的前几章中，作者两岁的女儿安妮-玛丽正陷入退化的漩涡，如同发育过程的倒放快进。在失去数月前学会的寥寥数语后，安妮-玛丽似乎退入了一个与世隔绝的存在，孤独而难以触及。



Her activities were becoming stranger, more bizarre. I watched her, feeling very close to panic, as she repetitively sorted through puzzle pieces, then held them up two by two, always at right angles to each other, and stared at them. Oh please baby. Please don’t do that. Why are you doing that?

她的行为变得越来越古怪、越来越离奇。我看着她反复整理拼图碎片，然后两两举起，总是保持直角相对，盯着它们看，内心感到极度恐慌。哦，求你了宝贝。别那样做。你为什么要那样做？



Anne-Marie’s increasingly autistic behavior filled Catherine and her husband, Marc, with a sense of doom, as if they were watching their daughter being devoured by a monster in front of their eyes. “We were racing against the days,” Catherine wrote, “racing to find some way of halting her inexorable progression backwards.” Grieving for the little girl she felt she was losing, Catherine fell to her knees and prayed. “Please make the diagnosis be wrong. Please make her not be autistic,” she cried. “Lord, make it not be. Give me back my baby girl. Give her back to me. Don’t let this happen. Stop it. Give her back!”

安妮-玛丽日益显现的孤独谱系行为让凯瑟琳和丈夫马克感到一种不祥的预感，仿佛眼睁睁看着女儿被怪物吞噬。"我们在与时间赛跑，"凯瑟琳写道，"拼命寻找方法阻止她不可逆转的倒退。"为那个她感觉正在失去的小女孩哀悼，凯瑟琳跪地祈祷。"请让诊断出错。请让她不是孤独谱系，"她哭喊道，"主啊，让这一切不要发生。把我的小女儿还给我。把她还给我。别让这种事发生。阻止它。把她还给我！"



Willing to try nearly anything, Catherine and Marc explored a number of interventions in vogue at the time, including one called holding therapy based on ornithologist Nikolaas Tinbergen’s observations of birds. The Dutch animal-behavior expert—who had no previous experience working with children—insisted that autism was caused by “upsetting experiences in early childhood” and “very serious” parents rather than by genetics or other factors. “We are not blaming these unfortunate parents,” he declared upon receiving the Nobel Prize in medicine in 1973. “The parents of autists deserve as much compassion, and may be as much in need of help, as the autists themselves.”

凯瑟琳和马克几乎愿意尝试任何方法，他们尝试了当时流行的多种干预措施，其中包括一种基于鸟类学家尼古拉斯·廷伯根对鸟类观察的"拥抱疗法"。这位荷兰动物行为专家——此前从未有过与儿童打交道的经验——坚称孤独症是由"童年早期的创伤经历"和"非常严肃"的父母造成的，而非遗传或其他因素。"我们并非在指责这些不幸的父母，"他在 1973 年获得诺贝尔医学奖时宣称，"孤独症患者的父母值得与患者本人同等的同情，他们可能同样需要帮助。"



Holding therapy required mothers to “tame” their children by hugging them for an hour each day—by force, if necessary—while gazing intently into their eyes and confessing their innermost feelings. The goal of this process was an emotional breakthrough called “resolution,” which Tinbergen promoted as a “new hope” for parents—a “cure” for their children. Catherine sought out a prominent practitioner of holding therapy named Martha Welch, who touted a 50 percent recovery rate among her clients. When pressed to provide evidence of the therapy’s effectiveness, Welch chided her for being obsessed with “numbers and statistics.” In one of her training videos, a mother lies on top of her autistic daughter in bed, telling her how angry she is because her daughter doesn’t listen.

拥抱疗法要求母亲每天拥抱孩子一小时——必要时可强制进行——同时专注地凝视孩子的眼睛，倾诉内心最深处的感受。这一过程的目标是达成名为"化解"的情感突破，廷伯根将其宣传为父母的"新希望"、孩子的"治愈良方"。凯瑟琳找到了一位名叫玛莎·韦尔奇的拥抱疗法知名实践者，对方宣称其客户中有 50%的康复率。当被要求提供疗法有效性的证据时，韦尔奇斥责她过于执着于"数字和统计"。在韦尔奇的一则培训视频中，一位母亲躺在患有孤独谱系障碍的女儿身上，告诉女儿自己有多生气，因为女儿不听话。



“How does that make you feel?” the mother says.

"你感觉怎么样？"母亲问道。



“I can’t breathe!” her daughter groans.

"我喘不过气了！"女儿呻吟着。



“Well, I don’t care about that right now,” the mother snaps back. “You’re not going to get up until we resolve this.”

"我现在不在乎这个，"母亲厉声回击，"不把这事解决，你别想起来。"



Seeking insight from an expert who blamed you for inadvertently causing your child’s autism was a common double bind for parents, but a key element of the appeal of holding therapy for Catherine was the fact that Welch seemed to empathize with her anguish. By contrast, ABA was strictly empirical: the child was rewarded with M&Ms, sips of apple juice, and phrases like “Good job!” for doing things like making eye contact and sitting at a table, and punished with a loud “NO!” for hand flapping and stimming. Against Welch’s advice, the Maurices also engaged the services of a young ABA therapist named Bridget Taylor who proffered no psychological theories of autism and declined to make any promises of miraculous recovery. “I’ve never seen it,” she bluntly told Catherine—despite the fact that Lovaas himself claimed that nearly half of the children enrolled in his most immersive program had achieved “normal” functioning. Though Catherine initially doubted ABA, which seemed robotic and mechanical compared to Welch’s emphasis on emotional “rebonding,” Taylor eventually became like a member of the family, arriving every weekday afternoon to work patiently with their daughter.

向一位曾指责你无意中导致孩子患上孤独症的专家寻求见解，对父母而言是一种常见的双重困境，但凯瑟琳之所以被“拥抱疗法”吸引，关键在于韦尔奇似乎能共情她的痛苦。相比之下，应用行为分析（ABA）严格遵循实证原则：孩子若完成眼神接触、坐在桌边等行为，会得到 M&M 巧克力豆、苹果汁奖励，以及“干得好！”等表扬；而出现拍手、自我刺激行为时，则会听到严厉的“不行！”作为惩罚。莫里斯夫妇违背韦尔奇的建议，还聘请了年轻的 ABA 治疗师布里奇特·泰勒。这位治疗师既不提出任何关于孤独症的心理学理论，也不承诺奇迹般的康复效果。“我从未见过这种情况，”她直率地告诉凯瑟琳——尽管洛瓦斯本人声称，在他最密集的干预项目中，近半数儿童达到了“正常”功能水平。虽然凯瑟琳起初质疑 ABA（与韦尔奇强调情感“重建”相比，这种方法显得机械刻板），但泰勒最终成为了家庭的一员，每个工作日下午都来耐心陪伴他们的女儿进行训练。



In the months that followed, Anne-Marie became more alert and engaged, and Catherine wrote that she seemed to enjoy the “highly predictable, stable, structured environment” provided by Taylor during their sessions. She eventually felt betrayed by Welch, who pressured her into providing a testimonial to the BBC for a broadcast that attributed her daughter’s progress to an hour a week of holding therapy. When the program aired, families all over Europe clamored to find holding therapists for their own children, and Catherine felt partially responsible for misleading them. In ABA, however, the Maurices felt they had found an authentic reason for hope. They threw themselves into the role of being Anne-Marie’s co-therapists with the fervor of religious converts. The slightest signs of autistic behavior were no longer tolerated. “Day by day we grew more relentlessly demanding of her,” Catherine wrote. “No gazing into space, no teeth grinding, no playing with her hands, no manneristic touching of surfaces, no anything that looked autistic.”

在接下来的几个月里，安妮-玛丽变得更加警觉和投入，凯瑟琳写道，她似乎很享受泰勒在治疗期间提供的"高度可预测、稳定、结构化的环境"。她最终感到被韦尔奇背叛了，因为韦尔奇迫使她向 BBC 提供证词，用于一档将女儿进步归功于每周一小时的拥抱疗法的节目。节目播出后，欧洲各地的家庭纷纷为自己的孩子寻找拥抱治疗师，凯瑟琳觉得自己对误导他们负有部分责任。然而，在 ABA 疗法中，莫里斯夫妇认为他们找到了真正的希望。他们以宗教皈依者的热情投入到安妮-玛丽共同治疗师的角色中。最轻微的孤独谱系行为迹象也不再被容忍。"日复一日，我们对她的要求越来越苛刻，"凯瑟琳写道，"不能凝视虚空，不能磨牙，不能玩手，不能有做作地触摸表面的动作，不能有任何看起来像孤独谱系的行为。"



As Anne-Marie’s behavior became more “normal,” Catherine admitted that there was no way of knowing what was responsible—holding therapy, ABA, or her own maturation process. But gradually, the proto-language she had seemingly lost began flooding back: Ba-ba (bye-bye), ju (juice), ka (cookie). By the end of the book, Anne-Marie is four, and her doctor declares her “clearly no longer autistic.” Driving home from the evaluation, Christine turns to Marc and whispers, “God has answered our prayers.”

随着安妮-玛丽的行为变得越来越“正常”，凯瑟琳坦言无法确定究竟是哪种因素起了作用——是拥抱疗法、应用行为分析（ABA），还是她自身的成长过程。但渐渐地，她似乎失去的原始语言开始重新涌现：Ba-ba（再见）、ju（果汁）、ka（饼干）。到书末时，安妮-玛丽已四岁，医生宣布她“显然不再有孤独症”。评估结束后驱车回家途中，克里斯蒂娜转向马克低声说：“上帝回应了我们的祈祷。”



After finishing the book, Shannon enrolled Leo in an intensive schedule of speech therapy, occupational therapy, and ABA sessions. This program cost the Rosas several thousand dollars a month, which they were able to afford only with help from Craig’s parents. But they felt that there was no time to waste. The prevailing belief among autism clinicians was that if kids like Leo missed a narrow developmental window in which their brains were still amenable to rewiring by experience (a process that is now known to last a lifetime), they would never reach the goal of becoming, as Lovaas had put it, indistinguishable from their peers.

读完这本书后，香农为利奥安排了密集的言语治疗、职业治疗和 ABA 课程。这套方案每月花费罗萨斯夫妇数千美元，他们只能靠克雷格父母的资助才能负担。但他们觉得一刻也不能浪费。当时孤独症临床医生的普遍看法是：如果像利奥这样的孩子错过了大脑仍可通过经验重塑的短暂发育窗口期（这一过程如今已知会持续终生），他们就永远无法达到洛瓦斯所说的“与同龄人无异”的目标。



If Catherine Maurice sought a cure for autism by modifying her child’s behavior—working from the outside in—Karyn Seroussi’s Understanding the Mystery of Autism and Pervasive Developmental Disorder tells the story of a mother pursuing the same goal by working from the inside out.

如果凯瑟琳·莫里斯试图通过改变孩子的行为来寻找治愈孤独症的方法——从外部着手——那么卡琳·塞鲁西的《理解孤独症与广泛性发育障碍之谜》则讲述了一位母亲从内部入手追求同一目标的故事。



The book opens in an emergency room, where the author’s eighteen-month-old son, Miles, is trembling with a 106-degree fever. The previous morning, the boy received his eighteen-month shot of diphtheria-pertussis-tetanus (DPT) vaccine. Are the two events related? The doctor doesn’t know. Miles had a similarly terrifying episode after his first DPT, and another after his MMR, the routine childhood inoculation against measles, mumps, and rubella. A month later, a psychologist gives him a preliminary diagnosis of autism spectrum disorder based on his delays in acquiring language. To Seroussi, the dreaded word autism conjures up one thing: the image of “a profoundly disturbed child rocking in a corner.” The diagnosis is subsequently confirmed by a formal evaluation.

本书以急诊室场景开篇，作者十八个月大的儿子迈尔斯正因高烧 106 华氏度而颤抖。前一天早上，这个男孩接种了十八个月龄的白喉-百日咳-破伤风（DPT）疫苗。这两件事有关联吗？医生不得而知。迈尔斯在第一次接种 DPT 后曾出现过类似的可怕症状，接种麻疹-腮腺炎-风疹三联疫苗（MMR）后也发生过同样情况。一个月后，心理学家根据他的语言发育迟缓，初步诊断为孤独症谱系障碍。对塞鲁西而言，"孤独症"这个令人恐惧的词只让她联想到一个画面："一个严重失常的孩子蜷缩在角落里摇晃。"随后，正式评估确认了这一诊断。



It didn’t exactly come out of the blue. Miles had always been a more remote and solitary child than his sister, Laura. Each morning, she would climb into her parents’ bed to cuddle, but Miles wasn’t the cuddling type. After scraping his knee on the playground, he would run up to his mother for comfort, but then turn away when she tried to hug him. Instead of just playing with his toys, he seemed to “systematically experiment” with them, Seroussi observed, as if he took after his father, Alan, a brilliant research chemist who didn’t know how to behave in social situations. On the night that Alan proposed to Karyn, he took her hand and said, “I can teach you all about the world, and you can teach me how to live in it.”

这并非完全出人意料。迈尔斯一直比他的姐姐劳拉更孤僻、更爱独处。每天早上，劳拉都会爬到父母床上撒娇，但迈尔斯不是那种爱撒娇的孩子。在操场上擦伤膝盖后，他会跑向母亲寻求安慰，但当母亲试图拥抱他时，他却转身躲开。塞鲁西观察到，迈尔斯似乎不是在单纯地玩玩具，而是在"系统性地实验"它们，仿佛他继承了父亲艾伦的特质——这位才华横溢的化学研究员在社交场合总是手足无措。艾伦向卡琳求婚的那个夜晚，他握着她的手说："我可以教你认识世界，而你可以教我如何在这个世界生活。"



Karyn goes to the local library and finds only two books about autism on the shelves: one by a doctor about his son who “likes to eat with his fingers,” which terrifies her. The other is Let Me Hear Your Voice. She enrolls Miles in an intensive ABA program. But she doesn’t stop there. Talking with her mother-in-law, she learns that Alan, too, exhibited severe developmental delays when he was a baby. He would sit silently in his crib, turning the same toy over and over or lining his Matchbox cars up in rows. There had even been speculation that he was intellectually disabled, which clearly wasn’t true. These speculations ended when his mother stopped letting him drink milk, one of only two foods he was willing to eat (the other was applesauce) on the advice of a doctor who feared he was becoming anemic. Soon after that, Alan spontaneously began walking and talking.

凯琳来到当地图书馆，发现书架上只有两本关于孤独症的书：一本是一位医生写的，讲述他那个"喜欢用手抓东西吃"的儿子，这让她感到恐惧。另一本是《让我听见你的声音》。她让迈尔斯参加了一个高强度的应用行为分析项目，但她的行动并未止步于此。在与婆婆交谈时，她得知艾伦小时候也出现过严重的发育迟缓。他会安静地坐在婴儿床里，反复摆弄同一个玩具，或者把火柴盒小汽车排成一排。甚至有人猜测他存在智力障碍——这显然不是事实。当艾伦的母亲听从医生建议（医生担心他贫血），停止让他喝牛奶——这是他当时唯一愿意吃的两种食物之一（另一种是苹果酱）——这些猜测便戛然而止。不久之后，艾伦突然开始走路和说话了。



Seroussi embarked on a path to curing her son known as biomedical intervention, developed by a network of parents, clinicians, and practitioners of alternative medicine under the guidance of one of the most trusted experts in the autism parents’ community, Navy psychologist Bernard Rimland. The foundation of this approach is the so-called GFCF diet, a strict regimen free of any traces of gluten and casein, two proteins commonly found in wheat and dairy products. Rimland believed that vaccines like the DPT and the MMR leave some children unable to adequately digest these proteins, while rendering the walls of their intestines abnormally permeable (“leaky gut syndrome”). The undigested proteins are then carried by the bloodstream to the brain, where they wreak havoc with normal development. Along with the GFCF diet, Seroussi employed an aggressive program of high-dose vitamins, minerals, enzymes, and supplements developed by Rimland’s Defeat Autism Now! (DAN!) network.

塞鲁西踏上了治愈儿子的道路，这条路被称为生物医学干预，由一群家长、临床医生和替代医学从业者组成，在孤独症家长群体中最受信赖的专家之一、海军心理学家伯纳德·里姆兰德的指导下发展而来。这种方法的基础是所谓的无麸质无酪蛋白饮食，这是一种严格禁止任何麸质和酪蛋白残留的饮食方案，这两种蛋白质常见于小麦和乳制品中。里姆兰德认为，像百白破疫苗和麻腮风三联疫苗这样的疫苗会导致一些孩子无法充分消化这些蛋白质，同时使他们的肠壁变得异常通透（即"肠漏症"）。未被消化的蛋白质随后通过血液进入大脑，对正常发育造成严重破坏。除了无麸质无酪蛋白饮食，塞鲁西还采用了里姆兰德的"战胜孤独症现在！"网络开发的一套激进方案，包括高剂量的维生素、矿物质、酶和补充剂。



Seroussi framed the battle against her son’s autism in biblical terms, as a primordial showdown between good and evil. “The shadow of the beast has fallen over my home, and my doorway has been darkened by its dreaded countenance,” she writes. “Miles will be a father someday, and there is a good chance that he will have to fear for his own children. By that time, I need to know that the beast has been slain.” Like Let Me Hear Your Voice, the book ends on a triumphant note. A member of the DAN! network tells Seroussi, “There is not a trace of autism left in that boy.”

塞鲁西用圣经式的语言来描述与儿子孤独症的抗争，将其视为一场善恶之间的原始对决。“野兽的阴影笼罩了我的家，它可怖的面容遮蔽了我的门廊，”她写道，“迈尔斯终有一天会成为父亲，他很可能要为他的孩子担惊受怕。到那时，我需要确保这头野兽已被斩杀。”和《让我听见你的声音》一样，这本书以胜利的基调收尾。DAN！网络的一位成员告诉塞鲁西：“那个男孩身上已找不到一丝孤独症的痕迹。”



In his introduction to Seroussi’s book, Rimland proclaimed that she had found “what all parents hope for: a cure for her son.” With his endorsement, the notion that autistic children could be cured by making changes in their diet sent ripples of hope through the parent community at a time when the fear of a worldwide epidemic caused by vaccines was reaching its peak.

在塞鲁西这本书的序言中，里姆兰宣称她找到了“所有父母都渴望的东西：治愈她儿子的方法”。在他的背书下，通过改变饮食就能治愈孤独谱系儿童的理念，在家长群体中激起了希望的涟漪——而当时，人们对疫苗引发全球性流行病的恐惧正达到顶峰。



—

——



AFTER READING SEROUSSI’S BOOK, Shannon decided that Leo’s days of munching on gluten-rich Goldfish crackers and sugary PB & J sandwiches were over. To share her own account of recovering her son, she launched a blog called “The Adventures of Leelo the Soon-to-Be-Not Autistic Boy and His Potty-Mouthed Mom.”

读完塞鲁西的书后，香农决定结束利奥吃含麸质的金鱼饼干和含糖花生酱果酱三明治的日子。为了分享自己帮助儿子康复的经历，她开设了一个博客，名为《即将摆脱孤独谱系的利奥和他那满嘴脏话的妈妈的冒险记》。



Through a Yahoo group for mothers of kids with special needs, Shannon found a DAN! doctor in nearby Los Altos. His walls were plastered with testimonials from dozens of grateful mothers and fathers paying tribute to the effectiveness of his therapies for autism, Lyme disease, mold exposure, fatigue, and a host of other conditions. His treatment protocols included some that Craig and Shannon had never heard of, such as infrared therapy. But they felt reassured that his practice was not some shady, fly-by-night operation. It was a huge, bustling office in a modern medical complex on the main street of town.

通过一个面向特殊需求儿童母亲的雅虎群组，香农在附近的洛斯阿尔托斯找到了一位 DAN！医生。他的诊室墙上贴满了数十位感激的父母们的推荐信，赞扬他在治疗孤独症、莱姆病、霉菌暴露、疲劳及其他多种病症方面的疗效。他的治疗方案包括一些克雷格和香农从未听说过的方法，比如红外线疗法。但他们感到放心的是，他的诊所并非那种可疑的、打一枪换一个地方的机构，而是位于镇中心主干道上一座现代化医疗综合楼里，规模庞大、人来人往。



The doctor—a boyish Indian man in his forties with earnest, knowing eyes and a reassuringly competent manner—was optimistic about Leo’s potential for a cure. He had seen “hundreds of kids” like him recover in his own practice, he said. The old view of autism as an untreatable condition, he added, was being replaced by a new science of hope based on an emerging understanding of autism as a reversible disruption of multiple systems in the body—the systems targeted by the DAN! protocol.

医生——一位四十多岁、长着娃娃脸的印度裔男子，眼神真诚而睿智，举止沉稳可靠——对利奥的康复前景持乐观态度。他说，在自己的诊疗实践中，曾见证过"数百名"像他这样的孩子恢复健康。他补充道，过去将孤独症视为不治之症的陈旧观念，正被一门基于新兴认知的希望科学所取代——这种认知将孤独谱系理解为身体多个系统（正是 DAN！方案所针对的系统）的可逆性紊乱。



Each child on the spectrum is different, requiring an individualized treatment strategy, he said. But certain steps were fundamental: a healthier diet and the elimination of problem foods; detection and treatment of undiagnosed allergies; megadose vitamin and mineral supplementation; antifungals and probiotics to create a healthier environment in the intestines; antioxidants to reduce oxidative stress, which affects everything from gut permeability to neurotransmitter synthesis; and, finally, a whole-body purge of heavy metals like mercury and aluminum, which had been identified by DAN! practitioners as playing key roles in impairing brain function in autistic kids. It was all a little overwhelming to the Rosas, but it made sense: a robust, aggressive, full-spectrum approach for their spectrum kid. Here, at last, was the promise of a happier future for Leo and his family.

他说，每个孤独谱系的孩子都不同，需要个体化的治疗策略。但有些基础步骤是必不可少的：更健康的饮食并排除问题食物；检测并治疗未确诊的过敏；大剂量补充维生素和矿物质；使用抗真菌药和益生菌来营造更健康的肠道环境；使用抗氧化剂来减少氧化应激——这会影响从肠道通透性到神经递质合成的方方面面；最后，还要对全身进行重金属（如汞和铝）的排毒——DAN！从业者认为这些重金属在损害孤独谱系儿童大脑功能中起着关键作用。这对罗萨斯夫妇来说有些难以招架，但这一切都说得通：为他们的谱系孩子采取一种强有力、积极、全方位的方案。终于，利奥和他的家人看到了更幸福未来的希望。



“Let’s start with some tests,” the doctor offered. “We can run some lab checks on Leo’s blood and hair and concretely measure for the presence of mercury, antibodies, antigens, and other imbalances. Then we can try some changes in his diet. Even if we don’t see huge results, you’ll end up with a healthier kid.” That sounded like a no-lose proposition, but then the conversation took a more sobering turn. “Of course, if the tests show that Leo’s carrying a lot of mercury, we’ll have to start thinking about chelation.”

“我们先做些检查吧，”医生提议道，“可以对利奥的血液和毛发进行化验，具体检测汞含量、抗体、抗原及其他指标是否失衡。之后我们可以尝试调整他的饮食。即便效果不显著，至少能让孩子更健康。”这听起来是个稳赚不赔的方案，但接下来的谈话却转向了更严峻的方向。“当然，如果检测显示利奥体内汞含量过高，我们就得考虑螯合疗法了。”



As science geeks, the Rosas knew a thing or two about chelation, the process of removing heavy metals from the body employed after industrial accidents. During World War I (dubbed by some historians “the chemists’ war”) the Germans started using poisonous gases like chlorine as airborne messengers of death that killed in a particularly gruesome fashion. Allied soldiers caught in trenches without their gas masks were often found dead with clenched fists, blue faces, and blackened lips. But then a British research team discovered that certain compounds could bind with the toxic gas, which then enabled the body to flush it out in urine. These compounds were christened chelating agents, from the Greek word for “claw,” which described their pincerlike action as they “grabbed” the toxic molecules out of the bloodstream and sequestered them for excretion. Over the years, alt-med practitioners have touted chelation for a wide range of ailments, including heart disease and ovarian cancer.

作为科学爱好者，罗萨斯一家对螯合作用颇有了解——这是工业事故后用于清除体内重金属的过程。第一次世界大战（被某些历史学家称为"化学家的战争"）期间，德军开始使用氯气等毒气作为空中死亡信使，以极其恐怖的方式致人死亡。困在战壕中未戴防毒面具的协约国士兵被发现时，往往紧握双拳、面色青紫、嘴唇发黑。后来英国研究团队发现某些化合物能与毒气结合，使身体通过尿液将其排出。这些化合物被命名为螯合剂，源自希腊语"爪子"一词，形容它们像钳子般从血液中"抓取"有毒分子并隔离以待排泄。多年来，替代医学从业者一直推崇用螯合疗法治疗包括心脏病和卵巢癌在内的多种疾病。



On a tour of the office, the Rosas saw the chelation clinic, whose walls were covered with letters from parents describing the ways that this process had benefited their kids. Craig told himself that he would do some Web searching at home to find out more about chelation and catch up in general with the state of autism research. The most attractive thing about the biomedical approach for the Rosas was its ethic of empowerment in the face of a medical establishment that had few practical suggestions to offer for raising kids like their son. “We saw other parents getting proactive, taking charge, and trying to do something good for their children,” Craig says.

参观办公室时，罗萨斯夫妇看到了螯合疗法诊所，墙上贴满了家长们的来信，描述这一疗法如何让他们的孩子受益。克雷格暗自决定回家后上网搜索更多关于螯合疗法的信息，并全面了解孤独症研究的现状。对罗萨斯夫妇而言，生物医学疗法最吸引人的地方在于，面对一个对养育像他们儿子这样的孩子几乎提不出实用建议的医学体系，这种疗法传递了一种赋权理念。"我们看到其他家长主动出击、积极行动，努力为孩子做些有益的事，"克雷格说。



Soon, samples of Leo’s blood, hair, stool, and urine were dispatched to a network of labs for analysis, including Doctor’s Data in Illinois and the Great Plains Laboratory in Kansas, which acted as central testing hubs for the whole DAN! network. Unlike most conventional medical testing outfits, these labs provide not only quantified results directly to doctors but printouts for patients with interpretive commentary and suggested baseline “reference ranges” for individual tests. The Great Plains Laboratory, for example, suggested that children with “abnormal” levels of peptides in their urine be put on the GFCF diet and be tested for food allergies.

很快，利奥的血液、头发、粪便和尿液样本被送往多个实验室网络进行分析，其中包括伊利诺伊州的医生数据实验室和堪萨斯州的大平原实验室，这些实验室是整个 DAN!网络的核心检测中心。与大多数传统医学检测机构不同，这些实验室不仅直接向医生提供量化结果，还会为患者打印带有解释性评论的报告，并针对单项检测建议“参考范围”的基线值。例如，大平原实验室建议，尿液中肽水平“异常”的儿童应接受无麸质无酪蛋白饮食，并进行食物过敏测试。



The overall effect was to reinforce the notion that definitive links between food allergies, heavy metals, and autism have been established, though these links were in fact still purely speculative. A disclaimer at the bottom of the page acknowledged that the peptide test “has not been cleared or approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration,” but cheerfully noted that “the FDA has determined that such clearance or approval is not necessary.”

整体效果是强化了食物过敏、重金属与孤独症之间存在明确关联的观念，尽管这些关联实际上仍纯属推测。页面底部的免责声明承认，该肽检测“尚未获得美国食品药品监督管理局的批准或认可”，但乐观地指出，“FDA 已认定此类批准或认可并非必要。”



The results of Leo’s tests, impressively illustrated with brightly colored bars and graphs, were not encouraging. “It’s just as I thought,” the doctor said solemnly, before leading Craig and Shannon through the lists and charts and laying out a game plan for their son’s recovery.

利奥的检测结果并不乐观，但那些色彩鲜艳的条形图和曲线图却令人印象深刻。"果然不出我所料，"医生严肃地说着，带领克雷格和香农浏览那些列表和图表，并为他们儿子的康复制定了一套治疗方案。



The allergist who had tested Leo months earlier hadn’t turned up any red flags, but the DAN! network of labs—apparently more attuned to the problems of kids on the spectrum—seemed much more thorough. Leo turned out to be extremely “reactive” to peanuts and soy, highly allergic to gluten and rye, and moderately sensitive to lentils, oats, and wheat. No wonder her son had been battling diarrhea for years, Shannon thought.

几个月前为利奥做检测的过敏专科医生并未发现任何警示信号，但 DAN！实验室网络——显然对自闭症谱系儿童的问题更为敏感——似乎要全面得多。结果显示利奥对花生和大豆有极强的"反应性"，对麸质和黑麦高度过敏，对扁豆、燕麦和小麦中度敏感。香农心想，难怪儿子多年来一直饱受腹泻之苦。



The lab results also indicated that the massive quantities of sugar in his favorite strawberry jelly (no other kind would do) had triggered an explosion of yeast in his intestines at the expense of healthy gut flora. The consequences of yeast overgrowth could include chronic inattention (check), bedwetting (check), bellyaches (check), anger and aggression (check), sugar cravings (check), stimming (check), plateaus in skill development (check), climbing and jumping off objects (check), inability to potty-train (check), inappropriate laughter (check), inexplicable bouts of crying (check), and picky eating (check)—as it happened, many of the same clinical manifestations of autism itself. Leo was a classic case of Candida gone wild, the doctor explained. He was being poisoned by his beloved PB & J sandwiches.

化验结果还显示，他最爱吃的草莓果冻（其他口味一概不碰）中大量糖分导致肠道内酵母菌过度繁殖，而有益菌群则受到抑制。酵母菌过度生长的后果包括：长期注意力不集中（符合）、尿床（符合）、腹痛（符合）、易怒好斗（符合）、嗜糖（符合）、刻板行为（符合）、技能发展停滞（符合）、攀爬跳跃物体（符合）、如厕训练困难（符合）、不合时宜的大笑（符合）、莫名哭泣（符合）、挑食（符合）——这些症状恰好与孤独症本身的临床表现高度重合。医生解释说，利奥是典型的念珠菌失控病例，他正被最爱的花生酱果冻三明治慢慢毒害。



Furthermore, he added, Leo’s inflammatory and immunological markers were extremely high as his body rebelled against this toxic onslaught: nearly off the charts, judging from the ominous black bars on his charts. His level of an antibody called immunoglobulin A—which plays a critical role in the layer of intestinal mucus that is one of the body’s first lines of defense—was fifteen times the reference range cited by the lab. Leo’s GI tract was evidently pumping out heroic quantities of antibodies in a futile effort to repel a hellstorm of allergens and pathogens.

此外，他补充道，利奥体内的炎症和免疫指标极高——他的身体正在对抗这种毒性侵袭：从他病历上那些不祥的黑色条状标记来看，这些指标几乎超出了图表范围。他体内一种名为免疫球蛋白 A 的抗体水平——这种抗体在肠道黏液层中起着关键作用，而肠道黏液层是人体第一道防线之一——是实验室参考范围的 15 倍。显然，利奥的胃肠道正在大量分泌抗体，徒劳地试图抵御一场由过敏原和病原体组成的猛烈风暴。



Leo’s heavy-metal profile was also extremely problematic in the doctor’s view. The lab report on his hair sample indicated that his body was shedding high levels of aluminum, which can cause a buildup of ammonia in cells, resulting in a disruption of DNA metabolism and protein synthesis. The test also showed elevated levels of boron, which is often a tip-off to the lurking presence of blatantly neurotoxic elements like mercury, cadmium, and lead. The doctor told Craig and Shannon that they should seriously consider chelation to kick-start their son’s recovery process—and sooner rather than later.

在医生看来，利奥的重金属指标也极为令人担忧。他的头发样本实验室报告显示，他的身体正在排出高浓度的铝，这可能导致细胞中氨的积累，进而扰乱 DNA 代谢和蛋白质合成。检测还显示硼含量升高，这通常是汞、镉和铅等明显具有神经毒性的元素潜伏存在的信号。医生告诉克雷格和香农，他们应该认真考虑螯合疗法，以启动儿子的康复进程——而且越早越好。



In the meantime, the Rosas could make many changes to improve their son’s quality of life immediately. Step one was to eradicate even trace amounts of gluten and casein from his diet, as described in Seroussi’s book. (Leo’s tests didn’t indicate any acute reactivity to casein, but the doctor warned Craig and Shannon that not all of his sensitivities would show up on the tests.) His allergen assessment came with a detailed chart describing an elaborate elimination and rotation diet deemed appropriate for a kid with his reactivity profile. (Glancing down the list, Shannon tried to suppress her doubts about convincing a boy who subsisted on Goldfish and Veggie Booty to start scarfing down oysters, grapefruit, herring, and kidney beans.) His penchant for eating the same meals day after day could no longer be indulged, the doctor said, because continuous exposure to single foods could engender new sensitivities.

与此同时，罗萨斯一家可以立即采取多项措施改善儿子的生活质量。第一步是按照塞鲁西书中的方法，从儿子的饮食中彻底清除哪怕微量的麸质和酪蛋白。（利奥的检测并未显示对酪蛋白有急性反应，但医生提醒克雷格和香农，并非所有过敏原都能在检测中显现。）他的过敏评估附带了一份详细图表，列出了一套针对他这种过敏体质儿童设计的复杂忌口与轮换饮食方案。（香农扫了一眼清单，努力压下心中的疑虑——要让一个靠吃金鱼饼干和蔬菜脆片过活的孩子开始大口吞食牡蛎、西柚、鲱鱼和芸豆，这谈何容易。）医生强调，不能再纵容他日复一日吃同样食物的习惯，因为长期单一饮食可能引发新的过敏反应。



He reassured the Rosas that while they prepared Leo for chelation, they could undertake a number of other treatments to help correct his systemic imbalances. One such therapy, called BioSET, was devised by a chiropractor as a way of clearing “dissonant” energy blockages from the body using enzyme therapy, acupressure, homeopathy, and chiropractic. This was especially important for people with chronic conditions like autism, BioSET’s inventor claimed, because their systems become “chaotic,” leaving “vital organ systems, which rely on proteins, carbohydrates and fatty acids for their proper functioning . . . effectively undernourished.”

他向罗莎一家保证，在他们为利奥准备螯合治疗的同时，可以采取一系列其他疗法来纠正他的系统性失衡。其中一种名为 BioSET 的疗法，由一位脊骨神经医师设计，旨在通过酶疗法、指压按摩、顺势疗法和脊骨神经调整，清除体内"不和谐"的能量阻塞。BioSET 的发明者声称，这对患有孤独症等慢性疾病的人尤为重要，因为他们的身体系统变得"混乱"，导致"依赖蛋白质、碳水化合物和脂肪酸才能正常运作的重要器官系统……实际上处于营养不良状态"。



Leo’s doctor happened to know of a skilled BioSET practitioner who had an office just down the block and said that he would provide the Rosas with a referral. He also furnished them with a list of vitamin and mineral supplements that he offered to sell them directly from his office. This one-stop-shopping approach is common in the biomed community. The founder of the Great Plains Laboratory, for example, also runs a supplement company called New Beginnings Nutritionals that specializes in products with names like BrainChild, Spectrum Support II, and Bio-Chelat. If Great Plains tests show deficiencies in certain minerals, supplies of the relevant supplements are just a click away. The New Beginnings website also features a video clip of a mother named Lori Knowles who tells the story of her son Daniel’s recovery from autism, which she attributes to his GFCF diet, chelation, and an extensive supplement program. (A voiceover briefly mentions that the boy also had years of intensive ABA and speech therapy.) “The dream of the child you had just goes up in a puff of smoke” after an autism diagnosis, Knowles says. But since Daniel’s recovery, “he looks and acts just like any normal boy,” she adds proudly, before the camera cuts to a scene of him immersed in a video game. Knowles is the general manager of New Beginnings.

利奥的医生恰好认识一位技艺精湛的生物能量同步疗法（BioSET）医师，其诊所就在同一条街上，并表示可以为罗萨斯夫妇开具转诊单。他还提供了一份维生素和矿物质补充剂清单，声称可以直接从诊所出售给他们。这种一站式购物方式在生物医学界十分常见。例如，大平原实验室（Great Plains Laboratory）的创始人同时经营着一家名为"新起点营养品"（New Beginnings Nutritionals）的补充剂公司，专门销售诸如"脑力宝贝"（BrainChild）、"谱系支持 II"（Spectrum Support II）和"生物螯合"（Bio-Chelat）等产品。如果大平原实验室的检测显示某些矿物质缺乏，只需轻点鼠标即可购买相关补充剂。新起点网站上还展示了一段视频：一位名叫洛莉·诺尔斯（Lori Knowles）的母亲讲述儿子丹尼尔（Daniel）从孤独症中康复的故事，她将其归功于无麸质无酪蛋白（GFCF）饮食、螯合疗法以及一套全面的补充剂方案。（画外音简短提及，这个男孩还接受了多年的密集应用行为分析（ABA）和言语治疗。）诺尔斯说，在确诊孤独症后，"你对孩子曾有的梦想瞬间化为乌有"。 但自从丹尼尔康复后，“他看起来和表现得就像任何一个正常男孩一样，”她自豪地补充道，随后镜头切换到他在玩电子游戏的场景。诺尔斯是“新起点”的总经理。



The Rosas walked out of the doctor’s office reeling but resolved—out with the gluten-infested Goldfish, the hyperallergenic peanut butter, and the yeast-producing jellies; in with the rice bread, almond butter, GFCF pancake mix, cod liver oil, K-Mag Aspartate, probiotics, CoQ10, B-12, zinc, selenium, digestive enzymes, glutathione cream, folic acid, antifungals, and immune-boosting mushroom extract. The monthly bill for these foods and supplements was enormous, on top of the thousands that the Rosas paid each month for ABA and other therapies. But they saw these expenses as crucial investments in Leo’s future.

罗萨斯夫妇跌跌撞撞地走出医生办公室，虽然震惊但决心已定——他们清除了含麸质的金鱼饼干、高度过敏的花生酱和产生酵母的果冻；换上了米面包、杏仁酱、无麸质无酪蛋白煎饼粉、鳕鱼肝油、K-Mag 天冬氨酸盐、益生菌、辅酶 Q10、维生素 B12、锌、硒、消化酶、谷胱甘肽乳霜、叶酸、抗真菌药物以及增强免疫力的蘑菇提取物。这些食物和补充剂的每月账单数额巨大，再加上罗萨斯夫妇每月为应用行为分析和其他疗法支付的数千美元。但他们将这些开支视为对利奥未来的关键投资。



The popularity of biomedical treatments for autism mirrored the general rise of interest in so-called complementary and alternative medicine in recent decades. By the first years of the twenty-first century, the trade in high-dose vitamins and supplements had become an economic powerhouse, with annual sales topping $33 billion. Americans now consult their homeopaths, naturopaths, herbalists, acupuncturists, chiropractors, and Reiki workers more often than they see their primary care physicians. Up to three quarters of all autistic children in the United States receive some form of alternative treatment, with dietary interventions often beginning even before their diagnosis.

近年来，针对孤独症的生物医学治疗日益流行，这与所谓补充与替代医学在近几十年间受到的普遍关注相呼应。到 21 世纪初，高剂量维生素和补充剂的交易已成为经济支柱，年销售额突破 330 亿美元。如今，美国人咨询顺势疗法师、自然疗法师、草药师、针灸师、脊椎按摩师和灵气治疗师的频率，甚至超过了他们看初级保健医生的次数。在美国，多达四分之三的孤独症儿童接受某种形式的替代治疗，而饮食干预往往在确诊前就已开始。



Soon after visiting the doctor in Los Altos, Shannon was holding her wriggling son in her lap as a BioSET therapist cleared energy blocks from his meridians by applying an electrical current along his spine. The therapist promised that when these blockages were removed, Leo’s sensitivities to dozens of problem foods would be reduced—but they had to be eliminated one at a time, at a cost of $70 per session. None of this would have been possible without more help from Craig’s parents. When Craig told his father, Marty, about Leo’s treatment regimen, Marty asked to see some of the medical literature they were reading that supported it. Craig sent him a list of websites. After poring through them, Marty told his son quietly, “This is not the science that I learned in med school.”

在洛斯阿尔托斯看完医生后不久，香农将扭动不安的儿子抱在膝上，一位生物系统能量治疗师正沿着他的脊柱施加电流，以清除经络中的能量阻塞。治疗师承诺，当这些阻塞被清除后，利奥对数十种问题食物的敏感度会降低——但必须逐一消除，每次疗程费用为 70 美元。若非克雷格的父母提供更多帮助，这一切都无法实现。当克雷格将利奥的治疗方案告诉父亲马蒂时，马蒂要求查看他们正在阅读的、支持该疗法的医学文献。克雷格发给他一份网站清单。马蒂仔细研读后，轻声对儿子说："这可不是我在医学院学到的科学。"



But that was precisely the point. After studying autism for decades, mainstream medicine had failed to come up with a gold standard of treatment. Usually, the next step of receiving a difficult diagnosis from your doctor is the moment she gives you a reassuring look and says, “But here’s what we can do. Here are the next steps we can take.” For the parents of kids like Leo, that moment never seemed to come. Within the biomed community, however, there were dozens of next steps you could take.

但这正是问题的关键所在。主流医学界研究孤独症数十年，却始终未能制定出黄金标准的治疗方案。通常，当医生告知你一个棘手的诊断结果后，下一步她会用安抚的眼神看着你说："但我们可以这样做。这是接下来可以采取的步骤。"对于像利奥这样的孩子的父母来说，这个时刻似乎从未到来。然而在生物医学领域，却有数十种可以采取的后续方案。



—



ONE OF THE FIRST THINGS that Shannon and Craig decided to do was to stop vaccinating their children. Having lived in Ghana, where she saw the terrible human cost of the great pandemics with her own eyes, Shannon felt conflicted about turning her back on what she had previously thought of as the signature triumph of public health in the twentieth century. But watching Leo struggle to express himself and learn basic life skills, she was determined not to inflict the same fate on her other kids. She agreed to give birth to India in the hospital only after extracting assurance from her doula that no one would try to immunize her newborn without her permission.

香农和克雷格决定做的第一件事就是停止给孩子们接种疫苗。曾在加纳生活的香农亲眼目睹了重大疫情带来的可怕人道代价，因此对于背弃自己曾视为二十世纪公共卫生标志性成就的疫苗接种，她内心充满矛盾。但看着利奥艰难地表达自己、学习基本生活技能，她决心不让其他孩子也遭遇同样的命运。她同意在医院生下英迪娅，前提是她的导乐向她保证，未经她允许，没有人会试图给她的新生儿接种疫苗。



When Shannon’s mother asked her if she was worried that her next child might turn out to be autistic too, she put on a brave face, but she broke down sobbing alone in the car afterward. As Leo went through one of his cycles of particularly difficult behavior, she wrote a journal entry to her daughter in utero:

当香农的母亲问她是否担心下一个孩子也可能患有孤独谱系障碍时，她强装镇定，但随后独自在车里崩溃痛哭。当利奥经历又一轮特别棘手的行为周期时，她给腹中的女儿写了一篇日记：



Trying to be optimistic. Little nugget, I am pleased that you are here. We are 8½ weeks along, you and I. Let’s stick it out. I will breastfeed you until kindergarten, and will keep you away from all those nasty vaccines. You will be perfect.

努力保持乐观。小宝贝，我很高兴你在这里。我们已相伴八周半，你和我。让我们坚持下去。我会母乳喂养你直到幼儿园，让你远离所有那些讨厌的疫苗。你一定会完美无缺。



She also launched a war against mandatory vaccination on her blog. In the face of an incendiary public debate about vaccines sparked by gastroenterologist Andrew Wakefield’s controversial claims that the MMR was driving a worldwide epidemic of a “leaky gut” condition he called autistic enterocolitis, media outlets tried to provide a fair and balanced view. When People magazine ran an article called “Desperate Measures” that quoted antivaccine activist Lyn Redwood alongside a doctor who treated a boy who died of the measles—while pointing out that rates of diagnosis were still climbing in Sweden, where thimerosal had been removed from vaccines in 1993—Shannon tore the author apart online for “fearmongering and misinformation.”

她还在博客上发起了一场反对强制接种疫苗的运动。面对胃肠病学家安德鲁·韦克菲尔德引发的关于疫苗的激烈公开辩论——他声称 MMR 疫苗正在全球范围内引发一种他称之为“孤独谱系肠炎”的“肠漏症”流行病——媒体试图提供公平平衡的报道。当《人物》杂志发表题为《绝望措施》的文章，引用反疫苗活动家林·雷德伍德的话，同时采访了一位治疗过一名死于麻疹的男孩的医生——并指出在 1993 年就已从疫苗中去除硫柳汞的瑞典，诊断率仍在上升——香农在网上痛斥该文作者“散布恐慌和错误信息”。



After India was born, the Rosas’ pediatrician pushed back on Shannon’s plan to either exempt her kids from immunization or have the MMR shots be administered individually. But she held her ground and he eventually kicked the family out of his practice. Shannon also enrolled India in a study of the siblings of autistic children at UC Davis’s MIND Institute. She later found a female pediatrician who told her that she would be willing to let her follow any modified vaccine schedule that the institute suggested.

印度出生后，罗萨斯家的儿科医生反对香农计划让孩子免于接种疫苗或单独接种 MMR 疫苗。但她坚持己见，最终医生将他们一家从自己的诊所除名。香农还让印度参加了加州大学戴维斯分校 MIND 研究所关于孤独谱系儿童兄弟姐妹的研究。后来她找到一位女儿科医生，对方表示愿意让她按照该研究所建议的任何改良疫苗接种方案行事。



V



Trading his PB & J sandwiches for GFCF pancakes without syrup made Leo a sad boy. He responded to his new regime by howling at the kitchen table as Shannon daydreamed about spiriting him away to a mountain aerie where he would eat only foods on the approved list. She consoled herself with the knowledge that for a child who ate a drastically limited menu by choice, he was now eating a much healthier diet, and his chronic diarrhea had finally stopped. Leo also occasionally surprised his mother by gulping down potions she never thought he’d tolerate, like cocktails of cod liver oil blended with watered-down pear juice. Now it was the sweetest thing he was allowed to consume.

利奥用无麸质无酪蛋白的无糖煎饼换掉花生酱果酱三明治后变得闷闷不乐。每当香农幻想带他逃到高山巢穴只吃批准清单上的食物时，他就在餐桌旁嚎啕大哭。她安慰自己：对于一个自愿饮食极度受限的孩子来说，他现在吃得健康多了，慢性腹泻也终于停止了。利奥偶尔还会让母亲惊喜——他大口喝下她原以为他绝不会接受的药水，比如混合了稀释梨汁的鳕鱼肝油鸡尾酒。如今这已是他被允许摄入的最甜的东西。



Like generations of autism parents before her, Shannon became a minute observer of her son’s behavior, filling notebooks and charts with his reactions to every tweak in his recovery program, hunting for elusive threads of causation in a dense web of correlations. She plotted Leo’s pills, elixirs, capsules, creams, and shots on a grid—an impressive twenty-five items at that point.

像她之前的几代孤独症家长一样，香农成了儿子行为的细致观察者，在笔记本和图表中记录他对康复计划每项调整的反应，试图在错综复杂的关联网络中寻找难以捉摸的因果线索。她在表格上标注了利奥服用的药片、药水、胶囊、药膏和注射剂——当时已多达令人惊叹的二十五种。



Leo’s doctor was happy with an apparent decrease in his hyperactivity but warned the Rosas that if they didn’t begin at least oral chelation soon to flush the mercury from his brain, he could end up permanently impaired. To go this route, however, Leo would have to start loading up on supplements, because the chelation process leaches out essential minerals with the heavy metals. The problem was that Leo’s BioSET practitioner had detected sensitivities to several of his supplements, which would have to be dealt with first—in twice-weekly sessions—before he could begin ramping up on minerals.

利奥的多动症明显减轻，医生对此感到满意，但警告罗莎夫妇：如果不尽快开始至少口服螯合疗法来清除他大脑中的汞，他可能会留下永久性损伤。然而要采用这种方法，利奥必须开始大量补充营养素，因为螯合过程在排出重金属的同时也会带走必需矿物质。问题在于，利奥的生物能量医学治疗师发现他对某些补充剂存在敏感反应——必须先通过每周两次的疗程处理这些问题，然后才能开始补充矿物质。



Shannon’s to-do list kept getting longer and longer, not even counting the many hours a week that were required to coordinate her son’s speech and OT sessions, which she often had to cancel or cut short to accommodate Leo’s BioSET schedule. She couldn’t believe how much work taking care of an autistic child could be—but if it resulted in his recovery, she told herself, it would all be worth it.

香农的待办事项清单越来越长，这还不包括每周需要花费大量时间协调儿子的语言治疗和作业治疗课程——她常常不得不取消或缩短这些课程，以便配合利奥的生物能量敏感治疗（BioSET）安排。她简直不敢相信照顾一个孤独谱系的孩子需要付出如此多的精力——但她告诉自己，只要能让儿子康复，这一切就都值得。



During one of Leo’s BioSET treatments, Shannon happened to glance at a photograph on the therapist’s desk and remembered that the doctor up the street had the identical photo of the same boy on his own desk. It turned out that the doctor and the therapist were a couple, and the boy in the picture was their son. But the doctor hadn’t said a word about their relationship when he made his referral, and the therapist hadn’t mentioned it either. It was a tidy little arrangement they had, referring clients to each other for expensive treatments that Leo seemed to need more of all the time if he wasn’t going to face a lifetime of disability. For her son’s sake, Shannon tried to put this awkward thought out of her mind.

在利奥接受生物能量同步疗法（BioSET）的一次治疗中，香农无意间瞥见治疗师桌上的一张照片，突然想起街角那位医生的办公桌上也摆着同一男孩的相同照片。原来医生和治疗师是一对夫妻，照片中的男孩正是他们的儿子。但医生在推荐治疗时对两人的关系只字未提，治疗师也从未提及。他们之间形成了某种默契的安排——互相推荐客户接受昂贵的治疗，而利奥若不想终身残障，似乎需要越来越多这样的治疗。为了儿子，香农努力将这个令人不安的念头抛诸脑后。



—



A FEW MONTHS LATER, the Rosas returned to the doctor’s office in Los Altos for another round of lab results and consultations. Unfortunately, the new batch of printouts showed that the Candida in Leo’s gut was more rampant than ever, along with thriving colonies of Lactobacillus, gamma and beta Streptococci, and an infestation of non-lactose-fermenting E. coli. As a result, the doctor said, Leo’s GI tract was seriously inflamed, which would require a new round of probiotics and enzymes. Now his almond butter—a poor substitute for his preferred PB, but better than nothing—also had to go.

几个月后，罗萨斯一家再次来到洛斯阿尔托斯的诊所，等待新一轮化验结果和咨询。不幸的是，新打印的报告显示，利奥肠道内的念珠菌比以往更加猖獗，同时乳酸杆菌、γ型和β型链球菌大量繁殖，还出现了不发酵乳糖的大肠杆菌感染。医生表示，利奥的胃肠道因此严重发炎，需要新一轮的益生菌和酶制剂治疗。如今，连他吃的杏仁酱——虽然远不如他钟爱的花生酱，但聊胜于无——也必须停用了。



And there was more bad news. The latest hair test showed that Leo’s body was now excreting low levels of mercury. The doctor explained that this meant that the neurotoxins were building up in his system again, threatening to reverse all the progress he’d made in the past year. This development made Leo an urgent candidate for IV chelation, which the doctor said he would be happy to provide in the back room.

坏消息还不止于此。最新的头发检测显示，利奥体内正在排出低浓度的汞。医生解释说，这意味着神经毒素正在他的系统中重新积聚，可能逆转过去一年取得的全部进展。这一情况使利奥急需接受静脉螯合治疗，医生表示很乐意在诊室后间为他提供这项治疗。



Craig had been trying to set aside his doubts about chelation for months. “I spent a long time trying to get to the root of this research,” he says. “It sounded like science: polymorphisms, environmental triggers, oxidative stress, molecules passing through the blood-brain barrier, glutathione, methylation, and the constant through-line of mercury detoxification. I read these reports and thought, ‘My God, I’ve got to figure this out.’”

克雷格几个月来一直试图放下对螯合疗法的疑虑。“我花了很长时间试图追查这项研究的根源，”他说，“它听起来很科学：多态性、环境诱因、氧化应激、穿越血脑屏障的分子、谷胱甘肽、甲基化，以及贯穿始终的汞解毒线索。我读着这些报告，心想：‘天哪，我必须弄清楚这件事。’”



The vaccination issue was particularly confusing for Craig, because he’d been reading a new batch of studies challenging the validity of Wakefield’s paper and the mercury/autism hypothesis in general. A study in Japan found that rates of autism diagnosis continued to rise steeply even after the combination MMR was replaced by single vaccines. Another study in Hong Kong found that mercury levels in the bloodstreams of kids with autism were not significantly higher than those in typical children.

疫苗接种问题尤其让克雷格感到困惑，因为他一直在阅读一批新的研究，这些研究质疑韦克菲尔德的论文以及汞/孤独症假说的有效性。日本的一项研究发现，即使在联合 MMR 疫苗被单一疫苗取代后，孤独症的诊断率仍在急剧上升。香港的另一项研究发现，孤独症儿童血液中的汞含量并未显著高于普通儿童。



Even overlooking the fact that the link between mercury exposure and autism was still unproven, it was impossible to tell from reading Leo’s lab reports if the levels detected by the tests were truly dangerous or even significantly elevated from normal background exposure. Since the labs also specified the alleged baseline levels, it was easy for parents to assume that any level above that was evidence of toxicity—an assumption actively encouraged by DAN! doctors.

即便忽略汞暴露与孤独症之间的关联尚未得到证实这一事实，仅从利奥的实验室报告中也无法判断检测到的汞含量是否真正危险，甚至是否显著高于正常背景暴露水平。由于实验室还标明了所谓的基线水平，家长们很容易认为任何高于该水平的数值都是中毒的证据——而 DAN！医生们则积极鼓励这种假设。



Meanwhile, rates of measles infection were spiking in England, Ireland, Wales, the United States, Italy, and other countries. In a few years, MMR coverage in some parts of London would fall to 50 percent, from a high of 92 percent just before Wakefield made headlines with his paper. Measles would eventually be declared endemic again in England for the first time in years, with 1,348 cases recorded in 2008, up from a mere 56 cases in 1998. One in ten of these children was sick enough to require hospitalization.

与此同时，英国、爱尔兰、威尔士、美国、意大利等国的麻疹感染率正在飙升。几年后，伦敦部分地区的 MMR 疫苗接种覆盖率将从韦克菲尔德发表那篇引发轰动的论文前的 92%高点骤降至 50%。麻疹最终在英国被宣布再次成为地方性流行病，这是多年来首次——2008 年记录到 1348 例病例，而 1998 年仅有 56 例。这些患病儿童中，每十人就有一人病情严重到需要住院治疗。



Still, Leo was their child, and the Rosas had to do what was best for him. Craig told his father that they were considering intravenous chelation on the advice of their doctor and sent him a paper outlining the DAN! consensus on heavy-metal toxicity. Marty replied with a lengthy letter in which he expressed his concerns. “To imply that there are similar symptoms between mercury poisoning and autism may be true, but that does not necessarily mean that they are the same,” he wrote. “Many of the physicians in the symposium have autistic children. I think that these folks may be somewhat biased and willing to grasp at something that looks possible, NOW! This is totally understandable, as we all hope that there is magic treatment that will heal our little boy, Leo. However, after reading many more scientific papers, I am not encouraged that we can put our hopes on chelation and food supplements.”

尽管如此，里奥毕竟是他们的孩子，罗萨斯夫妇必须为他做出最好的选择。克雷格告诉父亲，他们正考虑听从医生的建议进行静脉螯合疗法，并寄给他一份概述 DAN！共识关于重金属毒性的文件。马蒂回了一封长信，表达了自己的担忧。"说汞中毒和孤独症有相似症状或许没错，但这并不代表两者是同一回事，"他写道，"研讨会上的许多医生都有孤独谱系的孩子。我认为这些人可能有些偏见，急于抓住看似可行的方案，现在！这完全可以理解，因为我们都希望有神奇的疗法能治愈我们的小里奥。然而，在阅读了更多科学论文后，我并不认为我们可以把希望寄托在螯合疗法和营养补充剂上。"



He added that, as a health care professional, he was unnerved by the sheer number of disclaimers in the DAN! report. These ranged from an admission that “the theories and medical models on which these therapies are based are not universally accepted,” to the fact that “no well-controlled outcome studies have yet been performed,” to the sobering note that the therapies described “may potentially make some autistic children significantly worse.” Marty concluded his letter by saying that he felt the risks to Leo were too high.

他补充说，作为一名医疗专业人士，DAN!报告中大量的免责声明让他感到不安。这些声明包括承认“这些疗法所依据的理论和医学模型并未得到普遍接受”，以及“尚未进行过任何严格对照的结果研究”，还有令人警醒的说明——所描述的疗法“可能会使一些孤独谱系儿童的情况显著恶化”。马蒂在信的最后表示，他认为利奥面临的风险太高了。



Craig and Shannon kept reading encouraging stories online about kids who had recovered to the point of losing their diagnosis, but their son’s trajectory seemed to be much more uneven. At times, he took encouraging steps forward, but those advances seemed more related to progress in his other therapies than whatever supplements had been added to his list that month. On other days, Leo seemed to take three steps back.

克雷格和香农在网上不断读到一些令人鼓舞的故事，讲述孩子们康复到不再符合诊断标准的地步，但他们儿子的发展轨迹似乎要坎坷得多。有时，他会迈出令人鼓舞的前进步伐，但这些进步似乎更多地与他其他疗法的进展有关，而不是与当月给他添加的补充剂有关。而在其他日子里，利奥似乎又退步了。



With so many interventions going on simultaneously, it was difficult for them to accurately gauge the effectiveness of any single one. Only by going over her records carefully was Shannon able to determine that the abrupt cessation of Leo’s diarrhea was related not to the changes in his diet but to stopping the antibiotic that he’d been taking for ear infections. But she plowed on, terrified of missing the window when biomedical interventions could make a significant impact on his future.

同时进行如此多的干预措施，他们很难准确评估其中任何一项的效果。香农只有仔细查阅记录后，才确定利奥腹泻的突然停止与饮食变化无关，而是因为停用了治疗耳部感染的抗生素。但她仍坚持推进，生怕错过生物医学干预能对他未来产生重大影响的关键窗口期。



VI

第六章



One thing became clear: Leo’s new regime was making him miserable. He had always seemed to look forward to mealtimes with his family, but now he dragged himself to the table with a disconsolate look on his face. Sometimes he just started throwing his food on the floor. There was one way, however, that his diet accelerated his acquisition of expressive language: he started begging Shannon for specific foods that she didn’t even know he knew the names of, like yogurt and watermelon.

有一点变得清晰：利奥的新治疗方案让他痛苦不堪。他过去似乎总是期待与家人共进餐食，但现在却拖着脚步走到餐桌前，脸上带着沮丧的神情。有时他甚至直接把食物扔在地上。不过，他的饮食在加速语言表达能力发展方面确实有一个作用：他开始向香农讨要那些她甚至不知道他认识名称的特定食物，比如酸奶和西瓜。



He had always been an exceptionally cheerful boy, even with the many practical challenges he faced daily. Now he seemed to be in a continuous state of rebellion against the hourly swallowing of pills, the endless fussing over the contents of his diapers, and the nightly administration of a vitamin B-12 shot. He looked as exhausted as his parents felt.

他一直是个异常开朗的男孩，尽管每天要面对诸多现实挑战。如今他似乎持续处于反抗状态——反抗每小时吞服药片、没完没了地检查尿布内容、以及每晚注射维生素 B12。他看起来和父母一样疲惫不堪。



On the Rosas’ next pilgrimage to Los Altos, the doctor inevitably brought up chelation. But this time Craig challenged him. “Wait a second,” he told the doctor. “You’re telling me that the recommended course of action for a low reading of mercury toxicity is chelation?” “Yes,” the doctor replied. “And the recommended course of action for high mercury toxicity is chelation?” The doctor nodded yes again. Finally Craig asked him, “Is there any sort of outcome that would contraindicate chelation?” And the doctor said, “No.”

在罗萨斯家族下一次前往洛斯阿尔托斯的朝圣之旅中，医生不可避免地提到了螯合疗法。但这次克雷格向他提出了质疑。"等等，"他对医生说，"你的意思是，对于汞中毒指数偏低的情况，建议采取的治疗方案是螯合疗法？""是的，"医生回答。"那么对于汞中毒指数偏高的情况，建议的治疗方案也是螯合疗法？"医生再次点头确认。最后克雷格问他："是否存在任何不适用螯合疗法的情况？"医生回答说："没有。"



At that point, Craig and Shannon said, “Thank you very much,” walked out of the doctor’s office, and never went back to Los Altos to see him again.

那时，克雷格和香农说了声“非常感谢”，便走出了医生的办公室，从此再也没回洛斯阿尔托斯找他看病。



—



LEO WAS NOWHERE CLOSE to recovery, but he was thriving in his own ways. He made a deep connection with his ABA therapist, Fiona, a sunny Australian redhead with a no-nonsense manner both sweet and firm. For twenty-five hours a week, she worked with him on mastering simple tasks like greeting people if they walked into a room, correctly naming the parts of his body (he was up to twenty-one), and being able to dress and undress himself. Instead of aiming to extinguish Leo’s autistic behaviors, as the Maurices’ therapist had done, Fiona focused on teaching him skills that would enable him to care for himself and express his desires and preferences more effectively.

利奥离康复还差得远，但他以自己的方式茁壮成长。他与 ABA 治疗师菲奥娜建立了深厚的关系，菲奥娜是一位阳光开朗的澳大利亚红发女性，态度直率，既温柔又坚定。每周二十五小时，她教他掌握简单的任务，比如有人走进房间时打招呼、正确说出身体各部位的名称（他已经能说出二十一个部位），以及学会自己穿脱衣服。与莫里斯家的治疗师试图消除利奥的孤独谱系行为不同，菲奥娜专注于教他技能，让他能够照顾自己，并更有效地表达自己的愿望和偏好。



The beginning of autonomy is being able to communicate yes and no, something that Leo had been unable to do months earlier. Now if he wanted to go out to the backyard, he would ask his mother or father to open the door. He could also make simple requests like “I want to sit on bean bag,” “Watch Tubbies,” and “Give me hat.” This language was also a gateway to more reciprocal social interaction. Now Leo said “my turn” when it was his time to play with a toy, and yielded the toy when he heard another child say that. He could also marshal his attention on a task for up to fifteen minutes at a stretch—an accomplishment for any kid his age.

自主性的开端在于能够表达“是”与“不”，这是几个月前的利奥无法做到的事。如今，若想去后院，他会请父母开门。他还能提出简单的要求，比如“我要坐豆袋椅”“看天线宝宝”“给我帽子”。这种语言能力也为他打开了更互动的社交之门。现在，轮到利奥玩玩具时，他会说“轮到我了”，听到其他孩子说这句话时也会主动让出玩具。他还能连续专注在一项任务上长达十五分钟——这对同龄孩子而言已是了不起的成就。



Leo would still occasionally bail on an activity by vaulting headfirst onto the couch, galloping across the room, or bursting into song. But Fiona appreciated and encouraged his natural exuberance, as did Shannon. If he got frustrated and lashed out at Fiona, she would respond with kindness and redirection by telling him what he could do instead. If he became overwhelmed, she’d let him do a less stressful activity so he could blow off steam and try again later. Within this supportive framework, Leo made progress quickly, mastering dozens of tasks in a short time.

利奥偶尔还是会突然一头栽进沙发、满屋子狂奔或放声高歌，以此逃避某项活动。但菲奥娜和香农一样，欣赏并鼓励他这种天生的活力。当他感到沮丧而冲菲奥娜发脾气时，她会用善意回应并引导他，告诉他可以怎么做。当他情绪失控时，她会让他做些压力较小的活动，让他发泄情绪，之后再重新尝试。在这个充满支持的环境中，利奥进步神速，短时间内就掌握了几十项技能。



None of this was easy. Each day required a new steep learning curve for Leo and his family. But raising him was not the soul-shattering nightmare that the writer of the Salon article, and dozens like it, had predicted. It was more like a series of practical challenges, such as knowing what to look for in an occupational therapist, finding a school for Leo that focused at least as much on discovering his strengths as on managing his behavior, and learning to walk proudly when people gave the Rosas the side-eye in public. One thing that didn’t seem to help was dwelling on the cause of his autism or pitying him as the hapless victim of a Big Pharma conspiracy.

这一切都不容易。每一天对里奥和他的家人来说都是一道陡峭的学习曲线。但抚养他长大，并非《沙龙》杂志那篇文章以及数十篇类似文章所预言的那种令人心碎的噩梦。这更像是一系列实际挑战，比如知道如何挑选合适的职业治疗师，为里奥找到一所既注重管理他的行为、也至少同样注重发掘他优势的学校，以及学会在公众场合面对他人侧目时依然昂首挺胸地走路。有一件事似乎毫无帮助：那就是纠结于他孤独症的成因，或者把他当作大型制药公司阴谋的可怜受害者来同情。



Then Shannon read a book that inspired her to start thinking differently about Leo and her own fate as his mother. Making Peace with Autism was Susan Senator’s story of raising her autistic son, Nat, and his two brothers, Max and Ben, with her husband, Ned, a software programmer. With candor and compassion, Susan described the day-by-day, practical steps that she and her husband took to cope with their son’s behavior, nurture his intelligence, and fight for his right to an education. There was no whitewashing: she described dark periods when she and Ned were in “siege mode” because their son seemed so intent on behaving in destructive ways. But by working together, they found ways to adapt to Nat’s behavior—often having to improvise, because there seemed to be no good guidebooks for raising a child like him.

后来，香农读到一本书，这本书启发她开始以不同的视角看待利奥，以及自己作为他母亲的命运。《与孤独症和解》讲述了苏珊·参议员抚养患有孤独谱系障碍的儿子纳特，以及他的两个兄弟马克斯和本，与丈夫奈德（一位软件程序员）共同生活的故事。苏珊以坦诚和同情之心，描述了夫妻二人为应对儿子行为、培养其智力、争取教育权利而采取的日常实用步骤。书中没有粉饰太平：她描述了那些黑暗时期，当她和奈德进入"围城模式"时，因为儿子似乎执意要做出破坏性行为。但通过共同努力，他们找到了适应纳特行为的方法——常常需要即兴发挥，因为似乎没有一本好的指南书能指导如何抚养像他这样的孩子。



To prepare Nat for holiday dinners with relatives and other family outings, Susan started making what she called “crisis storybooks,” illustrated with pictures cut out from magazines, so she could offer him detailed previews of what was to come—like Leo’s visual schedule. These storybooks proved to be a stunning success. At Christmas dinner with Ned’s family, Nat looked around the table and said approvingly, “Christians!”

为了让纳特准备好与亲戚共度节日聚餐及其他家庭外出活动，苏珊开始制作她所谓的“危机故事书”，用从杂志上剪下的图片作为插图，以便像利奥的视觉日程表那样，为他详细预告即将发生的事情。这些故事书取得了惊人的成功。在奈德家举办的圣诞晚宴上，纳特环视餐桌，赞许地说道：“基督徒们！”



The book promoted no theories of autism causation and promised no astonishing recovery. The climax of the story was quite different: in the middle of one of Nat’s inexplicable storms of laughter, Susan realized that, even in his most difficult moments, he was trying to communicate with her. “He was looking at me warmly,” she wrote. “Now my throat was burning—this had cracked me wide open. Oh my God. He really does it to connect with us. Just doesn’t know how, other than to annoy us.”

这本书没有宣扬任何关于孤独症成因的理论，也没有承诺任何惊人的康复效果。故事的高潮截然不同：在纳特一次莫名的大笑发作中，苏珊意识到，即使在他最艰难的时刻，他也在努力与她沟通。“他温暖地看着我，”她写道，“此刻我的喉咙发紧——这让我彻底崩溃。天哪。他这样做真的是为了与我们建立联系。只是除了惹恼我们，他不知道还能怎么做。”



Her insight proved to be a pivotal moment for the whole family: “My epiphany about Nat’s laughter would mark a profound, positive change in how we dealt with Nat and how he responded to us.” Instead of being the story of a family’s triumph over autism, Susan’s book was an account of taking the first steps of a lifelong journey of discovery with her son. “We help Nat become the best he can be, and in the process, he makes us who we are,” she wrote. “We cannot be typical, we cannot be normal. But this is certain: We are OK.”

她的洞察成为了全家人的转折点：“我对纳特笑声的顿悟，标志着我们在与纳特相处以及他回应我们的方式上，发生了深刻而积极的转变。”苏珊的书并非讲述一个家庭战胜孤独谱系的故事，而是记录了她与儿子踏上终身探索之旅的第一步。“我们帮助纳特成为最好的自己，而在这个过程中，他也塑造了我们，”她写道，“我们无法变得典型，也无法变得正常。但有一点可以肯定：我们很好。”



Making Peace with Autism marked a turning point in the Rosas’ story too. “It was the first autism parenting book I encountered that was both practical and positive,” Shannon recalls. “Susan wasn’t selling a cure, a miracle, or anything, really, except the need to let other families know they weren’t alone, and to offer them advice grounded in personal experience to smooth their paths. It was the first time I encountered an autism parent who was in acceptance mode rather than in martyr mode or resignation mode. The book helped me realize that autism would always be a part of who my son is.” Instead of referring to him as low functioning, severely affected, or profoundly impaired—the standard clinical terms for kids like him—Shannon started calling Leo her “high-octane boy” so that she wasn’t constantly defining him in terms of his deficits.

《与孤独症和解》也成为罗萨斯一家的转折点。“这是我遇到的第一本既实用又积极的孤独症育儿书，”香农回忆道，“苏珊没有兜售疗法、奇迹或任何东西，她只是想让其他家庭知道他们并不孤单，并基于个人经验提供建议，帮助他们少走弯路。这是我第一次遇到一位处于接纳状态而非殉道或放弃状态的孤独症家长。这本书让我意识到，孤独症将永远是我儿子的一部分。”她不再用“低功能”“严重受影响”或“深度障碍”这类标准临床术语来形容他，而是开始称利奥为她的“高能量男孩”，这样就不会总用缺陷来定义他。



Both sets of grandparents told Shannon they were relieved to see her stop treating her son “like a science experiment,” she says, and family outings were certainly easier to manage once she no longer had to bring along a trunk full of special foods and supplements. As Susan’s family had done for Nat, the Rosas began improvising creative ways of making connections with Leo and meeting his needs. But abandoning the hope of his recovery also came at a cost. Friends who had cheered on Shannon’s efforts to cure her “soon-to-be-nonautistic boy” turned chilly. Readers of her blog accused her of jeopardizing Leo’s future by giving up on him too quickly. The Rosas soon felt isolated again, venturing with their son into unmapped territory.

香农说，她的两对祖父母都表示，看到她不再把儿子"当作科学实验"对待，他们感到如释重负。一旦她不必再携带装满特殊食物和补品的大箱子，家庭出游确实变得更容易管理。就像苏珊一家为纳特所做的那样，罗萨斯一家开始即兴创造各种方式与利奥建立联系并满足他的需求。但放弃治愈他的希望也付出了代价。那些曾为香农治愈"即将摆脱自闭症的儿子"而欢呼的朋友们变得冷淡起来。她博客的读者指责她过早放弃，危及了利奥的未来。罗萨斯一家很快再次感到孤立无援，他们带着儿子踏入了未知的领域。



—



MANY OF THEIR PEERS were moving in the opposite direction, flocking to conferences where presenters touted the amazing curative properties of camel milk, bleach enemas, and home hyperbaric chambers, conveniently available from the vendors lining the hallways. Nearly all of the emerging online forums for parents of newly diagnosed children were dominated by concerns about mercury and vaccines; those who expressed skepticism that the combination MMR was responsible for triggering a global autism epidemic were accused of “having their heads in the sand” and “shilling for Big Pharma.” Beleaguered parents dubbed these endlessly looping arguments the Autism Wars.

他们的许多同龄人正朝着相反的方向前进，纷纷涌向那些演讲者大肆宣扬骆驼奶、漂白剂灌肠和家用高压氧舱神奇疗效的会议——这些产品恰好由走廊两侧的供应商提供。几乎所有面向新确诊儿童家长的新兴网络论坛，都被对汞和疫苗的担忧所主导；那些对麻腮风三联疫苗引发全球孤独症流行的说法表示怀疑的人，被指责为"把头埋进沙子里"和"替大药厂当托"。备受困扰的家长们将这些无休止循环的争论称为"孤独症战争"。



Then Shannon came across a blog by a classics professor named Kristina Chew, whose son Charlie was Leo’s age and like him in many ways. As a baby, he spent hours alone watching sunbeams migrate across the floor and leafing through picture books. If these reveries were interrupted, Charlie would start battering his head with his hands and burst out screaming. But he was also an athletic and tirelessly energetic boy who loved swimming, biking, and other outdoor activities. The verdict on him from other parents generally ran along the lines of “He’s a handful.”

后来，香农偶然读到古典学教授克里斯蒂娜·周的博客。她的儿子查理与里奥同龄，在许多方面都很相似。婴儿时期，查理能独自花上数小时，看着阳光在地板上缓缓移动，或是一页页翻看图画书。若这些沉思被打断，他就会用手猛击头部，发出尖叫。但他也是个热爱运动、精力充沛的男孩，喜欢游泳、骑车和其他户外活动。其他家长对他的评价通常是："这孩子真难带。"



When Charlie was diagnosed just after his second birthday, Kristina and her husband, James Fisher, tried everything they could to avert the tragic future predicted for him. They purged all traces of wheat and dairy from his diet, pored through books like Biological Treatments for Autism and PDD and Children with Starving Brains, ordered test kits from DAN!-affiliated labs, stocked up on supplements and megavitamins, started him on a regimen of antifungals, and took him to a healer on Staten Island who claimed that he could redirect the flow of Charlie’s cerebrospinal fluid by massaging the bones of his skull.

查理刚过两岁生日就被确诊为孤独症。克里斯蒂娜和丈夫詹姆斯·费舍尔竭尽全力，试图避免人们预言的那个悲惨未来。他们清除了他饮食中所有小麦和乳制品的痕迹，研读《孤独症与广泛性发育障碍的生物治疗》《饥饿大脑的儿童》等书籍，从 DAN！关联实验室订购检测试剂盒，囤积补充剂和超大剂量维生素，让他开始服用抗真菌药物，还带他去找斯塔滕岛的一位治疗师——那人声称通过按摩查理颅骨，就能改变他脑脊液的流向。



After three years of intensive therapies, on top of forty hours a week of ABA, Charlie was still essentially nonverbal and unable to care for himself. One day it occurred to Kristina that the DAN! practitioner she was seeing seemed to have little interest in even meeting her son—all she wanted to talk about was expanding her list of treatments. “I realized that I wasn’t thinking so much about what Charlie needed as what I thought I had to do as a parent,” Kristina wrote on her blog. “I had an image of what Charlie ‘should’ be. I wasn’t keeping my eyes focused on the real boy in front of me. I realized that the ‘autism wars’ were inside of me.”

经过三年密集治疗，加上每周四十小时的 ABA 训练，查理依然基本无法言语，生活也无法自理。有一天，克里斯蒂娜突然意识到，她咨询的 DAN！疗法从业者似乎连见见她儿子都兴趣寥寥——对方只想不断扩充治疗方案清单。"我意识到自己更多是在考虑作为家长'应该'做什么，而非查理真正需要什么，"克里斯蒂娜在博客中写道，"我对查理'应该'成为的样子抱有执念，却忽略了眼前真实的他。我终于明白，'孤独谱系战争'其实存在于我的内心。"



Like Susan, she decided that her efforts would be better applied to fighting for her son’s education. This task proved to be even more daunting than pursuing his recovery. Kristina and her family had to move eight times in ten years—leaving behind a tenured position at St. Peter’s University in Jersey City, where she had built up a thriving classics department—to find the right school for him. But what made it even harder was that there seemed to be so little information available about raising kids on the spectrum that didn’t view their autism as the principal problem to be surmounted, rather than tackling the practical barriers that stood in the way of fulfilling their potential.

和苏珊一样，她认为自己的精力更应该用在为儿子争取教育机会上。事实证明，这项任务比帮助他康复更加艰巨。克里斯蒂娜一家在十年间搬了八次家——放弃了泽西市圣彼得大学终身教职，她曾在那里建立起蓬勃发展的古典学系——只为给儿子找到合适的学校。但更困难的是，关于如何抚养孤独谱系儿童的信息少之又少，且大多将他们的孤独症视为需要克服的主要问题，而非解决阻碍他们发挥潜力的实际障碍。



What had parents done in the past to ensure that their children got the help and resources that they needed? What happened to kids like Charlie and Leo when they grew up? Were they all institutionalized like Raymond Babbitt? How did they end up sharing the spectrum with chatty software engineers and eccentric scientists like Temple Grandin? Even prominent experts in the field struggled to answer these basic questions. Whole chapters of autism history seemed to be . . . missing somehow.

过去家长们是如何确保孩子获得所需帮助和资源的？像查理和利奥这样的孩子长大后怎么样了？他们是否都像雷蒙德·巴比特那样被送进收容机构？他们又是如何与健谈的软件工程师和像坦普尔·葛兰汀这样古怪的科学家共享同一个谱系的？就连该领域的知名专家也难以回答这些基本问题。孤独症历史的整章整章似乎……莫名缺失了。



As a result, more than half a century after Leo Kanner announced his discovery of a “unique ‘syndrome,’ heretofore unreported” among the young patients in his Baltimore clinic, parents like Shannon, Susan, and Kristina found themselves in Year Zero, trying to cobble together hopeful futures for their children out of scraps gleaned from regional-center brochures and threads on Yahoo mailing lists, with few visible role models of autism in maturity available to help them make wise choices. Actress and model Jenny McCarthy, who became the public face of an army of “mother warriors” by publishing a trio of bestsellers about “saving” her son Evan from autism with chelation, probiotics, and other treatments, insisted that “there weren’t any” autistic adults in the past. “It’s all now.”

因此，在利奥·坎纳宣布他在巴尔的摩诊所的年轻患者中发现了一种"此前未见报道的独特‘综合征’"半个多世纪后，像香农、苏珊和克里斯蒂娜这样的父母发现自己身处"元年"，只能从区域中心宣传册和雅虎邮件列表的只言片语中拼凑出孩子充满希望的未来，而几乎没有成熟的孤独症榜样可供参考，帮助他们做出明智的选择。演员兼模特珍妮·麦卡锡通过出版三本畅销书，讲述她如何用螯合疗法、益生菌及其他方法"拯救"儿子埃文脱离孤独症，成为"母亲战士"群体的公众代言人。她坚称过去"根本没有"成年孤独症患者，"这一切都是现在才有的"。



In the shadow of the rising numbers, making peace with autism—by viewing it as a lifelong disability that deserves support, rather than as a disease of children that can be cured—seemed like a new and radical idea. In fact, it was the oldest idea in autism research. But it had been forgotten, along with the story of a brave clinician who tried to rescue the children in his care from the darkest social engineering experiment in human history.

在日益增长的统计数据阴影下，与孤独症和解——将其视为一种需要支持的终身残障，而非一种可以治愈的儿童疾病——似乎是一个新颖而激进的想法。事实上，这是孤独症研究中最古老的观点。但它已被遗忘，连同一位勇敢的临床医生的故事——他曾试图将他照料的孩子们从人类历史上最黑暗的社会工程实验中拯救出来。







---


NeuroTribes








Three

三



WHAT SISTER VIKTORINE KNEW

维克托琳修女所知之事



Once one has learnt to pay attention to the characteristic manifestations of autism, one realizes that they are not at all rare.

一旦学会关注孤独谱系的典型表现，便会发现它们其实并不罕见。



—HANS ASPERGER

——汉斯·阿斯伯格



Gottfried K. was nine and a half when his grandmother brought him to the Children’s Clinic at the University Hospital for an examination. Tall and lanky for his age, he was a good-looking boy, with strikingly handsome brown eyes. Alas, he was so physically uncoordinated, and his facial expressions were so hard to read, that the first clinician to have a look at him—a young psychologist named Anni Weiss—assumed that he was “feeble-minded.”

戈特弗里德·K 九岁半时，祖母带他到大学医院儿童诊所做检查。他比同龄人高挑瘦长，是个俊俏的男孩，长着一双格外迷人的棕色眼睛。可惜他肢体极不协调，面部表情又难以解读，以至于第一位接诊的临床医生——年轻的心理学家安妮·韦斯——认定他"智力低下"。



That wasn’t the first time that had happened, Gottfried’s grandmother told her. People often misjudged her grandson as slow and stupid. His cruel classmates christened him with a nickname that made her flush with rage: Gottfried the Fool. She knew that they were wrong about him, because he was so clever and earnest when his teachers called on him in class. But she had to admit that she, too, was often confused by his behavior.

戈特弗里德的祖母告诉她，这并非第一次发生。人们常误以为她孙子迟钝愚笨。残忍的同学给他起了个让她气得脸红的绰号："傻瓜戈特弗里德"。她知道他们错看了他，因为课堂上被老师提问时，他表现得既聪明又认真。但她不得不承认，自己也常被他的行为弄得困惑不解。



At home and in the company of adults, Gottfried seemed cheerful and content, but the smallest changes in his routine discombobulated him. When he was upset, he would start fidgeting, giggling, and chattering away in a loud voice. Because he acted the same way when he was happy, it was hard for his grandmother to tell precisely how Gottfried was feeling. He was terrified of other children, which was not surprising, considering the abuse that they heaped on him. But he also seemed unusually helpless. He would often forget to brush his teeth and bathe and required adult assistance even to tie his shoes. And he had childish fears of things that most boys his age take in stride, including dogs, loud noises, clouds, and the wind.

在家中和成年人相处时，戈特弗里德显得开朗满足，但日常作息中哪怕最细微的变化都会让他不知所措。当他心烦意乱时，就会开始坐立不安、咯咯傻笑，并大声喋喋不休。由于他开心时也会表现出同样的行为，祖母很难准确判断他的真实情绪。他极度害怕其他孩子——考虑到他们对他施加的欺凌，这并不令人意外。但他也显得异常无助，常常忘记刷牙洗澡，连系鞋带都需要成人帮助。他对大多数同龄男孩习以为常的事物怀有孩童般的恐惧，包括狗、巨响、云朵和风。



Weiss listened carefully, taking notes. She liked Gottfried’s grandmother right away, describing her as “a simple woman of sixty, good-hearted, and full of common sense.” She felt warmly toward the boy too, though she could see why his grandmother was baffled by his behavior. Weiss believed her when she insisted that her grandson was not willfully mischievous or disobedient. On the contrary, he was softhearted and naïve, and felt terribly embarrassed when his failings were pointed out to him. He just seemed constitutionally incapable of behaving appropriately in public.

魏斯仔细地听着，一边做着笔记。她立刻喜欢上了戈特弗里德的祖母，形容她是"一位六十岁的朴实妇人，心地善良，充满常识"。她对那个男孩也怀有好感，尽管她能理解为什么他的祖母会对他的行为感到困惑。魏斯相信祖母坚称孙子并非故意调皮或不听话的说法。恰恰相反，他心肠柔软、天真单纯，当自己的缺点被指出来时会感到非常难为情。他似乎天生就无法在公共场合表现得体。



His grandmother had certainly brought him to the right place—perhaps the only clinic in the world where he would get the kind of care and attention he needed. Weiss looked forward to discussing this case with her colleagues, particularly a soft-spoken pediatrician who had recently joined the staff and seemed to take a special interest in gifted, sensitive children who had been cast out by their peers. His name was Hans Asperger.

他的祖母确实带他来到了正确的地方——也许是世界上唯一能让他得到所需关怀和照料的诊所。魏斯期待与同事们讨论这个病例，尤其是那位新加入团队、说话温和的儿科医生，他似乎对那些被同龄人排斥、具有天赋且敏感的孩子特别感兴趣。他的名字叫汉斯·阿斯伯格。



—

——



IN ONE OF THE few photographs of the clinic to survive to the present day, the shy doctor and a boy sit facing one another at a table, engaged in conversation. Boyish and trim in his round-rimmed glasses, Asperger is dressed more formally than his colleagues on the other side of the room, wearing a crisp collar and tie under his white doctor’s coat. He had been encouraged to do his postgraduate work at the clinic by his thesis advisor, an influential specialist in the infectious diseases of children, Franz Hamburger.

在为数不多的留存至今的诊所照片中，这位腼腆的医生与一个男孩面对面坐在桌旁交谈。戴着圆框眼镜的阿斯伯格显得稚气而整洁，着装比房间另一侧的同事们更为正式——白色医生袍下是挺括的衬衫领和领带。他的论文导师弗朗茨·汉布格尔，一位颇具影响力的儿童传染病专家，鼓励他在这家诊所完成研究生学业。



The University of Vienna housed one of the most prestigious hospitals in a city renowned the world over for the quality of its health care. Vienna was the home of Sigmund Freud, the former neurologist whose theories of the psyche dominated popular views of the mind for nearly a century. It was also the city of Carl von Rokitansky, the pathologist who revolutionized medicine in the nineteenth century by systematizing the clinical analysis of symptoms while reminding his colleagues that they must always regard their patients with respect rather than seeing them merely as guinea pigs for their research.

维也纳大学附属医院是这座城市最负盛名的医疗机构之一，而维也纳本身就以卓越的医疗水平享誉全球。这里曾是西格蒙德·弗洛伊德的故乡，这位前神经学家提出的心理理论主导了大众对精神世界的认知近一个世纪。这里也是病理学家卡尔·冯·罗基坦斯基的城市，他在 19 世纪通过系统化临床症状分析革新了医学，同时提醒同行们必须始终以尊重对待患者，而非将其视为研究的实验品。



Doctors from all over Europe flocked to the city to observe surgeries in vast operating theaters and consult with the leading experts of the day. Each September, Hamburger offered a popular course in children’s diseases at the clinic, attended by pediatricians who arrived on steamships from America. Nicknamed “Red Vienna” in the years after World War I because of its proudly socialist government, anticlerical tradition, and housing projects for working families paid for by taxes on the wealthy, the vibrant Austrian capital hosted dozens of ongoing salons where physicians and scientists mingled with artists and musicians for wide-ranging discussions of politics, art, science, and philosophy.

来自欧洲各地的医生纷纷涌入这座城市，在宽敞的手术室里观摩手术，并与当时顶尖的专家交流。每年九月，汉伯格都会在诊所开设一门广受欢迎的儿童疾病课程，来自美国的儿科医生乘蒸汽船前来参加。第一次世界大战后的几年里，这座充满活力的奥地利首都因自豪的社会主义政府、反教权传统以及用富人税收为工薪家庭建造的住房项目而被称为“红色维也纳”。这里举办着数十场持续进行的沙龙，医生和科学家与艺术家、音乐家齐聚一堂，广泛讨论政治、艺术、科学和哲学。



Much of this cultural ferment originated in Vienna’s lively Jewish community, which dated all the way back to the twelfth century. Gustav Mahler’s music echoed from radios and concert halls, while Jewish patrons commissioned the exquisite paintings by Gustav Klimt and Egon Schiele displayed in local galleries. In the years after the First World War, one in five inhabitants of the city were Jews, as were many of the faculty members who taught at the university.

这种文化激荡很大程度上源于维也纳活跃的犹太社区，其历史可追溯至十二世纪。古斯塔夫·马勒的音乐从收音机和音乐厅中回荡，而犹太赞助人委托创作的古斯塔夫·克里姆特和埃贡·席勒的精美画作则在当地画廊展出。第一次世界大战后的几年里，这座城市五分之一的居民是犹太人，大学里的许多教职员工也是如此。



Asperger and Weiss worked on a ward at the Children’s Clinic founded in 1911 by a physician, schoolteacher, and social reformer named Erwin Lazar. His approach to special education would still be considered innovative today. Instead of seeing the children in his care as flawed, broken, or sick, he believed they were suffering from neglect by a culture that had failed to provide them with teaching methods suited to their individual styles of learning. He had an uncanny knack for spotting signs of potential in every boy and girl no matter how difficult or rebellious they were alleged to be.

阿斯伯格和韦斯曾在儿童诊所的一个病房工作，该诊所由医生、教师兼社会改革家埃尔温·拉扎尔于 1911 年创立。他对待特殊教育的方式即便在今天看来仍具创新性。他并不认为所照料的儿童存在缺陷、残缺或疾病，而是认为他们因文化忽视而遭受痛苦——这种文化未能提供适合其个人学习方式的教学方法。他拥有一种不可思议的才能，能在每个男孩女孩身上发现潜能的迹象，无论他们被描述得多么难管教或叛逆。



Lazar became adept at intuiting which career path might offer a child the best chance to live a fulfilling life while making a meaningful contribution to society. He viewed each child as embodying a particular archetype, as if the mass of humanity were organized by innate predisposition into clans or tribes, each with its own distinctive attributes. Instead of viewing the children as “patients,” he saw them as future bakers, barbers, farmers, professors, and engineers. Some seemed to belong to another time, as if they were characters from the Gothic or Renaissance eras who had been transported to the twentieth century. Others seemed older or younger than their chronological ages, or of different classes or races than their parents. The devoted members of Lazar’s staff were in awe of his ability to accurately assess each child’s situation and sum it up in a single phrase:

拉扎尔逐渐擅长凭直觉判断哪条职业道路最有可能让孩子过上充实的生活，同时为社会做出有意义的贡献。他将每个孩子视为某种特定原型的化身，仿佛人类群体天生就按禀赋被划分为不同的部落或族群，每个群体都有其独特属性。他不把孩子看作"病人"，而是视他们为未来的面包师、理发师、农民、教授和工程师。有些孩子仿佛属于另一个时代，像是从哥特时期或文艺复兴时期穿越到二十世纪的人物；另一些则显得比实际年龄更老或更年轻，或者与父母属于不同的阶层或种族。拉扎尔那些忠诚的员工们对他精准评估每个孩子处境、并用一句话概括的能力惊叹不已。



His names for the various types were always quite incisive and informed by a good sense of humor, without any disrespect toward the child. When he characterized children with one word, this was the clearest possible way of describing their particular abilities, talents, and future prospects. One instantly understood the child’s problems and the way in which they were the natural consequence of his or her personality; one understood the child’s conflicts and knew which side of the child’s personality needed to be handled with care, what challenges he could face, and how his future path could be shaped.

他对各类孩子的命名总是十分精准，且充满幽默感，却丝毫不失对孩子的尊重。当他用一个词概括某个孩子时，这便成了描述其独特能力、天赋和未来潜质最清晰的方式。人们能立刻理解这个孩子的问题所在，明白这些问题如何源于其天性；也能洞悉孩子的内心冲突，知晓其性格中哪一面需要谨慎对待，他将面临何种挑战，以及未来的人生道路该如何塑造。



By combining elements of psychology, medicine, and progressive pedagogy, Lazar developed an approach to helping each child attain his or her potential based on the nineteenth-century concept of Heilpädagogik, “therapeutic education.” Rather than treating allegedly psychological problems in isolation, Lazar aimed to turn his clinic into a microcosm of a more humane society in which the children could learn to interact in a context of mutual respect and appreciation. Such a facility, he used to say, must never be too small: “It must give every child a chance to find a comrade like himself.”

拉扎尔融合心理学、医学与进步教育学的元素，基于 19 世纪"治疗教育学"（Heilpädagogik）理念，开创了一种帮助每个孩子发挥潜能的方法。他并非孤立地处理所谓的心理问题，而是致力于将诊所打造成更人性化社会的缩影，让孩子们在相互尊重与欣赏的环境中学习互动。他常说，这样的场所绝不能太小："必须让每个孩子都有机会找到志同道合的伙伴。"



He developed these compassionate theories with a teacher and psychoanalyst named August Aichhorn, who ran a boarding school in Vienna for troubled teenagers in the years following World War I. “It had never occurred to any of us to see [the children] as delinquents or criminals from whom society had to be protected,” Aichhorn wrote in a manifesto called Wayward Youth. “For us, they were people on whom life had imposed too great a burden, whose negative attitudes and hate of society was justified; a milieu therefore had to be created for them in which they could feel at ease.” Aichhorn’s notion of the therapeutic milieu proved to be widely influential, providing a blueprint for progressive institutions all over the world.

他与一位名叫奥古斯特·艾希霍恩的老师兼精神分析师共同发展了这些富有同理心的理论。艾希霍恩在第一次世界大战后的几年里，在维也纳经营着一所面向问题青少年的寄宿学校。“我们从未想过要把这些孩子视为需要社会防范的违法者或罪犯，”艾希霍恩在一份名为《迷途青年》的宣言中写道，“对我们而言，他们是生活强加了过重负担的人，其消极态度和对社会的仇恨情有可原；因此必须为他们创造一个能感到自在的环境。”事实证明，艾希霍恩提出的“治疗性环境”理念影响深远，为全球各地的进步机构提供了蓝图。



Lazar’s special-education unit, known as the Heilpädagogik Station, was in keeping with a tradition of bold innovations at the hospital. The co-founder of the Children’s Clinic, an immunologist named Clemens von Pirquet—who developed the modern concept of allergies—was a strong advocate for women’s equality. He elevated the status of the ward maids to the level of collaborators and transformed the hospital kitchen into a laboratory for the study of nutrition, which enabled a hundred thousand starving children in the city to be fed in the years after World War I. For young patients with tuberculosis, he built a cheerful open-air pavilion on the roof. Each morning, a procession of these children would descend a winding staircase while singing choral music, on their way to classes in the hospital garden.

拉扎尔的特殊教育单元，即“治疗教育站”，延续了该医院大胆创新的传统。儿童医院的联合创始人、免疫学家克莱门斯·冯·皮尔凯——他提出了现代过敏概念——是女性平等的坚定倡导者。他将病房女工的地位提升至合作者级别，并将医院厨房改造成营养研究实验室，使得一战后的几年里，城中十万名饥饿儿童得以获得食物。他为患有结核病的年轻患者，在屋顶建造了一座明亮的露天凉亭。每天早晨，这些孩子会排着队，唱着合唱曲，沿着螺旋楼梯下楼，前往医院花园上课。



Asperger joined a tight-knit staff at the Heilpädagogik Station that included Weiss, psychiatrist Georg Frankl, psychologist Josef Feldner, and a nun named Sister Viktorine Zak who had a special way of working with unusual children. There was also a young physician on staff who specialized in treating gastrointestinal disorders, Erwin Jekelius. Meeting at each other’s apartments for roundtable discussions several times a week, Asperger and his colleagues discussed their young patients in depth, viewing each case from as many perspectives as possible. Dispensing with Aichhorn’s enthusiasm for psychoanalysis, they nourished the developing minds of their patients by engaging them with an integrated program of music, literature, nature study, drama, art, speech therapy, and athletics, coordinated by Sister Viktorine, whom Asperger praised as the “true genius” of his clinic.

阿斯伯格加入了海帕教育站一个紧密协作的团队，成员包括韦斯、精神病学家乔治·弗兰克尔、心理学家约瑟夫·费尔德纳，以及一位名为维克托琳·扎克修女——她以独特方式与特殊儿童相处。团队中还有一位专攻胃肠疾病的年轻医生埃尔温·耶克利乌斯。阿斯伯格与同事们每周数次在彼此公寓举行圆桌讨论，深入探讨年轻患者的案例，尽可能从多角度审视每个病例。他们摒弃了艾希霍恩对精神分析的热衷，通过音乐、文学、自然研究、戏剧、艺术、言语治疗和体育等综合课程滋养患者心智发展——这些课程由维克托琳修女统筹协调，阿斯伯格称赞她为诊所的"真正天才"。



Their approach to diagnosis was based on a method of intensive observation developed by Lazar. He believed that only by watching a child in course of his or her daily life—in class, at play, at the dinner table, and at rest—could the true dimensions of the child’s condition be gauged. Putting children through a battery of tests, or hauling them into an examination room, was not enough. Sister Viktorine, who worked alongside Lazar before his death, used to say that it was crucial to observe the children’s behavior “down to their very toes.” No one mastered this intimate style of observation better than Georg Frankl, a Czech who started working at the clinic in 1927 after graduating from the university. Frankl became Asperger’s chief diagnostician.

他们的诊断方法基于拉扎尔开发的一种密集观察法。他认为，只有通过观察孩子在日常生活中的表现——课堂上、游戏中、餐桌旁以及休息时——才能真正衡量孩子的状况。让孩子接受一系列测试，或把他们带进检查室，是远远不够的。在拉扎尔去世前与他共事的维克托琳修女常说，观察孩子的行为必须细致到“他们的脚趾尖”。没有人比捷克人格奥尔格·弗兰克尔更擅长这种细致的观察方式，他于 1927 年大学毕业后开始在诊所工作。弗兰克尔后来成为阿斯伯格的首席诊断专家。



Asperger would often just sit with the children, reading poetry and stories to them from his favorite books. “I don’t want to simply ‘push from outside’ and give instructions, observing coolly and with detachment,” he said. “Rather, I want to play and talk with the child, all the while looking with open eyes both into the child and into myself, observing the emotions that arise in reaction to everything that occurs in the conversation between the two of us.”

阿斯伯格常常只是和孩子们坐在一起，从他最爱的书中朗读诗歌和故事给他们听。“我不想仅仅‘从外部推动’并下达指令，冷漠而疏离地观察，”他说，“相反，我想和孩子一起玩耍、交谈，同时睁大眼睛，既观察孩子，也审视自己，留意我们两人对话中因一切互动而升起的情绪。”



Previous accounts of life in his clinic have been based on a report published in 1991 by cognitive psychologist Uta Frith, but an overlooked paper by an American psychiatrist named Joseph Michaels, who visited the Heilpädagogik Station in the mid-1930s, provides valuable insight into the ways that Asperger’s staff put their ambitious theories into practice.

此前关于他诊所生活的描述，大多基于认知心理学家乌塔·弗里斯 1991 年发表的一份报告。然而，一位名叫约瑟夫·迈克尔斯的美籍精神病学家在 20 世纪 30 年代中期曾访问过海尔帕达戈吉克站，他撰写的一篇被忽视的论文，为我们提供了宝贵的视角，揭示了阿斯伯格的员工如何将他们雄心勃勃的理论付诸实践。



Mornings began with an hour of gymnastics and exercise led by Sister Viktorine, often set to music. Then academic lessons were offered to the children.
