But when she was forced to be in the proximity of other children, she moved among them “like a strange being, as one moves among the pieces of furniture of a room,” Frankl and Cameron observed. Instead of joining in the games at the Child Study Home, Elaine would wander off by herself to gaze at pictures of elephants, alligators, and dinosaurs in books for hours. While seeming to disregard them, she managed to learn a lot about the other children, including their names, the colors of their eyes, and where each one slept at night. But instead of trying to make friends, she just wanted to sit in her room alone, entranced in a reverie of simple, familiar activities like drawing, stringing beads, or playing with blocks. As she did these things, she would utter aphorisms that sounded like surrealist poetry: Butterflies live in children’s stomachs, and in their panties, too. Gargoyles have milk bags. Men cut deer’s leg. Dinosaurs don’t cry.

伊莱恩热爱动物，有时会趴在地上模仿它们的叫声。母亲在她房间里摆满了玩具狗和兔子，她把这些玩具当作朋友。但当她被迫与其他孩子相处时，弗兰克尔和卡梅伦观察到，她穿梭在他们中间"像个异类，如同在房间里移动家具一般"。在儿童之家的游戏活动中，伊莱恩总是一个人溜走，花上几个小时翻看书中大象、鳄鱼和恐龙的图片。虽然看似对其他孩子漠不关心，她却默默记住了许多细节——他们的名字、眼睛的颜色，以及每个人晚上睡在哪里。但她并不想交朋友，只想独自坐在房间里，沉浸在简单熟悉的活动中，如画画、串珠或搭积木。做这些事时，她会说出一些像超现实主义诗歌般的箴言：蝴蝶住在孩子的肚子里，也住在他们的内裤里。滴水嘴兽有奶袋。男人砍断鹿腿。恐龙不会哭泣。



In May, the Tripletts returned to Baltimore for another visit. Donald climbed over tables, smeared food in his hair, and threw books into the toilet. But he was making significant progress at home, despite the fact that he was receiving no particular “treatment.”

五月，特里普利特一家再次回到巴尔的摩探访。唐纳德爬过桌子，把食物抹在头发上，还把书扔进马桶。但他在家中的进步显著，尽管他并未接受任何特别的“治疗”。



For the next three years, Mary kept in touch with Kanner by mail, sending him regular reports on her son’s development.

接下来的三年里，玛丽通过信件与坎纳保持联系，定期向他汇报儿子的成长情况。



September, 1939. He continues to eat and to wash and dress himself only at my insistence and with my help. He is becoming resourceful, builds things with his blocks, dramatizes stories, attempts to wash the car, waters the flowers with the hose, plays store with the grocery supply, tries to cut out pictures with scissors. Numbers still have a great attraction for him. While his play is definitely improving, he has never asked questions about people and shows no interest in our conversation . . .

1939 年 9 月。他仍然只在坚持和我的帮助下才吃饭、洗漱和穿衣。他变得足智多谋，用积木搭建东西，表演故事，尝试洗车，用水管浇花，用杂货玩开店游戏，还试着用剪刀剪图片。数字对他仍有极大的吸引力。虽然他的游戏能力明显提高，但他从未问过关于人的问题，对我们的谈话也毫无兴趣……



March, 1940. The greatest improvement I notice is his awareness of things around him. He talks very much more and asks a good many questions. Not often does he voluntarily tell me of happenings at school, but if I ask leading questions, he answers them correctly. He really enters into the games with other children. One day he enlisted the family in one game he had just learned, telling each of us just exactly what to do. He feeds himself some better and is better able to do things for himself . . .

1940 年 3 月。我注意到他最显著的改善是对周围事物的感知能力。他说话明显增多，会提出许多问题。虽然很少主动告诉我学校里发生的事，但若我提出引导性问题，他都能正确回答。他真正融入了与其他孩子的游戏。有一天，他让全家参与他刚学会的一个游戏，精确地指导每个人该做什么。他吃饭自理能力有所提升，也能更好地独立处理事务……



Kanner apologized to Mary that he had still not come up with a name for her son’s condition. “Nobody realizes more than I do myself,” he wrote, “that at no time have you or your husband been given a clear-cut and unequivocal . . . diagnostic term.”

坎纳向玛丽道歉，表示仍未为她儿子的状况找到合适的名称。他写道："没有人比我更清楚，您和您的丈夫从未得到过明确且毋庸置疑的诊断术语。"



While Kanner may still have been groping for the right name, he was quickly learning to recognize the pattern. Shortly after Beaman’s letter arrived from Mississippi, someone in his office asked the mother of a boy called Alfred L., who had been seen at the clinic back in 1935, for an update on her son’s development. Was Frankl digging through old files, looking for similar cases that had fallen through the cracks? After seeing Donald and Elaine, Kanner invited Alfred and his mother back for a follow-up visit. The boy, who was eleven years old by then, instantly recognized the doctor who had previously examined him and started bombarding him with questions about the clinic’s windows, window shades, and X-ray room. He was perturbed by the fact that each sheet of paper for recording patient histories had Johns Hopkins Hospital printed at the top. Didn’t the doctors know where they were?

尽管坎纳仍在摸索合适的命名，但他已迅速学会识别这种模式。比曼从密西西比州寄来信件后不久，他办公室的同事联系了 1935 年曾在诊所就诊的男孩阿尔弗雷德·L 的母亲，询问她儿子的最新发育情况。弗兰克尔是否正在翻查旧档案，寻找那些被遗漏的类似病例？在接诊唐纳德和伊莱恩后，坎纳邀请阿尔弗雷德和母亲回访。这个当时已十一岁的男孩立刻认出了曾为他诊治的医生，并开始连珠炮般询问诊所的窗户、窗帘和 X 光室。他对每张病历记录页顶部都印有"约翰·霍普金斯医院"字样感到困扰——难道医生们不知道自己身在何处吗？



As word spread through Kanner’s social circle of his interest in these unusual children, his colleague Wendell Muncie asked him to evaluate his daughter Bridget (changed to “Barbara K.” in Kanner’s writings to protect the Muncies’ privacy). Like Donald, she had never responded warmly to people. When her parents leaned toward her crib, cooing affectionately, she didn’t burble or scrunch up her shoulders in anticipation of being picked up. At eight years old, she was clearly very bright and wanted to know everything there was to know about pendulums, smokestacks, and military transports. But her psychiatrist father bemoaned the fact that she had “no competitive spirit” and showed “no desire to please her teacher.” When Kanner intentionally pricked Bridget with a pin, she looked fearfully at the pin and said, “Hurt!” But she didn’t seem to connect the cause of her pain with the man who held it.

随着坎纳对这类特殊儿童的兴趣在他的社交圈中传开，他的同事温德尔·芒西请他评估自己的女儿布里奇特（为保护芒西家隐私，坎纳在著作中将其化名为“芭芭拉·K.”）。和唐纳德一样，她从未对人表现出热情。当父母俯身靠近婴儿床，温柔地呢喃时，她既不会咿呀回应，也不会因期待被抱起而耸起肩膀。八岁时，她已显露出过人的聪慧，渴望了解关于钟摆、烟囱和军用运输车的一切。但身为精神病学家的父亲却哀叹她“毫无竞争意识”，且“完全没有取悦老师的意愿”。当坎纳故意用针刺布里奇特时，她惊恐地看着针头说：“疼！”却似乎并未将疼痛的根源与持针之人联系起来。



—



KANNER DISCOVERED that Donald was not the only child in this group with a phenomenal memory. Before his second birthday, Charles N. could correctly distinguish between eighteen symphonies. When his mother put on one of his favorite records, he would announce, “Beethoven.” John F. had a similar gift for recognizing melody. If his father began whistling a tune, he would promptly identify it as Mendelssohn’s violin concerto. He could also recite many prayers and nursery rhymes from memory and recall the lyrics to songs in multiple languages, which made his mother very proud. But both boys had trouble keeping their pronouns straight. If a crayon snapped in half, Charles would say, “You had a beautiful purple crayon and now it’s two pieces. Look what you did.” Until he was four and a half, John habitually referred to himself in the second person. If his parents asked him to do something, he would ignore them. He was reluctant to wave bye-bye or play patty-cake, which he could do only clumsily.

坎纳发现，唐纳德并非这群孩子中唯一拥有惊人记忆力的。查尔斯·N. 在两岁前就能准确区分十八首交响曲。每当母亲播放他最喜欢的唱片时，他会宣布："贝多芬。"约翰·F. 在旋律识别方面也有类似天赋。如果父亲开始吹口哨，他会立刻辨认出这是门德尔松的小提琴协奏曲。他还能凭记忆背诵许多祷文和童谣，并回忆起多种语言的歌词，这让母亲非常自豪。但这两个男孩在正确使用代词方面都有困难。如果蜡笔断成两截，查尔斯会说："你有一支漂亮的紫色蜡笔，现在变成两段了。看看你干的好事。"直到四岁半，约翰都习惯用第二人称指代自己。如果父母让他做某事，他会置之不理。他不愿挥手告别或玩拍手游戏，即使做起来也显得笨拙。



As much as these children seemed remote and inaccessible to other people, they were keenly attuned to the smallest changes and asymmetries in their environment. John preferred to keep all doors and windows closed, and if his mother insisted on opening a door to “pierce through his obsession,” he would slam it violently and break down in tears if she opened it again. When the parents of a boy named Frederick W. dared to rearrange some bric-a-brac on a bookshelf at home, he immediately returned it to its proper position. The cracks in the old ceiling of Kanner’s office drove Susan T. to distraction—she kept asking over and over, “Who cracked the ceiling?” and “How did it crack itself?”

尽管这些孩子对他人显得疏离而难以接近，但他们却对环境中最细微的变化和不协调之处异常敏感。约翰坚持要关紧所有门窗，如果母亲执意打开一扇门来“打破他的执念”，他就会猛力摔门，若母亲再次打开，他便会崩溃大哭。一个名叫弗雷德里克·W 的男孩，当父母擅自调整家中书架上的一些小摆设时，他立刻将它们放回原位。坎纳医生办公室旧天花板上的裂缝让苏珊·T 心烦意乱——她不停地追问：“谁弄裂了天花板？”和“它怎么会自己裂开？”



A confirmed cigar smoker, Kanner had no compunctions about puffing away in front of his young patients. But one day while he was exhaling a long plume of smoke, a boy named Joseph C. snatched the offending stogie out of his fingers and jammed it back between his lips where it “belonged.”

坎纳医生是个不折不扣的雪茄爱好者，在年轻患者面前吞云吐雾毫无顾忌。但有一天，当他正吐出一缕长长的烟雾时，一个名叫约瑟夫·C 的男孩突然从他指间夺过那支冒犯的雪茄，猛地塞回他嘴里那个“该待的地方”。



It was as if the children were constantly generating rules about how things should be based on how they were when they happened to come across them. A walk taken along a certain route one day had to be taken the same way every time after that. A random sequence of actions—such as the flushing of a toilet and the switching off of lights before bedtime—instantly became a ritual that had to be endlessly reiterated. The most humble and ordinary day-to-day events became imbued with terrifying significance.

孩子们仿佛总在根据事物偶然呈现的样子，不断生成它们"本该如此"的规则。某天沿着某条路线散步后，此后每次都必须重复相同的路径。一系列随意的动作——比如睡前冲马桶和关灯——瞬间变成必须无限重复的仪式。最平凡琐碎的日常事件，竟被赋予了令人战栗的意义。



—



EVEN IN THEIR AWKWARDNESS, irritability, and intransigence, these children struck Kanner as exceptionally beautiful. He doted on their “strikingly intelligent physiognomies,” as if the face is not just a window to the soul but into the wiring of the brain itself. His belief in their cognitive potential was tremendously consoling to their parents, who had usually been through years of fruitless searches for the pediatrician, psychiatrist, neurologist, or other specialist who could finally make sense of their son’s or daughter’s behavior. Several of these mothers and fathers were psychiatrists themselves and specifically sought out Kanner’s opinion because they refused to believe that any child of theirs could be mentally retarded—a diagnosis historically associated with the working class, immigrants, and people of color.

即使这些孩子表现得笨拙、易怒且固执，坎纳仍觉得他们异常美丽。他偏爱他们"异常聪慧的面相"，仿佛脸庞不仅是心灵的窗户，更是大脑神经回路的映射。他对这些孩子认知潜能的坚信，给他们的父母带来了莫大慰藉——这些父母通常已历经数年徒劳的求医，遍访儿科医生、精神科医生、神经科医生或其他专家，却始终无人能解释清楚子女的行为。其中几位父母本身就是精神科医生，他们专程寻求坎纳的诊断意见，只因拒绝相信自己的孩子可能智力低下——这种诊断在历史上常与工人阶级、移民及有色人种相关联。



Kanner was under no illusions that the pattern he recognized in these children was a unique product of modern times, as antivaccine activists would later claim, pointing to the development of mercury-containing fungicides and vaccine preservatives like Merthiolate in the 1920s and 1930s as the alleged source of the condition he described. As a scholar of medical history, Kanner saw references to his young patients’ predecessors scattered throughout world literature, where they were often portrayed as unwitting agents of evil and malevolent forces. He quoted this eighteenth-century account by the Swiss poet Gottfried Keller as an example of how such a child might have fared in previous generations:

坎纳很清楚，他在这些孩子身上发现的模式并非现代社会的独特产物——这与后来反疫苗活动人士的主张截然相反。后者将 20 世纪 20 至 30 年代含汞杀菌剂及硫柳汞等疫苗防腐剂的研发，视为他所描述病症的所谓根源。作为医学史学者，坎纳在世界文学作品中发现了许多与他的小患者相似的先例，这些角色常被描绘成无意识的邪恶力量载体。他引用瑞士诗人戈特弗里德·凯勒在 18 世纪的一段记述，以此说明这类儿童在更早时代可能遭遇的命运：



This 7-year-old girl, the offspring of an aristocratic family, whose father remarried after an unhappy first matrimony, offended her “noble and godfearing” stepmother by her peculiar behavior. Worst of all, she would not join in the prayers and was panic-stricken when taken to the black-robed preacher in the dark and gloomy chapel. She avoided contact with people by hiding in closets or running away from home. The local physician had nothing to offer beyond declaring that she might be insane. She was placed in the custody of a minister known for his rigid orthodoxy. The minister, who saw in her ways the machinations of a “baneful and infernal” power, used a number of would-be therapeutic devices. He laid her on a bench and beat her with a cat-o’-nine-tails. He locked her in a dark pantry. He subjected her to a period of starvation. He clothed her in a frock of burlap. Under these circumstances, the child did not last long. She died after a few months, and everybody felt relieved. The minister was amply rewarded for his efforts.

这位 7 岁的女孩出身贵族家庭，父亲在第一次婚姻不幸后另娶。她因古怪行为冒犯了那位"高贵而敬畏上帝"的继母。最糟糕的是，她拒绝参与祈祷，被带到阴暗教堂里黑袍牧师面前时更是惊恐万分。她通过躲进壁橱或离家出走来逃避与人接触。当地医生除了断言她可能精神失常外束手无策。她被交给一位以严格正统著称的牧师看管。牧师从她的行为中看到了"邪恶阴险"力量的阴谋，使用了一系列所谓的治疗手段：将她按在长凳上用九尾鞭抽打、锁进黑暗的储藏室、实施饥饿惩罚、给她穿上粗麻布裙。在这样的境遇下，这个孩子没能撑多久。几个月后她死了，所有人都松了口气。牧师因他的"努力"获得了丰厚报酬。



Now instead of being starved and scourged with whips, children like this were being herded into gas chambers in Germany, while in America they were exiled to the margins of society—like Virginia S., the slender, neatly dressed eleven-year-old daughter of a psychiatrist who had been confined to a home for the feebleminded since she was five. One day, the head of the outpatient program at the Phipps, Esther Richards, watched Virginia calmly take down a box in which the pieces from two jigsaw puzzles were jumbled together. She patiently sorted out the pieces and then deftly assembled both puzzles. The school staff assured her that Virginia was mute and likely deaf, but Richards heard her humming a Christmas hymn while pasting together paper chains.

如今，这样的孩子不再遭受饥饿和鞭打，在德国，他们被赶进毒气室；而在美国，他们被放逐到社会的边缘——就像弗吉尼亚·S，一位精神科医生的女儿，她身材纤细，衣着整洁，年仅十一岁，却从五岁起就被关进了一家专为智力低下者设立的收容所。一天，菲普斯诊所门诊项目的负责人埃丝特·理查兹看到弗吉尼亚平静地取下了一个盒子，里面装着两幅拼图混在一起的碎片。她耐心地挑出碎片，然后灵巧地将两幅拼图拼好。学校的工作人员向她保证，弗吉尼亚不会说话，很可能也是聋人，但理查兹听到她在粘贴纸链时哼唱着一首圣诞颂歌。



Kanner knew there must be many more children like Virginia, passing the empty hours in dayrooms and lockdown wards without anyone knowing who they really were. After seeing eight children who fit the pattern, he was ready to tell the world about his discovery.

坎纳知道，一定还有更多像弗吉尼亚这样的孩子，在日间活动室和封闭病房里虚度光阴，却无人知晓他们的真实面目。在见过八个符合这种模式的孩子后，他准备向世界公布他的发现。



VII



In January 1942, Ernest Harms, the editor of a new journal called The Nervous Child, asked Kanner if he would consider guest-editing an upcoming issue. Seeing an opportunity to position his work at the leading edge of a wave of research on affect disorders of childhood, Kanner intimated that he was on the verge of a major breakthrough. “I have followed a number of children who present a very interesting, unique and as yet unreported condition, which has both interested and fascinated me for quite some time,” he said. “In fact, I eventually plan to use the material for a monographic presentation.” Harms took the bait.

1942 年 1 月，新创刊《神经质儿童》的编辑欧内斯特·哈姆斯询问坎纳是否愿意担任某期特刊的客座编辑。坎纳意识到这是将自身研究置于儿童情感障碍研究浪潮前沿的契机，便暗示自己即将取得重大突破。"我追踪了一批呈现非常有趣、独特且尚未被报道的病症的儿童，"他说，"这些病例长期以来一直令我着迷。事实上，我最终计划将这些素材整理成专著。"哈姆斯果然上钩了。



Kanner’s claims that his patients’ condition was “unique” and “unreported” were a stretch, considering the volume of papers coming out on childhood schizophrenia. In fact, just a couple of months later, Despert published a paper in the debut issue of Harms’s journal describing children who were relentlessly solitary, terrified of change and novel situations, given to rigid mannerisms and rituals, fascinated by mathematics and astronomy, and gifted with prodigious memories. She even referred to their “autism” in Bleuler’s sense of the term. As usual, though, her case descriptions were muddled by her assumptions that her patients were hallucinating and suffering from the initial stages of adult psychosis.

坎纳声称他的患者状况“独特”且“前所未有”，这有些牵强，毕竟当时关于儿童精神分裂症的论文已大量涌现。事实上，仅数月后，德佩特便在哈姆斯创办期刊的创刊号上发表论文，描述了一群儿童：他们极度孤僻、恐惧变化与陌生情境、固守刻板举止与仪式、痴迷数学与天文学，并拥有惊人的记忆力天赋。她甚至沿用了布洛伊勒术语中的“孤独症”一词。然而，一如往常，她的病例描述因预设患者存在幻觉、正经历成人精神病的初期阶段而显得混乱不清。



By comparison, Kanner’s “Autistic Disturbances of Affective Contact,” published in the June 1943 issue of The Nervous Child, was a paragon of clinical clarity. By interweaving Frankl’s and Cameron’s meticulous observations, excerpts from parents’ diaries and letters, and his own reflections on his patients’ behavior, he lifted the gestalt of the syndrome out of the psychoanalytic muck and made it visible as a diagnostic entity apart from the undifferentiated mass of “pre-psychotic” children. His vivid portraits of his first eleven patients would endure as the human face of autism for another half century.

相比之下，坎纳于 1943 年 6 月发表在《神经质儿童》期刊上的《情感接触的孤独谱系障碍》一文，堪称临床清晰度的典范。通过交织弗兰克尔和卡梅伦的细致观察、父母日记与信件的摘录，以及他对患者行为的个人反思，他将这一综合征的整体轮廓从精神分析的泥沼中提升出来，使其作为独立的诊断实体，从“前精神病性”儿童的未分化群体中显现。他对首批十一名患者的生动描绘，在接下来的半个世纪里，将作为孤独症的人类面孔而长存。



—



IN A VOICE SO self-assured that he might have been speaking in the majestic plural, Kanner began, “Since 1938, there have come to our attention a number of children whose condition differs so markedly and uniquely from anything reported so far, that each case merits—and, I hope, will eventually receive—a detailed consideration of its fascinating peculiarities.”

坎纳以如此自信的口吻开口，仿佛在用庄严的复数人称说话：“自 1938 年以来，我们注意到一些儿童，他们的状况与迄今为止所报道的任何情况都截然不同且独特，以至于每个病例都值得——并且我希望最终会得到——对其迷人特质的详细审视。”



His literary background served him well. Like a poet or a novelist uncovering universal truths in the humble particularity of a life, Kanner allowed the clinical picture of autism to emerge from an accumulation of minutely observed details.

他的文学功底为他提供了极大的帮助。如同诗人或小说家从平凡生活的细微之处揭示普遍真理，坎纳通过积累细致入微的观察，让孤独症的临床图景逐渐浮现。



He was in the habit of saying almost every day, “Don’t throw the dog off the balcony.” His mother recalled that she had said those words to him about a toy dog while they were still in England. At the sight of a saucepan he would invariably exclaim, “Peter-eater.” The mother remembered that this particular association had begun when he was 2 years old and she happened to drop a saucepan while reciting to him the nursery rhyme about “Peter, Peter, pumpkin eater.”

他几乎每天都会说：“别把狗扔下阳台。”母亲回忆说，这句话是她还在英国时，曾对着一只玩具狗对他说的。一看到平底锅，他总会脱口而出：“彼得-吃货。”母亲记得，这种特定的联想始于他两岁时，当时她碰巧一边念着“彼得，彼得，南瓜吃货”的童谣，一边失手掉下了一个平底锅。



Her grammar is inflexible. She uses sentences just as she has heard them, without adapting them grammatically to the situation of the moment. When she says, “Want me to draw a spider,” she means, “I want you to draw a spider.” She affirms by repeating a question literally, and she negates by not complying.

她的语法缺乏灵活性。她使用句子时完全照搬听到的原样，不会根据当下情境进行语法调整。当她说“想要我画蜘蛛”时，实际意思是“我想要你画蜘蛛”。她通过逐字重复问题来表示肯定，而通过不配合来表示否定。



Between tests, he wandered about the room examining various objects or fishing in the wastebasket without regard for the persons present. He made frequent sucking noises and occasionally kissed the dorsal surface of his hand. He became fascinated with the circle from the form board, rolling it on the desk and attempting, with occasional success, to catch it just before it rolled off.

在测试间隙，他在房间里四处走动，检查各种物品或翻找废纸篓，完全无视在场的人。他频繁发出吮吸声，偶尔还会亲吻自己的手背。他对拼图板上的圆形产生了浓厚兴趣，在桌上滚动它，并试图在它滚落前接住——有时能成功。



Kanner felt it was premature at this point to propose a set of criteria for diagnosing the condition he described; he was still just trying to extract the salient aspects of his patients’ behavior. To make the pattern visible to his peers, he proposed two “essential common characteristics” shared by all children with this syndrome.

坎纳认为，此时提出一套诊断他所描述状况的标准还为时过早；他仍在努力提炼患者行为中的显著特征。为了让同行看清这一模式，他提出了所有患有此综合征的儿童共有的两个"基本共同特征"。



The first was a will to self-isolation, present from birth, that he called extreme autistic aloneness.

第一个是与生俱来的自我隔离意愿，他称之为极端的孤独谱系式孤僻。



The outstanding, “pathognomonic,” fundamental disorder is the children’s inability to relate themselves in the ordinary way to people and situations from the beginning of life. Their parents referred to them as having always been “self-sufficient”; “like in a shell”; “happiest when left alone”; “acting as if people weren’t there”; “perfectly oblivious to everything about him”; “giving the impression of silent wisdom”; “failing to develop the usual amount of social awareness”; “acting almost as hypnotized” . . .

最显著、“特征性”的核心障碍，是这些儿童从生命之初就无法以正常方式与他人和情境建立联系。他们的父母描述这些孩子总是“自给自足”；“像活在壳里”；“独处时最快乐”；“表现得仿佛周围没人”；“对周遭一切完全漠然”；“给人沉默智慧的印象”；“未能发展出应有的社会意识”；“行为近乎被催眠”……



There is from the start an extreme autistic aloneness that, whenever possible, disregards, ignores, shuts out anything that comes to the child from the outside. Direct physical contact or such motion or noise as threatens to disrupt the aloneness is either treated “as if it weren’t there” or, if this is no longer sufficient, resented painfully as distressing interference.

从最初就存在一种极端的孤独谱系式的孤绝状态，这种状态会尽可能无视、忽略、排斥任何来自外界的事物。直接的身体接触，或可能打破这种孤绝状态的动静、声响，要么被“当作不存在”处理，要么——若此法不再奏效——则被痛苦地视为令人反感的干扰。



The second common characteristic was a fear of change and surprise, which Kanner memorably christened an anxiously obsessive desire for the maintenance of sameness. This desire, he theorized, reflected a deep-seated anxiety that could only be kept at bay by trying to maintain the status quo.

第二个常见特征是对变化与意外的恐惧，坎纳将其生动地命名为“对维持同一性的焦虑性执念”。他推测，这种执念反映了一种深层的焦虑，唯有通过竭力维持现状才能将其遏制。



Their world must seem to them to be made up of elements that, once they have been experienced in a certain setting or sequence, cannot be tolerated in any other setting or sequence; nor can the setting or sequence be tolerated without all the original ingredients in the identical spatial or chronologic order.

他们的世界在他们看来，似乎由这样的元素构成：一旦在特定场景或顺序中被体验过，便无法容忍任何其他场景或顺序；也无法容忍缺少所有原始成分、且不按完全相同空间或时间顺序排列的场景或顺序。



At no point in the paper did Kanner give the syndrome a name, though it has been widely assumed that he did. At this point, he was still just trying to map a distinctive constellation of behavior. (In other words, it was the children’s behavior that he was calling “autistic,” not the children themselves.) Only in 1944, when Kanner produced a condensed version of his paper for Pediatrics—a journal with a much larger readership—did he christen his syndrome with the name that stuck: early infantile autism.

坎纳在论文中始终未给这一综合征命名，尽管人们普遍认为他这么做了。当时他仍在试图描绘一组独特的行为特征。（换言之，他称之为"孤独谱系"的是儿童的行为，而非儿童本身。）直到 1944 年，坎纳为读者群更广的《儿科学》期刊撰写论文精简版时，才正式将这一综合征命名为沿用至今的名称：早期婴儿孤独症。



—



KANNER’S VIEW OF AUTISM had already diverged radically from the model that Asperger and his colleagues developed in Vienna. Because Kanner focused exclusively on the first years of childhood, adults and teenagers were out of the picture entirely. Instead of presenting his syndrome as a broad spectrum with widely varying manifestations, Kanner framed his patients as a strictly defined and monolithic group, to the point of being willing to overlook significant differences between them.

坎纳对孤独症的看法，早已与阿斯伯格及其同事在维也纳提出的模型截然不同。由于坎纳只关注童年早期阶段，成年人和青少年完全被排除在他的视野之外。他并未将这一综合征呈现为一个表现多样的广泛谱系，而是将患者界定为一个严格统一、界限分明的群体，甚至不惜忽视他们之间的显著差异。



For example, he made the startling assertion that “there is no fundamental difference between the eight speaking and the three mute children.” Kanner claimed that Elaine’s surreal aphorisms, Alfred’s persistent questions about the window shades and X-ray room, and Donald’s spinning of toys on the floor were fundamentally the same thing: solipsistic forms of self-stimulation and nothing more. He presented Donald’s fascination with quantities as a purely tedious exercise, pointedly putting the word conversation in scare quotes:

例如，他惊人地断言：“八名口语者与三名无口语儿童之间并无本质区别。”坎纳声称，伊莱恩那些超现实的格言、阿尔弗雷德对窗帘和 X 光室没完没了的追问、以及唐纳德在地板上旋转玩具的行为，本质上都是同一回事：唯我论式的自我刺激，仅此而已。他将唐纳德对数量的痴迷描述为纯粹乏味的重复行为，并刻意用引号将“对话”一词括起来。



The major part of his “conversation” consisted of questions of an obsessive nature. He was inexhaustible in bringing up variations: “How many days in a week, years in a century, hours in a day, hours in half a day, weeks in a century, centuries in half a millennium,” etc., etc.; “How many pints in a gallon, how many gallons to fill four gallons?” Sometimes he asked, “How many hours in a minute, how many days in an hour?” etc. He looked thoughtful and always wanted an answer.

他“对话”的主要内容是带有强迫性质的提问，且不厌其烦地变换花样：“一周有几天？一世纪有几年？一天有几小时？半天有几小时？一世纪有几周？半个千年有几世纪？”等等等等；“一品脱等于几加仑？几加仑能装满四加仑？”有时他会问：“一分钟有几小时？一天有几小时？”等等。他总是一副若有所思的样子，并且坚持要得到答案。



Frankl’s conception of autism, however, had not changed since his days at the Heilpädagogik Station. The differences between the two men’s approaches were highlighted in dramatic fashion in the issue of The Nervous Child in which Kanner’s landmark paper appeared. In a case study of his own called “Language and Affective Contact,” Frankl offered an account of a boy named Karl K. that he clearly considered autistic, referring to him as having a “lack of contact with persons in its most extreme form.” Like Anni’s portrait of Gottfried, Frankl’s paper—which has also been overlooked for decades—opens a rare window on the expansive Viennese view of autism that ended up being overshadowed by Kanner’s more constricted model.

然而，弗兰克对孤独症的理解自他在海利帕达吉克站工作以来并未改变。两人方法上的差异在《问题儿童》期刊中戏剧性地凸显出来——该期恰好刊登了坎纳里程碑式的论文。弗兰克在其题为《语言与情感接触》的个案研究中，描述了一个他明确认定为孤独症谱系的男孩卡尔·K，称其表现出"与人接触的极端缺失"。就像安妮对戈特弗里德的描述一样，弗兰克这篇被忽视数十年的论文，为我们打开了一扇罕见的窗口，得以窥见维也纳学派对孤独谱系更为宽泛的理解——这种理解最终被坎纳更为局限的模式所遮蔽。



To draw a firm line between his syndrome and mental retardation, Kanner touted his young patients’ “intelligent-looking appearance” as evidence of their “good cognitive potential”—a notion that carried more than a trace of the eugenicists’ theory that high mental capacity is expressed outwardly as pleasing physical symmetry. Karl, on the other hand, had “primitive facial features” and a “dull expression,” Frankl noted. The boy had also never spoken a word in his life, but he was capable of understanding language: “He came when something pleasant was offered to him; he ran away when asked to do something he did not like . . . Even when amid a crowd of people, [he] behaved like a solitary person.”

为了在他的综合征与智力障碍之间划清界限，坎纳强调他的年轻患者们“聪慧的面容”是“良好认知潜力”的证据——这一观点带有优生学理论的痕迹，即高智力能力会外显为令人愉悦的身体对称性。而弗兰克尔则记录道，卡尔有着“原始的面部特征”和“呆滞的表情”。这个男孩一生从未说过一句话，但他能够理解语言：“当有人给他提供愉快的东西时，他会过来；当要求他做不喜欢的事情时，他会跑开……即使身处人群之中，他也表现得像个孤独的人。”



When Frankl first saw Karl in a children’s hospital, he was confined to a locked bed. He passed his days “in monotonous emptiness,” rocking back and forth and performing other rhythmical movements, punctuated by occasional escapes onto the open ward, where he would run “with breath-taking speed,” overturning carts and otherwise disrupting the routines of the staff. To gain a clearer understanding of the boy’s true capabilities, Frankl visited him at home in classic Kinderklinik fashion. There, where Karl “had his daily routine well established,” Frankl observed that he was more relaxed and purposeful:

当弗兰克尔第一次在儿童医院见到卡尔时，他被束缚在一张带锁的病床上。他日复一日地"在单调的空虚中"度过，前后摇晃着身体，重复着其他有节奏的动作，偶尔会逃到开放的病房里，在那里他会"以惊人的速度"奔跑，撞翻推车，扰乱工作人员的工作秩序。为了更清楚地了解这个男孩的真实能力，弗兰克尔以经典的儿童诊所方式去他家中拜访。在那里，卡尔"有着自己固定的日常作息"，弗兰克尔观察到他的状态更加放松且目标明确：



There were things he wanted to do and did regularly. He had somewhere, high up on a shelf, a place where he liked to sit; he knew where he was permitted and where forbidden to climb around, where he could find some food. His mother even allowed him to leave the house unaccompanied, as he always stayed around the house, never caused damage or ran into danger.

他有自己想做的事情，并且会规律地去做。他在高处的架子上有一个喜欢坐的位置；他知道哪里允许攀爬、哪里禁止攀爬，也知道在哪里能找到食物。他的母亲甚至允许他独自出门，因为他总在房子附近活动，从不造成破坏或陷入危险。



The boy certainly displayed the two essential characteristics (autistic aloneness and elaborate ritualistic behavior) of the syndrome that Kanner introduced to the world in the same issue of The Nervous Child. But Kanner would have likely ruled out a diagnosis of autism in his case, because Karl also suffered from tuberous sclerosis, a genetic condition that causes tumors to grow in the brain, and Kanner considered such signs of organic brain damage disqualifying. Karl also suffered from epileptic seizures, another red flag to Kanner. Epilepsy is now considered one of the most common comorbidities in autism, affecting nearly a third of the diagnosed population.

这个男孩确实表现出坎纳在同一期《神经质儿童》中向世界介绍的那一综合征的两个核心特征（孤独谱系的孤僻和复杂的仪式化行为）。但坎纳很可能会排除他的孤独症诊断，因为卡尔还患有结节性硬化症——一种导致脑部肿瘤生长的遗传性疾病，而坎纳认为这种器质性脑损伤的迹象不符合诊断标准。卡尔还患有癫痫发作，这对坎纳而言是另一个警示信号。如今癫痫被视为孤独症最常见的共病之一，影响近三分之一的确诊人群。



Frankl stressed that Karl represented only one point on a continuum that stretched from children with profound intellectual disability to “astonishing” child prodigies. But his inclusive conception of autism was about to be doomed to obscurity by the man who had saved his life from the gas chambers. As the most prominent child psychiatrist in America, Kanner was in an ideal position to popularize his own view of autism through his extensive network of personal and professional connections. After the condensed version of his paper was published in Pediatrics and then anthologized in The Year Book of Neurology, Psychiatry and Endocrinology—an annual summation of research that was widely read throughout the medical profession—a reviewer for the Quarterly Review of Biology hailed it as the “most important” article in the field of child psychiatry that year. The reviewer happened to be Wendell Muncie, the father of Kanner’s patient Barbara K.

弗兰克尔强调，卡尔只是从重度智力障碍儿童到"令人惊叹"的神童这一连续谱系上的一个点。但他这种包容性的孤独症概念，却注定要被那个曾将他从毒气室救出的人推向湮没。作为美国最杰出的儿童精神病学家，坎纳凭借其庞大的个人与专业人脉网络，完全有条件推广自己对孤独症的见解。当他的论文精简版在《儿科学》期刊发表，随后被收录进《神经病学、精神病学与内分泌学年鉴》——这份医学界广泛阅读的年度研究汇编——之后，《生物学季评》的一位评论者盛赞其为当年儿童精神病学领域"最重要"的文章。这位评论者恰巧是坎纳患者芭芭拉·K 的父亲温德尔·芒西。



Meanwhile, four months after Kanner published his paper, Asperger submitted his thesis on Autistischen Psychopathen to his advisor, Franz Hamburger. His superiors had turned their focus of their efforts from the extermination of disabled children to die Endlösung der Judenfrage—the annihilation of the Jews. When Asperger’s thesis finally appeared in print a year later, his clinic lay in ruins.

与此同时，在坎纳发表论文四个月后，阿斯伯格向导师弗朗茨·汉布格尔提交了关于自闭症精神病人的论文。此时他的上级已将工作重心从消灭残疾儿童转向"最终解决犹太人问题"——即对犹太人的灭绝。当阿斯伯格的论文在一年后终于付梓时，他的诊所已化为废墟。







---


NeuroTribes








Five

五



THE INVENTION OF TOXIC PARENTING

有毒养育的发明



One is struck again and again by what I should like to call a mechanization of human relationships.

我一次又一次地被人际关系的机械化所震撼，我愿称之为一种现象。



—LEO KANNER

——利奥·坎纳



By the time Kanner sat down to write “Autistic Disturbances of Affective Contact,” Georg and Anni Frankl were long gone from Baltimore. They had been hoping to find permanent positions at Johns Hopkins, where their years of experience would have been a boon to his ongoing research. But it was not to be.

当坎纳坐下来撰写《情感接触的孤独谱系障碍》时，格奥尔格和安妮·弗兰克尔早已离开巴尔的摩。他们曾希望能在约翰·霍普金斯大学获得永久职位，凭借多年的经验，这本可对他正在进行的研究大有裨益。然而事与愿违。



On December 4, 1940, Anni apologized to Meyer for being unable to continue attending his seminar, explaining that a position had failed to “materialize” for her at the university, compelling her to take a job in Washington State as a psychiatric social worker. “I am extremely sorry about that,” she wrote, “because [the seminar] has taught me and would have continued to teach me more than anything else in the last few years.” Meanwhile, Kanner was enlisting Meyer’s help to find a position for Georg elsewhere that would pay him more than the Child Study Home could offer. The Frankls ended up teaching in the psychology department at the University of Kansas, far from the central hub of autism research that Kanner’s office became in the 1950s.

1940 年 12 月 4 日，安妮向迈耶致歉，表示无法继续参加他的研讨会，并解释称她在大学的职位未能"落实"，迫使她前往华盛顿州担任精神科社会工作者。"我对此深感遗憾，"她写道，"因为（研讨会）在过去几年里教会我的东西，比其他任何事物都多，而且本可以继续教会我更多。"与此同时，坎纳正寻求迈耶的帮助，为格奥尔格在别处谋得一份比儿童研究之家薪酬更高的职位。弗兰克尔夫妇最终在堪萨斯大学心理学系任教，远离了坎纳办公室在 20 世纪 50 年代成为的孤独症研究核心枢纽。



As a result, Kanner was on his own as he formulated his conception of his syndrome. He credited Georg for conducting his observations of Donald and Elaine but never mentioned him in his work again. In future accounts of his momentous discovery, he focused exclusively on the “serendipity” of the Tripletts’ arrival from Forest.

结果，坎纳在构建自己的综合征概念时只能孤军奋战。他曾感谢乔治对唐纳德和伊莱恩的观察记录，但此后著作中再未提及此人。在后来关于这一重大发现的记述中，他始终只强调特里普利特一家从福里斯特镇"偶然"到来的经历。



With the Frankls’ departure, Kanner lost more than Georg and Anni’s years of perspective on his patients as living exemplars of points on a broad continuum extending into adulthood. He also lost the prescient Viennese view of the eccentricities of their parents and relatives. Where Asperger saw threads of genius and disability inextricably intertwined in his patients’ family histories—testifying to the complex genetic roots of their condition and the “social value of this personality type,” as he put it—Kanner saw the shadow of the sinister figure that would become infamous in popular culture as the “refrigerator mother.”

随着弗兰克尔夫妇的离去，坎纳失去的不仅是乔治和安妮多年来将患者视为贯穿至成年期的广泛连续谱系中鲜活例证的视角，更失去了维也纳学派对患者父母及亲属怪癖行为的先见之明。当阿斯伯格在其患者家族史中看到天才与残障的脉络密不可分——正如他所说，这证明了患者状况的复杂遗传根源，以及"这种人格类型的社会价值"——而坎纳看到的，却是后来在流行文化中臭名昭著的"冰箱母亲"这一阴森形象的阴影。



He was an astute clinical observer and a persuasive writer, but in this case his errors in interpreting his patients’ behavior had wide-reaching implications. By blaming parents for inadvertently causing their children’s autism, Kanner made his syndrome a source of shame and stigma for families worldwide while sending autism research off in the wrong direction for decades.

他是一位敏锐的临床观察者和富有说服力的作家，但在此案例中，他对患者行为的解读错误产生了深远影响。通过将儿童孤独症的成因归咎于父母的无心之失，坎纳不仅让这一综合征成为全球家庭的羞耻与污名，更使孤独症研究在数十年间偏离了正确方向。



—



FOR THE MOST PART, the couples beating a path to Kanner’s office for second, third, or fourth opinions on their children were much like Kanner himself: upper-middle-class academics who were savvy and well connected. No less than four of the fathers of his original eleven patients were psychiatrists, including Wendell Muncie. Alfred L.’s mother was a psychologist, and his father was a chemist with a law degree who worked in the U.S. Patent Office. Frederick W.’s father was a plant pathologist, and Richard M.’s father was a professor of forestry. The mothers of these patients were equally distinguished. In an era when less than one in four women in the United States completed their college education, nine of the mothers had bachelor’s or graduate degrees. Even the grandparents, aunts, and uncles of these children seemed unusually bright.

大多数情况下，那些带着孩子辗转求医、希望获得第二、第三甚至第四诊疗意见的夫妇，与坎纳本人颇为相似：都是精明强干、人脉广泛的上层中产阶级学者。他最初接诊的十一名患儿中，至少有四位父亲是精神科医生，其中包括温德尔·芒西。阿尔弗雷德·L 的母亲是心理学家，父亲则是拥有法学学位的化学家，在美国专利局工作。弗雷德里克·W 的父亲是植物病理学家，理查德·M 的父亲是林业教授。这些患儿的母亲同样出类拔萃。在那个美国女性大学完成率不足四分之一的年代，九位母亲拥有学士或研究生学历。就连这些孩子的祖父母、叔伯姑姨，也显得格外聪慧。



Kanner’s thumbnail portrait of Frederick W.’s grandfather reads like a pitch for a Technicolor epic starring Laurence Olivier. After studying tropical medicine in England and organizing medical missions in Africa, he became an expert on manganese mining in Brazil while serving as an art museum director and dean of a medical school. Then he absconded to Europe with his novelist mistress for twenty-five years. “All but three of the families,” Kanner marveled, “are represented either in Who’s Who in America or in American Men of Science, or in both.”

坎纳对弗雷德里克·W.祖父的简短描述，读起来就像一部由劳伦斯·奥利弗主演的彩色史诗片的宣传文案。他在英国研究热带医学，在非洲组织医疗传教团，随后成为巴西锰矿开采专家，同时兼任艺术博物馆馆长和医学院院长。之后他与身为小说家的情妇私奔欧洲，长达二十五年之久。"几乎所有家族，"坎纳惊叹道，"都在《美国名人录》或《美国科学界名人录》中占有一席之地，有的甚至两者兼而有之。"



Asperger had also taken note of the fact that an unusual number of his patients’ parents and relations were highly accomplished. Not only was Fritz V.’s mother descended from one of Austria’s greatest poets, his great-uncle was a “brilliant” but reclusive pedagogue. In many cases, Asperger said, “the ancestors of these children have been intellectuals for several generations.” No doubt influenced by Lazar’s habit of predicting a child’s profession, he added that if a manual laborer was found among the relatives of these patients, it was likely someone who had “missed his vocation”—like Harro’s father, a painter and sculptor who was forced to make brooms and brushes for a living when the Austrian economy collapsed.

阿斯伯格还注意到，他的患者中异常多的父母和亲属都成就斐然。不仅弗里茨·V 的母亲是奥地利最伟大诗人的后裔，他的叔祖父还是一位"才华横溢"却隐居的教育家。阿斯伯格指出，在许多案例中，"这些孩子的祖先已经连续几代都是知识分子"。无疑受到拉扎尔习惯预测孩子职业的影响，他补充道，如果在这些患者的亲属中发现体力劳动者，那很可能是"错失了天职"的人——就像哈罗的父亲，一位画家兼雕塑家，在奥地利经济崩溃时被迫以制作扫帚和刷子为生。



But their inherited gifts also came at a cost. Asperger described Fritz’s mother as an unfashionable and habitually anxious woman, “strange and rather a loner,” who had “limited intuitive social understanding.” When she became overwhelmed by the practical demands of life, she would take off to her solitary refuge in the mountains for a week, leaving her husband and son to fend for themselves. One day, Asperger saw her walking her son to the clinic, holding her hands stiffly behind her back as Fritz raced around her “making mischief,” each seemingly oblivious to the other. But he emphasized the fact that their shared quirks of personality gave them an emotional basis for relating to one another. “The mother knew her son through and through and understood his difficulties very well,” he observed. “She tried to find similar traits in herself and in her relations and talked about this eloquently.”

然而，他们与生俱来的天赋也伴随着代价。阿斯伯格形容弗里茨的母亲是个不合时宜且习惯性焦虑的女性，"古怪且相当孤僻"，"对社交的理解直觉有限"。当现实生活的压力令她不堪重负时，她便会独自躲进山中的隐居处待上一周，任由丈夫和儿子自生自灭。某日，阿斯伯格看见她送儿子去诊所时，双手僵硬地背在身后，而弗里茨正绕着她"调皮捣蛋"，两人仿佛对彼此视而不见。但他强调，正是他们共有的性格怪癖为彼此建立了情感纽带。"这位母亲对儿子了如指掌，非常理解他的困境，"他观察到，"她试图在自己和亲属身上寻找相似特质，并对此侃侃而谈。"



Kanner ended up taking a decidedly dimmer view. Theories of toxic parenting were particularly thick in the air at Johns Hopkins, where Meyer was also mentoring Theodore and Ruth Lidz, who became the two leading exponents of the schizophrenogenic mother hypothesis. The Lidzes were suspicious of women with professional ambitions; if their dreams were thwarted by motherhood, they predicted, the result would be deep hostility for the children, cloaked in an overweening concern for their welfare.

坎纳最终持有了明显更为悲观的看法。在约翰·霍普金斯大学，关于有毒教养的理论尤为盛行，迈耶也在那里指导着西奥多·利兹和露丝·利兹，这两人后来成为“致精神分裂症母亲”假说的两位主要倡导者。利兹夫妇对怀有职业抱负的女性持怀疑态度；他们预测，如果这些女性的梦想因母职而受挫，结果将是对孩子产生深藏的敌意，这种敌意会伪装成对孩子福祉的过度关切。



These theories had a decisive and devastating impact on Kanner’s view of his patients’ unusual fascinations and extraordinary memories. He found it inconceivable that these children might actually be interested in the geeky minutiae they rattled on about with such intensity and fervor. Where Asperger and his colleagues recognized a specialized form of intelligence systematically acquiring data in a confusing world, Kanner saw a desperate bid for parental affection. “To a child 2 or 3 years old,” he wrote, “all these words, numbers, and poems (‘questions and answers of the Presbyterian Catechism’; ‘Mendelssohn’s violin concerto’; the ‘Twenty-third Psalm’; a French lullaby; an encyclopedia index page) could hardly have more meaning than sets of nonsense syllables to adults.” He theorized that overambitious parents like the Tripletts had “stuffed” the impressionable minds of their children with useless information to cast themselves in a culturally favorable light and bolster their own egos.

这些理论对坎纳看待患者异常迷恋与超凡记忆的方式产生了决定性且毁灭性的影响。他无法相信这些孩子竟会真正痴迷于那些他们带着强烈热情喋喋不休的古怪细节。当阿斯伯格及其同事将这种系统化收集混乱世界信息的模式视为特殊智力时，坎纳却将其解读为对父母关爱的绝望渴求。他写道："对于一个两三岁的孩子来说，所有这些单词、数字和诗歌（'长老会教理问答'、'门德尔松小提琴协奏曲'、'第二十三诗篇'、法语摇篮曲、百科全书索引页）的意义，恐怕不会比无意义音节对成年人的意义更大。"他推论，像特里普利特夫妇这样野心过大的父母，为了彰显自身文化优越感并满足虚荣心，将无用的信息"填塞"进孩子易受影响的心灵。



One of Kanner’s special gifts as a clinician was his uncanny ability to draw people out, cut through their defenses, and get them talking about the most intimate details of their lives—a skill he picked up from the disabled volunteer in the Stone Room at Yankton. “His interview with parents is remarkable for its capacity to elicit a sequential account of the vicissitudes of development,” recalled Leon Eisenberg, the psychiatrist who became his chief disciple at Johns Hopkins. “A sensitive listener, he rarely interrupts. His questions are disarmingly gentle but shrewdly penetrating.” As Kanner developed his theory of autism causation, he turned the detailed notes that parents had provided to him—which were so helpful in developing a clear picture of their children’s development—into a weapon, citing them as a “telling illustration of parental obsessiveness.”

作为临床医生，坎纳有一项特殊天赋：他拥有不可思议的能力，能让人敞开心扉、卸下防备，讲述自己生活中最私密的细节——这项技能是他从扬克顿石屋那位残疾志愿者那里学来的。莱昂·艾森伯格回忆道："他与家长的访谈令人惊叹，总能引导他们按时间顺序讲述孩子成长过程中的种种波折。"这位后来成为约翰·霍普金斯大学坎纳首席门徒的精神科医生补充说："他是一位敏锐的倾听者，极少打断对方。他的提问看似温和无害，实则犀利深刻。"当坎纳构建起孤独症成因理论时，他将家长们提供的详细记录——这些记录曾对清晰描绘孩子成长轨迹大有裨益——转而用作武器，称其为"家长强迫性思维的生动例证"。



He applied the word obsessive to his patients and their relatives nearly a dozen times in his paper, starting with his description of Beaman Triplett’s thirty-three-page letter. But his condescending attitude toward the families in his practice went far beyond that. Casting himself as the only reliable narrator of his patients’ lives, he described the mother of one boy as “supposedly a college graduate” and portrayed Alfred’s mother as a woman who “liked to call herself a psychiatrist and to make ‘psychiatric’ diagnoses of the child.” (This must have been particularly galling to Kanner, who set aside his own insecurity about calling himself a psychiatrist only after being hired by Meyer.) He described the mother of a boy called Richard M. as follows:

他在论文中近十次用“痴迷”一词形容患者及其家属，从描述比曼·特里普利特那封三十三页的长信开始。但他对执业中接触的家庭所持的居高临下态度远不止于此。他将自己塑造成患者生命中唯一可靠的叙述者，称某位男孩的母亲“据称是大学毕业生”，并将阿尔弗雷德的母亲描绘成一位“喜欢自称精神科医生，并对孩子进行‘精神科’诊断”的女性。（这想必令坎纳尤为恼火——他本人也是在得到迈耶聘用后，才克服了自称精神科医生的不安。）他如此描述一位名叫理查德·M 的男孩的母亲：



His mother brought with her copious notes that indicated obsessive preoccupation with details and a tendency to read all sorts of peculiar interpretations into the child’s performances. She watched (and recorded) every gesture and every “look,” trying to find their specific significance and finally deciding on a particular, sometimes very farfetched explanation. She thus accumulated an account that, though very elaborate and richly illustrated, on the whole revealed more of her own version of what had happened in each instance than it told of what had actually occurred.

他的母亲带来了大量笔记，显示出对细节的过度关注，以及倾向于从孩子的表现中解读出各种奇特含义的倾向。她观察（并记录）每一个手势和每一个“眼神”，试图找出它们的具体意义，最终往往得出某种特定、有时甚至牵强附会的解释。她就这样积累了一份记录，尽管内容详尽、例证丰富，但整体而言，与其说揭示了每件事的真实情况，不如说更多地反映了她自己对事件的理解版本。



Virtually the only couple that escaped his condescension was Wendell Muncie and his wife, a Johns Hopkins nurse named Rachel Cary, whom he referred to as a “prominent psychiatrist” and “a well educated, kindly woman.” Muncie would return the favor by giving his paper a rave review in the Quarterly Review of Biology.

几乎唯一能免于他居高临下评判的夫妇，是温德尔·芒西和他的妻子——一位名叫雷切尔·卡里的约翰·霍普金斯大学护士。他称她为“杰出的精神病学家”和“一位受过良好教育、和蔼可亲的女性”。芒西后来投桃报李，在《生物学季评》上对他的论文给予了高度评价。



“For the most part,” Kanner concluded, “the parents, grandparents, and collaterals are persons strongly preoccupied with abstractions of a scientific, literary, or artistic nature, and limited in genuine interest in people. This much is certain . . . In the whole group, there are very few really warmhearted fathers and mothers. Even some of the happiest marriages are rather cold and formal affairs.”

“在很大程度上，”坎纳总结道，“这些孩子的父母、祖父母及旁系亲属，大多是极度沉迷于科学、文学或艺术抽象概念的人，对真实的人际关系兴趣有限。这一点是确定的……在整个群体中，真正温暖的父母少之又少。即使是一些看似最幸福的婚姻，也显得相当冷淡和形式化。”



Thus he ended the paper that introduced his syndrome to the world on a poignant note of ambivalence. While emphasizing the likelihood that autism was innate and inborn, he left the door open to a more unsettling possibility: that these children had been pushed into mental illness by their selfish, compulsive, and emotionally frosty parents, who tried to substitute poems and symphonies and catechisms and encyclopedias for the nurturing love they were unable to provide.

就这样，他以一种充满矛盾情感的笔调，结束了这篇向世界介绍他所定义综合征的论文。尽管他强调孤独症很可能是先天性的，但他也为一个更令人不安的可能性留下了空间：这些孩子是被自私、强迫性且情感冷漠的父母推入精神疾病的——这些父母试图用诗歌、交响乐、教义问答和百科全书，来替代他们无法给予的养育之爱。



—



FOR THE PURPOSES OF advancing the field of child psychiatry, both theories had their virtues and drawbacks. The discovery of the first form of major psychosis that was present at birth would lend unprecedented urgency to the study of prenatal and postnatal development, genetics, and neurology, all of which Kanner was eager to integrate with psychology. But it would also undercut the role that his many colleagues in the field of “child guidance” had carved out for themselves: the prevention of delinquency and mental illness in adulthood. A condition that was inborn could not be prevented—it could only be ameliorated.

为了推动儿童精神病学领域的发展，这两种理论各有其优点和缺陷。第一种在出生时就存在的严重精神障碍的发现，将为产前和产后发育、遗传学及神经学的研究带来前所未有的紧迫性——这些都是坎纳渴望与心理学整合的领域。但这也会削弱他在"儿童指导"领域的众多同事为自己开辟的角色：预防成年期的行为偏差和精神疾病。先天性疾病无法预防——只能改善。



Implicating parenting style in the etiology of his syndrome, on the other hand, would place child psychiatrists firmly at the center of family life, giving them a role arguably more powerful than that of parents themselves: the ability to intervene therapeutically for the sake of the child. For obvious reasons, this way of looking at the problem proved more popular with Kanner’s psychoanalytically minded colleagues, for whom autism became an ideal platform for promoting their latest theories of psychic development.

另一方面，将养育方式归因于其综合征的病因学，将使儿童精神病学家牢牢占据家庭生活的中心，赋予他们一种可能比父母本身更强大的角色：为了孩子的利益进行治疗干预的能力。显而易见，这种看待问题的方式在坎纳那些具有精神分析倾向的同事中更受欢迎，对他们而言，孤独症成为了推广其最新心理发展理论的理想平台。



Kanner’s agnosticism on the matter was both strategic and inevitable given his background and training. Remaining open to all possibilities was the sensible, nondogmatic, Meyerian thing to do. It was also the politically expedient choice when the prevailing winds in American psychiatry were blowing in a decidedly Freudian direction, in part because so many Freudians had just washed up on America’s shores after being driven out of Eastern Europe. The fact that his patients’ parents would unjustly pay a heavy price if his theory about them turned out to be wrong didn’t factor into his calculations. He left the question hanging, hoping to attract the attention of other researchers who would help him figure out the answer.

坎纳对此事的不可知论既是策略性的，也是其背景和训练使然。保持对所有可能性的开放态度，是明智、非教条且符合迈耶式作风的做法。当美国精神病学的主流风向明显偏向弗洛伊德方向时——部分原因是大量弗洛伊德学派学者被驱逐出东欧后涌入美国海岸——这同样是一种政治上的权宜之选。至于若他的理论被证实错误，患者父母将不公正地付出沉重代价这一事实，并未纳入他的考量。他悬置了这个问题，希望吸引其他研究者的关注，助其寻找答案。



—



TRAGICALLY, HOWEVER, Kanner made another error in interpreting his data that had the effect of limiting interest in the study of autism altogether for the next four decades. In speculating on the prevalence of his syndrome, he posited that it was “rare enough,” though he offered that it was “possible that some such children have been viewed as feebleminded or schizophrenic.”

然而悲剧的是，坎纳在解读数据时又犯了一个错误，导致此后四十年间对孤独症研究的兴趣整体受限。在推测该综合征的患病率时，他断言其“相当罕见”，尽管他也承认“可能有些这类儿童曾被视作智力低下或精神分裂症患者”。



Considering the number of similar cases that had already come to light in the childhood schizophrenia literature, and the fact that nearly all of his patients had been previously diagnosed as feebleminded, his notion that more cases of autism would be uncovered by reevaluating children with those diagnoses was a safe bet. But his insistence that his syndrome was rare was decidedly premature. Kanner was one of very few child psychiatrists in the country at that point, and he had already seen thirteen cases that fit the pattern (the original eleven, plus two more mentioned in a footnote), and he would soon see seven more. Plus, families of limited means—who couldn’t afford to make the rounds of pediatricians, psychologists, and neurologists until they were referred to a specialist like him—weren’t even on his radar.

考虑到儿童精神分裂症文献中已有大量类似案例曝光，且他几乎所有的患者此前均被诊断为智力低下，他提出通过重新评估这些诊断的儿童会发现更多孤独症病例的观点，确实是个稳妥的推测。但他坚持认为自己的综合征极为罕见，显然为时过早。当时坎纳是美国极少数儿童精神科医生之一，而他已经发现十三例符合该模式的病例（最初十一例，加上脚注中提及的两例），很快又将发现七例。此外，那些经济拮据的家庭——他们无力辗转于儿科医生、心理学家和神经科医生之间，直到被转诊至像他这样的专家——甚至根本不在他的关注范围内。



Furthermore, if his syndrome had less blatantly disabling forms—as most developmental disabilities do—Kanner would likely have missed them altogether, because he had set up his referral network at the Harriet Lane in such a way that he was guaranteed to see only the most perplexing, unmanageable, and difficult cases. After the publication of his textbook, the pediatricians at the clinic felt empowered to handle less daunting cases on their own by referring them to an extensive network of social service agencies like the Children’s Aid Society, the Visiting Nurses’ Association, and the Baltimore Division of Special Education. By the time Kanner saw Donald, only one in ten children examined at the Harriet Lane required consultation with a psychiatrist, and only those cases considered “too complicated” or “time-consuming” for anyone else ended up being seen by Kanner or one of his associates.

此外，如果他的综合征存在不那么明显的致残形式——正如大多数发育障碍那样——坎纳很可能完全错过它们，因为他在哈丽特巷诊所建立的转诊网络，确保了他只会接触到最令人困惑、最难处理、最棘手的病例。在他的教科书出版后，诊所的儿科医生们感到有能力自行处理不那么棘手的病例，将其转介给儿童援助协会、访视护士协会和巴尔的摩特殊教育部门等广泛的社会服务机构网络。到坎纳接诊唐纳德时，哈丽特巷诊所每十名接受检查的儿童中只有一名需要精神科医生会诊，而只有那些被认为对其他人来说“过于复杂”或“耗时”的病例，最终才会由坎纳或其同事接诊。



In essence, he was sitting at the apex of a pyramid designed to filter all but the most profoundly disabled children of the most well-connected families in America out of his caseload. From this rarefied perspective, it’s not surprising that his syndrome seemed both exceptionally rare and strikingly monolithic. The milder cases among the two hundred children seen by Asperger in Vienna would likely have never made it to the top of his pyramid. What’s more surprising is how far Kanner was willing to go to ensure that other researchers saw his syndrome as exceptionally rare and monolithic too, even after evidence to the contrary began to emerge.

本质上，他处于一个金字塔的顶端——这个体系旨在将美国最有权势家族中那些并非重度残疾的儿童筛除出他的诊疗范围。从这种精英视角来看，他定义的综合征显得既异常罕见又惊人地单一，这并不令人意外。在维也纳，阿斯伯格接诊的两百名儿童中，那些症状较轻的病例很可能根本不会出现在这个金字塔顶端。更令人惊讶的是，即便相反的证据开始浮现，坎纳仍不遗余力地确保其他研究者也认为他的综合征极其罕见且具有高度一致性。



II



Three months after Kanner’s monograph appeared in The Nervous Child, he received a barbed letter from Louise Despert, who was unimpressed by his claims that the condition he described was “unique” and “heretofore unreported.” Had he not been reading her papers? “It seems to me that the greatest contribution this article is making is in its thorough, accurate, and illuminating description of clinical cases,” she wrote. “However, if you will permit me to say so, I object to the coining of new terminology for entities which, if not so carefully described, have been previously reported.”

在坎纳的专著发表于《神经质儿童》三个月后，他收到了路易丝·德佩尔的一封尖刻来信。德佩尔对他声称所描述的病症“独一无二”且“此前未见报道”的说法不以为然。难道他没有读过她的论文吗？“在我看来，这篇文章最大的贡献在于对临床病例进行了详尽、准确且富有启发性的描述，”她写道，“然而，恕我直言，我反对为那些虽未经如此细致描述却已有过报道的实体创造新术语。”



She had a point. Kanner had tried to finesse the potential competition by pretending that it didn’t exist. The casual reference in his paper to children being “misdiagnosed” with schizophrenia was particularly egregious, since many of Despert’s case descriptions overlapped so closely with his own. The sole justification for his claim to uniqueness was his notion that his syndrome was apparent “from the start”—at birth—while Despert fussed over relatively arbitrary categories of onset.

她言之有理。坎纳试图通过假装竞争不存在来巧妙回避。他在论文中轻描淡写地提及儿童被“误诊”为精神分裂症，这尤其令人反感，因为德佩尔的许多病例描述与他的描述高度重合。他声称其独特性的唯一依据，在于他认为自己的综合征“从一开始”——即出生时——就显而易见，而德佩尔则纠结于相对武断的发病分类标准。



There were numerous problems on both sides of this divide. Could a boy like Despert’s patient S.K.—who had only a limited expressive vocabulary, a “capacity above normal to retain words and use them in a mechanical way,” and a repertoire of more than a hundred nursery rhymes that he recited from memory—really be said to have been developing in a typical way before his regression? (Despert admitted that this indicated S.K.’s “previous difficulty in adaptation.”) Could Kanner legitimately assert that his syndrome was always apparent at birth when his patients were five years old on average when he first saw them?

这一分歧的双方都存在诸多问题。像德佩尔的患者 S.K.这样的男孩——他只有有限的表达性词汇，"记忆单词并机械使用的能力高于正常水平"，还能凭记忆背诵一百多首童谣——在退行之前，真的能说他的发育是典型的吗？（德佩尔承认，这表明 S.K."此前存在适应困难"。）坎纳能否合理断言，他的综合征在出生时就已显现，而他的患者首次就诊时平均年龄已达五岁？



The answer was no. By 1955, he would retract this claim, saying, “The case material has been expanded to include a number of children who reportedly developed normally through the first 18 to 20 months of life.” Clearly, the boundaries between Kanner’s “unique” syndrome and what other clinicians were calling childhood schizophrenia were blurrier than he tried to make them appear.

答案是否定的。到 1955 年，他撤回了这一说法，表示："病例资料已扩展至包括一些据称在出生后 18 至 20 个月内发育正常的儿童。"显然，坎纳所谓的"独特"综合征与其他临床医生所称的儿童精神分裂症之间的界限，比他试图呈现的更为模糊。



Kanner’s overreach may explain why his paper gained surprisingly little traction at first. In typically grandiose fashion, he later insisted that it “immediately received the attention of the profession,” but, he also admitted, “the earliest reactions to the issue did not appear in print for several years.” (Other than Muncie’s enthusiastic review, which omitted any mention of the fact that his daughter was part of the study.) In fact, only two papers on the subject, not written by Kanner, were published in the next decade, while the volume of childhood schizophrenia research was worthy of its own book-length annotated bibliography.

坎纳的过度自信或许可以解释，为何他的论文最初几乎未引起关注。他后来以一贯夸张的口吻坚称该文"立即引起了专业领域的注意"，但同时也承认"最早的相关回应在数年后才见诸文字"（芒西那篇热情洋溢的评论除外——该文刻意回避了其女儿参与研究的事实）。事实上，此后十年间仅有两篇非坎纳撰写的相关论文发表，而同期关于儿童精神分裂症的研究文献已多到足以编纂成书。



In 1946, Lauretta Bender, the chief of psychiatry at New York’s Bellevue Hospital, described one hundred children diagnosed with early-onset schizophrenia who exhibited a number of behaviors that are now considered classic signs of autism, including whirling, stimming, echolalia, and an apparently total lack of awareness of other people. She characterized this condition as pervasive, affecting every aspect of the child’s body and mind, including the nervous system and digestion. But she also noted that even some of the most profoundly disabled children—“underdeveloped, infantile in motor play, physically dependent, unconcerned with [their] body excreta and clothing, unsure of [their] own identity, inarticulate to the point of mutism, [and] unable to make any school or social adjustment”—were capable of remarkable displays of “accelerated creativeness” and “Picasso-like experiments” in music and art. Indeed, her accounts of childhood schizophrenia were closer to Asperger’s and Frankl’s descriptions of autistic psychopathy than Kanner’s constricted view of his syndrome.

1946 年，纽约贝尔维尤医院精神科主任劳蕾塔·本德描述了 100 名被诊断为早发性精神分裂症的儿童，他们表现出许多如今被视为典型孤独症特征的行为，包括旋转、自我刺激、模仿言语，以及明显完全缺乏对他人的意识。她将这种状况定性为广泛性障碍，影响儿童身心各个方面，包括神经系统和消化系统。但她也指出，即使是最严重残疾的儿童——"发育迟缓、运动游戏幼稚、生活依赖他人、不关心自身排泄物和衣物、不确定自我身份、言语不清近乎缄默、无法适应学校或社会生活"——也能在音乐和艺术中展现出惊人的"加速创造力"和"毕加索式的实验"。事实上，她对儿童精神分裂症的描述更接近阿斯伯格和弗兰克尔对孤独谱系精神病的描述，而非坎纳对其综合征的狭隘理解。



In an eerie preview of the autism “epidemic” to come four decades later, the prevalence of childhood schizophrenia started spiking in the midtwentieth century. By 1954, Bender saw 850 young patients with that diagnosis at Bellevue alone, including 250 cases added to her files in the previous three years. Bellevue was not unique in this respect: from 1946 to 1961, one in seven children admitted to the Langley Porter Neuropsychiatric Institute in San Francisco were diagnosed as “psychotic,” with most having a reported onset before three years of age.

在二十世纪中叶，儿童精神分裂症的患病率开始急剧上升，这仿佛是四十年后即将到来的孤独症"流行"的诡异预兆。到 1954 年，本德仅在贝尔维尤医院就接诊了 850 名被诊断为该病的年轻患者，其中过去三年间新增的病例就达 250 例。贝尔维尤并非特例：从 1946 年到 1961 年，旧金山兰利·波特神经精神病学研究所收治的儿童中，每七人就有一人被诊断为"精神病性障碍"，且多数报告称症状在三岁前就已出现。



Their case records contained descriptions of many types of behavior that would become part of the lore of autism, including “ritualistic” gestures, “circular movement of objects,” strict dietary preferences (one child would “not eat anything but spaghetti cooked in a particular pan”), and a fascination with taking apart toys and home appliances. None of these young patients exhibited hallucinations, delusions, or the other fulminant manifestations typically associated with the word psychotic. For the most part, they were nonverbal children with unusual sensory sensitivities who shied away from other people.

这些病例记录中描述了多种后来成为孤独症典型特征的行为，包括"仪式化"手势、"绕圈摆弄物品"、严格的饮食偏好（有个孩子"只吃特定锅具煮的意大利面"），以及痴迷于拆解玩具和家电。这些年轻患者中无人出现幻觉、妄想或其他通常与"精神病性"一词相关的暴发性症状。他们大多是不善言语的儿童，伴有异常感官敏感，且回避与他人接触。



Childhood schizophrenia researchers were well aware that the condition they were studying was not monolithic but had an astonishingly diverse range of presentations. “The concept of a gradient of severity of disorder, or that of a psychopathological spectrum,” wrote S. A. Szurek in 1956, “is for several reasons becoming for us one which fits our experience most closely.”

儿童精神分裂症的研究者们早已意识到，他们所研究的这种病症并非单一形态，而是呈现出令人震惊的多样化表现。S.A.苏雷克在 1956 年写道："基于多种原因，疾病严重程度梯度或精神病理学谱系的概念，正成为最贴合我们临床经验的理论框架。"



In fact, if Kanner’s syndrome was defined too narrowly, childhood schizophrenia had the opposite problem: its boundaries were so diffuse that it included too many different types of patients. By 1958, Hilde Mosse of the Lafargue Clinic in Harlem reported that children with the diagnosis “filled state hospitals and schools for mental defectives.” Childhood schizophrenia walked like a duck and quacked like a duck but was not a duck. Instead, it was the psychotic goose that suddenly seemed to be in everyone’s backyard.

事实上，如果说坎纳综合征的定义过于狭隘，那么儿童精神分裂症则面临相反的问题：其诊断边界过于模糊，以至于囊括了过多不同类型的患者。到 1958 年，哈莱姆区拉法格诊所的希尔德·莫斯报告称，被诊断为该病的儿童"挤满了州立精神病院和智力缺陷学校"。儿童精神分裂症看似形似鸭子、声似鸭子，却并非真正的鸭子——它更像是突然出现在每个人后院的"精神病态鹅"。



—



KANNER SAW THE WRITING on the wall early on. While continuing to insist that his syndrome was a condition sui generis, he quietly folded it into the schizophrenia section of the revised edition of his textbook in 1948. A year later, he officially waved the white flag. “Early infantile autism may . . . be looked upon as the earliest possible manifestation of childhood schizophrenia,” he wrote in the American Journal of Orthopsychiatry. “I do not believe that there is any likelihood that early infantile autism will at any future time have to be separated from the schizophrenias.”

坎纳很早就看出了端倪。尽管他仍坚持自己的综合征是一种独特病症，但在 1948 年修订版教科书中，他悄然将其归入精神分裂症章节。一年后，他正式举起了白旗。"早期婴儿孤独症或许……可被视为儿童精神分裂症最早期的表现形式，"他在《美国矫形精神病学杂志》上写道，"我不认为未来有任何必要将早期婴儿孤独症从精神分裂症中分离出来。"



In essence, Kanner attempted to negotiate a truce with researchers like Despert and Bender: if they would let him have his rare, narrowly defined syndrome, he would yield the rest of Szurek’s “spectrum” to the ever-expanding field of childhood schizophrenia. As a career gambit, it worked. Kanner was rewarded with speakerships at schizophrenia conferences and a surge of interest in his own work. In retrospect, however, this truce had a hidden cost. From that point on, the terms autism, childhood schizophrenia, and childhood psychosis were used virtually interchangeably throughout the clinical literature (Kanner’s 1974 anthology of writing on autism was titled Childhood Psychosis). This had a confounding effect on research, because virtually any sample of “psychotic” children was bound to include kids with a wide variety of heterogeneous conditions. It would also make accurate retrospective assessments of the prevalence of autism in the mid-twentieth century virtually impossible, because so many autistic children ended up hidden behind other labels.

本质上，坎纳试图与德斯佩特和本德等研究者达成休战协议：如果他们允许他保留自己那罕见且定义狭窄的综合征，他就将苏雷克提出的"谱系"中其余部分让给不断扩张的儿童精神分裂症领域。作为职业策略，这招奏效了。坎纳获得了在精神分裂症会议上发言的机会，其研究工作也引发广泛关注。然而事后看来，这场休战暗藏代价。自那时起，"孤独症""儿童精神分裂症"与"儿童精神病"这三个术语在临床文献中几乎被混用（坎纳 1974 年编纂的孤独症文集即命名为《儿童精神病》）。这给研究造成了混淆效应，因为任何"精神病"儿童样本几乎必然包含患有多种异质性疾病的儿童。这也使得对 20 世纪中期孤独症患病率进行准确的回顾性评估几乎成为不可能——因为太多孤独症儿童最终被其他诊断标签所掩盖。



Trying to make the best of changing trends in psychiatry, Kanner also yielded to the consensus of his colleagues on the role of parenting in autism. After Adolf Meyer’s retirement in 1941, the dominance of his school of psychiatry in America—with its emphasis on seeking “the facts” of each patient’s life rather than on elaborating unified theories of the psyche—was quickly eclipsed by the rise of psychoanalysis. To a generation of erudite intellectuals who had barely escaped extermination, Kanner’s suggestion that the fate of these children was sealed at birth seemed nearly traitorous to the profession. If autism was rooted in disturbed family dynamics, however, there was still reason for hope.

为了顺应精神病学领域不断变化的趋势，坎纳也接受了同事们关于养育方式在孤独症中作用的共识。1941 年阿道夫·迈耶退休后，他所倡导的美国精神病学学派——强调探寻每位患者生活的"事实"，而非构建统一的精神理论——迅速被精神分析学的兴起所掩盖。对于一群险些遭遇灭顶之灾的博学知识分子而言，坎纳提出的"这些孩子的命运在出生时便已注定"的观点，几乎是对专业的背叛。然而，如果孤独症源于紊乱的家庭关系，那么希望依然存在。



Kanner’s capitulation to his powerful peers was as swift as it was brutal to parents. By April 1948, when Time ran an article headlined “Frosted Children” (subtitle: “Diaper-Age Schizoids”), it was clear that he wasn’t going to be a stickler about insisting that his syndrome was present at birth. Addressing his colleagues at a conference in Manhattan, Kanner blasted his patients’ parents as cold perfectionists who barely had time to hug their children before rushing off to the lab or the next gallery opening. It wasn’t that they meant to do their children harm, he said; it was that their idea of responsible parenting was “the mechanized service of the kind which is rendered by an over-conscientious gasoline station attendant.” (Time reported ominously that “all but five of the mothers” of his patients “had a college degree.”) He added that the reason these children had turned their backs on other people was that they sought solace in solitude after being “kept neatly in a refrigerator which didn’t defrost.”

坎纳向权威同僚的屈服之迅速，对父母而言如同残酷打击。到 1948 年 4 月《时代》周刊刊登题为《冰封儿童》（副标题：《尿布期的精神分裂症患者》）的文章时，他已不再坚持其综合征必须先天存在的立场。在曼哈顿的学术会议上，坎纳将患者父母斥为冷漠的完美主义者——他们连拥抱孩子的时间都吝啬，便匆匆赶往实验室或画廊开幕式。他声称这些父母并非有意伤害子女，只是将尽责养育理解为"如同过度尽责的加油站服务员提供的机械化服务"。（《时代》周刊不祥地指出，这些患者的母亲"除五人外均拥有大学学历"。）他补充道，这些孩子之所以背弃他人，是因为在被"精心保存在永不解冻的冰箱里"后，只能在孤独中寻求慰藉。



The image of the refrigerator mother proved indelible in the popular imagination, but in Kanner’s view, fathers were equally culpable. His eager protégé Leon Eisenberg published his own case series focused on his patients’ fathers, as if bringing your son or daughter to the Harriet Lane for an evaluation was tantamount to an admission that you were mentally ill. In withering prose, he depicted a wealthy surgeon who “dealt with infected gall bladders, diseased bowels, or tumors, with little or no curiosity about the person in whom these anatomical problems were housed.” Another father, he reported, read “mathematical treatises” before making love to his wife “in an inept fashion,” leaving her unfulfilled and resentful. Eisenberg claimed that these characteristics recurred “with monotonous regularity” among the fathers in his practice, citing as an iconic example a man found in an upended railroad car after a derailment, fussing over his manuscript.

冰箱母亲的形象在大众想象中根深蒂固，但在坎纳看来，父亲同样难辞其咎。他热心的门生利昂·艾森伯格发表了自己的病例系列研究，聚焦于患者的父亲们，仿佛带孩子到哈丽特·莱恩诊所接受评估就等于承认自己患有精神疾病。他用尖刻的笔触描绘了一位富有的外科医生，此人"处理感染的胆囊、病变的肠道或肿瘤时，对承载这些解剖学问题的人却毫无好奇心"。他报告说，另一位父亲在"笨拙地"与妻子做爱前阅读"数学论文"，导致妻子欲求不满、心生怨恨。艾森伯格声称，这些特征在他诊疗中的父亲群体中"以单调的规律性"反复出现，并引用了一个标志性案例：一位父亲在火车脱轨后，竟在倾覆的车厢里忙着整理手稿。



In 1956, Kanner and Eisenberg published a summation of their research in the previous dozen years. The paper was informed by a study of child-rearing practices on Israeli kibbutzim, where “warm and demonstrative” nursery workers took on many of the nurturing roles traditionally fulfilled by parents. The families of their autistic patients, they explained, were like kibbutzim “in reverse.” The children were generally raised by their own parents, but not in a “warm, flexible, growth-promoting atmosphere.” Instead, “physical needs were met mechanically and on schedule, according to the rigid precepts of naïve behaviorism applied with a vengeance.” As a result, children were rewarded for “‘perfect’ behavior, cleverness, ‘self-sufficiency,’ and so on,” rather than being valued simply for existing. “It may be a measure of the intellectual aptitude of these children that they were able to parrot long and resonant strings of meaningless words,” they added, “but it even more clearly bespeaks the emphasis placed at home on such useless activities, which were a source of pride to the parents.”

1956 年，坎纳与艾森伯格发表了他们过去十余年研究的总结。这篇论文参考了以色列基布兹集体农庄的育儿实践研究——在那里，"热情洋溢、善于表达"的保育员承担了许多传统上由父母扮演的养育角色。他们解释道，孤独谱系患者的家庭就像"反向"的基布兹。这些孩子通常由亲生父母抚养，但并非在"温暖、灵活、促进成长的环境"中成长。相反，"生理需求被机械地按计划满足，遵循着被极端应用的朴素行为主义僵化教条"。结果，孩子们因"'完美'行为、聪慧、'自立'等特质"获得奖励，而非因其存在本身被珍视。"这些孩子能鹦鹉学舌般重复冗长空洞的词语，或许能衡量其智力天赋，"他们补充道，"但这更清楚地表明家庭对这类无益活动的强调——这些活动正是父母引以为傲的源泉。"



Though Kanner refrained from making recommendations about treatment, the predictable outcome of his statements was the widespread adoption of an approach to therapeutic intervention for autism that included years of psychoanalysis for the parents and removal of the children to an institution like Bellevue or Langley Porter “for their own good.” The most prominent advocate of this approach—which was archly christened parentectomy in the press—was another émigré from Eastern Europe who had come into psychiatry through a back door.

尽管坎纳并未就治疗提出具体建议，但他的言论可预见地导致了一种针对孤独症治疗干预方法的广泛采用：这种方法包括对父母进行长达数年的精神分析，并将孩子送往贝尔维尤或兰利·波特这样的机构，“为了他们好”。这种被媒体戏称为“父母切除术”的方法最著名的倡导者，是另一位通过非正统途径进入精神病学领域的东欧移民。



III



“This is what your mother is like—cold and hard,” said the world-renowned director of the Sonia Shankman Orthogenic School to an autistic boy in his care, pointing to a stone statue in the ornately decorated garden. The school, located at the University of Chicago, was designed to be the opposite of the kind of place where the director, Bruno Bettelheim, said that he had his first insights into autistic behavior: the concentration camps of Dachau and Buchenwald, where he was imprisoned for eleven months.

“这就是你母亲的样子——冷漠而坚硬，”索尼娅·尚克曼矫治学校的世界知名校长指着一座装饰华丽的花园中的石像，对他照料的孤独症男孩说道。这所学校位于芝加哥大学，其设计理念与校长布鲁诺·贝特尔海姆声称首次洞察孤独症行为的地方截然相反：那里是达豪和布痕瓦尔德集中营，他曾在那里被囚禁了十一个月。



The walls of the school were covered with paintings and tapestries, which Bettelheim personally selected. The children painted their rooms whatever colors they liked and ate off fine china on tables dressed with linen—a civilized touch designed to boost their self-esteem and self-control. The doors of the clinic were kept locked, but for the purpose of excluding the outside world, rather than to keep the patients in. The mothers of patients in particular were discouraged from visiting, but the children could come and go as they pleased.

学校的墙壁上挂满了贝特尔海姆亲自挑选的绘画和挂毯。孩子们可以随心所欲地粉刷自己的房间，在铺着亚麻桌布的餐桌上使用精致瓷器用餐——这种文明化的设计旨在提升他们的自尊与自控力。诊所的门始终上锁，但目的是隔绝外部世界，而非限制患者出入。尤其不鼓励患者母亲前来探视，但孩子们可以自由来去。



Bettelheim’s role model in designing this therapeutic milieu was August Aichhorn, the same man who inspired Erwin Lazar to launch the Heilpädagogik Station in Vienna. Unlike the Children’s Clinic, however, the Orthogenic School was founded on the principles and practice of psychoanalysis. The total environment of the school was designed to serve a single purpose: to enable the children to restart the process of ego development (with Bettelheim cast in the role of the collective superego), which had been arrested by toxic familial influences, with the help and guidance of the staff members, who would become their surrogate family.

贝特尔海姆在设计这一治疗环境时的榜样是奥古斯特·艾希霍恩——正是这位先驱启发了埃尔温·拉扎尔在维也纳创办特殊教育站。但与儿童诊所不同的是，正形学校建立在精神分析的原则与实践之上。学校的整体环境只为达成一个目标：在成为孩子们替代家庭的教职员工的帮助与指导下，让这些因有毒家庭影响而停滞的自我发展进程（贝特尔海姆扮演集体超我的角色）得以重启。



The son of a lumber merchant, Bettelheim came of age in the city he thought of as “Freud’s Vienna.” Bettelheim first heard about psychoanalysis when he was fourteen from Otto Fenichel, an older boy who was already sitting in on Freud’s seminars and would become a leading analyst. The young Bettelheim would go out of his way to walk down the steep slope of Bergasse as often as possible, because the great man himself lived at Number 19.

木材商的儿子贝特尔海姆在他视为“弗洛伊德的维也纳”的城市中长大。十四岁时，他从年长一些的男孩奥托·费尼谢尔那里首次听闻精神分析——后者当时已旁听弗洛伊德的研讨会，后来成为一位顶尖分析师。年轻的贝特尔海姆会特意尽可能多地走过伯格巷的陡坡，因为那位伟人本人就住在 19 号。



At first, he couldn’t understand why Freud had chosen to reside on such a nondescript street in such a dreary part of Vienna. Later, Bettelheim would tell himself that the Bergasse—which began in a dusty warren of junk shops owned by poor Jews and ended high on a hill at the University of Vienna—must have struck Freud as an external representation of the journey of his life. It may not have been strictly true, but it was a story that wove meaning out of the ragged threads of experience. Such symbol-laden narratives—complete with his own embellishments—became Bettelheim’s way of engaging the world.

起初，他无法理解弗洛伊德为何选择居住在维也纳如此沉闷街区的一条普通街道上。后来，贝特尔海姆告诉自己，伯格巷——始于贫苦犹太人经营的布满灰尘的旧货店，终于维也纳大学所在的山丘高处——必定让弗洛伊德感到这是他人生旅程的外在写照。这或许并非严格属实，但这个故事却从经验零散的线索中编织出意义。这种充满象征的叙事——加上他自己的润色——成了贝特尔海姆理解世界的方式。



He enrolled at the university, where he spent six years, eventually earning a doctorate without honors in art theory (not a PhD summa cum laude in psychology, as he would later claim). When his father died of syphilis in 1926, Bettelheim was forced to give up his daydreams of a life in academia and take over the family lumber and sawmill business. Four years later, he married Gina Alstadt, a bright, attractive, independent-minded young girl who found him “homely”—he was a short, nerdy man with enormous ears and thick glasses—but charming and well-spoken. Almost immediately after they moved in together, their relationship began to decline. Gina came to despise her husband’s habit of reading only the first and last dozen pages of a book, and skimming a few pages in the middle, and then pontificating about it as if he had read the whole thing. She would later say that she was never in love with him.

他进入大学，在那里度过了六年时光，最终获得艺术理论博士学位（并非他后来声称的心理学优等博士学位）。1926 年父亲因梅毒去世后，贝特尔海姆被迫放弃学术生涯的幻想，接手家族木材与锯木厂生意。四年后，他与聪明迷人、思想独立的年轻女孩吉娜·阿尔斯塔特结婚，她认为他"相貌平平"——这个矮个子书呆子长着硕大的耳朵，戴着厚厚的眼镜——但谈吐优雅。两人同居后不久，关系便开始恶化。吉娜逐渐厌恶丈夫读书只读首尾各十几页、中间随意翻几页就夸夸其谈的恶习，仿佛通读了全书。她后来坦言从未爱过他。



Partly due to her feelings of dissatisfaction in her marriage, Gina entered psychoanalysis, and her husband eventually followed. (They even saw the same husband-and-wife team of analysts, Richard and Editha Sterba.) At a time when wealthy people were flying in from all over the world to spend a few months on the couch, Gina became deeply involved in the culture of psychoanalysis, taking seminars with Anna Freud while working as an unpaid teacher at a Montessori school. In 1932, Editha Sterba asked her for help in trying to find a school for an American girl named Patsy who seemed to be terribly shy. On meeting Gina, Patsy looked out at her from under her pageboy haircut with terrified eyes and a blank expression on her face, knotting her fingers compulsively. To calm her, Gina gave her some crayons and was pleasantly surprised when Patsy drew beautiful pictures of animals. She felt instantly attached to this strange, quiet, tormented little girl.

部分出于对婚姻的不满，吉娜开始接受精神分析治疗，她的丈夫后来也加入了进来。（他们甚至找了同一对分析师夫妇——理查德和伊迪莎·斯特巴。）在那个富裕阶层从世界各地飞来花上几个月躺在沙发上的年代，吉娜深深融入了精神分析文化，一边在蒙台梭利学校担任无薪教师，一边参加安娜·弗洛伊德的研讨班。1932 年，伊迪莎·斯特巴请她帮忙为一个名叫帕齐的美国女孩寻找学校，这个女孩似乎极度害羞。初见吉娜时，帕齐从童花头下投来惊恐的目光，面无表情，手指不由自主地绞在一起。为了安抚她，吉娜给了她一些蜡笔，当帕齐画出栩栩如生的动物图画时，吉娜又惊又喜。她瞬间对这个古怪、沉默、饱受折磨的小女孩产生了依恋。



Thinking that Patsy might have some sort of serious emotional disturbance, Gina went to August Aichhorn for advice. “When you cannot decide if a child is disturbed or not, just turn to other children for an opinion,” he told her. Gina found that Patsy’s peers rejected her as a stranger in their midst. She had tried unsuccessfully for a long time to persuade Bettelheim to have a child, so she ended up taking Patsy into the household and treating her with the same devotion that she would have lavished on their own daughter. Under her loving care, Patsy learned to read and write and became more relaxed and sociable. Though it’s unclear precisely what sort of difficulties Patsy had, Bettelheim would later claim her as his first autistic success story, taking credit for her development, though it was really his wife’s doing.

吉娜怀疑帕齐可能患有某种严重的情感障碍，便去向奥古斯特·艾希霍恩求教。"当你无法判断一个孩子是否有心理问题时，不妨去问问其他孩子的看法，"他这样告诉她。吉娜发现帕齐的同龄人都视她为异类而排斥她。她曾长期试图说服贝特尔海姆收养一个孩子却未果，最终决定将帕齐带回家中，像对待亲生女儿般倾注全部心血照料。在吉娜的悉心呵护下，帕齐学会了读写，变得愈发开朗合群。尽管帕齐究竟遭遇何种困境已无从考证，但贝特尔海姆后来却将她标榜为自己首个孤独谱系成功案例，将她的成长归功于自己的指导——而实际上这全是妻子的功劳。



—



ON MAY 28, 1938, policemen acting on orders from Berlin arrived at Bettelheim’s door to arrest him and put him on a train to Dachau for the crimes of being a Jew and an advocate of Austrian independence. Gina had already escaped to the United States, but her husband’s visa application had gotten mired in red tape. On the train to the camp, his glasses were smashed and he was beaten in the head and stabbed with a bayonet. Upon arriving, he was given the prisoner number 15029. Another little J (for Jude) was entered in the sign-in book.

1938 年 5 月 28 日，奉命于柏林的警察来到贝特尔海姆家门口，以他是犹太人并主张奥地利独立为由将其逮捕，押上开往达豪的火车。吉娜已逃往美国，但丈夫的签证申请却陷入繁琐的官僚程序。在前往集中营的火车上，他的眼镜被砸碎，头部遭殴打，还被刺刀刺伤。抵达后，他被编为囚犯编号 15029。登记簿上又添了一个小小的"J"（代表犹太人）。



Gratuitous brutality and torture—physical and emotional—were everyday realities in the camp. Bettelheim struggled to maintain his sanity by using his powers of interpretation to make sense of the horrors he was witnessing. He interviewed his fellow inmates and listened to the stories of their lives, committing the details to memory. He took the advice of an old Communist who had managed to survive at the camp for four years and ate the disgusting soup that the Nazis ladled out for the prisoners with relish, because enjoying it was not something he had been ordered to do but a conscious assertion of his freedom.

无端的暴行与折磨——无论是肉体还是精神上的——在集中营里都是家常便饭。贝特尔海姆努力保持理智，他运用自己的解读能力来理解眼前目睹的恐怖。他采访狱友，倾听他们的生平故事，将细节铭记于心。他采纳了一位在集中营幸存四年的老共产党员的建议，津津有味地吃着纳粹分发给囚犯的恶心菜汤——因为享受这碗汤并非被迫之举，而是对自身自由的有意识宣示。



He also put into practice the lessons he learned in his psychoanalysis. He closely monitored his own emotional reactions as he adapted to the camp’s dehumanizing routines, and he observed the changes in the personalities of his fellow prisoners as they became progressively deranged by the surreal nature of their existence. He saw honest men become liars and strong men ground down until they were weeping hysterics. He felt that by noticing these things and deriving meaningful lessons from them instead of simply submitting to the process, he regained his pride and sense of himself as a human being.

他还将精神分析中学到的教训付诸实践。在适应集中营非人化的日常程序时，他密切监控自己的情绪反应，并观察同伴囚犯因生存环境的超现实本质而逐渐精神失常的性格变化。他目睹诚实者沦为说谎者，强者被磨蚀至歇斯底里地哭泣。他感到，通过觉察这些现象并从中汲取有意义的教训，而非单纯屈服于这个过程，他重新找回了自尊与作为人的自我认知。



He was particularly struck by the pitiful behavior of the emaciated prisoners known as Muselmänner because they would suddenly lie down on the ground like Muslims bowing to Mecca in prayer. They seemed to have lost their will to live entirely, as if they had collapsed inside themselves into a state of total numbness, listlessness, and apathy. He noted that these prisoners often died shortly after they reached this point, as if their physical deaths had been preceded by their psychological deaths. They trudged in the endless lines for food, showers, and the latrines like ghosts, barely able to place one foot in front of the other.

他尤其被那些被称为“穆塞尔曼”的骨瘦如柴的囚犯的可怜行为所震撼，因为他们会突然像穆斯林朝麦加祈祷一样趴在地上。他们似乎完全丧失了生存意志，仿佛内心已经崩塌，陷入了一种彻底的麻木、无精打采和冷漠的状态。他指出，这些囚犯往往在达到这种状态后不久就会死去，仿佛他们的心理死亡先于生理死亡。他们像幽灵一样在无尽的长队中蹒跚前行，等待食物、淋浴和厕所，几乎连一步都迈不动。



Then, on April 14, 1939, Bettelheim heard his number announced after morning roll call, informing him that he was to report to the administration. Though he feared that he was about to be shot, he discovered that he was to be released from the camp that day due to the efforts of relatives and influential friends who had been intensively lobbying the State Department to secure his freedom. (He later bragged that Eleanor Roosevelt herself had personally intervened in his favor, which may or may not have been true.) The SS men told him that if he didn’t leave the country within a week, he would be rearrested or shot.

随后，1939 年 4 月 14 日，贝特尔海姆在早晨点名后听到了自己的编号被叫到，通知他去行政办公室报到。尽管他担心自己即将被枪决，却发现由于亲戚和有影响力的朋友们一直在向国务院积极游说争取他的自由，他当天将被释放出集中营。（他后来吹嘘说埃莉诺·罗斯福本人曾亲自为他出面干预，此事真假难辨。）党卫军告诉他，如果一周内不离开这个国家，他将被重新逮捕或枪决。



Bettelheim arrived in New York City by steamship in early May, where Gina informed him that their marriage was over. Within months, he moved to Chicago, where he began reinventing himself to become the man who would be known at the Orthogenic School as “Dr. B,” embellishing the narrative of his own life as required. His doctorate in art theory became a doctorate in psychology—or two or three degrees in various subjects, all summa cum laude. Patsy had been his special project; over the years, she would morph into several autistic children that he had taken into his home and transformed utterly. He had been fully trained in psychoanalysis, and Freud himself had praised him as “exactly the person we need for psychoanalysis to grow and develop.” (The closest he ever came to meeting Freud was walking past his house.) Meanwhile, his years of running the family sawmill were just a memory he left behind in Austria with his former identity as a schlemiel. Who would dare challenge the veracity of a concentration camp survivor?

贝特尔海姆于五月初乘轮船抵达纽约市，吉娜在那里告诉他他们的婚姻已经结束。几个月内，他搬到了芝加哥，开始重塑自我，成为在矫正学校被称为"B 博士"的那个人，根据需要美化自己的人生经历。他的艺术理论博士学位变成了心理学博士学位——或者两三个不同学科的学位，全部以最优等成绩获得。帕齐曾是他的特殊项目；多年来，她逐渐演变成他带回家中并彻底改造的几个孤独谱系儿童。他接受过完整的精神分析训练，弗洛伊德本人曾称赞他是"精神分析成长和发展正需要的人"。（他与弗洛伊德最接近的一次接触，只是路过他的房子。）与此同时，他经营家族锯木厂的岁月，只是他留在奥地利的一段记忆，连同他作为倒霉蛋的旧身份一起被抛在身后。谁敢质疑一个集中营幸存者话语的真实性呢？



He exerted his personal charm and his gift for strategic confabulation to be hired as the principal of the Orthogenic School, which was itself in the process of reinvention. Founded by the Rush Medical College in 1912 as a place for medical students to conduct examinations of children with “doubtful mentality,” the school had allied with the University of Chicago and expanded its mission to encompass the study and treatment of children with a broad range of “adjustment difficulties”—educational, emotional, and social. The school was an ideal platform for Bettelheim to put his versions of psychoanalytic theory and ego psychology into practice and for becoming the influential figure that he had always wanted to be.

他凭借个人魅力和战略编造的天赋，成功应聘为正生学校的校长——这所学校当时正处于转型期。该校由拉什医学院于 1912 年创立，最初是供医学生检查"心智存疑"儿童的地方，后来与芝加哥大学合作，将使命扩展为研究和治疗存在教育、情感及社交等广泛"适应困难"的儿童。这所学校成为贝特尔海姆实践其精神分析理论与自我心理学理念的理想平台，也让他得以成为自己一直渴望成为的有影响力的人物。



Shortly before taking the job, he published a reflection on the conduct of prisoners in Dachau and Buchenwald called “Individual and Mass Behavior in Extreme Situations.” If he had framed it as a personal memoir, it would likely never have been published in a scholarly journal, so he cast it as the work of a highly trained independent researcher who happened to have shared the same living quarters as his subjects while claiming that he interviewed more than fifteen hundred prisoners to obtain his data, though this is unlikely. Widely reprinted in publications for lay readers like Politics, it attracted the attention and praise of a number of important figures, including Meyer Schapiro, Theodor Adorno, and Dwight D. Eisenhower.

就在接受这份工作前不久，他发表了一篇关于达豪和布痕瓦尔德集中营囚犯行为的反思文章，题为《极端情境下的个体与群体行为》。倘若他将此文定位为个人回忆录，恐怕永远无法在学术期刊上发表，于是他将其包装成一位训练有素、独立研究者的成果——这位研究者恰好与研究对象同住一室，并声称采访了超过一千五百名囚犯以获取数据，尽管这可能性微乎其微。该文在《政治》等面向普通读者的刊物上广泛转载，引起了包括迈耶·夏皮罗、西奥多·阿多诺和德怀特·D·艾森豪威尔在内的多位重要人物的关注与赞誉。



Bettelheim described the social structure of the camps as a laboratory for a diabolical experiment in producing the ideal servile citizens of Nazi society by forcing adults to regress to primitive, infantile states:

贝特尔海姆将集中营的社会结构描述为一场邪恶实验的实验室——通过迫使成年人退化为原始、婴儿般的状态，来制造纳粹社会中理想的奴性公民：



The prisoners developed types of behavior characteristic of infancy or early youth . . . They were forced to soil themselves. Their defecation was strictly regulated. Prisoners who needed to eliminate had to obtain the permission of the guard. It seemed as if the education to cleanliness would be once more repeated. It gave pleasure to the guards to hold the power of granting or withholding the permission to visit the latrines . . .

囚犯们发展出了婴儿或幼年时期特有的行为模式……他们被迫弄脏自己。排便受到严格管制。需要排泄的囚犯必须获得看守的许可。仿佛对清洁习惯的教化要再次重演。看守们乐于掌握批准或拒绝使用厕所的权力……



The prisoners lived, like children, only in the immediate present; they lost the feeling for the sequence of time; they became unable to plan for the future or to give up immediate pleasure satisfactions to gain greater ones in the near future.

囚犯们像孩童一样，只活在当下；他们失去了对时间顺序的感知；变得无法规划未来，也无法为了稍后更大的满足而放弃眼前的快感。
