But by promoting the hope that children like Mark could be fully “recovered” from autism with biomedical treatments, Rimland ended up diverting the energy and focus of the parents’ movement he helped create into an endless quest for a cure.

里姆兰提出的争议性理论——例如他认为所谓统一的孤独症实际上由多种截然不同的亚型构成——比主流科学的重大转变早了数十年。但当他宣扬像马克这样的孩子可以通过生物医学治疗完全"康复"的希望时，最终却将他亲手推动的家长运动的精力与焦点，引向了永无止境的治愈追寻。



—



BERNARD RIMLAND WAS BORN in Cleveland in 1928, the son of Russian parents who emigrated after World War I. Though he was raised in the Orthodox Jewish tradition, he grew up to become a fiercely independent thinker. He was not strictly observant, but his path in life was deeply informed by the traditional Talmudic concept of tikkun olam—the healing and reparation of a fractured world. When he was twelve, his father took a metalworking job with a defense contractor called Convair in California. The family relocated from northern Ohio to a San Diego neighborhood called Kensington, a cozy hamlet of palm trees and Mission-style houses with a quaint main street, an Art Deco movie house, and a thriving Jewish community.

伯纳德·林姆兰于 1928 年出生在克利夫兰，父母是第一次世界大战后移民的俄罗斯人。尽管他在东正教犹太传统中长大，却成长为一位极其独立的思想家。他并非严格遵循教规，但人生道路深受犹太法典中“修复世界”（tikkun olam）这一传统理念的影响——即治愈和修复破碎的世界。十二岁时，父亲在加州一家名为康维尔（Convair）的国防承包商处找到金属加工工作。全家从俄亥俄州北部迁至圣地亚哥的肯辛顿社区，那里棕榈成荫、遍布西班牙传教风格房屋，主街古雅，装饰艺术风格的电影院与繁荣的犹太社区相映成趣。



“Cleveland had been muggy and dirty,” Rimland recalled. “I got here and said, ‘this is heaven. I’m never leaving.’” He never really did. Seven decades later, his storefront office on Adams Avenue—now the headquarters of a group called the Autism Research Institute (ARI), dedicated to carrying on his work—is still there.

“克利夫兰又闷又脏，”林姆兰回忆道，“我来到这里就说：‘这是天堂，我再也不走了。’”他确实从未离开。七十年后，他在亚当斯大道上的临街办公室——如今是致力于延续其事业的孤独症研究所（Autism Research Institute, ARI）总部——依然矗立于此。



Defying his parents’ disdain for higher education (he and his sister Rose were told that college was strictly for “children of the rich”), Rimland enrolled at San Diego State University. In his junior year, he became interested in psychometrics, the quantitative measurement of human aptitude and intelligence. Its nuts-and-bolts, data-driven methodology—“how one determines what is true, or what might be true,” as Rimland put it—was infinitely more fascinating to him than the speculations about the unconscious that occupied most of his peers at the time. He got his bachelor’s degree in experimental psychology in 1950 and earned his master’s degree a year later.

不顾父母对高等教育的轻视（他和妹妹罗丝被告知，大学严格来说是“富家子弟”的专属），里姆兰还是进入了圣地亚哥州立大学。大三时，他对心理测量学产生了兴趣，这门学科研究人类能力与智力的量化测量。其注重实际数据的方法论——用里姆兰的话说，就是“如何判断什么是真实的，或可能什么是真实的”——远比当时同龄人热衷的无意识理论推测更令他着迷。1950 年他获得实验心理学学士学位，一年后又取得了硕士学位。



Then he met Gloria Alf, a spunky, blue-eyed Jewish girl from the neighborhood who loved to go down to the park and watch world-class badminton players tune up their game for the international championships, held every year in San Diego. Gloria’s older brother, Eddie, was a popular jock around town. He had so many friends that one night when he was cramming for an exam, he asked his sister to stand guard at his door and take down the names of everyone who came by to see him. When a friend named Bernie dropped by with his badminton racket, Gloria refused to let him in. With characteristic tenacity, Rimland pushed past her and jogged up the stairs as Gloria tried to pull him down. Eddie chided her for failing in her guard duty when they arrived at his door. “But who’s going to come with me while I restring my racket?” Rimland groaned. Impressed by his chutzpah, Gloria volunteered to go with him, which turned into their first date. Before heading east to Penn State to earn his doctorate, Bernie married Gloria in a local synagogue.

后来，他遇到了邻居家的女孩格洛丽亚·阿尔夫。她是个蓝眼睛的犹太姑娘，性格活泼，喜欢去公园看世界级羽毛球选手为每年在圣地亚哥举办的国际锦标赛备战。格洛丽亚的哥哥埃迪是镇上颇受欢迎的体育健将，朋友多到某晚备考时，竟让妹妹守在门口记录所有来访者的名字。当一位名叫伯尼的朋友带着球拍来访时，格洛丽亚执意不让他进门。但林姆兰以他特有的执拗推开她冲上楼去，格洛丽亚拽着他衣角试图阻拦。两人拉扯到埃迪房门口时，埃迪还责备妹妹失职。"可谁陪我去换球拍线呢？"林姆兰抱怨道。格洛丽亚被他的厚脸皮逗乐了，主动提出陪同——这成了他们的第一次约会。在前往宾州州立大学攻读博士学位前，伯尼与格洛丽亚在当地犹太教堂举行了婚礼。



They felt homesick for the West Coast, but then, in a happy turn of events, the Navy launched a personnel lab at Point Loma around the same time that Rimland was completing his degree. The newlyweds returned to San Diego, and Rimland became the director of research at the new naval base. With dreams of raising a family of their own, the young couple bought a modest home a short walk from San Diego State College. Because they didn’t care whether they had a boy or a girl, they painted the second bedroom, destined to be the nursery, yellow. Their son, Mark, was born in the spring of 1956.

他们怀念西海岸的生活，但幸运的是，就在里姆兰即将完成学业时，海军在洛马角设立了一个人事实验室。新婚夫妇回到了圣地亚哥，里姆兰成为新海军基地的研究主任。怀着组建家庭的梦想，这对年轻夫妇买了一栋简朴的房子，步行不远便是圣地亚哥州立学院。由于他们不在乎生男生女，便将第二间卧室——未来的婴儿房——漆成了黄色。他们的儿子马克于 1956 年春天出生。



—



IN THE MATERNITY WARD, Rimland was third in line at the viewing window. The young fathers in front of him kvelled effusively, though their newborns seemed hardly more aware of their surroundings than rag dolls. But Mark seemed different: he was “looking around wide-eyed, just as though he could talk. I was very proud of that,” Rimland recalled. “I thought, ‘Gee, what a precocious-looking little guy.’”

在产科病房的探视窗前，里姆兰排在第三位。前面的年轻父亲们激动地夸赞着，尽管他们的新生儿对周围环境的感知似乎还不如布娃娃。但马克看起来与众不同：他“睁大眼睛四处张望，仿佛能说话一般。我对此非常自豪，”里姆兰回忆道，“我想，‘天哪，真是个早熟的小家伙。’”



Mark turned out to be more than precociously alert; he could also be precociously loud, as Gloria discovered even before she took him home from the hospital. Amid the squalling of the other infants, she could hear Mark’s piercing wail all the way down the hall. She gleefully told her husband—a formidable swimmer in his youth—that their son had inherited his lungs.

马克不仅表现出超乎年龄的机敏，还展现出超乎年龄的响亮——格洛丽亚在带他出院前就发现了这一点。在走廊尽头，她都能从其他婴儿的哭闹声中分辨出马克刺耳的嚎哭声。她兴高采烈地告诉丈夫——年轻时曾是游泳健将——他们的儿子继承了他的肺活量。



In the months to come, however, Mark’s keening cry would become the never-ending soundtrack to the Rimlands’ life. Their newborn hardly ever seemed to stop screaming, other than the rare moments when he passed out from sheer exhaustion. Picking Mark up to cradle him only seemed to upset him more, and he would cry so violently that Gloria could barely nurse him, and he would explode in a rage at the smallest deviation from the daily routine. If Gloria dared to shampoo her hair, he would cry until it dried and looked the same again. When summer arrived, the Rimlands opened their back door so the coastal breezes could sweep the house, but as the weather turned cool again, they had to leave the door open or their son would howl implacably for hours. The neighbors complained about the racket so many times that the Rimlands became friends with the local cops, who expressed relief that Mark was not their own child.

然而，在接下来的几个月里，马克的哀嚎声成了边缘之地生活中永不停歇的背景音。这个新生儿似乎几乎没有停止尖叫的时候，只有偶尔因极度疲惫昏睡过去才能安静片刻。抱起马克哄他反而让他更加烦躁，他会哭得如此剧烈，以至于格洛丽亚几乎无法给他喂奶；只要日常作息稍有偏差，他就会暴怒发作。如果格洛丽亚胆敢洗头，他会一直哭到头发干了恢复原样才罢休。夏天来临时，边缘之家打开后门让海风吹进屋内，但天气转凉后他们仍不得不敞着门，否则儿子会毫不妥协地嚎哭数小时。邻居们多次投诉噪音，以至于边缘之家和当地警察成了朋友——警察们庆幸马克不是自己的孩子。



Eventually, Gloria decided to time her son’s caterwauling to see how long it would go on. By the time Mark was a year old, he was crying twelve hours a day. “We thought we were really living,” Gloria said. “That was so wonderful—only twelve hours!”

最终，格洛丽亚决定记录儿子的哭闹时长，看看他能持续多久。到马克一岁时，他每天要哭十二个小时。"我们当时还以为自己过得真不错，"格洛丽亚说，"那真是太棒了——才十二个小时！"



Then Mark started to hurt himself. He would bang his forehead against the wall so hard that he bore a perpetual bruise above his eyes. With his powerful little arms, he strained against the cage of his crib until it splintered. When he wasn’t screeching or thrashing around, he would gaze off into space, rocking back and forth, as if in a perpetual daydream. The only thing that seemed to divert him from his misery was the sound of machines. The drone of the vacuum cleaner mesmerized him.

随后，马克开始伤害自己。他会用额头猛撞墙壁，力道之大，以至于眉骨上方永远带着淤青。他用那双有力的小胳膊拼命摇晃婴儿床的围栏，直到木条断裂。当他不再尖叫或挣扎时，便会凝视虚空，前后摇晃身体，仿佛陷入永恒的恍惚。唯一能让他从痛苦中分神的，是机器的声响。吸尘器的嗡鸣声令他着迷。



Gloria came to feel like a prisoner in her own house. On a good day, she might eke out enough alone time to brush her teeth. Paradise, she thought, would be having the liberty to take a shower. Desperate for a couple of hours to herself, she accepted her housekeeper’s generous offer to babysit Mark. It was such a rare opportunity that she jumped into her car and started driving around town, getting out to gaze absently into shop windows. (She had forgotten to bring her purse.) Though she had long wished for such a chance to escape, she felt “like a fish out of water.” When she returned home, she found her son and her housekeeper together on the floor, sobbing. Gloria never asked the woman for help again.

格洛丽亚开始感觉自己像是自家房子里的囚徒。状态好的时候，她或许能挤出点独处时间刷个牙。在她看来，能自由地洗个澡就是天堂了。渴望能有几个小时属于自己的时间，她接受了管家主动提出帮忙照看马克的好意。这机会实在太难得，她跳上车开始在城里兜风，下车后茫然地凝视着商店橱窗（她忘了带钱包）。尽管她一直渴望这样的逃离机会，却感到"如鱼离水"。回到家时，她发现儿子和管家一起坐在地上抽泣。格洛丽亚再也没有向那个女人求助过。



Yet Rimland’s premonition in the maternity ward that his son would turn out to be a prodigy also seemed to come true. Mark was just eight months old when he started blurting out phrases like “Come on, let’s play ball!” which made his sports-loving father swell with pride. Gradually, Rimland realized that his son was just repeating the phrases that he heard around him. He would refer to both his grandfather and grandmother as “Grandpa.” One night before her husband got home from the office, Gloria held Mark up to a window and said, “It’s all dark outside, honey.” It’s-all-dark-honey became his all-purpose synonym for window for months.

然而，里姆兰在产房里预感儿子会成为神童的直觉似乎也应验了。马克八个月大时就开始脱口而出“快来，我们打球吧！”这样的短语，这让热爱运动的父亲倍感自豪。渐渐地，里姆兰意识到儿子只是在重复周围听到的话语。他会把祖父和祖母都称为“爷爷”。一天晚上，在丈夫下班回家前，格洛丽亚抱着马克走到窗前说：“外面全黑了，宝贝。”在接下来的几个月里，“外面全黑了宝贝”成了他形容窗户的万能词汇。



The family pediatrician, who had been in practice for thirty-five years, was at a loss to diagnose Mark’s condition. Rimland used to brag that he had skipped his undergraduate coursework in psychology because he instantly knew that psychometrics was for him, but his expertise in devising aptitude tests was of no help in understanding his son. He and Gloria seemed to be on their own.

那位行医三十五年的家庭儿科医生对马克的症状束手无策。里姆兰曾吹嘘自己跳过了心理学的本科课程，因为他瞬间就明白心理测量学才是自己的方向，但他设计能力测试的专业知识在理解儿子方面毫无用处。他和格洛丽亚似乎只能靠自己了。



Mark’s echolalia proved to be the key that unlocked the mystery of his condition. Hearing her son recite radio jingles in a monotone voice one day, Gloria remembered reading in college about some exotic disorder that made kids compulsively repeat nursery rhymes. Luckily, her old textbooks were stashed out in the garage. Bernie and Gloria tore open a cardboard box and finally had a name for their son’s condition: early infantile autism. Now at least they knew what they were dealing with.

马克的重复言语行为最终成为解开他病情之谜的关键。一天，格洛丽亚听到儿子用单调的声音背诵广播广告歌，突然想起大学时读过一种奇特病症——孩子会强迫性重复童谣。幸运的是，她当年的旧课本还堆在车库里。伯尼和格洛丽亚翻出一个纸箱，终于找到了儿子病情的名称：早期婴儿孤独症。至少现在他们知道了对手是谁。



—



THE RIMLANDS BEGAN MEETING twice a week with a psychotherapist who promised to unravel the deep-seated emotional issues that were surely at the root of Mark’s problems. “Tell me, why do you hate your son?” he would ask them over and over again. He advised them to commit him to an institution and move on.

边缘地带开始每周两次与心理治疗师会面，对方承诺要解开马克问题背后深藏的情感症结。"告诉我，你为什么恨你的儿子？"他反复追问这对父母，并建议他们将孩子送进精神病院，就此放手。



But Bernie and Gloria had no intention of abandoning their son. Despite all their difficulty in raising him, they adored him and just wanted him to be a happy baby. The notion that they had somehow brought about his condition by callously ignoring his feelings seemed absurd. They spent nearly every waking moment doting on Mark while trying to find practical ways of relieving his distress.

但伯尼和格洛丽亚从未想过抛弃儿子。尽管养育过程充满艰辛，他们依然深爱着马克，只希望他能做个快乐的婴儿。所谓他们因冷漠忽视孩子情感而诱发其病症的说法简直荒谬至极。他们几乎将所有清醒时间都用来呵护马克，同时不断寻找能缓解他痛苦的实际方法。



Pushing Mark in a carriage around the neighborhood seemed to soothe him, particularly when they rolled over places in the sidewalk that were bumpy and uneven. To simulate the jiggling produced by the rough pavement, Rimland taped a yardstick to the floor so he could push Mark’s cradle back and forth over it. Gloria’s response to Mark’s loud protests when she changed out of a certain dress was to order a closetful of identical dresses from Sears for her mother, her mother-in-law, and herself. She would do whatever was required to help him feel content.

推着马克在街区里转悠似乎能让他平静下来，尤其是当婴儿车碾过人行道上那些坑洼不平的地方时。为了模拟粗糙路面产生的颠簸感，里姆兰把一把码尺用胶带固定在地板上，这样他就可以推着马克的摇篮在上面来回晃动。当格洛丽亚换下某条裙子时，马克会大声抗议，而她的应对方式是从西尔斯百货为母亲、婆婆和自己各订购了一柜子一模一样的裙子。她会不惜一切代价让他感到满足。



Though the Rimlands were highly sociable people, they gradually found themselves almost completely isolated. One night they were halfway through a rare dinner out with another couple when the wife turned to Gloria and said, “You know, you just don’t seem like that kind of person—the terrible kind of person that would cause all those problems for your son.” Bernie and Gloria never spoke to them again.

尽管里姆兰夫妇非常善于社交，但他们渐渐发现自己几乎完全被孤立了。一天晚上，他们难得与另一对夫妇外出用餐，吃到一半时，那位妻子转向格洛丽亚说：“你知道吗，你看起来真不像那种人——那种会给孩子带来这么多麻烦的可怕的人。”伯尼和格洛丽亚从此再也没和他们说过话。



Then their daughter, Helen, was born. To their relief, she turned out to be an affectionate and cuddly baby. If Bernie and Gloria were so disturbed that they had hobbled their son’s mind in the cradle, why had their daughter been spared? They became determined to discover what the experts had overlooked.

后来，他们的女儿海伦出生了。令他们欣慰的是，她是个充满爱意、喜欢被抱着的宝宝。如果伯尼和格洛丽亚如此焦虑，以至于在摇篮里就束缚了儿子的心智，那为什么女儿却安然无恙？他们决心找出专家们忽略的真相。



When Rimland was a boy, his mother used to tell him a cautionary tale about his uncle who was a genius in math. During World War I, he came upon a crowd of German soldiers heaping abuse upon an elderly Jew. When he stepped in to defend the man, the soldiers beat him savagely and left him to bleed to death on the sidewalk. His mother would tell him this horrific story to teach him not to stick his nose in other people’s business. Instead, however, young Bernie thought of his uncle as a hero. Now he would wage an epic battle to rescue his son from the mysterious forces that were tormenting him.

当里姆兰还是个孩子时，母亲常给他讲一个关于他舅舅的警示故事——舅舅是个数学天才。第一次世界大战期间，他遇到一群德国士兵正在辱骂一位犹太老人。当他挺身而出保护老人时，士兵们野蛮地殴打他，任他躺在人行道上流血至死。母亲讲这个可怕的故事，是为了教导他不要多管闲事。然而，年幼的伯尼却把舅舅视为英雄。如今，他将展开一场史诗般的战斗，把儿子从折磨他的神秘力量中拯救出来。



II



Rimland’s research resources in the San Diego area were limited. There were no medical schools in town and no books on autism in the local libraries. Luckily, his job required him to take frequent trips across the country, visiting naval bases to evaluate their personnel-testing programs. (He would eventually publish more than forty reports and journal articles on psychometrics.) In his off-duty time on the road, he began scouring medical school libraries for any scrap of information that might shed light on his son’s condition.

里姆兰德在圣地亚哥地区的研究资源十分有限。当地没有医学院，图书馆里也找不到关于孤独症的书籍。幸运的是，他的工作需要频繁出差，前往全国各地的海军基地评估人员测试项目（他后来在心理测量学领域发表了四十多份报告和期刊文章）。出差途中，他利用业余时间开始翻遍医学院图书馆，搜寻任何可能揭示儿子病情的零散信息。



This quest became an all-consuming obsession. “You really would have had to see it yourself to know how little was known about autism back then, and what little was out there was speculation,” Gloria recalls. “Bernard wanted to read every word that had been written on the subject.” In the early 1960s, this was still a realistic goal. But the information he sought was scattered in thousands of separate collections. Copying machines were just coming into wide use, so Rimland began requesting photocopies and books by interlibrary loan. Much of the clinical literature on the subject wasn’t written in English, so he organized a team of Navy translators to help him mine the international journals. He also made trips to Washington to pore over rare volumes housed in the National Library of Medicine.

这一探索成了他全身心投入的执念。“你真得亲眼看看才能知道，当时人们对孤独症的了解有多么匮乏，仅有的那点信息也多是猜测，”格洛丽亚回忆道，“伯纳德想读遍所有关于这个主题的文字。”在 20 世纪 60 年代初，这仍是一个可以实现的目标。但他寻找的信息散落在成千上万个独立的收藏中。复印机刚刚开始普及，于是里姆兰开始通过馆际互借申请影印件和书籍。关于这一主题的临床文献大多不是英文写成的，于是他组织了一支海军翻译团队，帮助他挖掘国际期刊上的内容。他还多次前往华盛顿，仔细研读国家医学图书馆收藏的珍贵卷册。



When the Navy sent him to New Orleans, he declined his colleagues’ invitations to bars and strip clubs and went instead to the Tulane University Medical Library, where he talked a kindly guard into letting him read papers in the locked library overnight. When her husband returned from Louisiana, Gloria was shocked at how gaunt he looked. Rimland told her that he hadn’t eaten anything all weekend but chicken soup from a vending machine.

当海军派他去新奥尔良时，他拒绝了同事们去酒吧和脱衣舞俱乐部的邀请，转而前往杜兰大学医学图书馆，说服一位好心的保安让他整夜待在锁着的图书馆里阅读论文。当丈夫从路易斯安那州回来时，格洛丽亚看到他如此憔悴，大吃一惊。里姆兰告诉她，整个周末他只吃了自动售货机里的鸡汤，其他什么都没吃。



In his college days, he had been able to avoid taking notes during lectures, relying instead on his photographic memory. But this was different: “This was war. I envisioned autism as a powerful monster that had seized my child. I could afford no errors.”

大学时期，他能够凭借过目不忘的记忆力，在听课时不做笔记。但这次不同：“这是一场战争。我把孤独症想象成一个夺走我孩子的强大怪物。我绝不能犯任何错误。”



—



IN ADDITION TO READING everything that he could on the subject, Rimland went straight to the father of the diagnosis, writing a letter to Kanner in 1959 describing his son’s behavior and announcing his intention to write a paper on the subject. The following year, he told Kanner, “I have been continuing my study of the disease at the very intensive rate, and I now have developed a theory which, to me, accounts with surprising consistency for most of what is known.” He also mentioned that he had been experimenting by giving his son a new drug called Deaner, promoted with ads in medical journals as a “psychic energizer” for problem children.

除了阅读所有能找到的相关资料，里姆兰还直接联系了该诊断的创始人，于 1959 年写信给坎纳描述儿子的行为，并宣布打算就此主题撰写论文。次年，他告诉坎纳："我一直以极高强度持续研究这种疾病，现已发展出一套理论——在我看来，这套理论以惊人的一致性解释了目前已知的大部分现象。"他还提到，自己一直在尝试给儿子服用一种名为迪纳尔的新药，该药在医学期刊上被广告宣传为问题儿童的"精神振奋剂"。



It soon became clear to Rimland that his project would require much more than several months of research. He sent frequent updates to Kanner in the coming years informing him about Mark’s progress. The tone of these updates was solicitous and self-deprecating, in the manner of an earnest disciple addressing the master; often his letters referred to papers that he had recently sent to Kanner’s office, hoping to hear his reaction. Though Kanner had been the originator of the refrigerator-parenting theory, Rimland flattered him relentlessly. “Only Churchill comes to mind,” he wrote, “when I think of writers whose word-choice and rhetoric demonstrate similar mastery of subject matter and expression.” Kanner’s replies were usually brief and to the point.

里姆兰德很快意识到，他的项目所需的时间远不止几个月。在接下来的几年里，他频繁向坎纳发送最新进展，告知马克的情况。这些汇报的语气既殷勤又自谦，如同虔诚的弟子向大师汇报一般；他的信件中常提及最近寄到坎纳办公室的论文，期待得到反馈。尽管坎纳是"冰箱母亲"理论的创始人，里姆兰德仍不遗余力地奉承他。"每当想到那些用词和修辞展现对主题与表达同样精湛掌握的作家，"他写道，"我脑海中只浮现出丘吉尔。"而坎纳的回复通常简短扼要。



Mark’s development during this period was so rapid that it surprised and delighted Rimland. He told Kanner: “We feel that there is real progress. He is using a little speech now—just fragments in a high piping voice. He is naming pictures in books for the first time, and there is progress in toilet training. His disposition is vastly improved. Where before, on returning from work it was common to hear him screaming in part of an hour-long tantrum, I now often find him opening the door for me with a smile.” Because it was widely believed that autistic children were incapable of learning—a misconception largely caused by their being warehoused in institutions for the “feebleminded,” where education was not on the agenda—Rimland assumed that his experimental treatment was responsible for Mark’s improvement: “We think it is mostly due to Deaner,” he informed Kanner. He even canceled plans to bring Mark to Minneapolis for an evaluation by Kanner, “since Mark’s taking of Deaner has resulted in such striking improvement that additional diagnosis of autism might be difficult.”

马克在这一时期的发育速度之快，令里姆兰既惊讶又欣喜。他告诉坎纳："我们觉得确实有了进步。他现在能说一点话了——虽然只是用尖细的嗓音说出零碎的词语。他第一次能说出书中图片的名称，如厕训练也有了进展。他的性情大为改善。以前下班回家时，常听到他尖叫着闹上半小时的脾气，现在却常常看到他微笑着为我开门。"由于当时普遍认为孤独谱系儿童无法学习——这一误解很大程度上源于他们被安置在收容"智力低下者"的机构中，而那里根本没有教育计划——里姆兰认为马克的改善要归功于他的实验性治疗："我们认为这主要是迪安纳的作用，"他告诉坎纳。他甚至取消了带马克去明尼阿波利斯接受坎纳评估的计划，"因为服用迪安纳后马克的改善如此显著，以至于可能难以再做出孤独谱系的诊断。"



—



AFTER FIVE YEARS OF RESEARCH, Rimland had filled enough notebooks and index cards to open a medical library himself. He began compiling his observations into a monograph that he planned to call “Kanner’s Syndrome of Apparent Autism.” As the paper got longer and longer, he started mimeographing it and sending it out to experienced researchers in the field for comments and criticism. He was well aware that he was venturing beyond his realm of expertise.

经过五年的研究，里姆兰积累的笔记本和索引卡足以自己开一家医学图书馆。他开始将观察结果汇编成专著，计划命名为《坎纳式显性孤独症综合征》。随着论文篇幅不断增长，他着手用蜡纸油印机印制，寄给该领域资深研究者征求意见与批评。他深知自己正在涉足专业领域之外的疆域。



Rimland was beginning to find his day job with the Navy—supplemented by teaching courses on abnormal psychology at local colleges—a bit dull by comparison. His moonlighting as an autism researcher took him far beyond the domain of personnel testing and number crunching, enabling him to explore the frontiers of emerging fields like genetics, neurophysiology, biochemistry, and medical anthropology. Truly understanding his son’s condition would require input from experts in a dozen disciplines. “A wise man once observed that if you study an object of nature intently enough, if you focus upon it long enough with all your powers of concentration and attention,” he wrote, “there comes a point at which the macrocosm behind the object is suddenly revealed—in somewhat the way in which the vista beyond a keyhole is magnified if one purposely advances his eye toward it.”

相比之下，里姆兰开始觉得他在海军的日常工作——辅以在当地大学教授异常心理学课程——有些乏味。他作为孤独症研究者的兼职工作使他远远超越了人事测试和数字处理的范畴，得以探索遗传学、神经生理学、生物化学和医学人类学等新兴领域的前沿。要真正理解儿子的状况，需要十几个学科的专家共同参与。他写道："一位智者曾观察到，如果你足够专注地研究一个自然物体，用你所有的注意力和专注力长时间地聚焦于它，那么终将有一个时刻，物体背后的宏观世界会突然显现——就像当你有意将眼睛凑近锁孔时，锁孔外的景象会被放大一样。"



Gloria tried to persuade her husband to turn his ever-growing mountain of notes into a book rather than a mere paper in a journal. But the chances of Rimland’s manuscript being picked up by a major publisher were slim, because he had no relevant credentials in the field. Editors willing to take a chance on a book about a rare psychiatric disorder by a nonexpert were few and far between. But then Rimland heard that a highly respected imprint called Appleton-Century-Crofts was hosting the first in a series of annual awards for a distinguished manuscript in psychology. The submissions were to be judged by a panel of editors blindly: they wouldn’t know the author’s name until the winner had already been selected on the strength of the writing alone. Rimland submitted his manuscript, and a few months later the judges unanimously awarded him the first Century Psychology Series Award, which came with a $1,500 honorarium and a favorable publishing contract. Rimland’s Infantile Autism: The Syndrome and Its Implications for a Neural Theory of Behavior was published in 1964, featuring an introduction by Kanner himself.

格洛丽亚试图说服丈夫，将他日益堆积如山的笔记整理成书，而非仅仅发表一篇期刊论文。但里姆兰的手稿被大型出版社看中的可能性微乎其微，因为他在该领域毫无相关资历。愿意冒险出版一本由非专业人士撰写的罕见精神障碍书籍的编辑更是凤毛麟角。然而，里姆兰得知备受尊敬的出版社 Appleton-Century-Crofts 正在举办首届年度心理学杰出手稿评选。投稿将由编辑小组匿名评审——在仅凭作品质量选定获奖者之前，评审们不会知道作者姓名。里姆兰提交了手稿，数月后评审团全票授予他首届世纪心理学系列奖，奖金 1500 美元并附带优厚出版合同。1964 年，里姆兰的《婴儿孤独症：该综合征及其对行为神经理论的启示》正式出版，坎纳本人亲自作序。



Kanner spent the first half of his introduction asserting his own supremacy as the preeminent authority in the field. After relating his usual account of his serendipitous discovery, he complained that his concept of early infantile autism (“I could not think of a better name,” he added) had been applied so broadly that “the term was used as a pseudodiagnostic wastebasket for a variety of unrelated conditions.” He concluded by characterizing Rimland as a “passer-by” in the autism field that had “tarried . . . by the roadside” long enough to write a book worthy of “respectfully sober scrutiny.”

坎纳在引言的前半部分，极力强调自己作为该领域权威的至高地位。在讲述完他偶然发现的惯常叙述后，他抱怨自己提出的“早期婴儿孤独症”概念（“我想不出更好的名字了，”他补充道）被过度泛化，以至于“这个词被当作一个伪诊断垃圾桶，用来装各种毫不相干的病症。”最后，他将里姆兰描述为孤独症领域的“过客”，只是“在路边逗留”了足够长的时间，写了一本值得“恭敬而审慎审视”的书。



It was a gem of finely calibrated praise by an entrenched authority unwilling to cede an inch of his turf to an industrious upstart. Nevertheless, Rimland was thrilled by his mentor’s introduction and humble about his own accomplishment. “This is a working paper,” he wrote in the preface. “If it isn’t, I don’t know when it stopped being one.”

这是一篇由固守权威、不愿向勤奋的后起之秀让出丝毫地盘的人精心拿捏的赞美之作。尽管如此，里姆兰仍为导师的引言感到振奋，并对自己的成就保持谦逊。“这是一份工作论文，”他在前言中写道，“如果不是，我不知道它何时不再是了。”



He needn’t have been so modest. After decades of confusion, Rimland’s book finally put the science of autism back on the right track by arguing persuasively that it was an inborn “perceptual disability” rather than a form of psychosis caused by childhood trauma. By debunking neo-Freudians like Bettelheim, Rimland liberated parents from a soul-crushing burden of guilt while rendering the rationale for protecting children by putting them in institutions “for their own good” obsolete. He also demonstrated a more nuanced understanding of the special talents and abilities of these children than Kanner had done, granting them an independent existence outside the usual accounting of deficits and dysfunctions. “It is interesting to conjecture that the silent, unreachable autistic child,” he wrote, “may indeed be ‘lost in thought’—reliving an experience in minute detail, hearing music long since forgotten or perhaps never heard by others, or playing games with numbers or objects manipulatable only in the recesses of his brain.”

他本不必如此谦逊。在数十年的混乱之后，里姆兰的著作通过令人信服地论证孤独症是一种先天性的"感知残障"而非童年创伤导致的精神病，终于将孤独症科学重新引回正轨。通过揭穿贝特尔海姆等新弗洛伊德主义者的谬论，里姆兰将父母从摧残心灵的负罪感中解放出来，同时让"为了孩子好"而将他们送入收容机构的保护理由变得过时。他还比坎纳更细致地展现了这些儿童的特殊天赋与能力，赋予他们在通常的缺陷与功能障碍评估之外的独立存在。"不妨猜想，"他写道，"那个沉默、无法触及的孤独谱系儿童，或许真的'沉浸于思绪之中'——在细枝末节中重温经历，聆听早已被遗忘或他人从未听过的音乐，或是在大脑深处独自摆弄数字与可操控的物体。"



He even occasionally allowed himself to take the perspective of the children that he was writing about, as when he described “the fatigue and frustration experienced by the disturbed child in trying to deal with his environment when he was not cognitively equipped to do so . . . Imagine the child’s reaction to the futility of living in an incomprehensible world run by what must appear to him to be demanding, ritualistic, arbitrary and inconsistent psychotics—us!”

他甚至偶尔会让自己代入笔下儿童的视角，比如他描述道："当认知能力尚未发展成熟的孩子试图应对环境时，所感受到的疲惫与挫败……想象一下，孩子面对生活在一个无法理解的世界中的徒劳感——这个世界由一群在他看来苛刻、刻板、专横且反复无常的精神病患者——也就是我们——所掌控。"



Undoubtedly, one of the reasons that Kanner was willing to write an introduction to the book was that Rimland went notably easy on him and Eisenberg for originating the theory of toxic parenting, shifting most of the blame to Bettelheim. He said only that Kanner and Eisenberg had “subscribed” to the notion of psychogenic causation, as if they were innocent bystanders. Thus Bettelheim would go down in history as the primary source of the theory, though he had been virtually parroting Kanner and Eisenberg while adding his own misogynistic flourishes.

毫无疑问，坎纳愿意为这本书作序的原因之一，是里姆兰对他和艾森伯格格外宽容，将"有毒父母"理论的起源主要归咎于贝特尔海姆。里姆兰仅表示坎纳和艾森伯格"认同"了心因性病因的观点，仿佛他们只是无辜的旁观者。于是贝特尔海姆将作为该理论的主要源头载入史册——尽管他几乎是在鹦鹉学舌般重复坎纳和艾森伯格的观点，只是添上了自己厌女式的修饰。



III



Rimland’s comprehensive review of the literature enabled him to become conversant with ideas that wouldn’t be widely circulated in the field for decades. At one point in the book, he even referred to Asperger’s syndrome (without explaining it), though the concept was still virtually unknown outside of Eastern Europe.

里姆兰对文献的全面梳理使他能够熟悉那些在数十年后才在该领域广泛传播的观点。在书中某处，他甚至提到了阿斯伯格综合征（未作解释），尽管这一概念在当时的东欧以外地区几乎无人知晓。



The crux of the book was that autism is primarily a product of genetic inheritance rather than family dynamics, which dozens of studies would confirm in the coming years. But Rimland also presciently suggested that in some cases the syndrome was caused by unknown environmental factors acting upon a genetic predisposition. He speculated that parents who tend to be gifted in certain fields pass this vulnerability down to their children along with the genetic factors for high intelligence. Thus, autism represented a potential for genius that had been derailed somewhere along the line—“brightness gone awry,” as Rimland put it. “We must give serious consideration to the hypothesis that an infant’s road to high intelligence lies along a knife-edged path,” he wrote, “and the higher the potential intelligence, the steeper and more precarious the slope.”

该书的核心观点是，孤独症主要源于遗传因素而非家庭环境，这一结论在随后的几十年里得到了数十项研究的证实。但里姆兰也颇具先见之明地指出，在某些案例中，该综合征是由未知环境因素作用于遗传易感性所引发的。他推测，那些在特定领域具有天赋的父母，会将这种易感性连同高智商的遗传因子一同传递给子女。因此，孤独症代表着一种在某个环节偏离轨道的天才潜能——正如里姆兰所言，"被扭曲的聪慧"。他写道："我们必须认真考虑这样一个假设：婴儿通往高智商的道路如同行走在刀刃之上，潜在智商越高，这条道路就越陡峭、越危险。"



The seeds of this idea were present from the start in Asperger’s descriptions of his patients’ parents as brilliant eccentrics, but despite Rimland’s team of translators, his paper wasn’t cited in the book’s comprehensive bibliography—another sign of how thoroughly erased it had been from history. The notion of a link between autism and high intelligence was also implicit in Kanner’s claims that his patients’ parents were highly educated. By unbundling the “brightness gone awry” hypothesis from the theory of refrigerator parenting, Rimland was no doubt intending to do his mentor a favor.

这一想法的种子从一开始就存在于阿斯伯格对其患者父母的描述中——他将他们形容为才华横溢的怪人。然而，尽管里姆兰拥有一支翻译团队，他的论文却未被收录在该书的详尽参考文献中——这再次表明它已被彻底从历史中抹去。孤独症与高智商之间存在关联的观点，也隐含在坎纳关于其患者父母受过高等教育的论断中。里姆兰将“偏离正轨的天才”假说从冰箱父母理论中剥离出来，无疑是想帮他的导师一个忙。



This hypothesis would fall into disrepute in the 1970s as studies by Michael Rutter and others proved that autism does not discriminate by IQ or educational level and is equally prevalent across all socioeconomic strata. Yet even in the face of this evidence, neither Kanner nor Rimland ever disavowed the theory, claiming that their clinical experience had consistently shown it to be true. One of the reasons Rimland was so resistant to the idea of a spectrum is that he was convinced that only true cases of Kanner’s syndrome were linked to the potential for genius. “The conclusion I reached then, and by which I stand today,” he wrote in 1994, “is that Kanner’s finding is irrefutable if, but only if, one uses the strict and limited definition of autism insisted upon by him.”

这一假说在 20 世纪 70 年代因迈克尔·拉特等人的研究而声誉扫地——这些研究证明，孤独症与智商或教育水平无关，且在所有社会经济阶层中发病率均等。然而即便面对这些证据，坎纳和里姆兰都从未放弃该理论，坚称自己的临床经验始终证实其正确性。里姆兰之所以如此抗拒谱系概念，原因之一在于他坚信只有真正的坎纳综合征病例才与天才潜质相关。他在 1994 年写道："我当年得出的结论至今仍坚持——坎纳的发现无可辩驳，但前提是必须严格遵循他所坚持的孤独症狭义定义。"



Clues that Rimland may have been on to something have been popping up ever since. In 2003, Kathrin Hippler at the University of Vienna undertook a study of the case records of patients diagnosed by Asperger after the war; she found a significantly higher number of fathers working in the technical professions—particularly electrical engineering—than the fathers of a control group. Researchers at the University of Edinburgh discovered in 2015 that genes associated with austism are also associated with higher levels of cognitive ability—particularly problem-solving tasks requiring nonverbal, hands-on intelligence. The flaw in Rimland’s idea may have been his attempt to link autism to general intelligence—which is notoriously difficult to measure in autistic people anyway—rather than to a specific set of aptitudes.

此后，关于里姆兰可能有所发现的线索不断涌现。2003 年，维也纳大学的凯瑟琳·希普勒对战后阿斯伯格医生诊断的患者病历进行了研究，发现这些患者的父亲从事技术职业（尤其是电气工程）的比例显著高于对照组。2015 年，爱丁堡大学的研究人员发现，与孤独谱系相关的基因也与更高水平的认知能力有关——尤其是需要非语言、动手能力的解决问题任务。里姆兰观点的缺陷可能在于，他试图将孤独谱系与一般智力联系起来——而孤独谱系群体的智力本就极难衡量——而非与特定能力组相关联。



Though Rimland never identified himself in the book as the father of an autistic child, he was unstinting in chronicling the ravaging effects of misguided autism theories on families like his own. “If autism is solely determined by organic factors,” he writes, “there is no need for the parents of these children to suffer the shame, guilt, inconvenience, financial expense, and marital discord which so often accompany the assumption of psychogenic etiology.” He describes families having to take their child “from clinic to clinic in the hope of finding someone who understands the disease.” For parents who couldn’t afford to undertake this kind of doctor shopping, it was undoubtedly even harder, which is reflected in the continuing underdiagnosis of autism in minority communities to the present day.

尽管里姆兰德从未在书中表明自己是一名孤独症儿童的父亲，但他不遗余力地记录了错误的孤独症理论对像他这样的家庭造成的毁灭性影响。他写道："如果孤独症完全由生理因素决定，那么这些孩子的父母就无需承受因心理病因假设而常伴随的羞耻、内疚、不便、经济负担和婚姻矛盾。"他描述了家庭不得不带着孩子"辗转于各个诊所，希望能找到真正了解这种疾病的人"。对于那些无力承担这种四处求医费用的父母来说，情况无疑更加艰难——这一点至今仍体现在少数族裔社区中孤独症持续被低估诊断的现象上。



The most outdated aspect of the book is Rimland’s unquestioning faith in Kanner’s narrow definition of autism. At various points, it seems like he’s trying to outdo even his mentor by coming up with more and more inventive ways of excluding children from the diagnosis. Rimland describes autistic children as “almost invariably in excellent health, beautiful and well formed, and usually of dark complexion” (as opposed to those with childhood schizophrenia, whom he portrays as having blond hair and blue eyes, translucent skin, a receding chin, and “an almost foetus-like appearance”). He marvels at their “excellent, and in fact, often extraordinary motor ability . . . with regard to both gross body movement and finger dexterity,” and claims they enjoy a remarkable “freedom from allergies, asthma, metabolic disturbances, and skin problems.”

这本书最过时的部分在于里姆兰对坎纳狭隘的孤独症定义毫无保留的信任。在多个段落中，他似乎试图超越自己的导师，用越来越离奇的方式将儿童排除在诊断之外。里姆兰将孤独症儿童描述为"几乎总是健康极佳、容貌俊美、体态匀称，且通常肤色较深"（与之相对的是他笔下患有童年精神分裂症的儿童：金发碧眼、皮肤透亮、下巴后缩，呈现"近乎胎儿的样貌"）。他惊叹于他们"卓越的——事实上往往是超常的运动能力……无论是大肌肉群动作还是手指灵活性"，并声称他们享有"对过敏、哮喘、代谢紊乱和皮肤问题的罕见免疫力"。



In keeping with Kanner’s model, he also lists a multitude of other disqualifying factors, including signs of regression, seizures, abnormal EEG readings, pale skin, a “dull, retarded” appearance, “soft doughy” muscle tone, absence of savant skills, visible anxiety and confusion, the presence of mental illness in the family tree, spinning in place, and toe-walking. The latter two behaviors were particularly curious choices, since a number of Kanner’s patients exhibited them, and they are now considered telltale signs of autism.

遵循坎纳的模式，他还列举了大量其他排除因素，包括退化迹象、癫痫发作、脑电图异常、肤色苍白、“呆滞迟钝”的外表、“软面团般”的肌肉张力、缺乏学者技能、明显的焦虑和困惑、家族精神疾病史、原地旋转以及踮脚走路。后两种行为的选择尤为奇特，因为坎纳的许多患者都表现出这些特征，而如今它们被视为孤独症的典型标志。



But Rimland would brook no interlopers in his mentor’s walled garden. He even apologized to the reader in advance: “It may be that some of the cases the present writer cites in this book to illustrate the phenomena of autism may in fact be instances where only some of the symptoms are shared with infantile autism, although an attempt to guard against this sort of error has been made.” In Rimland’s view, true cases of Kanner’s syndrome were about as scarce as true Scotsmen.

但里姆兰绝不允许任何人闯入他导师的封闭花园。他甚至提前向读者致歉：“本书中笔者为说明孤独症现象而引用的某些案例，可能实际上仅与婴儿孤独症共享部分症状，尽管笔者已尽力避免此类错误。”在里姆兰看来，真正的坎纳综合征病例与真正的苏格兰人一样罕见。



—



BUT RIMLAND HAD ANOTHER good reason for wanting the diagnosis to be strictly defined. He nurtured the hope that autism would turn out to be a glitch in a single metabolic pathway that could be averted with a dietary intervention, like another genetic condition called phenylketonuria, or PKU.

但里姆兰还有另一个充分理由希望严格定义孤独症。他怀揣着一个希望：孤独症可能像另一种名为苯丙酮尿症（PKU）的遗传病一样，是单一代谢通路中的故障，可以通过饮食干预来避免。



When Rimland first read Pearl S. Buck’s touching memoir of raising her daughter, Carol, The Child Who Never Grew, he thought it was a story about autism. Moments after Carol was born, Buck turned to a nurse and said, “Doesn’t she look very wise for her age?” Even as a baby, Carol seemed to go into ecstasy when her mother played symphonies on the phonograph. But by the time she was three, it was obvious that she was losing ground, and she would explode in angry rages. The tale of PKU’s discovery became a template for Rimland, who wrote his book, in part, in an attempt to make history repeat itself.

当里姆兰第一次读到赛珍珠那本感人至深的回忆录《永不长大的孩子》——讲述她抚养女儿卡罗尔的经历时，他以为这是一个关于孤独症的故事。卡罗尔刚出生时，赛珍珠转向护士说：“她看起来是不是比同龄孩子聪明得多？”即使在婴儿时期，当母亲用留声机播放交响乐时，卡罗尔似乎也会陷入狂喜。但到了三岁时，她明显开始退步，并且会爆发出愤怒的脾气。PKU 的发现历程成为了里姆兰的模板，他撰写自己的书，部分原因正是为了让历史重演。



In the 1920s, a young couple in Oslo named Harry and Borgny Egeland had their first child—a daughter, Liv. Like Carol Buck and Mark Rimland, she had an aura of precocious wisdom, but by the time she was three she had still never spoken a word. The family pediatrician assured the Egelands that their daughter was perfectly healthy and would talk on her own schedule. Then the couple had a son named Dag. At first, he was a spunky and alert baby, but gradually he seemed to lose all interest in the world around him.

20 世纪 20 年代，奥斯陆一对年轻夫妇哈里·埃格兰德和博格尼·埃格兰德迎来了他们的第一个孩子——女儿莉芙。和卡罗尔·巴克、马克·里姆兰一样，她周身萦绕着早慧的气质，但直到三岁仍不曾开口说话。家庭儿科医生向埃格兰德夫妇保证，女儿完全健康，只是说话较晚。随后这对夫妇又生下了儿子达格。起初他是个活泼机敏的婴儿，但渐渐地他似乎对周围的世界失去了所有兴趣。



Then Borgny happened to notice an odd, musty odor emanating from her children’s diapers. She began to wonder if this odor might be related to their failure to thrive. The Egelands took their children to doctor after doctor and finally brought Dag down to the University Hospital in Oslo for an extensive round of tests. But they revealed nothing. Desperate for any sort of help, Borgny called on healers, herbalists, and psychics who brewed up teas for the children, concocted healing baths, and sought the source of their malady in visions. Then Harry remembered that one of his professors at dental school, Asbørn Følling, was a specialist in metabolic diseases. Borgny asked her sister, who knew Følling socially, to ask him about the smell in her children’s urine, and he agreed to run a detailed analysis.

这时，博格尼偶然注意到孩子们的尿布散发出一股奇怪的霉味。她开始怀疑这股气味是否与孩子们发育不良有关。埃格兰夫妇带着孩子辗转求医，最后带达格到奥斯陆大学医院做了一系列全面检查，却一无所获。走投无路的博格尼求助于治疗师、草药师和通灵者，他们为孩子熬制草药茶、配制药浴，试图通过幻象寻找病因。这时哈里想起牙医学院的一位教授阿斯比约恩·福林是代谢疾病专家。博格尼请与福林有私交的姐姐代为询问孩子尿液异味的问题，福林同意进行详细分析。



Before doing that, Følling asked Borgny to immediately stop giving her children all the herbal remedies she’d been feeding them, to ensure that the signal of whatever was causing this condition was not lost in statistical noise. Only then did he begin his testing. Scans for blood, pus, albumen, and sugar revealed nothing unusual, but then Følling tried adding a few drops of ferric chloride solution to Liv’s urine sample. Instead of turning the usual reddish-brown, indicating the presence of ketones, it flashed brightly with an ominous green color that quickly faded. Adding drops of ferric chloride to Dag’s sample produced the same unusual result. Never having seen this kind of reaction before, Følling checked his chemistry textbooks but found no useful clues. For two months, he beavered away in the lab, analyzing more than twenty liters of the children’s urine in total.

在此之前，福林要求博格尼立即停止给孩子们服用所有草药，以确保导致这种病症的信号不会被统计噪音掩盖。直到这时，他才开始进行检测。血液、脓液、白蛋白和糖分的扫描均未发现异常，但随后福林尝试在莉芙的尿液样本中加入几滴氯化铁溶液。溶液并未像通常那样呈现红褐色（表明存在酮体），而是闪现出令人不安的绿色，随即迅速消退。对达格的样本进行同样操作后，也出现了相同的不寻常结果。福林从未见过这种反应，他查阅了化学教科书，却未找到任何有用线索。整整两个月，他埋头在实验室里，总共分析了孩子们超过二十升的尿液。



Finally, after evacuating the air from his test tubes with nitrogen, he isolated the crystals of a compound called phenylpyruvic acid that is not normally present in urine. Then he got in touch with mental institutions in the area and asked them to send him samples. Eight out of 430 samples from intellectually disabled children yielded the same odd compound. With more detective work, he discovered that the crystals were a by-product of the children being unable to metabolize a common amino acid called phenylalanine, which is present in cow and breast milk. As a result, the acid slowly built up in their bloodstreams, damaging the children’s developing brains and cascading into their urine and producing that musty smell.

最后，在用氮气排空试管中的空气后，他分离出了一种名为苯丙酮酸的化合物晶体，这种物质通常不存在于尿液中。随后，他联系了当地的精神病院，请求寄送样本。在 430 份智力障碍儿童的样本中，有 8 份检测出了这种异常化合物。通过进一步的调查，他发现这些晶体是孩子们无法代谢一种常见氨基酸——苯丙氨酸的副产品，这种氨基酸存在于牛奶和母乳中。结果，这种酸在血液中逐渐积累，损害了孩子们正在发育的大脑，并渗入尿液，产生了那股霉味。



Følling named this syndrome “imbecillitas phenypyruvica,” which was eventually rechristened phenylketonuria. By studying affected families, he was able to determine that the syndrome was carried on a single recessive gene. If a child inherits copies of the gene from both parents, they are born with PKU. After Buck’s book was published, clinicians developed a simple diaper test for PKU that could be administered a few weeks after birth, which was promptly replaced by a blood test that could be given before a newborn left the hospital. Meanwhile, researchers developed a low-phenylalanine diet that could avert PKU’s disabling effects if it was started in infancy. In countries where most children have access to good health care, PKU-induced intellectual disability is now a thing of the past. None of these breakthroughs would have happened, Rimland reflected, if Følling had written off Liv and Dag as hopeless cases of a generic disorder called mental retardation.

Følling 将这种综合征命名为“苯丙酮尿性智力低下”，后来改称为苯丙酮尿症。通过研究受影响的家庭，他确定这种综合征由单个隐性基因携带。如果孩子从父母双方各继承一个该基因的拷贝，就会天生患有 PKU。在巴克的书出版后，临床医生开发了一种简单的 PKU 尿布检测法，可在出生后几周内进行，但很快被新生儿出院前即可完成的血液检测所取代。与此同时，研究人员开发出一种低苯丙氨酸饮食，若在婴儿期开始使用，可避免 PKU 的致残影响。在大多数儿童能获得优质医疗服务的国家，PKU 导致的智力障碍如今已成为历史。里姆兰感慨道，如果 Følling 当初将利夫和达格视为某种名为“智力低下”的泛泛障碍的绝望病例而放弃研究，这些突破便无一可能实现。



IV



Rimland’s highest hope for his book was that it would kick-start a new era of autism research. To facilitate this process, he came up with a smart idea for soliciting data from his readers by making his book interactive. In the appendix, he included a questionnaire called the “Diagnostic Check List for Behavior-Disturbed Children (Form E-1),” designed as a template for clinicians to copy and give to parents. In keeping with Kanner’s notion that autism was a disorder of infancy, most of the seventy-six questions focused on a child’s behavior in the first six years of life.

里姆兰德对他的书寄予的最大期望，是它能开启孤独症研究的新纪元。为了推动这一进程，他想出了一个巧妙的主意：让书具有互动性，从而向读者征集数据。在附录中，他附上了一份名为《行为异常儿童诊断检查表（E-1 表）》的问卷，旨在作为临床医生复制并分发给家长的模板。遵循坎纳关于孤独症是婴幼儿期障碍的观点，七十六个问题大多聚焦于孩子生命最初六年的行为表现。



Did (does) the child stare into space for long periods of time as though lost in thought?

孩子是否曾长时间凝视虚空，仿佛陷入沉思？



Did you ever suspect the child was very nearly deaf?

您是否曾怀疑孩子几乎完全失聪？



Does the child ever “look through” or “walk through” people, as though they weren’t there?

孩子是否曾“视若无睹”或“穿人而过”，仿佛他人不存在？



Did (does) he say phrases over and over in a hollow, parrot-like or echo-like voice, to no purpose?

他（是否）曾毫无意义地、空洞地、像鹦鹉学舌或回声一样反复说某些短语？



Did (does) he consistently use the word “You” when he should say “I”?

他（是否）在应该说“我”的时候，却一直用“你”这个词？



Did (does) the child seem to want to be liked?

这个孩子（是否）似乎渴望被人喜欢？



Other sections of the questionnaire were designed to serve Rimland’s interest in the biomedical dimensions of autism, featuring questions about the child’s eating habits and digestion, skin condition, and temperature regulation. He was delighted when a biochemist read the book and said that he would start searching for signs of a metabolic failure in autism in the hope of developing a nutritional regime similar to the low-phenylalanine diet.

问卷的其他部分旨在服务于里姆兰对孤独症生物医学维度的兴趣，包含关于孩子饮食习惯、消化情况、皮肤状况和体温调节的问题。当一位生物化学家阅读这本书后表示，他将开始寻找孤独症中代谢障碍的迹象，以期开发出类似低苯丙氨酸饮食的营养方案时，里姆兰感到非常高兴。



Amazingly, the E-1 was the first standardized clinical tool for autism assessment. Up to that point, the diagnosis was made strictly on the basis of subjective observation by clinicians schooled in Kanner’s and Eisenberg’s methods. Children who didn’t precisely fit Rimland’s version of Kanner’s model got only a diagnosis of “autistic-like.” In this act of winnowing, Rimland was again following in the footsteps of his mentor, who told him in a letter that nine out of ten children sent to his office with an autism diagnosis by other clinicians were not “true cases.”

令人惊讶的是，E-1 是首个用于孤独症评估的标准化临床工具。在此之前，诊断完全基于接受过坎纳和艾森伯格方法训练的临床医生的主观观察。那些不完全符合里姆兰版本中坎纳模型的孩子，只能被诊断为"类孤独症"。在这场筛选过程中，里姆兰再次追随导师的脚步——导师曾在信中告诉他，其他临床医生送到他办公室的十个被诊断为孤独症的孩子中，有九个并非"真实病例"。



—



AFTER THE BOOK CAME OUT, Rimland had no particular plans to do further writing in the field, and he assumed that he would turn his attention back to his day job. But the future of quiet anonymity in Kensington that he imagined for himself and his family was not to be.

书出版后，里姆兰并未计划在该领域继续写作，他以为会将注意力转回日常工作。但他在肯辛顿为自己和家人设想的平静匿名生活，终究未能实现。



Hardly a week had gone by after publication when E-1 forms that had been torn out of the book, filled in by a parent (usually the mother), and sent directly to the address of the U.S. Naval Personnel Research Laboratory listed on the title page—often accompanied by a handwritten letter—started flooding Rimland’s mailbox. “That just killed Bernard,” Gloria recalls. “He said, ‘look what they’re doing to my book!’” But he quickly realized that the forms piling up at his door were the most earnest kind of praise he could get from his fellow parents. He opened a file in his office for each child whose mother or father reached out to him directly. In the months to come, hundreds of E-1s would make their way back to Rimland. After scoring the checklists with a proprietary algorithm, he would report the results to parents by mail, typically following up with a personal phone call.

书出版后不到一周，从书中撕下的 E-1 表格（通常由母亲填写）便如潮水般涌入里姆兰的信箱——这些表格直接寄往扉页上列出的美国海军人事研究实验室地址，往往还附有手写信件。格洛丽亚回忆道："这简直要了伯纳德的命。他说'看看他们对我的书做了什么！'"但他很快意识到，堆在门口的表格正是其他父母能给予他最真诚的赞美。他为每位主动联系他的孩子的父母在办公室建立了档案。在接下来的几个月里，数百份 E-1 表格陆续回到里姆兰手中。他用专有算法对清单进行评分后，通过邮件将结果告知家长，通常还会亲自打电话跟进。



After years of isolation, he and Gloria knew all too well how lonely raising an autistic child could be. His conversations with parents engaged a gregarious and empathetic side of him, and he became “Uncle Bernie” to a generation of families—ready to pick up the phone any time of day or night, and eager to sit down with any distraught mother or father who showed up at his door looking for help.

经过多年的与世隔绝，他和格洛丽亚深知抚养一个孤独谱系的孩子有多么孤独。他与家长们的交谈激发了他健谈且富有同理心的一面，他成了一代家庭的"伯尼叔叔"——随时准备接听电话，无论白天黑夜，也乐于与任何找上门来寻求帮助的焦虑父母坐下来交谈。



He also used the notes that parents scribbled in the margins of the E-1 to refine his questionnaire. When a second edition of Infantile Autism was published a couple of years later, it featured an updated version of the checklist called the E-2, designed for parents to send to Rimland directly. Persuading parents desperate for information about their children to fill out a form turned out to be a lot easier than persuading other researchers to make use of his data. Studies of the E-1 and E-2 in peer-reviewed journals raised questions about their accuracy. One problem was that the questionnaires depended on parental memories of a child’s behavior in infancy, which could be unreliable. Also, the results of his algorithm correlated only moderately well with clinical assessment by other means. This was the inevitable result of his highly selective winnowing process, but it added to doubts about the accuracy of his methods, which hurt his professional pride.

他还利用家长在 E-1 表格边缘潦草写下的笔记来完善自己的问卷。几年后，《婴儿孤独症》第二版出版时，其中包含了一份名为 E-2 的更新版检查表，专为家长直接寄给里姆兰设计。说服那些急于了解孩子情况的家长填写表格，远比说服其他研究人员使用他的数据要容易得多。同行评审期刊中对 E-1 和 E-2 的研究对其准确性提出了质疑。其中一个问题是，这些问卷依赖于家长对孩子婴儿期行为的记忆，这并不可靠。此外，他的算法结果与其他临床评估方法的相关性仅为中等水平。这是他高度选择性的筛选过程不可避免的结果，但这进一步加深了对其方法准确性的怀疑，也伤害了他的职业自尊心。



By forging a direct connection with the parents who wrote to him, Rimland ended up taking a much more subversive path that directly challenged the authority of the psychiatric establishment. Instead of becoming the gold standard of autism assessment, Rimland’s questionnaires planted the seeds of a revolution.

通过与那些写信给他的父母建立直接联系，里姆兰最终走上了一条更具颠覆性的道路，直接挑战了精神病学界的权威。他的问卷并未成为孤独症评估的黄金标准，反而播下了一场革命的种子。



V



The hardest thing to come by for the parents of children like Mark in the 1960s was hope. Clinicians had little to offer beyond the standard advice to institutionalize the child and quietly remove their pictures from the family album. Parents determined to raise their sons and daughters at home, like Clara Claiborne Park (mother of Jessy Park) and Eustacia Cutler (mother of Temple Grandin), were condemned for endangering their welfare by trapping them in a psychically toxic environment.

对于 20 世纪 60 年代像马克这样的孩子的父母来说，最难以获得的是希望。临床医生除了建议将孩子送入收容机构、悄悄从家庭相册中移除他们的照片之外，几乎无法提供任何帮助。像克拉拉·克莱本·帕克（杰西·帕克的母亲）和尤斯塔西亚·卡特勒（坦普尔·格兰丁的母亲）这样决心在家抚养子女的父母，被指责将孩子困在精神上有毒的环境中，从而危及他们的福祉。



Two decades after Asperger wrote his guide to appropriate methods for teaching autistic children, most psychologists in America were still convinced that they were constitutionally incapable of learning. Rimland’s book doesn’t even touch on the topic of education, instead using the terms training and conditioning, employed by behaviorists to describe the process of training an animal to respond to certain stimuli in Pavlovian fashion. To his credit, Rimland also noted that “very little has been published relating to true cases of autism grown to maturity,” and the few studies available were decidedly discouraging.

在阿斯伯格撰写孤独谱系儿童教学指南二十年后，美国大多数心理学家仍坚信这些儿童天生不具备学习能力。里姆兰的著作甚至未涉及教育议题，而是沿用行为主义者的术语"训练"与"条件作用"——这些术语原本用于描述巴甫洛夫式动物对特定刺激形成条件反射的过程。值得肯定的是，里姆兰也指出"关于孤独症患者成年后真实案例的文献极少"，而仅有的几项研究结果也令人沮丧。



In 1956, Eisenberg published a paper called “The Autistic Child in Adolescence” based on his case files from the Harriet Lane. Of the sixty-three teenagers he was able to locate, more than half were confined to institutions. He divided the group into three categories of outcome: good, fair, and poor. A good outcome was defined as “a patient who is functioning well at an academic, social, and community level and who is accepted by his peers, though he may remain a somewhat odd person.” A poor outcome described a patient who “has not emerged from autism to any extent and whose present function is markedly maladaptive, characterized by apparent feeblemindedness and/or grossly disturbed behavior.” Only three patients in the group rated a good outcome, while forty-six were classified as poor. The factor most predictive of their outcome, Eisenberg said, was the presence of “useful speech.”

1956 年，艾森伯格根据他在哈里特·莱恩诊所的病历档案发表了一篇题为《孤独谱系青少年》的论文。在他能找到的 63 名青少年中，超过半数被收容在福利机构。他将这些患者按预后分为三类：良好、一般和不良。良好预后被定义为"患者在学业、社交和社区层面功能良好，能被同龄人接纳，尽管可能仍有些古怪"。不良预后则描述为"患者未从孤独谱系中走出任何程度，当前功能明显适应不良，表现为明显的智力缺陷和/或严重行为紊乱"。该组中仅有 3 名患者被评为良好预后，46 名被归为不良预后。艾森伯格指出，最能预测预后的因素是是否具备"有效语言能力"。



Even Bettelheim had offered families a twisted version of hope with his claims that years at the Orthogenic School could unravel the knots tied in a baby’s psyche by an icy and domineering mother. It haunted Rimland that, by reframing autism as a genetic disorder instead of a psychogenic one, he had subtracted even that comforting illusion from the equation, contributing to an attitude of “therapeutic hopelessness.” With hundreds of parents now looking to him for help and advice, what did he have to offer them?

就连贝特尔海姆也曾给家庭带来一种扭曲的希望，他声称在正统学校多年的治疗可以解开婴儿因冷漠专横的母亲而在心灵中打下的结。这让里姆兰深感不安：他将孤独症重新定义为遗传性疾病而非心理源性疾病，从而从方程式中移除了那个令人安慰的幻觉，助长了“治疗无望”的态度。如今，数百名家长向他寻求帮助和建议，他又能给他们什么呢？



—



ON AN OCTOBER DAY IN 1964, Rimland found his answer in a blunt-spoken psychologist at the University of California in Los Angeles named Ole Ivar Lovaas. On the surface, the two men were polar opposites: Rimland was a warm, attentive teddy bear from the Midwest, while Lovaas was a ruddy Nordic outdoorsman who would flash his dazzling smile before shaming his colleagues at dinner by saying, “There are more brains in this salad than in the people at this table.” But both men were driven and ambitious, disillusioned with the state of psychology, and marginalized by their peers because of their fascination with the same obscure childhood disorder.

1964 年 10 月的一天，里姆兰在加州大学洛杉矶分校找到了一位直言不讳的心理学家——奥勒·伊瓦尔·洛瓦斯，答案就此浮现。表面上看，两人截然相反：里姆兰是来自中西部的温暖、体贴的泰迪熊，而洛瓦斯则是一位面色红润的北欧户外运动者，他会露出灿烂的笑容，然后在晚餐时羞辱同事说：“这沙拉里的脑子比这桌人加起来都多。”但两人都充满干劲和雄心，对心理学的现状感到失望，并因对同一种鲜为人知的儿童障碍的痴迷而被同行边缘化。



Like so many other first-generation autism researchers, Lovaas came of age in Hitler’s shadow, the son of a journalist and a farmer’s daughter. In 1927, he was born in a village near Oslo called Lier, celebrated for its prolific orchards and fertile fields. As a boy, Ivar would take the train with his family to the mountains, where the snowpack sparkled like diamonds. But everything changed on the morning of April 9, 1940, when he arrived at school to find his teachers in tears. They told him that he must return home immediately because the Nazis had invaded Norway by sea and air to claim it as their own territory. That afternoon, young Ivar saw “green-colored men in their funny helmets” crawling all over his family’s valley “like aphids in the Garden of Eden.”

与众多第一代孤独症研究者一样，洛瓦斯在希特勒的阴影下长大，他的父亲是记者，母亲是农民的女儿。1927 年，他出生在奥斯陆附近一个叫利尔的村庄，这里以丰饶的果园和肥沃的田野闻名。童年时，伊瓦尔常随家人乘火车前往山区，那里的积雪如钻石般闪耀。但 1940 年 4 月 9 日清晨，一切戛然而止——当他到校时，发现老师们泪流满面。他们告诉他必须立刻回家，因为纳粹已从海空两路入侵挪威，将其据为己有。那天下午，年幼的伊瓦尔目睹"戴着滑稽头盔的绿衣人"爬满他家的山谷，"就像伊甸园里的蚜虫"。



By June, the Allied defense forces had been utterly crushed, King Haakon was in exile, and all the radios had been confiscated from the Jewish families in Oslo in preparation for mass deportation to concentration camps. For the next five years, Lovaas and his family were forced to work as migrant laborers, eating only what they could grow themselves, picking cabbages and turnips in the frigid air ten hours a day until their arms and legs felt numb.

到六月时，盟军防御部队已彻底溃败，哈康国王流亡海外，奥斯陆犹太家庭的收音机全被没收，为大规模驱逐至集中营做准备。此后五年间，洛瓦斯一家被迫成为流动劳工，只能靠自种作物果腹，每天在凛冽寒风中采摘卷心菜和芜菁十小时，直到四肢麻木失去知觉。



When the war ended, Lovaas was allowed to immigrate to the United States on the strength of his violin playing. He got a music scholarship at Luther College in Iowa and earned his bachelor’s degree in a year by sleeping three or four hours a night. After seeing a photograph of the snowcapped Olympic mountain range in a newspaper, he hopped on a Greyhound bus to Seattle, knocking on doors until he found a family that would rent him a room in exchange for performing household chores. Then he strolled down to the University of Washington and talked his way into the graduate program in psychology. In another uncanny coincidence of autism history, one of his roommates at UW was Eddie Alf, Gloria Rimland’s brother.

战争结束后，洛瓦斯凭借小提琴技艺获准移民美国。他在爱荷华州路德学院获得音乐奖学金，每晚只睡三四个小时，一年内便取得学士学位。某日看到报纸上积雪覆盖的奥林匹克山脉照片后，他跳上灰狗巴士直奔西雅图，挨家挨户敲门，最终找到一户人家——以做家务换取食宿。随后他漫步至华盛顿大学，凭三寸不烂之舌说服校方录取他攻读心理学研究生。在孤独症史上又一桩奇妙的巧合中，他在华盛顿大学的室友正是格洛丽亚·里姆兰德的弟弟埃迪·阿尔夫。



Lovaas set out to become a psychoanalyst, like nearly every other psychology student in America at the time. But he didn’t have the knack for it: “My clients would ask me, ‘Do you mean that by talking to you, I will get better?’ I answered ‘Yes.’ But they often didn’t get better; instead, they got worse.” Weary of pretending to be interested as his clients free-associated on the couch, he took a post as a psychiatric aide at the Pinel Institute, a private asylum that housed the wayward scions of Seattle’s upper crust. One summer, two patients killed themselves by plummeting to the pavement out of second-story windows. “The doctors were all medically oriented, so they called it a ‘suicide epidemic,’ as if it was a contagious disease,” Lovaas recalled in disgust. He quickly lost patience with the tendentious speculations of theory-based psychiatry. After hearing his colleagues droning on in a symposium, he said, “They were like Nero, playing fiddles as the world burned. When you see a war and how horrible it can be to people, you want to be relevant—you want to do something about this world.”

洛瓦斯最初立志成为一名精神分析师，就像当时美国几乎所有的心理学学生一样。但他并不擅长此道：“我的来访者会问我，‘你的意思是，通过和你谈话，我就能好起来吗？’我回答‘是的。’但他们往往没有好转，反而恶化了。”厌倦了在来访者躺在沙发上自由联想时装出感兴趣的样子，他接受了皮内尔研究所精神科护理员的职位，这是一家私立精神病院，收治西雅图上流社会那些任性的子弟。那年夏天，两名患者从二楼窗户跳下，摔死在路面上。“医生们都是医学导向的，所以他们称之为‘自杀流行病’，仿佛这是一种传染病，”洛瓦斯厌恶地回忆道。他很快对基于理论的精神病学中那些带有偏见的推测失去了耐心。在一次研讨会上听到同事们冗长乏味的发言后，他说：“他们就像尼禄，在世界燃烧时还在拉小提琴。当你看到战争，看到它给人们带来的可怕后果时，你会想要有所作为——想要为这个世界做点什么。”



Fortunately for Lovaas, many of his professors at UW felt the same way. His advisor was not a psychotherapist but a behaviorist who encouraged him to pursue lab research. One of the stars of the department was Sid Bijou, a former student of B. F. Skinner’s who pioneered the use of operant conditioning with intellectually disabled children. The paradigmatic example of operant conditioning was Skinner and his rats. To train a rat to press a bar, he would give the animal a food pellet (the reward) if it accidentally approached the bar. If the rat happened to brush the bar with its paw, he would give it another pellet, and then another if it pressed its paw down firmly on the bar. Typically, the end result of this painstaking step-by-step process was the rat frantically punching the bar to get more pellets.

幸运的是，洛瓦斯在华盛顿大学的许多教授也持相同观点。他的导师并非心理治疗师，而是一位行为主义者，鼓励他从事实验室研究。系里的明星人物之一是西德·比茹，他是 B.F.斯金纳的学生，率先将操作性条件反射应用于智力障碍儿童。操作性条件反射的典型范例是斯金纳和他的老鼠。为了训练老鼠按压杠杆，斯金纳会在老鼠偶然靠近杠杆时给予一颗食物丸（奖励）。如果老鼠碰巧用爪子擦过杠杆，他会再给一颗；若老鼠用力将爪子按在杠杆上，则继续给予奖励。通常，这种费力的逐步训练最终会导致老鼠疯狂地按压杠杆以获取更多食物丸。



Conversely, to condition the animal to stop pressing the bar, Skinner would quit delivering pellets until the rat stopped emitting the behavior (a process known as extinction). Another way to extinguish the behavior would be to zap the rat with an electric shock instead of delivering the pellet (the term of art for this was punishment). The use of punishment on animals was controversial among behaviorists but not because it seemed cruel; the animal’s internal state—if it had one—was considered completely irrelevant, a black box. In practice, however, punishment turned out to be an inefficient method of training an animal to extinction because it tended to increase the emission of behaviors unrelated to the task at hand. (In other words, the panicked animal would start trying anything to escape the painful shocks.)

相反，为了让动物停止按压杠杆，斯金纳会停止投喂食丸，直到老鼠不再做出该行为（这一过程称为消退）。另一种消除该行为的方式是用电击代替食丸（专业术语称为惩罚）。在行为主义者中，对动物使用惩罚存在争议，但这并非因为其显得残忍——动物的内在状态（如果存在的话）被认为完全无关紧要，如同一个黑箱。然而在实践中，惩罚被证明是训练动物消退行为的低效方法，因为它往往会增加与当前任务无关的行为频率。（换言之，受惊的动物会开始尝试各种方法逃避痛苦的电击。）



To adapt Skinner’s model for use with human beings, Bijou analyzed behavior in terms of its antecedents (its triggers in the environment) and its consequences (which could include reward or punishment, depending on whether the experimenter wanted the behavior to increase or decrease). He called the meticulous recording and study of this sequence behavior analysis. By experimentally manipulating antecedents and consequences, Bijou found that behavior analysis could be a powerful tool for facilitating change in the responses of human subjects. Furthermore, in these cases, the rewards and punishments available to the experimenter were not limited to food pellets and electric shocks. “That’s a good boy!” could be as rewarding to a child as a food pellet to a rat; and a sharp “No!” could serve as the equivalent of a punishing jolt. In the lexicon of the field, language was a powerful discriminative stimulus in human subjects.

为使斯金纳模型适用于人类，比茹从行为的前因（环境中的触发因素）与后果（可能包含奖励或惩罚，取决于实验者希望该行为增加或减少）两个维度分析行为。他将这种对行为序列的细致记录与研究称为行为分析。通过实验操控前因与后果，比茹发现行为分析能成为促进人类受试者反应改变的有力工具。此外，在这些案例中，实验者可用的奖励与惩罚并不局限于食物颗粒与电击。"真是个好孩子！"对儿童的激励作用堪比食物颗粒对老鼠的效果；而严厉的"不行！"则能起到类似惩罚性电击的作用。在该领域的术语体系中，语言是人类受试者中一种强有力的辨别性刺激。



Or rather, in most human subjects. After earning his doctorate at UW, Lovaas stayed on in Seattle for three years, teaching and conducting research at the Child Development Institute near the university, where he had two experiences that decisively shaped the course of his career. He saw a girl who could not talk, did not make eye contact, and refused to play with toys, spending whole days rocking back and forth and flapping her hands. He knew that the most likely fate in store for her was to be sent to a state hospital where she would live out the rest of her days on a locked ward. Could nothing more be done for her?

更准确地说，是在大多数人类受试者身上。洛瓦斯在华盛顿大学获得博士学位后，在西雅图待了三年，在大学附近的儿童发展研究所从事教学和研究工作。在那里，他经历了两个决定性地塑造了他职业生涯的事件。他看到一个女孩不会说话，不会进行眼神交流，拒绝玩玩具，整天来回摇晃并拍打双手。他知道她最可能的命运是被送进州立医院，在封闭病房里度过余生。难道就没有更多能为她做的事情了吗？



Then he observed an experiment that suggested a more promising possibility. Watching a typically developing boy being conditioned to use speech to obtain a toy trinket—a trivial task at best—it occurred to Lovaas that improving the language skills of children with developmental delays might give them more leverage over their problematic behavior. In 1961, he accepted a position as an assistant professor in the psychology department at UCLA. In children with autism, he felt he had found the ideal pool of subjects for testing his hypothesis—children with severe language deficits whose behavior seemed totally out of control.

随后，他观察到一项实验，其中暗示了一种更有前景的可能性。当看到一个发育正常的男孩被训练通过言语来获取一个小玩具饰品——这充其量只是一项微不足道的任务——洛瓦斯突然意识到，提升发育迟缓儿童的语言能力，或许能让他们更好地控制自己的问题行为。1961 年，他接受了加州大学洛杉矶分校心理学系助理教授的职位。在患有孤独症的儿童身上，他觉得自己找到了检验假设的理想对象——这些孩子存在严重的语言缺陷，行为似乎完全失控。



A psychologist at Indiana University named Charles Ferster was another critical influence on Lovaas’s thinking. Ferster was firmly in the camp of psychologists who believed that Kanner’s syndrome was a rare early-onset form of schizophrenia. He felt so confident that it was rare, in fact, that his 1961 paper “Positive Reinforcement and Behavioral Deficits of Autistic Children” begins with an apology for taking up the reader’s time with such an arcane subject. Autism “is not important from an epidemiological point of view,” he acknowledged, while offering that “the analysis of the autistic child may be of theoretical use, however, since his psychosis may be a prototype of the adult’s.” Then Ferster goes on to describe the idiosyncratic behavior of autistic children in behaviorist terms as being contingent on reinforcement—the receipt of rewards—just like the typical behavior of typical children. A child who repeated the word candy over and over would eventually be reinforced for this behavior by getting a piece. If the same child had a violent tantrum, the reward would be seeing their concerned mother rush in to see what was the matter. But what happened if a child gave a tantrum and nobody came?

印第安纳大学的心理学家查尔斯·费斯特对洛瓦斯的思考产生了另一个关键影响。费斯特坚定地站在认为坎纳综合征是一种罕见的早发性精神分裂症的心理学家阵营中。事实上，他对此罕见性如此确信，以至于在 1961 年发表的论文《孤独谱系儿童的积极强化与行为缺陷》开头，他先为占用读者时间探讨如此冷僻的课题而致歉。他承认孤独症"从流行病学角度而言并不重要"，同时提出"对孤独谱系儿童的分析可能具有理论价值，因为其精神病态或许可视为成人精神病的原型"。随后费斯特用行为主义术语描述孤独谱系儿童的异常行为，认为这些行为与普通儿童的典型行为一样，都取决于强化机制——即获得奖励。一个反复念叨"糖果"这个词的儿童，最终会因得到一颗糖而强化这种行为。若同一儿童爆发剧烈情绪，其奖励则是看到忧心忡忡的母亲冲进来查看情况。 但如果一个孩子发脾气而无人理会，会发生什么？



Ferster described an experiment that involved locking an autistic child in a small room alone every day for a year. Lo and behold, the child’s tantrums eventually subsided—which the psychologist took as a clear sign that tantrums are also contingent on reinforcement. He proposed that parents had inadvertently conditioned their children to be more and more autistic by rewarding their misbehavior with doting attention. Ferster extracted a fateful lesson from these experiments: the best way for parents to deal with their children acting out, he said, was for them to ignore their distress entirely until the undesirable behavior extinguished of its own accord.

费斯特描述了一项实验，内容是将一名孤独谱系儿童每天单独锁在小房间里，持续一年。果然，孩子的发脾气行为最终消退了——心理学家将此视为明确信号，表明发脾气行为同样依赖于强化作用。他提出，父母通过溺爱式的关注来奖励孩子的不良行为，无意中强化了孩子越来越严重的孤独谱系特征。费斯特从这些实验中得出了一个决定性的教训：他主张，父母应对孩子行为问题的最佳方式，是完全无视他们的痛苦，直到不良行为自行消退。



Though Lovaas had little patience for Freudian psychology, he still suspected that the parents of autistic children somehow played a decisive role in the genesis of their condition, and he was impressed with Ferster’s unsentimental analysis. Lovaas would eventually make films of young autistics in institutions like Camarillo State Hospital, located north of Los Angeles—the real-life setting of Olivia de Havilland’s noir psycho-thriller The Snake Pit—who had broken their noses with their knees and chewed their arms to the bone. Seemingly immune to any drug that the pharmaceutical industry could throw at them, these children struck Lovaas as fundamentally inhuman but with some margin of redemptive potential. “The fascinating part to me was to observe persons with eyes and ears, teeth and toenails, walking around yet presenting few of the behaviors that one would call social or human,” he wrote. “Now, I had the chance to build language and other social and intellectual behaviors where none had existed, a good test of how much help a learning-based approach could offer.”

尽管洛瓦斯对弗洛伊德心理学缺乏耐心，但他仍怀疑孤独谱系儿童的父母在某种程度上对其病症的起源起着决定性作用，费斯特那种不带感情色彩的分析给他留下了深刻印象。洛瓦斯最终拍摄了洛杉矶北部卡马里奥州立医院等机构收治的孤独谱系幼童的影片——这正是奥丽维娅·德哈维兰主演的黑色心理惊悚片《蛇穴》的真实场景——这些孩子用膝盖撞断了自己的鼻梁，把手臂啃咬得露出骨头。这些孩子似乎对制药业能提供的任何药物都免疫，在洛瓦斯看来，他们本质上不像人类，但仍有被救赎的潜力。"最令我着迷的是，观察到那些拥有眼睛耳朵、牙齿趾甲、能四处走动却几乎不展现任何可称为社交或人类行为的人，"他写道，"现在，我有机会在原本不存在语言及其他社交与智力行为的地方构建它们，这是检验基于学习的方法能提供多大帮助的绝佳测试。"



He explained to Psychology Today, “You see, you start pretty much from scratch when you work with an autistic child. You have a person in the physical sense—they have hair, a nose, and a mouth—but they are not people in the psychological sense. One way to look at the job of helping autistic kids is to see it as a matter of constructing a person. You have the raw materials, but you have to build the person.”

他向《今日心理学》解释道：“你看，当你与一个孤独谱系的孩子一起工作时，基本上是从零开始。从生理意义上说，你面对的是一个有头发、鼻子和嘴巴的人——但从心理意义上说，他们还不是一个完整的人。帮助孤独谱系儿童的工作，可以看作是在构建一个人。你拥有原材料，但必须亲手塑造出这个人。”



VI



To Lovaas’s frustration, the clinic referred only one child to his lab at UCLA in his first year on the job: a chubby, blue-eyed nine-year-old brunette named Beth who spoke mostly in echolalia and bore scars all over from banging herself against walls and furniture. To justify his use of laboratory time and the services of a team of grad students, Lovaas began spending entire days with Beth, picking her up at nine in the morning and dropping her off at three, five days a week. He was abashed to admit that he spent more time with Beth than he did with his own children. For a year, she became the subject of an epic experiment with an n of 1. The psychologist outfitted a suite of rooms with one-way mirrors and hidden microphones, along with a push-button device that enabled his assistants to record the frequency and duration of her behaviors. In this state-of-the-art panopticon, Lovaas crafted his lasting legacy: a style of intensive intervention called applied behavior analysis, or ABA.

令洛瓦斯沮丧的是，在他任职的第一年，诊所只向他在加州大学洛杉矶分校的实验室转介了一个孩子：一个胖乎乎、蓝眼睛、九岁的棕发女孩，名叫贝丝。她说话时大多重复别人的话，身上满是因为撞墙和家具留下的伤疤。为了证明自己使用实验室时间和研究生团队服务的合理性，洛瓦斯开始整天陪伴贝丝——每周五天，早上九点接她，下午三点送她回家。他羞愧地承认，自己陪伴贝丝的时间比陪伴亲生子女还要多。整整一年，她成为了一场 n=1 的史诗级实验对象。这位心理学家在一套房间里安装了单向镜、隐藏麦克风，以及一个按钮装置，让助手们能记录她行为的频率和持续时间。在这个最先进的圆形监狱中，洛瓦斯铸就了他永恒的遗产：一种名为应用行为分析（ABA）的密集干预模式。



His genius was breaking down complex everyday activities like getting dressed, going to the toilet, and toothbrushing into a sequence of smaller, simpler actions that could be conditioned through sheer repetition. He called his method discrete-trial training because each conditioning session was broken up into a series of beats with a distinct beginning and end. In this way, the therapist ensured that each stimulus (called a prompt) was strongly associated with a specific behavior, forming a tight loop of cause and effect. While this process might sound mechanical and formulaic, what Lovaas was attempting to do was to teach these children skills that would enable them to stay out of places like Camarillo and lead semi-independent lives. The strapping Nordic psychologist came to regard ABA as an art as well as a science. Some people had an inborn aptitude for it (including, of course, Lovaas himself) and some did not. But most importantly, it was an art that could be taught.

他的天才之处在于将穿衣、如厕、刷牙等复杂的日常活动分解成一系列更小、更简单的动作，通过反复训练形成条件反射。他将这种方法称为离散试验训练，因为每次训练过程都被分解成一系列具有明确开始和结束的节拍。通过这种方式，治疗师确保每个刺激（称为提示）都与特定行为形成强烈关联，构建出紧密的因果循环。虽然这个过程听起来机械刻板，但洛瓦斯试图通过这些训练教会这些孩子掌握技能，使他们能够避免进入卡马里奥州立医院这类机构，并过上相对独立的生活。这位体格健壮的北欧心理学家将应用行为分析视为一门科学与艺术的结合。有些人天生就具备这种天赋（当然包括洛瓦斯本人），而有些人则不然。但最重要的是，这是一门可以传授的艺术。



What did this art look like in practice? Here is Lovaas’s own description of teaching a child to give a hug. (Fading means offering a prompt with decreasing regularity until it is no longer required to elicit the target behavior.)

这种疗法在实践中是怎样的？以下是洛瓦斯对教孩子拥抱的描述。（“消退”指逐渐减少提示频率，直至无需提示即可引发目标行为。）



Step 1: Say, “Hug me,” and prompt (e.g., physically move) the child so that his cheek makes momentary contact with yours. Reward him with food the moment his cheek makes contact.

第一步：说“抱抱我”，并给予提示（例如，用手引导孩子），让他的脸颊短暂接触你的脸颊。一旦他的脸颊接触，立即用食物作为奖励。



Step 2: Gradually fade the prompt while keeping the instruction (“Hug me”) loud and clear.

第二步：在保持指令（“抱抱我”）清晰响亮的同时，逐渐减少提示。



Step 3: Gradually withhold the reward contingent on longer and longer hugs. Move in slow steps from a 1-second hug to one lasting 5 or 10 seconds. At the same time, require a more complete hug such as placing his arms around your neck, squeezing harder, etc. Prompt these additional behaviors if necessary.

第三步：逐步延长拥抱时间后才给予奖励。从 1 秒的拥抱缓慢过渡到 5 秒或 10 秒的拥抱。同时，要求更完整的拥抱动作，例如让孩子将手臂环住你的脖子、抱得更紧等。必要时可对这些额外动作给予提示。



Step 4: Generalize this learning to many behaviors and many persons. Gradually thin the reward schedule so that you get more and more hugs for less and less rewards.

第四步：将这一学习模式推广到多种行为和不同的人身上。逐步减少奖励频率，让你用越来越少的奖励换来越来越多的拥抱。



Lovaas used to say that the most important thing to establish at the outset of discrete-trial training is “You are the boss.” To make clear that his tough-minded approach was not about being pleasing and supportive, he added, “People whose voices are very tender, who have difficulty asserting themselves, or who are obsessive about right and wrong, just don’t make good teachers of developmentally disabled children.” He described the ideal ABA therapist as “assertive, confident, and outgoing”—all adjectives that applied readily to Lovaas.

洛瓦斯曾指出，在离散试验训练开始时，最重要的事情是确立“你是主导者”的原则。为了强调他这种强硬作风并非为了取悦或支持他人，他补充道：“那些声音过于温柔、难以坚持己见、或对是非对错过于执着的人，根本不适合教导发育障碍儿童。”他将理想的 ABA 治疗师描述为“果断、自信且外向”——这些形容词恰好也适用于洛瓦斯本人。



The protocols of ABA, as they were initially developed, were inextricably interwoven with Lovaas’s personality, which was both dominating and disarming. Operant conditioning was commonly known as behavior modification, but that sounded too mild to him. He called what he was doing with these difficult children behavioral engineering. (Most parents would simply refer to ABA as “the Lovaas method.”) A lifelong skier who was as competitive on the slopes as he was in a lab, he would charm his students with fractured versions of American idioms, as when he described his critics as “beating up old horses” or chided a graduate assistant, “You’re shrinking your responsibilities.” Instead of shying away from confrontation, he was energized by criticism and supremely unconcerned with political correctness. When his students voted him the department’s biggest “male chauvinist pig,” he was delighted.

ABA 协议最初制定时，与洛瓦斯那种既强势又令人卸下心防的个性密不可分。操作性条件反射通常被称为行为矫正，但在他看来这个说法过于温和。他将自己对这些棘手儿童所做的干预称为行为工程。（多数家长则直接称 ABA 为"洛瓦斯疗法"）这位终身热爱滑雪的学者，在雪道上和在实验室里同样争强好胜，常以支离破碎的美式习语逗乐学生——比如把批评者说成"鞭打死马"，或责备研究生助理"你在缩水责任"。他从不回避冲突，反而因批评而充满活力，对政治正确毫不在意。当学生们投票选他为系里最大的"大男子主义猪"时，他竟欣喜不已。



The deepest impression that Lovaas made on his students, however, was of being a man who never “shrank” his own sense of responsibility to the children in his care. He became a mentor to a generation of psychologists, therapists, and teachers at UCLA and believed so much in the redemptive power of ABA that he once bragged to a reporter from Los Angeles magazine, “If I had gotten Hitler here at UCLA at the age of 4 or 5, I could have raised him to be a nice person.”

然而，洛瓦斯给学生留下的最深刻印象，是他从不"推卸"自己对所照顾儿童的责任感。他成为加州大学洛杉矶分校一代心理学家、治疗师和教师的导师，对 ABA 疗法的救赎力量深信不疑，以至于曾向《洛杉矶》杂志记者夸口："如果我在四五岁时把希特勒带到加州大学洛杉矶分校，我本可以把他培养成一个好人。"



—



RIMLAND WAS SKEPTICAL OF the Lovaas method when he first heard about it: “The technique seemed much better suited to training dogs or seals than people,” he said. But after seeing the psychologist’s footage of self-mutilating children before and after ABA, he set his doubts aside and began scheming about ways that the technique could be exported from the lab. If grad students could be schooled in the art, why not parents?

里姆兰最初听闻洛瓦斯疗法时持怀疑态度："这种技术似乎更适合训练狗或海豹，而非人类。"但在目睹这位心理学家拍摄的自残儿童接受 ABA 治疗前后的影像后，他搁置了疑虑，开始谋划如何将这项技术从实验室推广出去。既然研究生能掌握这门技艺，为何父母不能？



“To my wife’s horror, I began to use Lovaas’s techniques in training our very difficult eight-year-old autistic son,” Rimland recalled in 1987. “I realized that the extremely permissive, indulgent attitude toward autistic children which had been fostered by previous authorities in the field of autism was in fact terribly damaging to the children. I used behavior modification to ‘shape up’ my son. Self-stimming was no longer tolerated. I used Lovaas’ techniques to ensure that Mark paid close attention to what he was told and what was going on around him.”

“让我妻子惊恐的是，我开始运用洛瓦斯的方法训练我们那个非常难带的八岁孤独谱系儿子，”里姆兰在 1987 年回忆道。“我意识到，此前孤独症领域的权威们所倡导的极度纵容、溺爱的态度，实际上对孩子造成了可怕的伤害。我采用行为矫正法来‘塑造’儿子的行为。自我刺激行为不再被容忍。我用洛瓦斯的技术确保马克能认真听从指令，并关注周围发生的一切。”



He also brought in one of Lovaas’s grad students, David Ryback, who prompted Mark to make eye contact and imitate phonemes with rewards of M&Ms and Coca-Cola. Stimming was punished with a loud “No!” as Ryback slapped himself. “Mark was a very nice kid, very alert, very oriented to his surroundings,” Ryback recalls. “He developed very quickly.” Soon Mark had learned to distinguish between “Grandma” and “Grandpa.”

他还请来了洛瓦斯的一名研究生大卫·赖贝克，后者用 M&M 巧克力豆和可口可乐作为奖励，引导马克进行眼神接触和模仿发音。当赖贝克拍打自己时，会用一声响亮的“不行！”来惩罚刻板行为。“马克是个非常乖巧的孩子，非常机敏，对周围环境很关注，”赖贝克回忆道，“他进步很快。”不久，马克就学会了区分“奶奶”和“爷爷”。



Lovaas was already thinking about inviting parents into the process to address a weakness in his method that showed up in early studies: the lessons learned in ABA often didn’t generalize beyond the highly artificial situation in the lab. (In typical fashion, this was blamed on a global deficit of the children’s ability to learn and generalize rather than on any flaw in the method.) The best hope for prompting lasting behavior change was to train the children in their natural environment: at home. A colleague of Lovaas’s named Todd Risley had already taught the mother of one of his patients to become her son’s ABA therapist, shaping his behavior with bites of ice cream.

洛瓦斯已在考虑邀请家长参与治疗过程，以解决其方法在早期研究中暴露的缺陷：通过 ABA 疗法习得的技能往往难以泛化到实验室高度人为情境之外。（按照典型思维，这被归咎于儿童学习与泛化能力的整体缺陷，而非方法本身的任何瑕疵。）促进持久行为改变的最佳希望，是在儿童的自然环境中进行训练：即在家中。洛瓦斯的一位同事托德·里斯利，已指导其一名患者的母亲成为儿子的 ABA 治疗师，通过喂食冰淇淋来塑造他的行为。



At a dinner that Rimland arranged with a few of the couples who wrote to him after reading his book, Lovaas compared behavioral engineering to the techniques employed by Anne Sullivan to teach Helen Keller to talk. Over heaping plates of spaghetti and copious amounts of red wine, he told the parents that ABA was their best chance to rescue their children from being forever trapped behind their “autistic shells.” Before the meal was over, they were begging Lovaas to train them in his method. He told Rimland that the dinner was one of the most important nights of his life.

在里姆兰组织的一次晚宴上，几位读过他著作后写信给他的夫妇受邀出席。洛瓦斯将行为工程学比作安妮·沙利文教导海伦·凯勒说话所用的技巧。在堆满意大利面和大量红酒的餐桌上，他告诉这些父母，ABA 疗法是他们拯救孩子、使其永远不被困在“孤独谱系外壳”中的最佳机会。晚餐结束前，家长们已恳求洛瓦斯传授他们这种方法。他告诉里姆兰，那晚是他人生中最重要的夜晚之一。



Working alone, both men were vulnerable to the kind of marginalization faced by any researchers who attempt to subvert the dominant paradigms in their field. By forming an alliance and reaching out directly to parents, they gained a level of credibility and influence far beyond what they could have achieved by waiting for confirmation of their theories through the usual peer-reviewed channels. Together, they would build an empire of their own: a shadow infrastructure for autism research in which parents, rather than medical professionals, were the ultimate authorities on their children’s well-being.

两人各自为战时，都容易遭受任何试图颠覆领域内主流范式的研究者所面临的边缘化。通过结盟并直接与家长沟通，他们获得了远超通过常规同行评审渠道等待理论验证所能达到的可信度与影响力。他们共同建立了一个属于自己的帝国：一个孤独症研究的影子基础设施，在这里，家长而非医疗专业人士，才是孩子福祉的最终权威。



—



THE GRAND SCALE OF Rimland’s ambitions was apparent in an application he filed for a year’s fellowship at the Center for Advanced Study in the Behavioral Sciences at Stanford University in 1964. Each year, the center extended an invitation to fifty distinguished scholars, and he was flattered to receive one.

林姆兰德的雄心壮志之大，从他 1964 年申请斯坦福大学行为科学高级研究中心一年期研究员的申请书中可见一斑。该中心每年向五十位杰出学者发出邀请，他为自己能收到邀请而深感荣幸。



His top priority for a residency at the prestigious university, he wrote to the center’s founder and director, would be expanding on his book’s investigation of how the genetic roots of autism cast light on “the nature of perception, motivation, thought, and intelligence.” This might have been enough to keep most visiting scholars busy for a year, but Rimland didn’t stop there. He took aim squarely at the foundation of psychiatry, aiming to prove that the notion that conditions like autism and schizophrenia could be “caused” (his scare quotes) by psychological factors was “no more than a highly prevalent and tenaciously believed myth—a modern day scientifically sanctioned superstition.” He offered to organize a symposium at Stanford called “What’s Wrong with Psychology?”

他致信该中心的创始人与主任，表示自己入驻这所名校的首要任务，是深化其著作中关于孤独症遗传根源如何揭示“感知、动机、思维与智力的本质”的研究。这或许已足以让大多数访问学者忙碌一整年，但林姆兰德并未止步于此。他直指精神病学的根基，试图证明诸如孤独症和精神分裂症等病症“由”（他特意加引号）心理因素“引发”的观点，“不过是一种广为流传且根深蒂固的神话——一种现代科学认可的迷信”。他提议在斯坦福大学组织一场名为“心理学出了什么问题？”的研讨会。



The next item on his agenda was equally lofty: exploring methods of augmenting human intelligence by manipulating maternal hormones and other perinatal factors in the womb. “Man is living in an atomic age with a brain mostly evolved during stone and pre–stone age. Not good enough,” Rimland declared.

他议程上的下一个目标同样宏大：探索通过操控母体激素及子宫内其他围产期因素来增强人类智能的方法。“人类生活在原子时代，但大脑大多是在石器时代及前石器时代进化而来的。这远远不够，”里姆兰宣称。



Needless to say, he got the fellowship. Once Rimland settled into academic life in Palo Alto with his family, he fell under the spell of Linus Pauling, one of the true Renaissance figures of twentieth-century science. Pauling had a quick wit and a photographic memory, and his insatiable curiosity ranged from chemistry to molecular biology, quantum mechanics, immunology, and beyond. He made a series of discoveries about the nature of chemical bonding that won him the Nobel Prize in chemistry in 1954. By applying insights from the physical sciences to biology, he discovered sickle-cell anemia, pioneering the field of molecular medicine. At the height of the cold war, he won a second Nobel for his role in negotiating a global ban on atomic-weapons testing in the atmosphere—the Peace Prize this time.

毋庸置疑，他获得了研究基金。当里姆兰携家人定居帕洛阿尔托的学术生活后，他深深折服于二十世纪科学界真正的文艺复兴式人物莱纳斯·鲍林。鲍林思维敏捷，拥有过目不忘的记忆力，其永不满足的好奇心横跨化学、分子生物学、量子力学、免疫学等多个领域。他关于化学键本质的一系列发现，使他在 1954 年获得诺贝尔化学奖。通过将物理科学的洞见应用于生物学，他发现了镰状细胞贫血症，开创了分子医学领域。在冷战最激烈的时期，他因在推动全球禁止大气层核武器试验谈判中发挥的作用，第二次获得诺贝尔奖——这次是和平奖。



In 1941, Pauling was embarking on a study of antibodies when he was laid low by a chronic inflammation of the kidneys called Bright’s disease (now known as nephritis), which can be triggered by a whole group of ailments including hepatitis C, mononucleosis, and type 2 diabetes. He was referred to a renal specialist named Thomas Addis, who advocated adherence to a strict low-salt, low-protein diet to give the kidneys a chance to “rest” and heal. By following Addis’s stringent diet—which included taking supplementary vitamins and minerals while guzzling gallons of water—Pauling was permanently relieved of his debilitating symptoms in just four months.

1941 年，鲍林正着手研究抗体时，却被一种名为布莱特氏病（现称肾炎）的慢性肾脏炎症击倒。这种疾病可由丙型肝炎、传染性单核细胞增多症和 2 型糖尿病等一系列疾病诱发。他被转诊给肾脏专家托马斯·阿迪斯，后者主张严格遵循低盐、低蛋白饮食，让肾脏有机会"休息"并愈合。通过遵循阿迪斯严苛的饮食方案——包括补充维生素和矿物质，同时大量饮水——鲍林仅用四个月就永久摆脱了那些令人衰弱的症状。



His consultations with Addis took place in the doctor’s bustling clinic at Stanford, where patients could watch him openly performing his experiments. He treated his patients’ wives and mothers as his “colleagues” while playing chamber music by Brahms and Beethoven on a phonograph.
