“我从未见过如此非凡的能力，”萨克斯回忆道，“何塞和我一样，热爱非人类的世界，尤其是植物世界。就像他画我的手表一样，他画的蒲公英和其他事物既充满情感，又极其精准。”受何塞和那对双胞胎经历的启发，萨克斯开始探索其他方式，与 23 号病房的患者建立联系。他带他们去纽约植物园散步，邀请他们到休息室一起打台球，还把自己的钢琴搬来，用音乐为他们助兴。“当我坐下弹琴时，他们会围拢过来。他们可能会跟着打拍子，会微笑，会跳舞，甚至会唱歌，”他说，“其中一些人颇有音乐天赋，能弹奏几个音符，那意思就是：‘你能弹这个吗？’”



On one of his walks with patients in the botanical garden, Sacks saw a boy named Steve pick a flower, gaze at it, and say the first word that any of the doctors in the hospital had ever heard him say: “Dandelion.”

在一次与患者们漫步植物园时，萨克斯看到一个名叫史蒂夫的男孩摘下一朵花，凝视着它，说出了医院里所有医生都从未听他说过的第一个词：“蒲公英。”



Using his acute powers of observation, Sacks came to realize that, instead of being incommunicative, his patients were communicating all the time—not in words, but in gestures and other nonverbal forms of utterance, particularly among themselves. He wrote an essay called “Culture and Community among Mental Defectives” for the hospital journal to make his colleagues more aware of the subtle forms of interaction unfolding all around them.

凭借敏锐的观察力，萨克斯逐渐意识到，他的患者并非无法沟通，而是一直在交流——不是通过语言，而是通过手势和其他非言语的表达方式，尤其是在他们彼此之间。他为医院期刊撰写了一篇题为《智力缺陷者的文化与社群》的文章，旨在让同事们更关注身边正在发生的微妙互动形式。



But his days on the ward were numbered once he started raising objections to what was known as “therapeutic punishment” among the staff. “I finally spoke up at one of our Wednesday meetings and said that I thought it was morally reprehensible,” Sacks says. “I emphasized that I did not want to be associated with it, and that I was happy to have found other ways of contacting the patients.” Looking around the table, he saw a circle of dark faces. A few days later, a hospital administrator transferred him off the ward.

然而，他在病房的日子屈指可数，因为他开始对工作人员所谓的“治疗性惩罚”提出异议。“最终，我在一次周三会议上直言不讳，表示我认为这在道德上是应受谴责的，”萨克斯说，“我强调自己不愿与此有任何关联，并很高兴找到了其他与患者接触的方式。”环顾桌旁，他看到一张张阴沉的面孔。几天后，医院的一名行政人员将他调离了病房。



In the weeks that followed, Sacks consoled himself by writing his first book: a collection of case histories that he called Ward 23. But in a fit of self-doubt, he tossed his only copy of the manuscript into the fireplace. “Jonathan Swift had thrown Gulliver’s Travels into the fire, and his friend Alexander Pope pulled it out,” he says, wincing at the memory. “But I didn’t have a Pope.”

在接下来的几周里，萨克斯通过撰写他的第一本书来安慰自己：一本名为《23 号病房》的病例集。但在一阵自我怀疑中，他将唯一的手稿副本扔进了壁炉。“乔纳森·斯威夫特曾把《格列佛游记》扔进火里，他的朋友亚历山大·蒲柏又把它救了出来，”他回忆时痛苦地皱起眉头，“但我没有蒲柏。”



That night, he had a vivid dream of hearing passages of melancholy vocal music in German, a language that he didn’t understand. These unwelcome melodies continued playing loudly in his mind throughout the following day. After hearing Sacks hum a few bars over the phone, a friend identified the score as Mahler’s Kindertotenlieder—songs of mourning for dead children.

那天晚上，他做了一个生动的梦，梦见自己听到一段忧郁的德语声乐片段，而他并不懂这门语言。这些不受欢迎的旋律在接下来的整整一天里，在他脑海中大声回荡。萨克斯在电话里哼了几个小节后，一位朋友辨认出这段乐谱是马勒的《亡儿之歌》——为死去的孩子而作的哀歌。



IX



Lovaas’s crusade to “normalize” deviance was not limited to autistic children. In the 1970s, he lent his expertise to a series of experiments called the Feminine Boy Project, the brainchild of UCLA psychologist Richard Green. After interviewing one hundred men and women who applied for gender reassignment surgery, Green became interested in tracing the roots of sexual identity back to childhood. He teamed up with Lovaas to see if operant conditioning could be employed as an early intervention in cases of gender confusion to prevent the need for reassignment surgery in the future.

洛瓦斯试图“正常化”偏差行为的运动并不仅限于孤独谱系儿童。20 世纪 70 年代，他将自己的专业知识贡献给了一系列名为“女性化男孩项目”的实验，这是加州大学洛杉矶分校心理学家理查德·格林的心血结晶。在采访了 100 名申请性别重置手术的男性和女性后，格林开始对追溯性身份认同的童年根源产生兴趣。他与洛瓦斯合作，研究是否可以将操作性条件反射作为性别困惑的早期干预手段，从而避免未来需要进行性别重置手术。



The project’s most celebrated success story was Kirk Andrew Murphy, enrolled at UCLA by his parents at age five. Bright and precocious, Kirk would ask for his favorite snacks by their brand names at the supermarket. But after seeing Green interviewed on TV about “sissy-boy syndrome”—his term for early-onset gender dysphoria—Kirk’s parents became concerned that he was exhibiting behavior that was inappropriate for a little boy. One day, his father caught him posing in the kitchen in a long T-shirt and saying, “Isn’t my dress pretty?” Children with this syndrome, Green claimed, often grew up to become transsexual or homosexual. Lovaas assigned a young graduate student named George Rekers to become Kirk’s behavioral therapist.

该项目最著名的成功案例是柯克·安德鲁·墨菲，他五岁时被父母送入加州大学洛杉矶分校。柯克聪明早熟，在超市里会直接叫出最爱零食的品牌名。但父母看到格林在电视上谈论"娘娘腔男孩综合征"（他用来描述早期性别焦虑症的术语）后，开始担心儿子表现出不符合小男孩身份的行为。某天父亲撞见柯克穿着长 T 恤在厨房摆姿势，嘴里念叨着："我的裙子漂亮吗？"格林声称，患有这种综合征的儿童长大后往往会成为跨性别者或同性恋者。洛瓦斯指派年轻研究生乔治·雷克斯担任柯克的行为治疗师。



In a case report that would go on to become a classic in undergraduate psychology courses, Rekers and Lovaas wrote that Kirk (called “Kraig”) possessed “a remarkable ability to mimic all the subtle feminine behaviors of an adult woman.” They framed his “offer to ‘help mommy’ by carrying her purse” as an example of the boy’s devious manipulation of his mother to “satisfy his feminine interests.” Their descriptions of the little boy’s behavior, compared with the transcripts of Green’s intake interviews with Kirk’s parents, were decidedly more extreme, as if the boy were clearly a world-class drag queen in the making at age five. They claimed that he had an elaborate “history of cross-dressing” that included plundering his grandmother’s makeup kit for cosmetics and “swishing around the home and clinic, fully dressed as a woman with a long dress, wig, nail polish, high screechy voice, [and] slovenly seductive eyes.” (In family photographs, Kirk more resembles a Mouseketeer.)

在一份后来成为本科心理学课程经典案例的报告中，雷克斯和洛瓦斯写道，柯克（化名"克雷格"）拥有"模仿成年女性所有细微女性化行为的非凡能力"。他们将柯克"主动帮妈妈拎包"的行为，描述为这个男孩为了"满足其女性化兴趣"而狡猾操控母亲的例证。与格林对柯克父母进行初次访谈的记录相比，他们对这个男孩行为的描述明显更为极端，仿佛这个五岁孩童俨然已是世界级变装皇后的雏形。他们声称柯克有着详尽的"变装史"，包括翻找祖母的化妆包偷用化妆品，"穿着长裙、戴着假发、涂着指甲油、发出尖利嗓音、摆出慵懒魅惑的眼神，在住宅和诊所里招摇过市"。（而在家庭照片中，柯克更像是个米老鼠俱乐部成员。）



Paying lip service to the idea of tolerance at a time when gay liberationists had started marching in the streets, Lovaas and Rekers proposed that “society probably could afford to become more tolerant with individuals with sex-role deviations” but insisted that “the facts remain that it is not tolerant. Realistically speaking, it is potentially more difficult to modify society’s behaviors than Kraig’s.”

当同性恋解放运动者开始走上街头游行时，洛瓦斯和雷克斯却空谈宽容理念，提出"社会或许应该对性别角色偏差者更加宽容"，但坚持认为"事实是它并不宽容。现实地说，改变社会行为可能比改变克雷格的行为更困难。"



To nip the little boy’s inappropriate behavior in the bud, they devised a program of total immersion based on Lovaas’s work on autism. This time, instead of hand-flapping, gaze aversion, and echolalia, the behaviors targeted for extinction included the “limp wrist,” the submissively yielding “hand clasp,” the notorious “swishy gait,” the girlish “hyperextension” of the limbs in moments of exuberance, and prissy declarations like “goodness gracious” and “oh, dear me.”

为了将小男孩的不当行为扼杀在萌芽状态，他们基于洛瓦斯在孤独谱系方面的研究设计了一套沉浸式干预方案。这次，针对消除的行为不再是拍手、回避目光和模仿言语，而是包括"软手腕"、顺从的"双手交握"、臭名昭著的"扭捏步态"、兴奋时女性化的"肢体过度伸展"，以及"天哪"和"哎呀"这类矫揉造作的感叹。



At home, Kirk’s “masculine” behaviors were rewarded with blue chips that could be redeemed for candy and other treats, while his “feminine” behaviors were punished with red chips that were subtracted from the total. In interviews conducted by blogger Jim Burroway, who undertook a thorough investigation of the case in 2011, Kirk’s brother, Mark, recalled their father punishing the boy—with Rekers’s approval—by converting each red chip into a “swat.” Mark broke down sobbing as he confessed to hiding red chips from his brother’s pile so that Kirk wouldn’t have to endure the abuse.

在家中，柯克的“男性化”行为会获得蓝色筹码作为奖励，这些筹码可兑换糖果等零食；而他的“女性化”行为则会受到红色筹码的惩罚，最终从总数中扣除。博主吉姆·伯罗威在 2011 年对此案进行了深入调查，他在采访中得知，柯克的弟弟马克回忆道，父亲在雷克斯的默许下，将每个红色筹码转化为一次“掌掴”来惩罚男孩。马克在坦白自己曾偷偷从哥哥的筹码堆里藏起红色筹码，以免柯克遭受虐待时，泣不成声。



Meanwhile, at UCLA, Kirk was presented with tables full of things to play with—“right” tables, loaded up with gender-appropriate objects like football helmets, army belts with hatchet holders, plastic handcuffs, dart guns, rubber knives, and electric razors, and “wrong” tables, piled with costume jewelry, cosmetics, Barbie dolls, baby powder, and miniature clotheslines. (In pilot studies, the researchers were dismayed to discover that “normal subjects frequently mixed toys from the two tables in their play, complicating the scoring.”) Before leaving the room, the experimenter would instruct Kirk to play with only “right” toys. Then his behavior was scored through one-way mirrors. If he asked questions not directly related to the instructions, they were ignored. Eventually, Kirk’s mother was brought in to sit in a chair and reward him by smiling and telling him he was a good boy if he strapped on a football helmet or brandished a rubber knife, and punish him by pretending to read if he sat down with his legs crossed or fancied a pretty bracelet instead. (“Plays with Barbie dolls at five, sleeps with men at 25,” Green ominously intoned on TV.)

与此同时，在加州大学洛杉矶分校，柯克面前摆满了可供玩耍的桌子——"正确"的桌子上堆满了符合性别刻板印象的玩具，如橄榄球头盔、带斧头套的军用腰带、塑料手铐、飞镖枪、橡胶刀和电动剃须刀；而"错误"的桌子上则摆满了人造珠宝、化妆品、芭比娃娃、婴儿爽身粉和迷你晾衣绳。（在试点研究中，研究人员沮丧地发现"正常受试者经常在玩耍时混用两张桌子上的玩具，导致评分复杂化。"）离开房间前，实验者会指示柯克只玩"正确"的玩具。随后通过单向镜对他的行为进行评分。如果他提出与指令无直接关联的问题，这些问题会被忽略。最后，柯克的母亲被请进房间坐在椅子上——如果柯克戴上橄榄球头盔或挥舞橡胶刀，她会微笑并称赞他是好孩子作为奖励；如果他盘腿坐下或偏爱漂亮手镯，她则会假装看书以示惩罚。（格林在电视上阴沉地强调："五岁玩芭比娃娃，二十五岁就会和男人同床共枕。"）



After sixty sessions in the lab, Rekers and Lovaas declared victory over Kirk’s “sissy-boy” behavior. “There is no doubt that our treatment intervention produced a profound change” in the boy, they wrote, offering as evidence the fact that he was “no longer ‘fussy’ about color-coordinating his clothes,” had quit fretting when his hair got mussed, and expressed desire to attend Indian Guide campouts with his father. They argued that the success of their experiments cast doubt on the notion that sexual preference is the product of “irreversible neurological and biochemical determinants” and touted the potential of their model for the treatment of other deviant children.

在实验室进行了六十次治疗后，雷克斯和洛瓦斯宣布战胜了柯克的"娘娘腔"行为。他们写道："毫无疑问，我们的治疗干预对这个男孩产生了深远的影响。"证据是：他"不再对衣服的颜色搭配挑剔"，不再因头发被弄乱而烦躁，还表达了想和父亲一起参加印第安向导露营活动的愿望。他们认为，实验的成功对"性取向由不可逆的神经和生化因素决定"这一观点提出了质疑，并大力宣扬其模式在治疗其他异常儿童方面的潜力。



The Feminine Boy Project turned into a cash cow for the university, attracting six-figure grants from the NIMH and the Playboy Foundation until 1986. Children wore wrist counters to monitor whenever they were tempted to play with the “wrong” toys, and parents were enlisted to surveil their children’s closets, steer boys away from the kitchen, and keep girls out of the garage.

"女性化男孩项目"成为大学的摇钱树，直到 1986 年，该项目从美国国家心理健康研究所和《花花公子》基金会获得了六位数的资助。孩子们佩戴腕部计数器，记录他们何时想玩"错误"的玩具；家长们则被要求监视孩子的衣柜，引导男孩远离厨房，让女孩远离车库。



Kirk effectively became Rekers’s version of Beth—the case that launched a career. The psychologist published nearly twenty papers related to the boy’s alleged metamorphosis, several of them co-authored with Lovaas. The case propelled Rekers to teaching positions at the University of Miami, Kansas State University, and other institutions, and he was awarded more than $1 million in grants from the NIMH and the National Science Foundation. He also became a sought-after speaker on the subject of treating sexual deviancy before committees of the U.S. Senate and House of Representatives.

柯克实际上成为了雷克斯版本的贝丝——那个开启职业生涯的案例。这位心理学家发表了近二十篇与该男孩所谓的转变相关的论文，其中几篇是与洛瓦斯合著的。这个案例推动雷克斯在迈阿密大学、堪萨斯州立大学及其他机构获得教职，并从美国国家心理健康研究所和国家科学基金会获得了超过 100 万美元的资助。他还成为美国参众两院委员会中关于治疗性偏差议题的热门演讲者。



In 1983, he co-founded the Family Research Council, an influential Christian lobbying group that helped craft the plank in the 2012 Republican national platform calling for an amendment to the Constitution defining marriage as the union of one man and one woman. Rekers’s ubiquity in courtrooms coast to coast, furnishing expert testimony against gay marriage and gay adoption in pivotal cases, inspired the New York Times’ Frank Rich to call him “the Zelig of homophobia.” In the meantime, his star patient wasn’t faring nearly as well. Kirk hanged himself in 2003 at age thirty-eight, following decades of depression.

1983 年，他共同创立了颇具影响力的基督教游说团体"家庭研究委员会"，该组织协助起草了 2012 年共和党全国纲领中关于修宪将婚姻定义为一男一女结合的条款。雷克斯在全美各地法庭频繁出庭，在关键案件中提供反对同性婚姻和同性收养的专家证词，这促使《纽约时报》的弗兰克·里奇称他为"恐同界的泽利格"。与此同时，他的明星患者却境况堪忧。柯克在经历数十年抑郁症后，于 2003 年 38 岁时上吊自杀。



Rekers’s lucrative career as an expert witness came to an abrupt end in 2010 when two photojournalists ambushed him at the Miami International Airport returning from a holiday in Madrid with a young male companion who turned out to be a paid escort from Rentboy.com. In the scandal that followed, he told the press that his handsome “travel assistant” had been hired to lift his luggage as he recuperated from hernia surgery, claiming that they had spent their time together in Spain “sharing scientific information on the desirability of abandoning homosexual intercourse.” Informed of Kirk’s suicide by CNN’s Anderson Cooper, he dismissed the Murphys’ assertion that their son’s experiences at UCLA contributed to his despair as “a hypothesis” that would require empirical proof.

雷克斯作为专家证人的高薪职业生涯在 2010 年戛然而止，当时两名摄影记者在迈阿密国际机场伏击了他——他刚从马德里度假归来，身边带着一名年轻男性同伴，后来证实这名男子是来自 Rentboy.com 的付费伴游。在随后的丑闻中，他向媒体宣称这位英俊的"旅行助理"是雇来帮他提行李的，因为他当时正在从疝气手术中恢复，并声称他们在西班牙共度时光时"分享了关于放弃同性性行为可取性的科学信息"。当 CNN 的安德森·库珀告知他柯克自杀的消息时，他驳斥了墨菲夫妇关于儿子在加州大学洛杉矶分校的经历导致其绝望的说法，称这只是"一个假设"，需要实证证明。



—



THOUGH REKERS CREDITED HIM with coming up with the idea of Kirk’s treatment in the first place, Lovaas downplayed his own role in the Feminine Boy Project, claiming that he had only served on a committee. In the context of his work on autism, his role in treating “sissy-boy syndrome” was surely a footnote. But both projects were based on the same fundamental view: that it’s easier to change a child’s behavior than it is to destigmatize that behavior in society—whether it’s limp wrists or flapping hands.

尽管雷克斯认为是他首先提出了对柯克的治疗方案，但洛瓦斯却淡化了自己在"娘娘腔男孩项目"中的角色，声称自己只是委员会成员。在他从事孤独症研究的背景下，他治疗"娘娘腔综合征"的角色无疑只是一个小注脚。但这两个项目都基于相同的基本观点：改变孩子的行为，比消除社会对这种行为的污名化更容易——无论是软绵绵的手腕还是拍打双手的行为。



Despite Lovaas’s unflagging enthusiasm for aversives, an ethical debate raged in the larger ABA community about whether intentionally inflicting pain in the name of treatment is any way to treat young human beings, even if they’re autistic and self-injurious. “One could argue, for example, that a locked iron mask would prevent nail biting, but the law and common sense would argue against such an intrusive intervention,” wrote ABA expert Gary LaVigna and autism researcher Anne Donnellan in a book called Alternatives to Punishment. Two years later, in 1988, the board of directors of the Autism Society of America (ASA) passed a resolution calling for a ban on aversive techniques. Yet the ASA continued to promote the use of aversives well past that, and some ABA practitioners rely on techniques like withholding food and administering physical punishment to modify behavior to the present day. Painful electric shocks are still employed to punish autistic children at an institution called the Judge Rotenberg Educational Center in Massachusetts, even in the face of a public outcry against their use.

尽管洛瓦斯对厌恶疗法热情不减，但在更广泛的 ABA 学界，一场关于"以治疗之名故意施加痛苦是否适合对待年幼人类（即便是孤独谱系且存在自伤行为的儿童）"的伦理辩论仍在激烈进行。ABA 专家加里·拉维尼亚与孤独症研究者安妮·唐纳伦在合著《惩罚的替代方案》中写道："例如，有人可能主张用上锁的铁面具防止咬指甲，但法律与常识都会反对这种侵入性干预。"两年后的 1988 年，美国孤独症协会（ASA）董事会通过决议呼吁禁止厌恶疗法。然而 ASA 此后仍长期推广该疗法，直至今日，部分 ABA 从业者仍通过限制进食、实施体罚等手段矫正行为。在马萨诸塞州的罗滕伯格法官教育中心，即便面对公众强烈抗议，电击惩罚仍被用于孤独症儿童。



By the late 1970s, however, Lovaas had changed his mind about a few things. He was no longer convinced that teaching a child to talk freed up a normal child trapped inside. Inside every “autistic shell” was an autistic person. “We were disappointed,” he admitted. “There were no sudden awakenings. There seemed to be no large internal reorganizations. Would it not have been nice if the child had said: ‘Now that I can speak well, I see how I have been very sick, but now I am well.’ No one said that.”

然而，到了 20 世纪 70 年代末，洛瓦斯对某些问题的看法发生了转变。他不再坚信教会孩子说话就能释放出被困在其中的正常儿童。每个“孤独谱系外壳”之下，都是一个孤独谱系的人。“我们很失望，”他承认道，“没有突然的觉醒，似乎也没有大规模的内在重组。如果孩子能说：‘既然我现在能好好说话了，我明白自己之前病得很重，但现在我好了。’那该多好。但没有人说过这样的话。”



But as Sacks had discovered on Ward 23, Lovaas learned that even self-injurious children were communicating in their own ways. Exuberant use of echolalia turned out to be a distinctively autistic way of acquiring language: children who parrot their favorite Disney movies and Pokémon cartoons learn to use expressive language more readily. He also came to recognize that many of the behaviors he had put children through hell to extinguish were attempts to find channels for self-expression. In an unusually candid interview in 1989, Lovaas told psychologist Richard Simpson that he had come to identify with the children who had seemed most resistant to his brutal methods:

但正如萨克斯在 23 号病房所发现的，洛瓦斯也认识到，即使是自伤行为的孩子也在用自己的方式交流。过度使用回声语言原来是孤独谱系人群习得语言的一种独特方式：那些模仿最喜欢的迪士尼电影和宝可梦卡通片的孩子，更容易学会使用表达性语言。他还逐渐意识到，他让孩子们经历地狱般折磨以消除的许多行为，其实是他们寻找自我表达渠道的尝试。在 1989 年一次异常坦诚的采访中，洛瓦斯告诉心理学家理查德·辛普森，他已经开始认同那些对他残酷方法最抗拒的孩子们：



When I think back upon the kids that I tried to treat back in the 1960s, who were so extremely self-injurious, I think, “Boy, they were tough!” What they were really saying is, “You haven’t taught me right, you haven’t given me the tools whereby I can communicate and control my environment.” So the aggression that these kids show, whether it is directed toward themselves or others, is an expression of society’s ignorance, and in that sense I think of them as noble demonstrators. I have a great deal of respect for them.

回想上世纪 60 年代我试图治疗的那些极度自伤的孩子们，我不禁感叹：“天哪，他们可真难对付！”他们真正想表达的是：“你没有教会我正确的方式，没有给我沟通和控制环境的工具。”因此，这些孩子表现出的攻击性——无论是对自己还是对他人——都是社会无知的一种体现。从这个意义上说，我认为他们是高尚的抗议者。我由衷地敬佩他们。



—



BUT ONE THING THAT LOVAAS never changed his mind about was that the best hope for such children was for them to aspire to become “normal”—purged of all visible traces of autistic behavior.

但洛瓦斯从未改变的一个信念是：这些孩子最大的希望在于努力变得“正常”——彻底消除所有可见的孤独谱系行为痕迹。



For most of his career, Lovaas professed that prompting full recovery from autism was beyond the scope of even the most intensive behavior engineering. “The program does not turn out normal children,” he cautioned parents at the Second NSAC Congress. “Should a child become normal as we treat him, then that, no doubt, is based on the fact that he had a lot going for him when he first started treatment.” In a popular ABA manual called The ME Book, he told parents and therapists not to expect a cure: “Find pleasure in small steps forward. You should be pleased at reaching a set of smaller goals, rather than hoping and struggling for some often unattainable and absolute ideal of normalcy.”

在洛瓦斯职业生涯的大部分时间里，他都公开表示，即使是最强化的行为工程，也无法实现让孤独症患者完全康复的目标。他在第二届 NSAC 大会上告诫家长：“这个项目并不能培养出正常的孩子。如果一个孩子在我们治疗过程中变得正常，那无疑是因为他在开始治疗时本身就具备了很多有利条件。”在一本名为《ME 手册》的流行 ABA 操作指南中，他告诉家长和治疗师不要期待治愈：“要在微小的进步中找到快乐。你们应该为达成一系列小目标而感到欣慰，而不是寄希望于并苦苦追求那个往往遥不可及、绝对正常的理想状态。”



But then, in 1987, Lovaas dropped a bombshell by claiming that nearly half of the children in an experimental group at UCLA had achieved “normal intellectual and educational functioning” by undertaking intensive ABA starting at age three. He described a totally immersive program requiring participation from “all significant persons in all significant environments,” including parents, teachers, and teams of graduate students working in the home. In essence, Lovaas replaced the world in which the child didn’t fit in with one that would train him or her to do so. “One may assume that normal children learn from their everyday environments most of their waking hours,” he wrote. “Autistic children, conversely, do not learn from similar environments. We hypothesized that construction of a special, intense, and comprehensive learning environment for very young autistic children would allow some of them to catch up with their normal peers.”

然而，1987 年，洛瓦斯投下了一枚重磅炸弹：他声称，在加州大学洛杉矶分校的一个实验组中，近半数儿童从三岁起接受高强度的应用行为分析后，达到了"正常的智力与教育功能水平"。他描述了一个完全沉浸式的项目，要求"所有重要环境中的所有重要人物"参与其中，包括家长、教师以及在家工作的研究生团队。本质上，洛瓦斯用一套训练孩子适应世界的体系，取代了孩子原本无法融入的世界。他写道："可以假设，正常儿童在清醒时的大部分时间都能从日常环境中学习。而孤独谱系儿童则无法从类似环境中学习。我们假设，为非常年幼的孤独谱系儿童构建一个特殊、密集且全面的学习环境，将使他们中的一部分人能够追赶正常同龄人。"



Lovaas’s study, which was covered in glowing terms in the mainstream press and a special report on CBS, was the breakthrough that many parents had been waiting for: empirical proof that their children could be rendered indistinguishable from their typical peers given enough devotion, effort, and expense. Though he carefully danced around the word cure, opting for the more neutral-sounding term recovery in his paper, his meaning was never in doubt: “If you met [the children] now . . . you would never know that anything had been wrong with them,” Lovaas told the New York Times. “I’m positive now that autism need not be chronic.”

洛瓦斯的研究得到了主流媒体和哥伦比亚广播公司特别报道的热烈赞誉，这成为许多家长翘首以盼的突破：实证表明，只要投入足够的奉献、努力和金钱，他们的孩子就能变得与普通同龄人无异。尽管他谨慎地回避了"治愈"一词，在论文中选择了听起来更中性的"康复"说法，但他的意图从未被质疑："如果你现在见到这些孩子……你绝不会知道他们曾有任何问题，"洛瓦斯告诉《纽约时报》，"我现在确信，孤独谱系障碍不必是慢性疾病。"



Part of his strategy for proving that neurology was not destiny was to separate the child from the diagnosis. Some preschool teachers were not told that his experimental subjects were autistic. (“If we had to admit that the child had a problem, we’d say that it was ‘language delay,’” Lovaas said.) He took the unusual step of changing the name of his lab at UCLA from the Autism Clinic to the Clinic for the Behavioral Treatment of Children to avoid tipping off school administrators. If a diagnosis leaked out anyway, parents were prompted to move the child to another school. Lovaas also believed it was essential to insulate his subjects from “the detrimental effects of exposure to other autistic children.” The mere presence of other such children in a classroom, he declared in the Times, was “the kiss of death.”

他证明神经学并非命运的策略之一，是将孩子与诊断结果分开。一些幼儿园老师并未被告知他的实验对象是孤独谱系儿童。（洛瓦斯说：“如果我们不得不承认孩子有问题，我们会说是‘语言发育迟缓’。”）他采取了不同寻常的举措，将自己在加州大学洛杉矶分校的实验室名称从“孤独症诊所”改为“儿童行为治疗诊所”，以避免引起学校管理人员的注意。如果诊断结果还是泄露了，家长就会被要求将孩子转到另一所学校。洛瓦斯还认为，让实验对象远离“接触其他孤独谱系儿童的有害影响”至关重要。他在《泰晤士报》上宣称，教室里只要有其他这类孩子存在，就是“致命一击”。



In addition to conducting an average of fourteen thousand hours of discrete-trial sessions for each child, his tireless graduate students helped parents negotiate educational placements and manage household chores, and they went to bat for the children and their families in dozens of other ways. For a boy who had no playmates, they hosted parties at his home for the neighborhood kids, “making him a social star of sorts.” Needless to say, Lovaas’s program entailed a level of commitment and support that was beyond the reach of most families, but compared to the cost of lifelong institutionalization—which he estimated at $2 million—it was a bargain, he said.

除了为每个孩子平均进行一万四千小时的离散试验训练外，他不知疲倦的研究生们还帮助家长协商教育安置、处理家务琐事，并以数十种其他方式为孩子们及其家庭挺身而出。对于一个没有玩伴的男孩，他们在他家为邻居的孩子们举办派对，"让他成了某种社交明星"。毋庸置疑，洛瓦斯项目所需的投入和支持水平是大多数家庭难以企及的，但相比他估算的两百万美元的终身机构照护费用，他认为这已经很划算了。



The psychologist’s supporters hailed the study as a milestone. “If true, these results are absolutely extraordinary,” Leon Eisenberg told the Times. Rimland followed suit with a banner headline in his parents’ newsletter. The following year, Lovaas’s work became the subject of an award-winning documentary that claimed that, without ABA, “more than 95 percent [of autistic children] will require custodial care for the rest of their lives.”

这位心理学家的支持者将这项研究誉为里程碑。"如果这些结果属实，那绝对非同凡响，"利昂·艾森伯格对《泰晤士报》表示。里姆兰随后在其家长通讯中刊登了头条标题。次年，洛瓦斯的研究成为一部获奖纪录片的主题，该片声称，没有应用行为分析疗法，"超过 95%的孤独症儿童将需要终身监护照护"。



Other longtime experts in the field, however, found reasons to be more skeptical. TEACCH founder Eric Schopler accused Lovaas of front-loading his data by excluding “low-functioning” children from his sample while favoring those with unusually high IQs. He also observed that families in Lovaas’s experimental group had more resources available to them in general than families in the control group, which Lovaas tried to explain by saying he had an insufficient number of graduate assistants to meet the needs of both. Schopler pointed out that calling autistic children “the kiss of death” in a classroom could result in kids all across the country being denied an education.

然而，该领域的其他资深专家则持更为审慎的态度。TEACCH 创始人埃里克·肖普勒指责洛瓦斯在数据中"掺水"，其样本排除了"低功能"儿童，却优先选取智商异常高的孩子。他还发现，洛瓦斯实验组的家庭普遍比对照组拥有更多资源——对此洛瓦斯解释称，是因为研究生助理人手不足，无法同时满足两组需求。肖普勒指出，若将孤独谱系儿童称为课堂上的"死亡之吻"，可能导致全国各地的孩子被剥夺受教育权。



Rimland fired back with a full-page defense of Lovaas in his newsletter. “It’s not unusual for humanity to treat its pioneers with hostility,” he said. But even Lovaas’s former colleague Catherine Lord—a pioneer of autism research in her own right—eventually admitted that the psychologist “tried to structure things in a way that . . . did not reflect what really happened and certainly cannot be used as scientific evidence.” Independent researchers have never been able to replicate the extraordinary findings reported in his 1987 paper.

里姆兰德在自己的通讯中发表了一整版文章为洛瓦斯辩护，他写道：“人类对待先驱者往往充满敌意，这并不罕见。”但即便是洛瓦斯的前同事凯瑟琳·洛德——她本人也是孤独症研究的先驱——最终也承认，这位心理学家“试图以某种方式构建事物……这种方式并未反映真实情况，当然也不能作为科学证据。”独立研究人员始终无法重现他 1987 年论文中报告的那些非凡发现。



The spectacular nature of his claims even created problems for other researchers at UCLA, as their phones began ringing off the hook with calls from parents desperate for a cure for their children. “But we at the Medical School Neuropsychiatric Institute weren’t promising a cure,” psychiatrist and autism expert Ed Ritvo recalled. “Ivar Lovaas in the Department of Psychology was.”

他那些耸人听闻的主张甚至给加州大学洛杉矶分校的其他研究人员带来了麻烦，因为他们的电话被那些渴望为孩子寻求治愈方法的家长们打爆了。“但我们医学院神经精神病学研究所并没有承诺治愈，”精神病学家、孤独症专家埃德·里特沃回忆道，“是心理学系的伊瓦尔·洛瓦斯在承诺。”



Soon, so was Rimland, though his pathway to recovery was quite different from intensive behavioral engineering.

不久之后，里姆兰德也加入了这一行列，尽管他所谓的康复之路与高强度的行为工程学截然不同。



X



Shortly after publishing Infantile Autism, Rimland started getting letters from parents claiming that their sons and daughters had become more calm and engaged after taking megadoses of certain nutrients. In particular, the same two classes of vitamins—B and C—kept popping up.

《婴儿孤独症》出版后不久，里姆兰开始收到家长来信，声称他们的子女在服用大剂量特定营养素后变得更加平静、更愿意互动。其中反复被提及的正是两类维生素——B 族和 C 族。



This wasn’t totally surprising: Pauling was touting heroic quantities of ascorbic acid as a panacea in his best-selling books, and Hoffer and Osmond’s experiments with B vitamins and schizophrenia were already part of the rapidly burgeoning alt-med lore. Rimland was initially skeptical, but as he came to know the parents in his network at NSAC meetings, they struck him as perceptive, careful, and reliable people. (Indeed, many of them were fellow psychologists.) After talking with doctors convinced of the therapeutic value of the megavitamin regimen, Rimland decided that he “could not in good conscience fail to pursue this lead.” Christening his storefront in San Diego the Institute for Child Behavior Research (later renamed the Autism Research Institute), he launched an ambitious study by relying on his parents’ network as a source of volunteers.

这并不完全出人意料：鲍林在其畅销书中大力推崇大剂量抗坏血酸作为万能药，而霍弗与奥斯蒙德关于 B 族维生素与精神分裂症的实验，早已成为迅速发展的另类医学知识体系的一部分。里姆兰起初持怀疑态度，但在全国孤独症协会会议上结识这些家长后，他发现他们敏锐、谨慎且可靠。（事实上，其中许多人本身就是心理学家。）在与坚信大剂量维生素疗法具有治疗价值的医生交流后，里姆兰认定"若凭良心行事，绝不能放弃这条线索"。他将圣地亚哥的临街诊所命名为儿童行为研究所（后更名为孤独症研究所），依托家长网络招募志愿者，启动了一项雄心勃勃的研究。



Rimland started the children on a potent multiple B-vitamin tablet plus several grams of vitamin C per day. After two weeks, more B vitamins (niacinamide and pyridoxine) were added at several hundred times the recommended minimum daily requirement. Then pantothenic acid, another vitamin, was thrown into the mix. (He would eventually supplement the vitamins with magnesium, on the advice of celebrity nutritionist Adelle Davis.) At each stage, a physician enlisted by the parents would rate the child’s behavior as parents submitted biweekly reports on the child’s speech, eating patterns, tantrums, and alertness. Finally, these data were transferred from printed forms to IBM punched cards for computer analysis.

里姆兰德开始让这些儿童每天服用一片高效复合 B 族维生素片，外加数克维生素 C。两周后，又在每日最低推荐量数百倍的基础上增加了更多 B 族维生素（烟酰胺和吡哆醇）。随后，另一种维生素——泛酸也被加入配方。（后来，根据知名营养学家阿黛尔·戴维斯的建议，他最终会在维生素中补充镁元素。）在每个阶段，由家长聘请的医生会根据家长每两周提交的关于孩子语言能力、进食习惯、情绪爆发和警觉性的报告，对孩子的行为进行评分。最后，这些数据从纸质表格转录到 IBM 穿孔卡片上，以供计算机分析。



In the pharmaceutical industry, the gold standard of drug development is the so-called double-blind placebo-controlled trial. Volunteers are randomly assigned to receive either the active drug or an inert placebo, and neither the volunteers nor the experimenters are aware of who is getting the real drug and who is getting the equivalent of sugar pills. Inevitably, both groups of patients will show some improvement because of a phenomenon known as the placebo effect.

在制药行业，药物开发的黄金标准是所谓的双盲安慰剂对照试验。志愿者被随机分配接受活性药物或无效安慰剂，且志愿者和实验者均不知道谁在服用真实药物、谁在服用相当于糖丸的安慰剂。由于安慰剂效应，两组患者都不可避免地会出现一定程度的改善。



At the root of the placebo effect is the fact that the attention of medical professionals, in an environment of care, produces beneficial changes in the mind and body of the patient even in the absence of an active drug. Researchers like Ted Kaptchuk at Harvard and Fabrizio Benedetti at the University of Milan have discovered that the mere act of swallowing a pill triggers cascades of hormones and neurotransmitters that can reduce pain and inflammation, enhance motor coordination, boost brain activity, lift mood, and improve digestion. These effects are pervasive, as if the body contains a self-healing network that is activated by the knowledge that one is receiving care. (Exercise and meditation also prompt this network into action.) While no one has ever cured cancer or dispelled pneumonia with a sugar pill, powerful placebo effects have been observed in an astonishingly broad range of conditions, from Parkinson’s and hypertension to chronic depression and Crohn’s disease. In placebo-controlled trials, if the volunteers in the placebo group and the experimental group show comparable amounts of benefit, the FDA judges the drug to be ineffective—often at the cost of tens of millions of dollars to the company that spent years developing it.

安慰剂效应的根源在于，即便没有活性药物，医疗专业人员在关怀环境中投入的关注，也能在患者身心引发有益变化。哈佛大学的泰德·卡普恰克与米兰大学的法布里齐奥·贝内代蒂等研究者发现，仅吞咽药片这一行为就能触发激素与神经递质的连锁反应，从而减轻疼痛与炎症、增强运动协调性、提升大脑活跃度、改善情绪并促进消化。这些效应广泛存在，仿佛人体内存在一个自愈网络，当个体意识到自己正在接受照护时便会激活（运动与冥想同样能激发此网络）。尽管从未有人用糖丸治愈癌症或消除肺炎，但在帕金森病、高血压、慢性抑郁症乃至克罗恩病等令人震惊的广泛病症中，均观察到了显著的安慰剂效应。 在安慰剂对照试验中，如果安慰剂组和实验组的志愿者表现出相近的获益程度，美国食品药品监督管理局（FDA）便会判定该药物无效——这往往让耗费数年研发该药物的公司损失数千万美元。



But Rimland decided not to use this well-established model of drug testing for his study. Instead, he employed his psychometric prowess to develop a home-brewed form of data analysis that he called “computer clustering”—in essence, an algorithmic search for clinically significant ripples in a sea of Big Data. He insisted that standard methods of conducting trials presuppose that the patients under study have a single unified condition, which made them inappropriate for a condition composed of distinct subtypes like autism. His go-to analogy was intellectual disability and PKU. “Until it became possible to fractionate the mass of ‘retardates’ into smaller groups such as PKU, cretinism, galactosemia, mongolism, etc., it was hopeless to try to devise means of prevention or treatment,” he observed. “I believe the children loosely called ‘autistic’ or ‘schizophrenic’ actually represent a dozen or more different diseases or disorders, each with its own cause.”

但里姆兰决定不在他的研究中采用这种成熟的药物测试模型。相反，他运用自己的心理测量学才能，开发了一种自创的数据分析方法，他称之为"计算机聚类"——本质上是在大数据海洋中通过算法寻找具有临床意义的波动。他坚持认为，标准的试验方法预设了研究对象患有单一统一的疾病，这使得它们不适用于像孤独症这样由不同亚型组成的疾病。他常用的类比是智力障碍和苯丙酮尿症。"直到有可能将'智力迟滞者'这个群体细分为苯丙酮尿症、克汀病、半乳糖血症、唐氏综合征等更小的群体，试图制定预防或治疗方法才变得可行，"他观察到。"我相信那些被笼统称为'孤独谱系'或'精神分裂'的儿童实际上代表了十几种或更多不同的疾病或障碍，每种都有其独特的病因。"



With 45 percent of parents reporting that the vitamins “definitely helped” their children, Rimland was thrilled with the results of his experiment. “There is no reasonable explanation for these findings other than that the vitamins do help some children,” he wrote in the Journal of Orthomolecular Psychiatry. He went on to include accounts from parents testifying to their children’s dramatic regression when the vitamins were stopped.

45%的家长报告称维生素“确实帮助了”他们的孩子，里姆兰对自己的实验结果感到振奋。“除了维生素确实对部分孩子有帮助之外，这些发现没有其他合理解释，”他在《正分子精神病学杂志》上写道。他接着引用了家长们的陈述，证明当停止服用维生素时，孩子们出现了明显的退步。



He admitted, though, that his failure to employ a placebo control group had come under fire from other researchers in the field. “Some of our critics have suggested that our findings reflect only wishful thinking,” he said. “They assert that our positive results might stem from the fact that many parents would be inclined to over-rate the vitamins because they want so badly to see their child improve.” He deflected these criticisms wholesale, claiming that the people making them “did not understand the experimental design,” and insisting that the accusations of wishful thinking were “not valid, since parent expectation could not influence the computer grouping.”

不过，他承认自己未能设置安慰剂对照组，这一做法遭到了该领域其他研究者的批评。“一些批评者认为我们的发现只是反映了主观愿望，”他说，“他们断言，我们的积极结果可能源于许多家长因极度渴望看到孩子好转而倾向于高估维生素的效果。”他全面驳斥了这些批评，声称提出这些批评的人“不了解实验设计”，并坚称关于主观愿望的指责“并不成立，因为家长的期望无法影响计算机分组”。



It was not a scientifically sound argument for him to make, since all of the data tabulated on his punched cards was derived from subjective reports by parents and physicians who were likely to be enthusiastic about his project. Indeed, three independent analyses of his dataset revealed more problems with his design than his claims suggested. A Navy statistician with access to the raw data concluded that no reliable information about the reaction to the vitamins by various subtypes in the sample population could be obtained by using Rimland’s computer-clustering scheme.

他提出的这个论点在科学上并不严谨，因为所有记录在穿孔卡片上的数据都来自家长和医生的主观报告，而这些人很可能对他的项目充满热情。事实上，对他的数据集进行的三项独立分析显示，其研究设计存在的问题比他声称的更多。一位接触过原始数据的海军统计学家得出结论：使用里姆兰德的计算机聚类方案，无法获得关于样本群体中不同亚型对维生素反应的可靠信息。



Furthermore, the design of the experiment—with parents as evaluators of changes in their children’s behavior—was anything but “blind” in the statistical sense, and a perfect incubator for placebo effects. Rimland knew that accurately gauging the efficacy of new treatments for autism is tricky because the condition is so mercurial. “These children spurt ahead or fall apart periodically for no discernible reason,” he said, “and whatever treatment is being used at the time gets the credit or the blame.” Yet even Rimland was not immune to the pitfalls of wishful thinking.

此外，实验设计——让家长作为孩子行为变化的评估者——在统计学意义上绝非“双盲”，而是安慰剂效应的完美温床。里姆兰德深知，准确评估孤独症新疗法的效果十分棘手，因为这种病症变化无常。“这些孩子会毫无缘由地阶段性突飞猛进或状态下滑，”他说，“而当时使用的任何疗法都会被归功或归咎。”然而，即便是里姆兰德本人，也未能幸免于一厢情愿的陷阱。



A thinly veiled account of his experiments with Deaner appeared in his book, referencing an unnamed “four-year-old autistic child who unquestionably belonged to the Kanner category.” He wrote that Mark’s mutism abruptly “disappeared” after taking the drug. For the first time, he said, “simple commands such as ‘Bring it here’ and ‘Close the door’ were understood and obeyed. Later, simple tasks such as opening the door for the family cat and placing milk bottles on the porch were performed with obvious pleasure.” He reported that the effects of Deaner were so dramatic and immediate that this unnamed boy’s sister would tell her parents to slip him another dose when he engaged in “disturbing behavior.”

在他的书中，有一段几乎不加掩饰地描述了他用 Deaner 进行的实验，提到一个未具名的“四岁孤独谱系儿童，无疑属于坎纳类型”。他写道，马克的缄默症在服药后突然“消失”。他说，这是第一次，“像‘拿过来’和‘关上门’这样简单的指令被理解并执行。后来，像为家里的猫开门、把牛奶瓶放在门廊上这样简单的任务，他都带着明显的愉悦完成。”他报告称，Deaner 的效果如此显著和迅速，以至于这个未具名男孩的妹妹会告诉父母，在他出现“令人不安的行为”时，偷偷给他再服一剂。



If Rimland’s description in the book of Deaner as a “new psychic-energizer” sounded suspiciously like a marketing term from a brochure, that was because it was precisely the phrase that Riker Laboratories used to promote the drug in ads in medical journals. Deaner was aggressively marketed to pediatricians and child psychologists for a wide variety of fuzzily defined symptoms, including “problem” behavior, emotional instability, hyperactivity, and underachievement at school. The drug was allegedly so well tolerated that the company recommended it for children who were already taking tranquilizers to offset their depressant effects.

如果里姆兰在书中将迪纳描述为“新型精神能量剂”听起来像宣传册上的营销术语，那正是因为这是莱克实验室在医学期刊广告中用来推广该药物的原话。迪纳被积极向儿科医生和儿童心理学家推销，用于治疗各种定义模糊的症状，包括“问题”行为、情绪不稳定、多动症以及学业不佳。该药物据称耐受性极佳，以至于公司建议已服用镇静剂的儿童用它来抵消镇静剂的抑制作用。



The American Medical Association (AMA) was decidedly less impressed. Months before Rimland enthused about the drug’s salutary effects on his son to Kanner, the AMA’s drug council issued a cautionary note about Deaner in its journal. The litany of “vague complaints” for which it was commonly prescribed, the AMA cautioned, “are characterized by the difficulty in their evaluation, their spontaneous fluctuations, and their great susceptibility to suggestion.”

美国医学会（AMA）对此显然不以为然。在里姆兰向坎纳热情洋溢地描述该药物对儿子的有益效果数月前，AMA 药物委员会就在其期刊上对迪纳发出了警示。该委员会警告称，该药物常被开具用于治疗的“模糊主诉”清单，“其特点是难以评估、症状自发波动，且极易受暗示影响”。



In other words, Deaner was the perfect placebo. It was also a gold mine for Riker Laboratories until it was finally taken off the market by the FDA in 1983 after a thorough review of independent studies concluded that the drug didn’t even rate as “possibly effective” and also put children with epilepsy at heightened risk for grand mal seizures. Supplement manufacturers quickly stepped into the breach, promoting a “mixed berry flavor” analog of the drug called DMAE, combining it with fatty acids, soy, and other health food staples, and promoting it with the slogan “If yelling, begging, and pleading doesn’t get your child to do their homework, maybe this will.”

换句话说，迪纳尔是完美的安慰剂。对莱克实验室而言，它也曾是一座金矿，直到 1983 年，美国食品药品监督管理局在全面审查独立研究后，认定该药物甚至算不上“可能有效”，并且会增加癫痫儿童发生全身性强直-阵挛发作的风险，最终将其撤出市场。保健品制造商迅速填补了这一空白，推出了一种名为 DMAE 的“混合浆果口味”类似物，将其与脂肪酸、大豆及其他健康食品原料结合，并以这样的口号进行推广：“如果喊叫、乞求、恳求都无法让孩子完成作业，也许这个可以。”



—



THE DISAPPOINTING RESPONSE OF his peers to his megavitamin experiment bugged Rimland. Though he once had yearned to become a prominent member of the medical establishment, confident that his innovative ideas would be eagerly embraced as clearly superior, he was rapidly turning against it.

里姆兰对同行们对其大剂量维生素实验的失望反应感到不满。尽管他曾渴望成为医学界的显赫人物，自信自己创新的想法会因其明显优越性而被热切接受，但他很快便转而反对这一体制。



The turning point in his thinking was a question that Humphry Osmond asked him after he published two charts in his newsletter. One chart compared the results of giving children megadoses of various vitamins, and the other compared the effects of prescription drugs like Dexedrine and Mellaril. Upon seeing the two charts side by side, Osmond asked Rimland, “Why didn’t you compare the drugs with the vitamins directly?” Noting the serious side effects caused by the prescription drugs, Rimland concluded that the future of his work was not to be found in conventional medicine. The charts also further convinced him that he could ignore the role of placebo effects in his studies, because the drugs had performed so badly compared to the vitamins despite equally high parental expectations. Rimland would eventually encourage his growing army of parent-experimenters to try several treatments at once, making it nearly impossible to tease out the benefits and side effects of any single one. “You are not undertaking a scientific experiment in order to publish an article in a professional journal,” he advised, “but rather are trying to help your child, and you know time should not be wasted.” One of his mottoes was “Help the child first, worry later about exactly what it is that’s helping the child.”

他思想的转折点源于汉弗莱·奥斯蒙德在其简报中发布两张图表后提出的一个问题。一张图表对比了给儿童大剂量服用各种维生素的效果，另一张则比较了右旋安非他命和美拉瑞尔等处方药的作用。当奥斯蒙德将两张图表并列展示时，他向里姆兰发问："为什么你不直接比较药物和维生素的效果？"注意到处方药引发的严重副作用后，里姆兰得出结论：自己未来的研究方向不应停留在传统医学领域。这些图表也进一步让他确信，研究中可以忽略安慰剂效应的影响——因为尽管家长对两种疗法抱有同等期待，药物的表现却远逊于维生素。最终，里姆兰鼓励日益壮大的家长实验者群体同时尝试多种疗法，这使得厘清单一疗法的疗效与副作用几乎成为不可能。"你们不是在为专业期刊发表论文而进行科学实验，"他建议道，"而是试图帮助自己的孩子，要知道时间经不起浪费。"他的座右铭之一是：“先帮助孩子，之后再操心到底是什么在帮助孩子。”“沉浸式翻译”



This try-everything-at-once approach gave the parents in his network a tremendous sense of hope and momentum at a time when the mainstream science of autism was advancing at a snail’s pace. But there was irony in the fact that Rimland’s quest for a cure for autism in orthomolecular medicine had been inspired by Følling’s discovery of PKU. If the cautious doctor hadn’t instructed Borgny Egeland to immediately stop giving Liv and Dag all the tonics, herbal teas, and other nostrums they were taking before performing his chemical analyses of their urine, he might never have zeroed in on the crystals of phenylpyruvic acid that provided him with the key to the mystery.

这种“一次性尝试所有方法”的做法，让里姆兰网络中的家长们感受到了巨大的希望和动力，而当时主流孤独症科学的发展却如蜗牛般缓慢。但颇具讽刺意味的是，里姆兰通过正分子医学寻找孤独症疗法的探索，竟源于福林对苯丙酮尿症的发现。如果那位谨慎的医生没有在分析尿液化学成分前，要求博格尼·埃格兰立即停止给利夫和达格服用所有补剂、草药茶及其他偏方，他或许永远无法锁定那些苯丙酮酸晶体——正是这些晶体为他揭开了谜团的关键。



Rimland’s disdain for placebo-controlled trials, the process of peer review, and other traditional safeguards also made it hard for other researchers to take his work seriously, even when he was right. As a result, he found himself gradually growing more isolated from his colleagues while being regarded as a lone voice in the wilderness by the parents in his network.

里姆兰德对安慰剂对照试验、同行评审流程及其他传统保障措施的轻视，使得其他研究者即便在他正确时也难以认真对待他的工作。结果，他发现自己逐渐与同事们疏远，而在他的社交网络中的家长们眼中，他则被视为荒野中的孤独声音。



For Ruth Sullivan, Rimland’s obsession with finding a cure for autism was a distraction from the enormous challenge of building a better world for their children. “Bernie got very into the vitamins. He was always pushing something,” she recalls. “I think that put him off track.” Things finally came to a head at NSAC (by then called the Autism Society of America) when Rimland called for a motion requiring all members to put their children on a high-dose vitamin B-12 regimen immediately after diagnosis. Ed Ritvo stood up and said, “This is a parent-run organization. There is no evidence that vitamin B-12 works and we don’t want to submit to this regime.” Instead of backing down, Rimland went all in: “If you go with Ritvo, I resign.”

对于露丝·沙利文来说，里姆兰对寻找孤独症疗法的执念，反而分散了为孩子们建设更美好世界这一巨大挑战的精力。“伯尼非常痴迷于维生素。他总是在推销某种东西，”她回忆道，“我认为这让他偏离了正轨。”事情最终在 NSAC（当时已更名为美国孤独症协会）达到顶点——里姆兰提出动议，要求所有成员在确诊后立即让孩子接受高剂量维生素 B-12 治疗方案。埃德·里特沃起身说道：“这是一个由家长运营的组织。没有证据表明维生素 B-12 有效，我们不愿屈从于这种疗法。”里姆兰非但没有让步，反而孤注一掷：“如果你们支持里特沃，我就辞职。”



But he no longer had the clout to make such a power play, and he was voted off the board of his own organization. The once-strong fabric of NSAC had been rent in two.

但他已不再具备发起这种权力博弈的影响力，最终被自己创立的组织董事会投票罢免。NSAC 曾经紧密的纽带就此一分为二。



—



TO PLAN HIS NEXT MOVE, Rimland decamped to his office in Kensington, where he forged a productive alliance with the only undergraduate in Lovaas’s lab, a nineteen-year old psychology/sociology major named Steve Edelson. Like Rimland, Edelson was religiously agnostic but culturally Jewish—a lanky, curly-haired Ramones fan who might slip away from a lecture on child development to catch an Andy Warhol book signing. When Edelson was growing up in Oregon, his mother and sister converted to Christian Science, a sect founded in 1875 by a self-anointed prophet named Mary Baker Eddy who believed that diseases are healed not by doctoring but by submission to God. Traditionally, Christian Scientists eschew most aspects of modern medicine, including drugs, tests, hospitals, and vaccines. Though Edelson never converted, his mother refused to have him vaccinated as a child, signing the equivalent of a religious conscience waiver to exempt him.

为了规划下一步行动，里姆兰搬到了他在肯辛顿的办公室，并与洛瓦斯实验室唯一的一名本科生——19 岁的心理学/社会学专业学生史蒂夫·埃德尔森——建立了富有成效的合作关系。和里姆兰一样，埃德尔森在宗教上是不可知论者，但在文化上认同犹太身份——他身材瘦长，卷发，是雷蒙斯乐队的粉丝，可能会从儿童发展讲座上溜走，赶去看安迪·沃霍尔的新书签售会。埃德尔森在俄勒冈州长大时，他的母亲和妹妹改信了基督教科学派——这个教派由一位自称先知、名叫玛丽·贝克·埃迪的人在 1875 年创立，她认为疾病不是通过医疗而是通过服从上帝来治愈的。传统上，基督教科学派信徒回避现代医学的大部分内容，包括药物、检测、医院和疫苗。尽管埃德尔森从未改信，但他的母亲拒绝让他小时候接种疫苗，签署了相当于宗教良心豁免的文件来为他免除接种。



He first heard the word autism at UCLA while watching a documentary called The Invisible Wall that featured interviews with Rimland, Lovaas, and the Sullivans. Rimland was in top form, delivering a nuanced view of the biology of the condition that was decades ahead of its time. He reiterated his belief in a connection between autism and genius, suggesting that children with the syndrome inherit “a double dose of the extreme ability to concentrate—to narrow their attention to a very fine point, like a searchlight, to illuminate with great intensity a very small matter.”

他第一次听到“孤独症”这个词是在加州大学洛杉矶分校，当时正在观看一部名为《隐形的墙》的纪录片，片中采访了里姆兰、洛瓦斯和沙利文一家。里姆兰状态极佳，对孤独症的生物学基础提出了细致入微的观点，领先时代数十年。他重申了自己对孤独症与天才之间存在关联的信念，并指出患有该综合征的儿童继承了“双倍的极致专注能力——将注意力收窄至一个极小的点，如同探照灯一般，以极高的强度照亮一件非常微小的事情。”



It occurred to Edelson that self-injurious behavior might be an attempt to mediate a barrage of overwhelming sensations from the environment. He wrote a paper on the subject that attracted the attention of Lovaas, who invited Edelson to assist him in data collection at Camarillo State Hospital. While going through records there, Edelson noticed that the autistic patients reacted in unusual ways to anesthesia and started thinking about the role of serotonin in the autistic brain. Lovaas suggested that he pay a visit to his friend Bernie, drawing a map to ARI that Edelson has kept all these years. His curiosity about the neurochemistry of autism meshed perfectly with Rimland’s interest in orthomolecular medicine, and he would play a key role in ARI’s study of biomedical interventions. Together, they would author a book called Recovering Autistic Children that became one of the bibles of the biomed movement, along with books like Jacqueline McCandless’s Children with Starving Brains.

埃德尔森意识到，自伤行为可能是试图调节环境中铺天盖地的感官刺激。他以此为主题撰写了一篇论文，引起了洛瓦斯的注意，后者邀请他协助在卡马里奥州立医院收集数据。在查阅档案时，埃德尔森注意到孤独谱系患者对麻醉的反应异于常人，开始思考血清素在孤独谱系大脑中的作用。洛瓦斯建议他去拜访朋友伯尼，并画了一张通往孤独症研究所的地图——这张地图埃德尔森珍藏至今。他对孤独症神经化学机制的好奇，与里姆兰德对正分子医学的兴趣不谋而合，后来他在孤独症研究所的生物医学干预研究中发挥了关键作用。两人合著了《治愈孤独谱系儿童》一书，与杰奎琳·麦坎德莱斯的《饥饿大脑的儿童》等著作并称为生物医学运动的圣经。



This effort would culminate in the launch of Defeat Autism Now!—the network of clinicians and alt-med practitioners that Shannon Rosa turned to for advice on the GFCF diet and other treatments after Leo was diagnosed in 2002. At DAN!-sponsored events all over the country, “recovered” children were paraded in front of cheering crowds in an atmosphere befitting tent revival meetings. The fact that some children who displayed all the classic signs of early infantile autism—like Kanner’s patients Donald T. and Richard S.—had managed to grow up to become happy and well-adjusted autistic adults without the benefit of elaborate elimination diets and gray-market drugs like secretin (a digestive hormone heavily promoted by Rimland that showed no evidence of benefit in placebo-controlled studies) had been forgotten. So had Kanner’s observation that one of the most crucial factors in determining the outcome of his patients was a “sympathetic and tolerant reception” by their teachers.

这一努力最终促成了“战胜孤独症！”（Defeat Autism Now!）的启动——这是一个由临床医生和替代医学从业者组成的网络，2002 年利奥被诊断后，香农·罗莎曾就无麸质无酪蛋白饮食及其他疗法向该网络寻求建议。在全国各地由该组织赞助的活动中，“康复”的孩子们在欢呼的人群面前列队展示，现场氛围堪比帐篷复兴布道会。有些孩子曾表现出卡纳患者唐纳德·T 和理查德·S 那样的典型早期婴儿孤独症特征，却最终成长为快乐且适应良好的成年孤独谱系个体——他们既未受益于复杂的排除饮食疗法，也未使用过分泌素（一种由里姆兰大力推广的消化激素，但安慰剂对照研究未显示其疗效）这类灰色市场药物——这一事实已被遗忘。同样被遗忘的还有卡纳的观察：决定患者预后最关键的因素之一，是教师给予的“同情与包容的接纳”。



By then, the estimated prevalence of autism was spiking dramatically. Rimland’s decades of work in orthomolecular medicine led him to look beyond genetics for an explanation for the increase hidden somewhere in the toxic modern world. Eventually, he would zero in on vaccines and mercury as the most likely triggers of what appeared to be a rapidly accelerating epidemic of Kanner’s once-rare disorder, launching the Autism Wars in earnest.

到那时，孤独症的预估患病率正急剧攀升。里姆兰在正分子医学领域数十年的研究，促使他超越遗传学范畴，在充满毒素的现代世界中寻找这一增长背后的解释。最终，他将疫苗和汞锁定为最可能的诱因——这些因素似乎正推动着坎纳曾认为罕见的疾病迅速演变为一场流行病，从而正式拉开了"孤独症战争"的序幕。



From his unique perspective at the central hub of a network of parents committed to their children’s recovery, Rimland was ideally positioned to track the initial surge of diagnoses in the last decade of the twentieth century. But his alienation from mainstream medicine made other things harder to see, like the behind-the-scenes machinations at the American Psychiatric Association that led to the radical transformation of the diagnostic criteria for autism, prompted by the mother of a little girl in England who was much like his son Mark.

作为致力于孩子康复的家长网络核心枢纽，里姆兰凭借其独特视角，得以追踪 20 世纪最后十年间孤独症诊断的首次激增。但他与主流医学的疏离，却使其他问题难以被察觉——例如美国精神病学会幕后操纵诊断标准彻底变革的运作，而这场变革的导火索，竟是一位英国小女孩的母亲——她的女儿与里姆兰的儿子马克情况极为相似。






---


NeuroTribes








Eight

八



NATURE’S SMUDGED LINES

自然模糊的线条



Nothing exists until it has a name.

万物无名，便不存在。



—LORNA WING

——洛娜·温



For parents of newly diagnosed children like the Rosas, the turn of the millennium was a time of great fear and great hope. The fear was that their children had been stolen away from them by a mysterious and terrifying disorder triggered by routine events like taking a recommended drug during pregnancy or inoculating them against measles. The hope was that intensive interventions like ABA or the DAN! protocol could make their children normal enough that they would lose their diagnoses and could go on to attend mainstream schools. Furthermore, the advent of new technologies like high-throughput DNA sequencing was widely touted in the press as the long-awaited breakthrough that would finally uncover the elusive “autism gene” and make this baffling condition a thing of the past.

对于像罗萨斯这样孩子刚被确诊的家庭而言，千禧年之交既是极度恐惧与巨大希望交织的时期。恐惧源于他们的孩子被一种神秘而可怕的疾病夺走——这种疾病可能由常规事件诱发，比如孕期服用推荐药物或给孩子接种麻疹疫苗。而希望则在于，通过 ABA（应用行为分析）或 DAN！方案等密集干预，孩子能够恢复得足够"正常"，从而摘掉诊断标签，进入主流学校就读。此外，高通量 DNA 测序等新技术的问世，被媒体广泛吹捧为期待已久的突破，认为它终将揭开 elusive 的"孤独症基因"之谜，让这种令人困惑的疾病成为历史。



Peter Bell had just graduated from Northwestern University when his wife, Liz, became pregnant. It was actually her second pregnancy; her first had ended in a miscarriage, so she followed her obstetrician’s advice and took progesterone during the first trimester. By the time their son, Tyler, was born, Peter was working in the marketing division of Johnson & Johnson, located just outside Philadelphia, promoting over-the-counter medications like Motrin and Tylenol.

彼得·贝尔刚从西北大学毕业，妻子莉兹就怀孕了。实际上这是她的第二次怀孕；第一次以流产告终，因此她听从产科医生的建议，在孕早期服用了黄体酮。当他们的儿子泰勒出生时，彼得正在强生公司位于费城郊外的营销部门工作，负责推广美林和泰诺等非处方药。



Tyler was born in January 1993. At first, he seemed like “the perfect baby,” Peter recalls. He was highly social, he slept peacefully through the night, and though he didn’t talk as much as the other children in his play group, he had an extensive repertoire of animal noises that he loved making, like moo and meow. A couple of years later, the Bells had a second child, another boy, Derek, who had obvious language delays from the start. But during Tyler’s well-baby check at his second birthday, the Bells’ pediatrician dismissed their concerns about his not talking much, saying that boys often talk later than girls.

泰勒出生于 1993 年 1 月。起初，他看起来像个"完美宝宝"，彼得回忆道。他非常善于社交，整夜安睡，虽然不像游戏小组里其他孩子那样爱说话，但他会模仿各种动物叫声，比如"哞"和"喵"。几年后，贝尔家又添了第二个儿子德里克，他一开始就表现出明显的语言发育迟缓。但在泰勒两岁生日时的健康检查中，儿科医生对贝尔夫妇关于孩子说话少的担忧不以为意，称男孩通常比女孩说话晚。



Then both boys got mild cases of the chicken pox. The Bells had decided not to inoculate their children against the disease, because the vaccine had just been introduced in the United States and their friends told them it might be better not to try out a relatively new vaccine on their children.

随后，两个孩子都患上了轻微的水痘。贝尔夫妇决定不给孩子接种水痘疫苗，因为这种疫苗当时刚在美国推出，朋友们告诉他们，最好不要在孩子身上尝试这种相对较新的疫苗。



The effect of chicken pox on Tyler appeared to be catastrophic. He exploded in tantrums, started throwing his toys around, and lost what little spoken language he had, sharply withdrawing into himself. He also suffered terrible bouts of diarrhea. Liz told Peter that it was like their son was possessed.

水痘对泰勒的影响似乎是灾难性的。他大发脾气，开始乱扔玩具，原本就有限的言语能力也丧失了，整个人急剧地自我封闭起来。他还遭受了严重的腹泻。莉兹告诉彼得，他们的儿子就像被附身了一样。



The Bells put him through the usual round of hearing tests and other evaluations, and in 1996, Tyler was diagnosed with pervasive developmental disorder not otherwise specified—one of several shades of the autism spectrum that had been added to the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders in the preceding years. When the diagnostician delivered the results of Tyler’s evaluation, she drew a little X on the left side of a bell curve representing the whole spectrum. She could see that the Bells were upset, because they had never even considered the possibility that Tyler had autism. She tried to reassure them by saying, “I didn’t tell you your son has autism. I told you he has PDD-NOS, which is very different.” That night, Peter and Liz called their relatives in tears, feeling like they had lost the child they once knew.

贝尔夫妇带泰勒做了一系列常规听力测试和其他评估。1996 年，泰勒被诊断为"未分类的广泛性发育障碍"——这是孤独谱系中的一种亚型，该谱系在之前几年刚被纳入《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》。当诊断医生告知泰勒的评估结果时，她在代表整个谱系的钟形曲线左侧画了个小叉。她看出贝尔夫妇很沮丧，因为他们从未想过泰勒可能患有孤独症。医生试图安慰他们："我没有说您儿子得了孤独症，我说他患的是 PDD-NOS，这两者完全不同。"那晚，彼得和莉兹含泪给亲戚们打电话，感觉他们失去了曾经熟悉的那个孩子。



Like the Rosas, the Bells found very little information about autism available to parents, and most of it was horrifying, based on the little research that had been done during the Kanner era. Even the medical library at Johnson & Johnson had only a handful of articles on PDD-NOS, Peter discovered. But he eventually found Catherine Maurice’s Let Me Hear Your Voice, which gave the Bells the hope that if they invested enough time, effort, and expense, Tyler could eventually lose his diagnosis. A month later, they had a team of therapists lined up to give him forty hours a week of one-on-one ABA at home, on top of speech and occupational therapy.

与罗萨斯一家相似，贝尔夫妇发现可供家长参考的孤独症资料少之又少，而仅有的那些基于坎纳时代有限研究的资料，大多令人不寒而栗。彼得发现，就连强生公司的医学图书馆里，关于待分类的广泛性发育障碍的文章也屈指可数。但他最终找到了凯瑟琳·莫里斯的《让我听见你的声音》，这本书给了贝尔夫妇希望：只要投入足够的时间、精力和金钱，泰勒最终或许能摘掉孤独症的诊断标签。一个月后，他们组建了一支治疗师团队，每周为泰勒提供四十小时的一对一家庭应用行为分析治疗，此外还有言语治疗和职业治疗。



Peter started going to DAN! conferences, and Tyler seemed to benefit from some of the alternative treatments recommended by the members of Rimland’s network. But he was nowhere close to being “recovered.” In 1997, Peter attended a conference in New York City where he heard Portia Iversen, the co-founder of a new group called Cure Autism Now (CAN), give a presentation on the steps they were taking to eradicate autism in future generations. Peter and Liz formed a local chapter in Philadelphia. After raising a million dollars for the organization by hosting a walk for the cure, along with an equally remunerative golf tournament and corporate breakfast, Peter was asked to join the CAN board. In 2004, he became the executive director.

彼得开始参加 DAN!会议，泰勒似乎从林兰网络成员推荐的某些替代疗法中受益。但他远未达到"康复"状态。1997 年，彼得在纽约市参加了一场会议，会上他听到波西亚·艾弗森——新成立的"即刻治愈孤独症"（CAN）组织联合创始人——就他们为消除后代孤独症所采取的步骤发表演讲。彼得和莉兹在费城成立了地方分会。通过举办治愈孤独症步行活动、同样收益丰厚的高尔夫锦标赛和企业早餐会为该组织筹集了一百万美元后，彼得受邀加入 CAN 董事会。2004 年，他成为执行董事。



CAN was one of a number of parents’ groups launched in the late 1990s with similar names (Talk About Curing Autism, founded in 2000, was another) that focused primarily on biomedical interventions and genetic research rather than on improving access to services for families. For parents like the Bells, focusing on the need for services seemed like an admission of defeat at a time when the possibility of vanquishing autism permanently seemed to be in the air.

CAN 是 20 世纪 90 年代末发起的众多名称相似的家长团体之一（2000 年成立的“谈论治愈孤独症”是另一个），这些团体主要关注生物医学干预和基因研究，而非改善家庭获取服务的渠道。对于像贝尔夫妇这样的家长来说，在彻底战胜孤独症的可能性似乎近在咫尺的时代，关注服务需求无异于承认失败。



By 2012, when I visited the Bells at their home in Princeton, Peter was the vice president of programs and services at Autism Speaks, the largest autism fund-raising organization in the world. As I spoke with the couple in their sunny living room, Tyler—who had grown into a lanky and handsome teenager—was pivoting gracefully over a canvas, painting in silent immersion as his sister played the piano. The walls of the basement were lined with his art, which often features the cars and motorcycles that fascinate him, rendered in vivid, luminescent hues. There was also an enormous freeform chart on the wall called “Tyler’s Map,” drawn by his father, featuring signposts on the road to a satisfying adult life: art, education, self-expression, support, employment, and making a positive impact on other people’s lives.

到 2012 年，我去普林斯顿拜访贝尔一家时，彼得已是全球最大的孤独症募捐组织"孤独症之声"的项目与服务副总裁。在阳光明媚的客厅里，我与这对夫妇交谈时，已长成清瘦俊朗少年的泰勒正优雅地俯身在画布上，在妹妹的钢琴声中沉浸于无声的创作。地下室的墙上挂满他的画作，那些令他着迷的汽车与摩托车，常以鲜艳明亮的色彩跃然纸上。墙上还有一幅巨大的自由形态图表，名为"泰勒的地图"，由他父亲绘制，上面标注着通往充实成年生活的路标：艺术、教育、自我表达、支持、就业，以及为他人生活带来积极影响。



Four decades after a young British psychiatrist named Lorna Wing embarked on a quest to discover the kinds of assistance and services that would be most useful to families of children like her daughter, parents are still trying to fill in the holes in the map.

四十年前，当年轻的英国精神病学家洛娜·温开始探索能为像她女儿这样的孩子家庭提供哪些最有效的帮助与服务时，父母们至今仍在努力填补这张地图上的空白。



—



IN THE LATE 1960S, Lorna set out to help her husband, John, a schizophrenia researcher at the University of London, compile a database of case records in a borough called Camberwell to determine if the National Health Service was providing the families of cognitively disabled children with adequate resources. The fact that the Wings’ daughter had a classic case of Kanner’s syndrome gave them special insight into the challenges that these families faced every day, and a number of historical forces were conspiring to bring the problems of these long-neglected children to the fore.

20 世纪 60 年代末，洛娜开始协助丈夫约翰——伦敦大学的精神分裂症研究员——在坎伯韦尔区建立病例记录数据库，以评估英国国家医疗服务体系是否为认知障碍儿童家庭提供了充足资源。温家女儿患有典型的坎纳综合征，这让他们对这类家庭日常面临的挑战有了独特认知，而当时多重历史力量正共同推动这些长期被忽视儿童的问题浮出水面。



The emotional difficulties of a generation of boys and girls evacuated from central London during the German air raids, and thus separated from their parents for a time, sparked a wave of interest in the psychology of development in the 1950s, exemplified by the work of John Bowlby on attachment theory. Another impetus for this work was the passage of the Mental Health Act in 1959 in response to a series of scandals about overcrowding and inhumane conditions in the country’s mental institutions and homes for the “subnormal.”

二战德国空袭期间，一批从伦敦中心城区撤离的儿童因与父母分离产生的心理创伤，在 20 世纪 50 年代引发了发展心理学研究热潮，约翰·鲍尔比的依恋理论便是典型代表。另一推动力源于 1959 年《精神卫生法案》的通过——此前英国多家精神病院及"低能者"收容所接连曝出过度拥挤、非人道待遇等丑闻。



At Exminster Hospital in Devon, up to 1,400 patients were crowded into a facility built to accommodate 440. The beds had been pushed together so they could be wedged into the available space. More than 80 percent of the patients were “certified,” meaning that they had been committed to the hospital against their will. In the exercise yard, patients were chained together in groups of three to discourage escape attempts.

在德文郡的埃克塞斯特医院，多达 1400 名患者挤在一座原本设计容纳 440 人的设施中。病床被推挤在一起，以便塞进可用的空间。超过 80%的患者被“认证”，意味着他们是被强制送入医院的。在活动场地上，患者被三人一组用链条拴在一起，以防逃跑。



The Mental Health Act dismantled the legal apparatus that oversaw this certification process and turned over the responsibility for the care of many people who would have been destined for institutions to local authorities. Thousands of children who would have been invisible in previous generations were dumped back into communities that had few resources and services prepared for them. Suddenly, understanding the emotional problems and prognosis of these children became a pressing social need.

《精神卫生法》废除了监督这一认证过程的法律机制，并将许多原本会被送进收容机构的人的护理责任移交给地方当局。数千名在前几代人眼中本会隐形的儿童被抛回社区，而社区几乎没有为他们准备任何资源和服务。突然间，理解这些儿童的情感问题和预后成为一项紧迫的社会需求。



One of the leaders in this emerging field was Mildred Creak, a psychiatrist at the Great Ormond Hospital in central London. Founded during the Victorian era as the Hospital for Sick Children, it was the first such facility in Britain to offer state-of-the-art medical care to the children of families of limited means. (Meanwhile, an army of servants, nurses, nannies, and visiting physicians doted on the ailing sons and daughters of the wealthy.) In its early days, the venerable institution counted as its most illustrious patron Charles Dickens, who was well acquainted with the hardships of London’s least fortunate families.

这一新兴领域的领军人物之一是米尔德里德·克里克，她是伦敦市中心大奥蒙德医院的精神病医生。这家医院成立于维多利亚时代，原名“患病儿童医院”，是英国首家为经济拮据家庭的儿童提供先进医疗服务的机构。（与此同时，成群的仆人、护士、保姆和出诊医生悉心照料着富裕家庭生病的子女。）在早期，这家备受尊敬的机构最著名的赞助人是查尔斯·狄更斯，他对伦敦最不幸家庭的苦难深有体会。



To raise funds for the purchase of a home for the new facility, the author of Bleak House and Oliver Twist penned an article called “Drooping Buds” in the popular magazine Household Words. He reported that of every hundred children born in the gray city, only sixty-five survived long enough to celebrate their eighth birthday. “Think of it, of all the coffins that are made in London, one in every three is made for a small child, a child that has not yet two figures to its age,” he wrote with inimitable pathos. When his posh readers in Knightsbridge and Belgravia finished daubing their cheeks and resurrecting themselves from their fainting couches, they would post a benefaction to Great Ormond Street. The author also gave a benefit reading of A Christmas Carol at St. Martin’s Hall, raising more than three thousand pounds in one night.

为筹集资金购置新院址，《荒凉山庄》与《雾都孤儿》的作者在流行杂志《家常话》上发表了一篇题为《垂蕾》的文章。他写道，在这座灰暗城市中诞生的每百名孩童里，仅有六十五人能活到庆祝八岁生日的那天。"试想，伦敦制造的所有棺材中，每三具就有一具是为尚未满十岁的幼童准备的，"他以无与伦比的悲悯笔触写道。当他在骑士桥与贝尔格莱维亚区的上流读者们拭去泪痕、从昏厥的躺椅上缓过神来后，便会向大奥蒙德街医院寄去善款。这位作家还在圣马丁大厅举办了《圣诞颂歌》慈善朗诵会，一夜之间筹得逾三千英镑。



In the late 1800s, a physician at the hospital named William Howship Dickinson described dozens of children with a variety of neurological disorders in meticulous detail. Medical scholar Mitzi Waltz has identified several likely cases of autism in Dickinson’s records, including a boy named Ralph Sedgwick who spent his waking hours in endless cycles of repetitive motion, tensing his tiny fists, rubbing and slapping his eyes, arching his neck, jerking his head, and waving his fingers in front of his face. He had spoken only one word in his two and a half years on earth: “Mum.”

19 世纪末，该医院一位名叫威廉·豪希普·迪金森的医生以极其细致的笔触描述了数十名患有各种神经疾病的儿童。医学学者米齐·沃尔茨在迪金森的病例记录中发现了多个疑似孤独症的案例，其中包括一个名叫拉尔夫·塞奇威克的男孩。他清醒时总在无休止的重复动作中度过：紧握小拳头、揉搓拍打眼睛、弓起脖子、猛甩头部、在眼前摆动手指。这个两岁半的孩子一生只说过一个词：“妈妈。”



When Creak established the first department of pediatric psychology in the country at the hospital in 1946, she had to make the case to her colleagues that “psychosis” among children was not rare. The constellation of traits shared by many of these children—a lack of “social awareness,” “rigidity” of behavior, and irregularities of speech—could have been lifted directly from Kanner:

1946 年，克里克在该医院建立了全国首个儿童心理学科室，当时她必须向同事们证明，儿童中的“精神病”并非罕见现象。这些儿童普遍表现出的特征组合——缺乏“社交意识”、行为“刻板”以及言语异常——几乎可以直接套用坎纳的描述：



An action, once started, continues indefinitely. Words, phrases, motor behaviour and even reaction patterns such as sleep and appetite tend to become stereotyped. An example of this was given by a psychotic child who liked chocolate but would only take it if cut in squares. Round chocolate croquettes he would reject.

一个行为一旦开始，便会无限持续下去。词语、短语、运动行为，甚至睡眠和食欲等反应模式，都倾向于变得刻板。一个患有精神病的儿童便是一个例子：他喜欢巧克力，但只接受切成方块的，圆形的巧克力球他则会拒绝。



The diagnosis and care of these children was severely hampered by the welter of competing labels in use by various clinicians. It was unclear whether they should be classified as cases of Kanner’s syndrome, Despert’s childhood schizophrenia, Bowlby’s reactive attachment disorder, Margaret Mahler’s symbiotic psychosis, or none of the above. The absurdity of this situation was wryly summed up by a child psychotherapist named James Anthony, who wrote in 1958, “The cult of names added chaos to an already confused situation, since there did not seem to be a sufficiency of symptoms to share out among the various prospectors, without a good deal of overlap.” Kanner admitted, “We seem to have reached a point where a clinician . . . can say honestly: he is schizophrenic, because in my scheme I must call him so. Another clinician, equally honest, can say: he is not schizophrenic because in my scheme I cannot call him so.”

这些儿童的诊断和护理因不同临床医生使用的相互矛盾的标签而严重受阻。他们究竟应被归类为坎纳综合征、德斯珀特的儿童期精神分裂症、鲍尔比的反应性依恋障碍、玛格丽特·马勒的共生性精神病，还是以上皆非，始终难以明确。儿童心理治疗师詹姆斯·安东尼在 1958 年以讽刺口吻总结这一荒诞处境时写道："标签崇拜给本已混乱的局面雪上加霜，因为各种症状似乎不足以分配给不同的探索者，重叠之处比比皆是。"坎纳承认："我们似乎已陷入这样的境地——一位临床医生可以坦诚地说：他是精神分裂症患者，因为在我的分类体系中必须如此诊断。而另一位同样诚实的临床医生可以说：他不是精神分裂症患者，因为在我的分类体系中不能这样诊断。"



To cut through this muddle, Lauretta Bender prompted Creak to convene a working party of experts and design the first set of standardized criteria for the diagnosis of what she called “schizophrenic syndrome in childhood.” These criteria, which became known as the Nine Points, were imported into autism research wholesale:

为了厘清这一混乱局面，劳蕾塔·本德促使克里克召集了一个专家工作组，并设计了第一套标准化诊断标准，用于诊断她所称的“儿童精神分裂症综合征”。这套标准后来被称为“九点标准”，并被全盘引入孤独症研究：




Gross and sustained impairment of emotional relationships with people.

与他人情感关系的严重且持续的损伤。



Apparent unawareness of his own personal identity to a degree inappropriate to his age.

对自己的个人身份表现出与其年龄不相称的明显无意识。



Pathological preoccupation with particular objects or certain characteristics of them, without regard to their accepted functions.

对特定物体或其某些特征产生病态性的专注，而不考虑这些物体的公认功能。



Sustained resistance to change in the environment and a striving to maintain or restore sameness.

对环境变化的持续抗拒，并努力维持或恢复原状。



Abnormal perceptual experience (in the absence of discernible organic abnormality).

（在无明显器质性异常的情况下）出现异常感知体验。



Acute, excessive, and seemingly illogical anxiety as a frequent phenomenon.

频繁出现急性、过度且看似不合逻辑的焦虑。



Speech either lost, or never acquired, or showing failure to develop beyond a level appropriate to an earlier age.

语言能力丧失、从未获得，或发展停滞在低于实际年龄的早期阶段。



Distortion in motility patterns.

运动模式中的扭曲。



A background of serious retardation in which islets of normal, near-normal, or exceptional intellectual function or skill may appear.

在严重发育迟缓的背景下，可能出现正常、接近正常或超常的智力功能或技能的孤立区域。




There were significant departures from Kanner’s model in this list, particularly the notion that intellectual disability and organic conditions like tuberous sclerosis could also be part of the clinical picture. Kanner was clearly losing control over the scope of his syndrome, but Creak’s Nine Points turned out to be hard to apply in practice. How was a clinician supposed to establish a child’s “unawareness of his own personal identity”? It was precisely this type of fog that John Wing was striving to dispel. In the days before computers, he would bring a hand-cranked calculator to meetings at the Institute of Psychiatry so he could crunch the data. For decades psychiatrists had been spinning out theories of childhood psychosis that were never subjected to empirical scrutiny.

这份列表与坎纳模型存在显著差异，尤其是认为智力障碍以及结节性硬化症等器质性疾病也可能属于临床表现的一部分。坎纳显然已无法完全掌控其综合征的范畴，但克里克的九点标准在实践中却难以应用。临床医生要如何确定一个孩子“对自身个人身份缺乏认知”？正是这类模糊不清的问题，让约翰·温致力于厘清。在计算机尚未普及的年代，他会带着手摇计算器参加精神病学研究所的会议，以便处理数据。几十年来，精神病学家们不断提出关于儿童精神病的理论，却从未经过实证检验。



Soon, John would change that. In Lorna, he found more than an intellectual equal who operated on the same wavelength as he did. He found a soul mate.

很快，约翰将改变这一现状。在洛娜身上，他找到的不仅是一位与他思维同频的智力伙伴，更是一位灵魂伴侣。



II



Growing up in a little town called Gillingham on the southeastern tip of England in the 1930s, Lorna was bored by cooking and sewing and all the other domestic activities that girls were supposed to care about. Instead, she emulated her father, who was an engineer. When she was six years old, she decided that what she wanted to do for a living was to figure out how things work. Rather than enrolling in art classes, as she was expected to do, she studied biology and chemistry and signed up for physics courses at a local boys’ school.

20 世纪 30 年代，洛娜在英格兰东南端一个叫吉林厄姆的小镇长大。她对烹饪、缝纫以及所有女孩本该关心的家务活毫无兴趣，反而效仿当工程师的父亲。六岁时，她就决定自己未来的职业是弄清楚事物的运作原理。她没有按预期去上艺术课，而是学习生物和化学，还在当地一所男校报名了物理课程。



By the time the war began, her family had moved north to Mitcham, a suburb of central London. Her father shipped out with the Navy, and Lorna read his letters eagerly, fascinated by his accounts of life on a ship in the theater of war. She was horrified by the newsreel footage from Germany that started appearing in local cinemas but charmed by the brash young Americans who were suddenly everywhere, carousing in the local pubs and shops. They were so much more outgoing than the people she was used to, and she loved hearing them call out to each other on the high street in their strange and colorful accents.

战争爆发时，她家已北迁至伦敦中部的郊区米查姆。父亲随海军出海，洛娜热切地读着他的来信，对信中描述的战争前线舰船生活着迷不已。当地影院开始播放来自德国的新闻片，画面令她震惊，但那些突然出现在各处、在酒吧和商店喧闹的莽撞美国青年却让她着迷。他们比她习惯见到的人外向得多，她喜欢听他们在主街上用奇特而多彩的口音互相呼喊。



Lorna was sixteen when she made up her mind to study medicine at the University College London, which placed a heavier emphasis on science than on clinical practice. The teachers there were also known to be more hospitable to female students at a time when the long-standing prejudice against female doctors was finally starting to wane. (Two decades earlier, after Mildred Creak earned her medical degree from the same school, she applied unsuccessfully for more than ninety posts in London, finally taking a job at a mental hospital run by Quakers in York.)

洛娜十六岁时下定决心要在伦敦大学学院学医，这所学院更侧重科学而非临床实践。当时，对女医生的长期偏见终于开始消退，那里的老师也以对女学生更友好而闻名。（二十年前，米尔德里德·克里克在同一所学校获得医学学位后，在伦敦申请了九十多个职位均未成功，最终在约克郡贵格会开办的一家精神病院工作。）



John’s childhood years were more difficult by comparison. When he was five, his father, who owned a bookstore, died of pneumonia from the delayed effects of being gassed in World War I. His mother suffered a fatal heart attack a few months later. John and his older sister, Barbara, were placed in a boarding school for orphans, where he pushed himself to excel, enabling him to transfer to a better school. At thirteen, he set his sights on becoming a doctor, but none of his aunts and uncles could afford to send him to university. When World War II began, he enlisted in the Navy, hoping to get a government grant to medical school if he survived combat. He spent most of his military service in Australia, leading bombing runs against enemy shipping lines, and when he returned home, he was awarded a scholarship to the University College London. There he met his future wife in a dissecting room. “It was very romantic,” Lorna told me. “We were both assigned the same dead body.” She found John dashing and brilliant, and they were married a short time later.

相比之下，约翰的童年岁月更为艰难。五岁时，经营书店的父亲因一战中吸入毒气引发的肺炎并发症去世，数月后母亲又因突发心脏病离世。约翰与姐姐芭芭拉被送入孤儿寄宿学校，在那里他发奋图强，最终转入更好的学校。十三岁时他立志成为医生，但叔伯姑姨们无力资助他上大学。二战爆发后他加入海军，期望若能生还战场，便可获得政府资助进入医学院。服役期间他主要在澳大利亚执行轰炸敌方航线的任务，归国后获得伦敦大学学院奖学金。在那里，他与未来妻子在解剖室相遇。"非常浪漫，"洛娜告诉我，"我们被分配解剖同一具遗体。"她眼中的约翰英俊睿智，不久后两人便结为连理。



After Lorna had served a year’s residency in the university hospital as a general physician, she and John decided to have a child—hopefully the first of many. They were thrilled when Susie was born in 1956, but almost immediately it was clear that she was having problems feeding. She refused to nurse, so Lorna had to place the bottle in her daughter’s mouth and squeeze it so that she would swallow. Lorna’s breasts ran dry of milk—a memory so painful that it still made her wince describing it to me more than fifty years later. But when she talked to the doctors and nurses at the hospital, they didn’t seem to think much of it. Eventually, her daughter moved on to eating solid foods and began gaining weight, so she tried to push her worries out of her mind.

洛娜在大学医院担任全科医生一年后，她和约翰决定要个孩子——希望这是众多孩子中的第一个。1956 年苏西出生时，他们欣喜若狂，但几乎立刻发现她进食困难。她拒绝吃奶，洛娜只好把奶瓶塞进女儿嘴里，再挤压瓶身让她吞咽。洛娜的乳房渐渐干涸——这个痛苦的记忆如此深刻，以至于五十多年后她向我描述时仍会皱眉。但当她与医院的医生护士们交谈时，他们似乎并不太在意。最终，女儿开始吃固体食物并逐渐增重，洛娜便努力将忧虑抛诸脑后。



This was not easy. Susie began staying up all night screaming, so Lorna and John started switching off caretaking duties so they could at least sleep every other night. Nothing in their medical education had prepared them for this experience with their daughter. The word autism had never been mentioned in any of their classes.

这并不容易。苏西开始整夜尖叫不睡，洛娜和约翰只好轮流照看，这样至少能隔夜休息。他们所受的医学教育从未教过他们如何应对女儿的这种状况。在他们的课程中，从未提及过"孤独谱系"这个词。



Six months later, Lorna got on a train and took a seat with Susie in her lap. Another young mother, carrying a boy about the same age as her daughter, sat down directly across from her. As the train rolled through the lush countryside, the boy got excited, spotting sheep and cows passing by the window. He kept glancing into his mother’s eyes expectantly and smiling, making sure that he had engaged her attention before directing it to what he was seeing by pointing his finger out the window and laughing. Lorna felt a shiver pass through her and thought: Susie has never done that. She had never pointed to direct her mother’s attention toward anything. Instead, if Susie wanted something, she would grab Lorna’s hand and place it on the thing that she desired.

六个月后，洛娜带着苏茜坐上了火车，让她坐在自己膝上。对面坐着的年轻母亲抱着一个和女儿差不多大的男孩。火车驶过葱郁的乡野时，男孩看见窗外掠过的牛羊兴奋不已。他不断期待地望向母亲的眼睛，笑着确认母亲注意到自己后，才用手指着窗外咯咯笑着引导她看过去。洛娜感到一阵战栗掠过全身，心想：苏茜从未这样做过。她从未用手指指向任何事物来引导母亲的注意。相反，如果苏茜想要什么，她会抓住洛娜的手，放在她想要的东西上。



Susie had a toy panda that she clearly loved—she carried it everywhere and seemingly couldn’t be happy without it. She would smell it, rub her cheeks against it, and enjoy the sensation of feeling its fur with her fingers. But what she never did, Lorna noticed, was to play games in which she pretended that the panda was a real bear. She also had a little tea set that Lorna had given her, and she would occasionally stage imaginary tea parties, but she never invited other children. She always sipped her imaginary tea alone.

苏西有一只她显然非常喜爱的玩具熊猫——她走到哪儿都带着它，似乎没有它就无法快乐。她会闻它，用脸颊蹭它，享受手指触摸它绒毛的感觉。但洛娜注意到，她从未玩过假装熊猫是真熊的游戏。她还有一套洛娜送的小茶具，偶尔会举办想象中的茶会，但从不邀请其他孩子。她总是一个人啜饮想象中的茶。



One day, John came home from work and told Lorna that he thought he knew what was going on with their daughter. He had seen a lecture by Creak about a form of childhood psychosis called early infantile autism, and he felt like she was describing Susie. The Wings arranged for Creak to evaluate her. She confirmed the diagnosis.

一天，约翰下班回家告诉洛娜，他觉得自己知道女儿出了什么问题。他听了克里克关于一种名为"早期婴儿孤独症"的儿童精神病的讲座，感觉她描述的就是苏西。温夫妇安排克里克为苏西做了评估。她确认了这一诊断。



It didn’t take John and Lorna long to figure out that there were almost no resources in place to support the families of children like their daughter and to ensure that they had any sort of future outside an institution. Psychotic children were considered uneducable, so they were excluded from the school system and shunted into sheltered workshops called Junior Training Centres for the Severely Subnormal, where they were occupied with make-work projects like basket weaving. No one seemed to know what became of these children when they got older. Like the Rimlands after Mark’s diagnosis, the Wings felt very much alone. But they were not alone.

约翰和洛娜很快发现，几乎没有现成的资源来支持像他们女儿这样的孩子的家庭，也无法确保这些孩子能在机构之外拥有任何未来。患有精神病的儿童被认为无法接受教育，因此被排除在学校系统之外，转而进入名为“严重低能青少年培训中心”的庇护工场，在那里他们从事像编织篮子这样无实际意义的工作。似乎没有人知道这些孩子长大后去了哪里。就像马克确诊后的里姆兰一家一样，温一家也感到非常孤立无援。但他们并非孤军奋战。



—



IN 1958, A SCHOOL SECRETARY named Sybil Elgar, who was taking a correspondence course to become a Montessori teacher, visited an institution for “severely and emotionally disturbed children” called the Marlborough Day Hospital near her home in St. John’s Wood in London. Though the facility was advertised as progressive and based on psychoanalytic principles, she was deeply shaken by what she saw, and the children were clearly miserable.

1958 年，一位名叫西比尔·埃尔加的学校秘书正在通过函授课程学习成为蒙台梭利教师。她参观了位于伦敦圣约翰伍德家附近的一家名为“马尔伯勒日间医院”的机构，那里收治“严重情绪障碍儿童”。尽管该机构号称采用先进的精神分析原则，但她所见到的景象令她深感震撼，孩子们显然非常痛苦。



Vowing to do better, Elgar started teaching classes for a small group of autistic children in the basement of her house in St. John’s Wood in London at the behest of two mothers, Helen Allison and Peggie Everard. In his first two weeks at the school, Helen’s son, Joe, smashed all the lightbulbs and nearly tore the place apart. But Elgar persisted in her efforts to find ways of reaching him. Though she knew very little about autism when she started, she was a formidable woman and an extraordinarily perceptive reader of what her students were thinking and feeling. Under her firm but compassionate tutelage, Joe Allison calmed down and learned to speak.

埃尔加决心做得更好，她在伦敦圣约翰伍德家中的地下室里，应两位母亲海伦·艾莉森和佩吉·埃弗拉德的请求，开始为一小群孤独谱系儿童授课。在她执教的前两周，海伦的儿子乔打碎了所有灯泡，几乎把地方拆了个底朝天。但埃尔加坚持不懈地寻找与他沟通的方法。尽管她刚开始时对孤独谱系知之甚少，但她是一位令人敬畏的女性，对学生的所思所感有着异常敏锐的洞察力。在她坚定而富有同情心的教导下，乔·艾莉森平静下来，并学会了说话。



Susie Wing also became one of her early students and would eagerly exclaim “Mrs. Elgar!” when it was time to go to school. As word of Elgar’s success spread among parents, it became clear that her basement was not large enough to accommodate all of the children on her waiting list.

苏西·温也是她早期的学生之一，每当该去上学时，她都会热切地喊道：“埃尔加夫人！”随着埃尔加的成功在家长间传开，显然她的地下室已不足以容纳所有在等待名单上的孩子。



In 1961, Joe and Helen Allison were featured on an episode of the BBC’s popular Women’s Hour. Hundreds of calls and letters poured in after the broadcast. The following January, a group of parents—many of whom had heard the BBC segment—met at a private house to form the Society for Psychotic Children, which changed its name to the Autistic Children’s Aid Society of North London on Lorna’s advice. (It’s now known simply as the National Autistic Society.) Like NSAC, founded in the United States two years later, the group saw media outreach as essential to building up its membership so that it could exert pressure on local authorities to achieve its goals. The following year, the society received full-page coverage in the Evening News (under the unfortunate headline “Children in Chains”), which produced another influx of letters and phone calls.

1961 年，乔和艾伦·艾利森夫妇登上了 BBC 热门节目《女性时光》。节目播出后，数百通电话和信件涌入。次年一月，一群家长——其中许多人正是通过这期 BBC 节目得知消息——在一处私人住宅集会，成立了"精神病儿童协会"，后在洛娜的建议下更名为"北伦敦孤独症儿童援助协会"（如今简称为"全国孤独症协会"）。与两年后在美国成立的 NSAC 一样，该组织将媒体宣传视为扩大会员规模的关键手段，以便向地方政府施压实现目标。次年，该协会在《晚报》上获得整版报道（标题不幸为"被锁链束缚的儿童"），再次引发大量来信来电。



The logo adopted by the society—a puzzle piece drawn by a father named Gerald Gasson—would eventually become the universal symbol of autism parents’ organizations worldwide.

该协会采用的标志——由一位名叫杰拉德·加森的家长绘制的拼图碎片——最终成为全球孤独症家长组织的通用象征。



—



WHEN THE GROUP HAD raised enough money to convert an old railway hostel in Ealing into the Sybil Elgar School, even the Beatles got into the act. Though the band members promised to drop in for just an hour, they spent the entire afternoon there gleefully rolling on the floor with the children. John Lennon became one of the school’s first major donors and attracted other celebrities to the cause.

当团体筹集到足够资金，将伊灵的一座旧铁路旅馆改建为西比尔·埃尔加学校时，连披头士乐队也参与其中。尽管乐队成员承诺只逗留一小时，他们却整个下午都欢快地和孩子们在地板上打滚。约翰·列侬成为该校首批主要捐助者之一，并吸引了其他名人加入这项事业。



As Elgar’s initial group of students became teenagers, she turned her attention to the need for the care and support of autistic adults, recognizing that while the children had made tremendous gains, they were not “cured” and would require a living environment suited to their needs for the rest of their lives. “Children need praise and encouragement,” she said, “but most of all they need the opportunity to continue their education and training so that they can maintain and extend educational abilities . . . and acquire occupational skills.” In 1972, the society launched Somerset House, the first residential facility and school in Europe for autistic adults. Elgar and her husband moved into a flat on the top floor.

随着埃尔加最初的学生群体步入青少年时期，她将注意力转向了成年孤独谱系个体的照护与支持需求——她意识到这些孩子虽已取得巨大进步，却并未被"治愈"，余生仍需适合其需求的生活环境。"孩子们需要赞美和鼓励，"她说，"但最重要的是，他们需要继续接受教育和训练的机会，以维持并拓展学习能力……并掌握职业技能。"1972 年，该协会创办了萨默塞特之家，这是欧洲首个面向成年孤独谱系个体的住宿设施兼学校。埃尔加与丈夫搬进了顶层的公寓。



These achievements put Lorna and her professional colleagues light-years ahead of their American peers in their understanding of autism. By 1973, when Kanner finally admitted that autism might manifest itself in varying degrees of severity, this was already common knowledge in London. She was also free of the heavy load of guilt that Kanner, Eisenberg, and Bettelheim laid on American parents. “When I read Kanner’s later papers,” Lorna told me, “I thought they were bloody stupid.
