I knew I wasn’t a refrigerator mother.”

这些成就让洛娜及其专业同行在对孤独症的理解上，远超美国同行数光年之遥。到 1973 年，当坎纳终于承认孤独症可能以不同程度的严重性表现出来时，这在伦敦早已是常识。她也摆脱了坎纳、艾森伯格和贝特尔海姆强加给美国父母的那种沉重负罪感。“当我读到坎纳后来的论文时，”洛娜告诉我，“我觉得它们简直愚蠢透顶。我知道自己不是‘冰箱母亲’。”



One of the leading lights of the London group was Michael Rutter, also at the Institute of Psychiatry. He conducted the first twin study of autism with a research fellow named Susan Folstein, which provided proof of the genetic basis of the condition for the first time. Rutter’s early work also decisively untangled autism from schizophrenia, showing that they were separate conditions that only rarely occur together.

伦敦团队的领军人物之一迈克尔·拉特同样来自精神病学研究所。他与研究员苏珊·福尔斯坦共同开展了首项孤独症双胞胎研究，首次为这种疾病的遗传基础提供了证据。拉特早期的研究还果断地将孤独症与精神分裂症区分开来，证明它们是两种不同的疾病，极少同时出现。



Despite all the anecdotal evidence to the contrary accumulated by parents, the biggest empirical question that remained unanswered into the 1960s was whether autism was as rare as Kanner continued to insist that it was. As the provision of services hinged on this point, the time for examining his claims was long overdue. Settling questions like that was the core mission of the Social Psychiatry Unit of the Medical Research Council (MRC), which was led by John Wing and based in the Maudsley Hospital in Camberwell.

尽管家长们积累了大量与之相悖的轶事证据，但直到 20 世纪 60 年代，最大的实证问题仍未得到解答：孤独症是否如坎纳一直坚持的那样罕见？由于服务供给取决于这一点，审视其论断的时机早已成熟。解决这类问题正是医学研究委员会（MRC）社会精神病学部的核心使命——该部门由约翰·温领导，设在坎伯韦尔的莫兹利医院。



III



In 1964, Guy Wigley, the medical officer of health for the county of Middlesex—encompassing a huge area stretching north of the river Thames and west of the City of London—came to the MRC with a problem. He had no idea how to calculate how many children with autism might live in the county, because a study of its prevalence had never been done.

1964 年，米德尔塞克斯郡（涵盖泰晤士河以北、伦敦金融城以西的广阔区域）的卫生医疗官盖伊·威格利带着一个难题来到 MRC。他完全无法估算该郡可能有多少孤独症儿童，因为从未进行过相关患病率研究。



John put a graduate student named Victor Lotter on the case. By sending out thousands of questionnaires to schoolteachers, training center supervisors, nurses, and parents, he managed to screen nearly the entire population of eight-, nine-, and ten-year-olds in Middlesex. Basing his selection criteria for autism on Creak’s Nine Points, Lotter came up with a group of fifty-four children for whom complete medical and social records were available. He calculated a prevalence estimate of 4.5 cases of autism in 10,000—that is, thirty-two children in total, a very small number indeed. After being replicated by other researchers using similarly restrictive criteria, this number became the oft-quoted baseline against which all future autism prevalence estimates would be compared in the coming decades.

约翰指派了一位名叫维克多·洛特的研究生负责此事。通过向学校教师、培训中心主管、护士和家长发放数千份问卷，他成功筛查了米德尔塞克斯地区几乎全部八、九、十岁儿童。洛特依据克里克九点标准制定孤独症筛选标准，最终确定了五十四名拥有完整医疗和社会记录的儿童。他估算出孤独谱系障碍发生率为万分之 4.5——即总共三十二名儿童，这个数字确实非常小。当其他研究者采用同样严格的筛选标准重复验证后，这个数字成为此后数十年间所有孤独谱系障碍发生率研究反复引用的基准参照值。



A closer look at these numbers, however, reveals a number of problems. Though Kanner insisted at first that early infantile autism was apparent at birth, nearly half of the children identified in the study experienced a “definite and recognizable setback in development” at some point in their early lives. (Antivaccine activists would later claim that “regressive autism” was a novel phenomenon linked to the combination measles-mumps-rubella vaccine, but the MMR wasn’t introduced in Britain until 1988.) Nine of the children in the group also displayed evidence of neurological abnormalities that would have likely disqualified them for a diagnosis by Kanner.

然而，仔细审视这些数字，会发现诸多问题。尽管坎纳起初坚持认为早期婴儿孤独症在出生时便已显现，但研究中近半数儿童在生命早期经历了“明确且可识别的发育倒退”。（反疫苗活动人士后来声称“退行性孤独症”是与麻疹-腮腺炎-风疹三联疫苗相关的新现象，但 MMR 疫苗直到 1988 年才在英国引入。）该组中还有九名儿童表现出神经系统异常的迹象，这很可能使他们不符合坎纳的诊断标准。



Lotter’s interviews also revealed the crass attitudes taken by medical professionals toward parents in this era. A pediatrician told the mother of one three-year-old boy, “He’s mentally defective. There is no hope for him whatsoever.” After asking for further guidance on his care, she was advised to “let him play in the garden with a ball.” Nearly half of the children had received no education of any sort. “Services leave a great deal to be desired,” Lotter concluded, citing the Elgar School as a rare and promising exception.

洛特的访谈还揭示了那个时代医疗专业人员对父母所持的粗鲁态度。一位儿科医生对一名三岁男孩的母亲说：“他智力有缺陷，完全没有希望。”当这位母亲进一步询问如何照顾孩子时，得到的建议是“让他在花园里玩球”。近半数儿童未接受过任何形式的教育。“服务远未达到理想水平，”洛特总结道，并指出埃尔加学校是一个罕见且充满希望的例外。



—



FEW RESEARCHERS AT THE MRC were more aware of the consequences of this than Lorna Wing. Suspicious of the empirical validity of Kanner’s criteria after a decade of talking with her fellow parents, she decided to take a different approach in following up the Middlesex study in the early 1970s. Rather than using a top-down method as Lotter had done—starting with Kanner’s definition of autism, and looking for examples of it—she decided to employ a bottom-up approach, searching for aspects of autistic behavior among children in Camberwell who were already identified as cognitively disabled.

在 MRC 的研究人员中，很少有人比洛娜·温更清楚这些后果。在与同为父母的家长们交流十年后，她对坎纳诊断标准的实证有效性产生了怀疑，于是在 20 世纪 70 年代初决定采用不同方法跟进米德尔塞克斯研究。她没有像洛特那样采用自上而下的方法——从坎纳对孤独症的定义出发，寻找符合该定义的案例——而是决定采用自下而上的方法，在坎伯韦尔已被认定为认知障碍的儿童中寻找孤独谱系行为的特征。



Lorna and another MRC researcher named Judith Gould reached out to pediatricians, psychologists, teachers, public-health workers, and clinic directors in the area—anyone whose job might bring them in contact with a child with special needs. To locate the families that most needed help, they included only children with IQs of less than seventy in their sample, screening them for signs of autism with a questionnaire developed by Lorna called the Handicaps, Behaviour and Skills schedule. For months, they made phone calls and wrote letters while visiting hospitals, clinics, group homes, and special schools to dig through dusty cabinets of records. Though Lorna is usually quite reserved, she wasn’t above using whatever means were necessary to get the data she needed.

洛娜和另一位 MRC 研究员朱迪思·古尔德联系了该地区的儿科医生、心理学家、教师、公共卫生工作者和诊所主任——任何可能因工作接触到特殊需求儿童的人。为了找到最需要帮助的家庭，她们在样本中只纳入了智商低于 70 的儿童，并使用洛娜开发的《障碍、行为与技能量表》问卷对他们进行孤独症筛查。数月间，她们一边打电话、写信，一边走访医院、诊所、集体之家和特殊学校，翻查积满灰尘的档案柜。尽管洛娜通常相当内敛，但为了获取所需数据，她不惜动用一切必要手段。



“I had completely given up with one particular psychiatrist who was being very resistant,” Gould recalls. “But Lorna put on all her charm and her feminine wiles because she was determined to get this information, which she did.”

古尔德回忆道：“我曾对一位特别抗拒的精神科医生完全放弃希望。但洛娜施展了她所有的魅力和女性手腕，因为她决心要拿到这些信息——而她确实做到了。”



Just as the Middlesex study predicted, they found only a handful of children in Camberwell—4.9 in 10,000—who met Kanner’s criteria. But Lorna and Judith didn’t stop there. As they made their rounds of the neighborhood, they couldn’t help but notice a much larger group of children who clearly had traits reminiscent of his syndrome but were not eligible for a diagnosis under his guidelines. These children exhibited the same cluster of social aloofness, repetitive behavior, and insistence on sameness as Kanner’s patients in Baltimore, but in a more diverse and colorful range of presentations.

正如米德尔塞克斯郡的研究所预测的那样，他们在坎伯韦尔只发现了极少数符合坎纳诊断标准的儿童——每万名儿童中仅有 4.9 例。但洛娜和朱迪丝并未止步于此。当她们在社区走访时，不由自主地注意到一个规模更大的儿童群体——这些孩子明显带有类似坎纳综合征的特质，却不符合其诊断标准。这些儿童表现出与巴尔的摩坎纳患者相同的社交疏离、重复行为和对一成不变的执着，但表现形式更加多样且丰富多彩。



They saw kids who flapped their hands and reversed their pronouns but never lined up their toys in rows. They met teenagers who engaged in elaborate repetitive rituals and were terrified by changes in routine but helped their mothers clear the table before retiring to a corner to play their favorite songs on the phonograph. Some of these children were completely nonverbal, but others were eager to wax on at length about their fascination with astrophysics, dinosaurs, or the genealogy of royalty.

他们看到有些孩子会拍手、颠倒人称代词，却从未将玩具排成行；遇到一些青少年，他们有着复杂的重复仪式，对日常变化感到恐惧，但会帮母亲收拾餐桌，然后退到角落用留声机播放心爱的歌曲。这些孩子中有些完全不会说话，另一些却热衷于滔滔不绝地谈论自己对天体物理学、恐龙或皇室谱系的痴迷。



While Lorna was trying to make sense of what she was seeing, she came across Dirk Arn Van Krevelen’s paper in the Journal of Autism and Childhood Schizophrenia arguing that Kanner’s autism and Asperger’s syndrome were distinct conditions. As she read it, however, she saw reflections of the children in Camberwell in descriptions of both syndromes. This was despite the fact that her study had specifically excluded kids in mainstream schools and likely left out most of the children likely to fall on the Asperger side of the line. In other words, says Gould, “these children didn’t fit into nice, neat boxes.”

当洛娜试图理解眼前所见时，她偶然发现了德克·阿恩·范·克雷维伦发表在《孤独症与儿童精神分裂症杂志》上的论文，该论文主张坎纳的孤独症与阿斯伯格综合征是两种不同的病症。然而，当她阅读这篇论文时，却从两种综合征的描述中看到了坎伯韦尔那些孩子的影子。尽管她的研究特意排除了主流学校的学生，很可能遗漏了大多数可能属于阿斯伯格谱系的孩子。换句话说，古尔德表示："这些孩子无法被简单归类。"



Because Asperger’s paper had still not been translated into English, Lorna asked John to translate it for her. Reading it, Lorna realized that Asperger had seen the same thing in his Vienna clinic that she was seeing in Camberwell.

由于阿斯伯格的论文尚未被翻译成英文，洛娜请约翰为她翻译。阅读后，洛娜意识到阿斯伯格在维也纳诊所观察到的现象，与她本人在坎伯韦尔所见如出一辙。



—



THE VALIDITY OF ASPERGER’S MODEL became even more apparent to Lorna once her colleagues started sending her “kids that no one knew what to do with,” as she put it. They clearly didn’t fit into Kanner’s narrow box, so most of them had been diagnosed with schizophrenia. They were also obviously highly intelligent but seemed naïve, as if they couldn’t pick up on subtle social signals from the people they were talking to.

一旦洛娜的同事开始把“没人知道该怎么处理的孩子”送到她那里，阿斯伯格模式的合理性就变得更加明显了。这些孩子显然不符合坎纳狭窄的定义范畴，因此大多数被诊断为精神分裂症。他们同样明显非常聪明，但似乎很天真，仿佛无法捕捉到交谈对象发出的微妙社交信号。



One young man had been fished out of the Thames by the police after jumping off a bridge and brought to the Maudsley. Lorna noticed that he was wearing two wristwatches. He explained that he kept one set to Greenwich mean time and the other to local time, even when the two times were the same. He was quite distraught that the time in London had recently changed to the British equivalent of daylight saving time. As a child, he had not learned to speak until he was three, and he had no friends until he was fourteen. He loved reading books on physics and chemistry, and he had memorized a large number of facts related to both. He dressed in old-fashioned clothes and was very particular about keeping his things in order and following a strict daily routine. But he was painfully aware that people generally didn’t like him. His father had an intuition that something was different about him, but he had never been able to quite put his finger on it.

一名年轻男子跳桥后被警方从泰晤士河中救起，随后被送往莫兹利医院。洛娜注意到他戴着两块手表。他解释说，一块表调成格林威治标准时间，另一块调成当地时间，即便两个时间完全一致。最近伦敦时间改为英国夏令时，这让他十分焦虑。他三岁才学会说话，十四岁前没有朋友。他酷爱阅读物理和化学书籍，并记住了大量相关知识。他穿着老式服装，对物品摆放和日常作息有着近乎偏执的讲究。但他痛苦地意识到，人们普遍不喜欢他。父亲隐约觉得他有些与众不同，却始终无法确切指出问题所在。



He was often ridiculed for being clumsy, rude, and unfashionable, though he went to great effort to be polite. He was very articulate, but he tended to add extraneous details in conversation, as when Lorna asked him about his relationship with his father and he replied, “My father and I get on well. He is a man who likes gardening.” After his attempt to commit suicide by leaping from the bridge was defeated by the fact that he was an excellent swimmer, he tried to strangle himself. Clearly, this young man needed help and support in navigating daily life, but there was no diagnostic label on the books that would enable him to access psychiatric services. Lorna knew that the parents of young people like this were not likely to readily embrace a diagnosis of autism, which was irrevocably linked with nonverbal preschool-aged children. The disabilities of these other young people were just as real and deserving of professional attention, but they were harder to see.

他常因笨拙、粗鲁和不合时宜而受人嘲笑，尽管他竭力表现得彬彬有礼。他口齿伶俐，但交谈时总爱添加无关细节——比如洛娜问起他与父亲的关系，他答道：“我和父亲相处融洽。他是个喜欢园艺的人。”他曾试图跳桥自杀，却因水性极佳而失败，于是转而尝试勒死自己。显然，这个年轻人在应对日常生活时需要帮助与支持，但当时的诊断手册中没有任何标签能让他获得精神科服务。洛娜知道，这类年轻人的父母不太可能轻易接受孤独症的诊断——这个诊断与不会说话的学龄前儿童密不可分。其他这些年轻人的障碍同样真实，同样值得专业关注，只是更难以被察觉。



In their 1979 paper on the Camberwell study, Wing and Gould reported, “The behavior pattern described by Kanner could be identified reliably, but the findings of the present study bring into question the usefulness of regarding childhood autism as a specific condition.” From the perspective of the MRC’s mission of advising the government on guidelines for service provision, this was particularly true because it was obvious that there were more of these people, of all ages, struggling to get by without help or any explanation for their difficulties, than there were children with Kanner’s syndrome.

在他们 1979 年关于坎伯韦尔研究的论文中，温与古尔德报告称：“坎纳所描述的行为模式确实可以被可靠识别，但本研究的发现却对将儿童孤独症视为一种特定状况的实用性提出了质疑。”从医学研究委员会为政府提供服务指南建议的使命来看，这一点尤为关键，因为显而易见的是，在各个年龄段中，有更多人在没有帮助或无法解释自身困境的情况下艰难求生，其数量远超患有坎纳综合征的儿童。



IV



Lorna began a quiet but determined campaign to expand the concept of autism to include the people who had been systematically excluded from Kanner’s walled garden. Her strategy was to work on two fronts simultaneously.

洛娜悄然却坚定地发起了一场运动，旨在将孤独症的概念扩展至那些曾被系统性地排除在坎纳封闭花园之外的人群。她的策略是双线并进。



First, she would attempt to persuade her colleagues that autism was not a categorical diagnosis but a dimensional one (not a “yes” or “no” but rather “of what type?”). To replace Kanner’s unified syndrome, she proposed the term the autistic continuum. While there were clearly many shades and hues along this continuum, all autistic people seemed to benefit from the same highly structured and supportive educational approaches, just as Asperger predicted.

首先，她会试图说服同事们，孤独症并非一种分类诊断，而是一种维度诊断（不是“是”或“否”，而是“属于哪种类型？”）。为了取代坎纳的统一综合征，她提出了“孤独谱系连续体”这一术语。尽管这一连续体上显然存在许多不同的色调和层次，但所有孤独谱系群体似乎都能从同样高度结构化且支持性的教育方法中受益，正如阿斯伯格所预测的那样。



It was equally apparent that a person could occupy one point on the continuum at a given point in their lives and another point later. Some children, like Susie, would remain profoundly disabled into middle age and beyond. But others blossomed in unexpected ways when given an accommodating environment and special consideration by their teachers (like several of Kanner’s patients, including Donald T. and Richard S.). An Elgar graduate named David Braunsberg, for example, went on to earn an art degree at a university and become an accomplished painter and textile artist.

同样显而易见的是，一个人在其生命中的某个阶段可能处于连续体上的某一点，而在后期则可能处于另一点。有些孩子，比如苏茜，会一直保持严重残疾状态直至中年甚至更晚。但另一些人，在获得适宜的环境和老师的特别关照后，会以意想不到的方式绽放光彩（例如坎纳的几位患者，包括唐纳德·T.和理查德·S.）。比如，一位埃尔加学校的毕业生大卫·布劳恩斯伯格，后来在一所大学获得了艺术学位，并成为了一名成就卓著的画家和纺织艺术家。



Next, Lorna introduced a new diagnostic label, conscious of the social stigma that the word autism carried. This was less a strictly empirical decision on her part and more like smart marketing. She wrote:

接下来，洛娜引入了一个新的诊断标签，她深知"孤独症"一词所承载的社会污名。这与其说是她基于严格实证的决策，不如说是一种巧妙的营销策略。她写道：



Parents without special experience tend to overlook or reject the idea of autism for their socially gauche, naïve, talkative, clumsy child, or adult, who is intensely interested in the times of tides around the coast of Great Britain, the need for the abolition of British Summer Time, or the names and relationships of all the characters who have ever appeared in a television soap opera, such as Coronation Street. The suggestion that their child may have an interesting condition called Asperger’s syndrome is more acceptable.

没有特殊经验的父母，往往会忽视或拒绝接受他们的孩子（或成年子女）患有孤独症——这些孩子可能社交笨拙、天真、话多、动作不协调，却对英国海岸潮汐时刻、废除英国夏令时的必要性，或是《加冕街》等电视肥皂剧中所有登场角色的姓名与关系网有着浓厚兴趣。相比之下，若建议他们的孩子可能患有名为"阿斯伯格综合征"的有趣状况，则更容易被接受。



Lorna wasn’t the first person to come up with the term Asperger’s syndrome, which the Viennese pediatrician never used. In 1970, a German psychologist named Gerhard Bosch published a book called Infantile Autism in which he referred to “the Asperger and Kanner” syndromes. “From our experience,” he concluded, “it is to be assumed that there is an intermediate realm between the two syndromes which cannot easily and clearly be ascribed to this or to that side.” As Kanner had done for early infantile autism, Lorna codified the condition by writing a case series of her own called “Asperger’s Syndrome: A Clinical Account,” which included descriptions of the man with two wristwatches and five other young adults. It was published in 1981.

洛娜并非第一个提出“阿斯伯格综合征”这一术语的人，这位维也纳儿科医生从未使用过这个说法。1970 年，德国心理学家格哈德·博施出版了《婴儿孤独症》一书，书中提及了“阿斯伯格综合征”和“坎纳综合征”。他总结道：“根据我们的经验，可以推测这两种综合征之间存在一个难以明确归类的中间地带。”正如坎纳对早期婴儿孤独症所做的研究那样，洛娜通过撰写自己的病例系列《阿斯伯格综合征：临床报告》来界定这一病症，其中描述了一位戴两只手表的中年男子及其他五名年轻成人的案例。该报告于 1981 年发表。



Resurrecting Asperger’s name from a place and time that no one wanted to remember was not easy. When German cognitive psychologist Uta Frith finally made an elegant English translation of Asperger’s paper in the late 1980s for a book of her own, her publisher turned down the manuscript. (Cambridge University Press eventually published it.) Lorna’s proposal also came under fire from Eric Schopler for adding yet another label to a field just starting to recover from the confusion between autism and schizophrenia. (He preferred the term high-functioning autism.) There were persistent backstage whispers that Asperger had worked for the Nazis—did such a man truly deserve recognition?

将阿斯伯格的名字从一个无人愿意记起的地方和时代重新唤醒并非易事。当德国认知心理学家乌塔·弗里思在 20 世纪 80 年代末为她的著作精心翻译阿斯伯格的论文时，出版社最初拒绝了这份手稿（最终由剑桥大学出版社出版）。洛娜的提议也遭到埃里克·肖普勒的抨击，他认为在刚刚摆脱孤独症与精神分裂症混淆的领域增加新标签并不妥当（他更倾向使用"高功能孤独症"一词）。后台始终流传着阿斯伯格曾为纳粹效力的低语——这样的人真的值得被铭记吗？



Over time, Lorna would lose her taste for the word continuum, because it suggested an incremental gradient of severity, from least to most severe, when she was suggesting something more individualized, nuanced, and multidimensional. While she was trying to think of a better term, she heard a phrase of Winston Churchill’s echo in her mind: “Nature never draws a line without smudging it.” This seemed particularly true of autism. One of the most subversive aspects of Lorna’s concept was her suggestion that the continuum shades imperceptibly into garden-variety eccentricity. (“All the features that characterize Asperger’s syndrome,” she observed, “can be found in varying degrees in the normal population.”)

随着时间的推移，洛娜逐渐对"谱系"一词失去了好感，因为它暗示着从最轻微到最严重的渐进式严重程度梯度，而她所倡导的却是更个体化、更微妙、更多维度的概念。当她试图寻找更恰当的术语时，脑海中回响起温斯顿·丘吉尔的一句话："大自然从不画一条清晰的界线。"这句话对孤独症而言尤为贴切。洛娜理论中最具颠覆性的观点之一，是她提出这个谱系会不知不觉地延伸到普通人的古怪特质中。（她指出："所有构成阿斯伯格综合征的特征，都能在正常人群中以不同程度找到。"）



Ultimately, she adopted the term autism spectrum. She liked the sound of it, which evoked pleasing images of rainbows and other phenomena that attest to the infinitely various creativity of nature. Clinicians readily adopted the phrase, because it helped explain what they’d been seeing in the real world for decades. It was a meme destined to go viral, so to speak—with assistance from a collusion of cultural forces that Lorna could not have foreseen, including a film that would turn Kanner’s formerly obscure disorder into a household word virtually overnight.

最终，她采用了“孤独谱系”这一术语。她喜欢这个词的发音，它让人联想到彩虹等令人愉悦的景象，这些景象证明了自然无穷无尽的创造力。临床医生们欣然接受了这个说法，因为它有助于解释他们几十年来在现实世界中观察到的情况。可以说，这是一个注定要像病毒一样传播的模因——在洛娜无法预见的多种文化力量的共同作用下，包括一部电影几乎一夜之间将坎纳曾经鲜为人知的障碍变成了家喻户晓的词汇。






---


NeuroTribes








Nine

九



THE RAIN MAN EFFECT

雨人效应



He remembers things. Little things.

他记得事情。小事情。



—CHARLIE BABBITT

——查理·巴比特



Barry Morrow drove his 1954 Studebaker around the back of the Minikahda Club in Minneapolis to pick up his new bride, Beverly, who was closing out her shift as a cocktail waitress. As he waited in his freezing car, he watched the valets running to fetch the Cadillacs and Lincolns that filled the parking lot in front of the grand plantation-style clubhouse. As a twenty-three-year-old singer in a rock-and-roll band working a variety of odd jobs, Morrow wasn’t going to be fixing the busted heater in his jalopy anytime soon. On his second (and last) day as a door-to-door encyclopedia salesman, he persuaded a poor old woman to cancel her order, telling her that a better set was available at the library down the street.

巴里·莫罗开着他那辆 1954 年款的斯图德贝克绕到明尼阿波利斯米尼卡达俱乐部后面，去接他的新婚妻子贝弗莉——她刚结束鸡尾酒女招待的轮班。当他坐在冰冷的车里等待时，看着代客泊车员们穿梭着去取那些停满俱乐部前庭的凯迪拉克和林肯轿车。这位二十三岁的摇滚乐队歌手兼零工杂役，短期内显然修不起他那辆破车里坏掉的暖气。在他当上门推销百科全书的第二天（也是最后一天），他劝说一位可怜的老妇人取消了订单，告诉她街角的图书馆里有更好的套装书可借。



While waiting for his wife to change out of the skimpy French frock that she was required to wear, Morrow caught the eye of a man in an upstairs window, who smiled and waved at him. He waved back. The next night, the same thing happened, and again the night after that. This little ritual went on for months. Morrow started to feel a bit weird about this mysterious figure who seemed to wait for him to appear each night. He christened him “the Waver.” Beverly informed her husband that the man’s name was Bill and that he scrubbed the club’s ovens on the graveyard shift. She also told him that Bill was “retarded”—and that he was also the happiest and friendliest person she’d ever met.

在等待妻子换下工作要求的暴露法式连衣裙时，莫罗注意到楼上窗口有个男人正朝他微笑挥手。他也挥手回应。第二天晚上同样的事情再次发生，此后夜夜如此。这个小仪式持续了数月。莫罗开始对这个似乎每晚都在等他出现的神秘人物感到些许不安，私下称其为"挥手先生"。贝弗莉告诉丈夫，那个男人名叫比尔，负责在夜班时段清洗俱乐部的烤箱。她还说比尔虽然"智力有障碍"，却是她见过最快乐友善的人。



At the Minikahda’s Christmas party for staff that year, the Morrows glimpsed Bill sitting at a table by himself across the room. As a roving string quartet played holiday songs and black-tied waiters circulated with trays of canapés, he was bundled up in his winter parka, nursing a glass of water, with a glossy black Beatle wig perched precariously on his head. Unable to contain his curiosity any longer, Morrow strolled over to Bill’s table and wished him a merry Christmas. The older man stood up, ceremoniously doffed his wig with his left hand and extended his right. Morrow sat down and they availed themselves of several flutes of complimentary champagne, which swiftly went to their heads.

那年明尼哈哈俱乐部为员工举办的圣诞派对上，莫罗夫妇瞥见比尔独自坐在房间另一头的桌旁。当巡回弦乐四重奏演奏着节日歌曲、系着黑领结的侍者端着盛满小食的托盘穿梭往来时，他裹着冬季派克大衣，啜饮着一杯水，一顶亮黑色披头士假发摇摇欲坠地架在头顶。莫罗再也按捺不住好奇，踱步到比尔桌前，向他道了声圣诞快乐。这位年长者站起身，左手郑重地摘下假发，伸出右手。莫罗落座后，两人畅饮了几杯免费香槟，酒意很快涌上心头。



As they laughed together, Morrow couldn’t help but notice that the few teeth that Bill had left in his mouth were brown from smoking and that his neck was swollen with a goiter. He had also doused his wig with so much Aqua Net that it was as stiff and glistening as a bowling ball. Being a naturally inquisitive person, Morrow started asking him questions about his life. Bill wasn’t shy, but his conversation ranged over such a broad and random variety of topics that the younger man had a hard time keeping up. “It was as if he had some epic tale he wanted to weave for me,” Morrow observed, “but could remember none of the particulars.”

他们一起大笑时，莫罗不禁注意到比尔嘴里仅剩的几颗牙齿因吸烟而泛黄，脖子上还肿着一个甲状腺肿。他往假发上喷了太多发胶，那假发硬邦邦、亮闪闪的，活像个保龄球。生性好奇的莫罗开始询问他的生活。比尔并不腼腆，但他聊起天来天马行空，话题跳跃得让这个年轻人难以跟上。"他仿佛想为我编织一个史诗般的故事，"莫罗感叹道，"却记不起任何细节。"



Morrow would learn that there was a lengthy period of Bill’s life that he never liked to talk much about. This was the forty-four years that he had spent in a place he referred to as “that hellhole”—Faribault State Hospital, fifty miles away. Bill had been committed there in 1920 at age seven. His parents, Sam and Mary Sackter, were Russian-born Jews who owned a corner grocery store. When Sam died suddenly of a heart attack at age thirty-five, the business collapsed, and Bill started doing badly in school because of his “filthy habits,” as one teacher’s report put it.

莫罗后来才得知，比尔生命中有一段漫长的岁月是他不愿多提的。那是在五十英里外的法里博州立医院度过的四十四年，他称之为"那个地狱般的地方"。1920 年，七岁的比尔被送进了那里。他的父母山姆和玛丽·萨克特是俄裔犹太人，经营着一家街角杂货店。山姆三十五岁时因心脏病突发去世，店铺随之倒闭，而比尔在学校也开始表现不佳——用一位老师的评语来说，是因为他"肮脏的习惯"。



The principal insisted that Bill was feebleminded and that there was no place for him in the public school system. His mother fought for her son’s right to attend classes as long as she could, but eventually the local mental health authorities deemed that Bill was at risk of becoming a burden to the community. He was declared a ward of the state and shipped off to the asylum, then known as the Faribault State School for the Feebleminded and Epileptic.

校长坚称比尔智力低下，公立学校系统容不下他。母亲竭尽全力为儿子争取上学的权利，但最终当地精神卫生部门认定比尔有可能成为社区的负担。他被宣布为州监护对象，送往当时被称为"法里博州立低能及癫痫儿童学校"的疯人院。



During his first five years there, he received letters and care packages of food and clothing from his mother. But as he had been diagnosed as an “imbecile,” Bill was never taught to read or write, and Mary’s letters were only occasionally answered by a member of the staff, who misspelled his last name in the replies. In 1925, Mary asked the hospital superintendent if her son might be briefly “paroled” (the term in use at Faribault, where the residents were called “inmates”) for a weekend visit with his family. When she was informed that he was considered too “subnormal” to leave the hospital grounds, she sent the superintendent one last request: for a photograph of her son.

在最初的五年里，他收到了母亲寄来的信件以及装满食物和衣物的爱心包裹。但由于比尔被诊断为"低能儿"，他从未被教导读写，玛丽的信件偶尔由工作人员代为回复，回信中甚至拼错了他的姓氏。1925 年，玛丽向医院院长请求，能否让儿子短暂"假释"（这是法里博使用的术语，那里的居民被称为"囚犯"）一个周末与家人团聚。当她被告知儿子被认为"智力严重低下"而不得离开医院范围时，她向院长提出了最后一个请求：一张儿子的照片。



Fearing that the stigma of mental illness in the family would exert a chilling effect on her daughters’ chances of finding husbands, Mary told them that they should consider their brother dead. She remarried, moved to Canada, and never attempted to contact him again.

由于担心家族中精神疾病的污名会影响女儿们的婚嫁前景，玛丽告诉她们应当当这个兄弟已经死了。她再婚并移居加拿大，从此再未尝试联系过他。



Bill’s ill health and unkempt appearance were the aftermath of nearly half a century of institutional neglect and abuse. He had never been taught how to tell time or handle money, and had never received proper dental care. Like the other inmates, he was paid the equivalent of 30 cents to $1.50 a month—redeemable only in goods from the hospital store—for backbreaking work like pushing food trolleys through the miles of dank tunnels that connected the various areas of the hospital. He also volunteered to help feed and care for fellow residents who were more profoundly disabled than he was. “You know, buddy,” Bill (who called everyone “buddy,” even his parakeet) told Morrow, “I was there for so long, I didn’t even know I was there.” The high fences on the outskirts of the facility defined the horizons of his universe.

比尔糟糕的健康状况和邋遢的外表，是近半个世纪机构性忽视与虐待留下的后遗症。他从未学过如何看时间或处理金钱，也从未接受过正规的牙科护理。和其他病友一样，他每月仅能获得相当于 30 美分至 1.5 美元的报酬——这些钱只能在医院商店兑换商品——却要承担推着餐车穿过连接医院各区域、长达数英里的潮湿隧道等繁重工作。他还自愿帮助照料那些比他残疾程度更重的病友。"你知道吗，老兄，"比尔（他管所有人都叫"老兄"，连他的虎皮鹦鹉也不例外）对莫罗说，"我在那里待了太久，久到甚至不知道自己身在其中。"设施外围的高耸栅栏，就是他整个世界的天际线。



One night, one of the men on his ward had a seizure. Fearing for the man’s life, Bill woke up an orderly who was sleeping off a bout of heavy drinking. The orderly became so enraged that he threw Bill down a flight of stairs while maintaining a tight grip on his hair, scalping him. That was why Bill wore a wig. He also had an ulcer on his leg that had never been adequately treated. In the 1960s, a group of parents from the Minnesota chapter of the Association for Retarded Citizens demanded increased scrutiny of living conditions in the custodial care facilities in the state. (A group of senators’ wives had to abort their tour of Faribault when they became too nauseated to go on.) In the wave of liberal reforms that followed, Bill was judged to be a promising candidate for community placement. On his own for the first time, he boarded a train to Minneapolis and found a room in a local boardinghouse, where he lived with other former institutional residents for several years, doing yard work, shoveling snow, and cleaning up in an auto body shop. Eventually, a social worker found him his job at the Minikahda.

一天夜里，比尔病房里的一名男子癫痫发作。比尔担心那人有生命危险，便叫醒了正在酣睡以解宿醉的勤务员。勤务员勃然大怒，揪着比尔的头发将他扔下楼梯，导致他的头皮被扯掉。这就是比尔戴假发的原因。他的腿上还有一处从未得到妥善治疗的溃疡。20 世纪 60 年代，明尼苏达州智障公民协会的一群家长要求加强对该州寄养机构生活条件的监管。（一群参议员夫人在参观法里博时因恶心难忍而不得不中止行程。）在随后的自由主义改革浪潮中，比尔被认为是有望融入社区生活的候选人。他第一次独自生活，登上了前往明尼阿波利斯的火车，在当地一家寄宿公寓找到了房间，与其他前收容所居民一起住了几年，做些庭院杂活、铲雪，并在汽车修理厂打扫卫生。最终，一名社会工作者在米尼卡达俱乐部为他找到了工作。



In spite of everything he had been through, Bill struck Morrow as a remarkably cheerful man. (“I’m as good as downtown!” he exclaimed often.) He was delighted to be out among people, even if they mostly ignored him. By the end of the night, Bill had stuck his wig in his pocket and pulled out his harmonica—inherited from a friend who died in the institution—and transformed the slightly stuffy affair into a raucous hoedown by blowing dozens of choruses of the “Too Fat Polka.” At the end of the night, Morrow scribbled down his number and told Bill that if he ever needed anything, he shouldn’t hesitate to give him a call.

尽管经历了那么多磨难，比尔在莫罗眼中仍是个异常开朗的人。（他常喊道："我跟市中心一样棒！"）他很高兴能置身人群之中，即便大多数人对他视而不见。夜幕降临时，比尔把假发塞进口袋，掏出从疗养院去世的朋友那里继承的口琴，吹奏了数十遍《太胖波尔卡》，将这场略显沉闷的聚会变成了喧闹的乡村舞会。临别时，莫罗匆匆写下自己的电话号码，告诉比尔若有需要尽管联系他。



Bill didn’t hesitate. At six a.m. the following morning, Morrow awoke with a throbbing headache to the sound of the phone ringing. A woman at the other end identified herself as “the dialer” before handing the phone to Bill. He told his new buddy that he needed a lift to the drugstore because he was out of toothpaste. When Morrow pulled up in his Studebaker two hours later, Bill was sitting out on the stoop, covered in inches of snow like a snowman, because after hanging up he had immediately stepped outside to wait for Morrow to arrive. It was the beginning of a beautiful and unlikely friendship that would change the course of autism history.

比尔毫不犹豫。第二天早上六点，莫罗被一阵电话铃声吵醒，头痛欲裂。电话那头的女人自称是“拨号员”，随后将电话递给比尔。比尔告诉他的新朋友，自己需要搭车去药店，因为牙膏用完了。两小时后，当莫罗开着斯图贝克轿车抵达时，比尔正坐在门廊台阶上，浑身覆盖着几英寸厚的积雪，活像个雪人——因为挂断电话后，他立刻出门等待莫罗到来。这段美好而不可思议的友谊就此开启，它将改变孤独症的历史进程。



II



Bill wasn’t autistic himself, though childhood schizophrenia was a common diagnosis on the overcrowded wards of Faribault. He was the opposite: a born schmoozer and people-pleaser who said hello to strangers in the street if he sensed that they might be friendly. At Faribault, the staff openly referred to residents like Bill as “crack-minded.” They hadn’t even tested his IQ until he had already been there for thirteen years.

比尔本人并非孤独谱系患者，尽管在法里博拥挤的病房里，“童年精神分裂症”是常见的诊断。他恰恰相反：天生擅长交际、乐于取悦他人，若察觉路人可能友善，便会主动打招呼。在法里博，工作人员公开将比尔这样的居民称为“疯子”。直到他在那里待了十三年后，院方才首次测试他的智商。



But instead of taking Bill on as a charity case, Morrow and his wife accepted him as a friend—as eccentric in his own ways as the other members of their circle, a scruffy crowd of artists, writers, and musicians. Soon, the errands for toothpaste and “wig spray” turned into leisurely drives around the city narrated by Bill’s unending monologues.

然而，莫罗夫妇并未将比尔视为施舍对象，而是以朋友相待——他就像他们圈子里那些特立独行的艺术家、作家和音乐家一样，有着自己独特的古怪之处。很快，原本只是帮忙买牙膏和"假发喷雾"的跑腿差事，变成了由比尔滔滔不绝的独白相伴的悠闲城市兜风。



“His head was swiveling every which way as he observed everything happening around us,” Morrow recalls, adopting Bill’s raspy delivery, the result of decades of smoking Old Rip, the harsh tobacco sold at the hospital store. Nice buses, yeah. Look at them buses, they’re really big, hold a lot of people. The school kids, yeah, they’re learning, and the men are working, they gotta work, and a man’s gotta have a good job. Morrow adds, “I only realized later that Bill was describing a world he was seeing for the very first time.”

"他的脑袋不停地转来转去，观察着周围发生的一切，"莫罗模仿着比尔沙哑的嗓音回忆道——那是他几十年抽医院商店卖的劣质 Old Rip 香烟留下的痕迹。"好棒的公交车，是啊。看看那些公交车，真大啊，能装好多人。上学的孩子们，是啊，他们在学习，大人们在工作，他们得工作，男人得有个好工作。"莫罗补充道："后来我才意识到，比尔描述的是一个他第一次亲眼看到的世界。"



As an undergraduate at the University of Minnesota, Morrow became fascinated by emerging technology like Super 8 and the Sony Portapak, the first portable video system, which he used to start filming Bill and their friends on their odysseys through the city. Bill was twice the age of anyone else in this crowd of midwestern bohemians but fit right in; his perpetual wonder was just another altered state. “Bill was not the elephant in the room,” Morrow laughs. “The room was full of elephants, and half of them were high.”

在明尼苏达大学读本科时，莫罗对新兴技术产生了浓厚兴趣，比如超 8 毫米胶片和索尼 Portapak——首款便携式摄像系统。他用这些设备开始拍摄比尔和朋友们在城市中的漫游之旅。在这群中西部波西米亚人中，比尔的年龄是其他人的两倍，但他完全融入了这个圈子；他永不停歇的好奇心不过是另一种迷幻状态。"比尔不是房间里的大象，"莫罗笑着说，"房间里到处都是大象，而且有一半都嗨着呢。"



When the Morrows had a son, Clay, Bill became his unofficial “grandpa” and a frequent guest at Sunday-night chicken dinners with Clay’s actual grandparents. On the nights that Morrow’s band, the Blue Sky Boys, played in bars and hotel lounges, “Wild Bill” would get his own moment in the spotlight to bring down the house with his trusty harmonica. At age sixty, for the first time since he was a child, he had a family.

当莫罗夫妇有了儿子克莱后，比尔成了他非正式的"爷爷"，经常在周日晚上与克莱的亲祖父母一起享用鸡肉晚餐。每当莫罗的乐队"蓝天男孩"在酒吧和酒店休息室演出时，"狂野比尔"总会获得属于自己的聚光灯时刻，用他信赖的口琴引爆全场。六十岁时，他第一次自童年以来拥有了一个家。



Bill reciprocated Morrow’s kindness by admitting him to his own inner sanctum: a little room at the rear of the Minikahda, next to the shed where the lawn mowers were parked, furnished with little more than a bed and a metal locker filled with cans of Aqua Net. In hushed and reverent tones, Bill showed Morrow his wig stand, the centerpiece of a personal shrine of pictures of children playing, dogs leaping, and suns rising that he had cut out of magazines. In the corner of the room was an old black-and-white TV for watching his favorite show, I Dream of Jeannie. Bill rarely missed an episode, and he would ask Morrow’s friends, “Do you dream of Jeannie too?”

比尔以善意回报莫罗，将他带入自己的私密空间：米尼卡达俱乐部后方的一间小房间，紧邻停放割草机的棚屋，屋内仅有一张床和一个装满 Aqua Net 发胶罐的金属储物柜。比尔用虔诚而低沉的语调向莫罗展示了他的假发架——那是他个人圣坛的核心，四周贴满从杂志剪下的孩童嬉戏、狗儿跳跃、朝阳初升的图片。房间角落放着一台老旧的黑白电视，用来收看他的最爱《太空仙女恋》。比尔几乎每集不落，还会问莫罗的朋友们：“你们也梦见珍妮吗？”



Morrow admits that his deep feeling of connection with Bill was inexplicable to outsiders. Their exchanges had a gently teasing, Zen-like quality:

莫罗坦言，他与比尔之间深厚的情感联结在外人看来难以理解。他们的对话带着温和的戏谑，颇有禅意：



Bill: See, to be a regular good man, buddy, you need three things in life: You need a good job, that’s what I think, and you need a good buddy.

比尔：“听着，要做一个正派的好人，伙计，人生需要三样东西：一份好工作——我是这么想的，还有一个好哥们。”



Morrow: That’s only two, Bill. What’s the third?

莫罗：“这才两样，比尔。第三样是什么？”



Bill: Hair, like what you got. That’s why you’re a regular good man, see?

比尔：头发，就像你这样的。这就是为什么你是个正经的好男人，明白吗？



After years of constant immersion in Aqua Net, Bill’s wig “broke” one day, and Morrow persuaded him to grow a distinguished-looking beard instead. He also arranged for Bill to see a dentist and get fitted for a pair of false teeth. As Bill took more care in his appearance, people treated him with more respect, and his self-confidence increased. It was a virtuous circle. “I wasn’t Bill’s friend to do him a favor. I don’t believe in pure altruism,” Morrow says. “If it hadn’t been fun to have him around, I wouldn’t have done it.”

多年持续浸泡在 Aqua Net 发胶中，比尔的假发有一天“报废”了，莫罗劝他蓄起一副有风度的胡子。他还安排比尔去看牙医，配了一副假牙。随着比尔更加注重外表，人们对他更加尊重，他的自信心也随之增强。这是一个良性循环。“我帮助比尔并非出于施恩。我不相信纯粹的利他主义，”莫罗说，“如果和他相处不愉快，我根本不会这么做。”



—



INEVITABLY, THOUGH, the young filmmaker was soon caught up in the necessity of making a living to support his growing family. When he got an offer to become a multimedia specialist for the School of Social Work at the University of Iowa, he decided to take it. He was heartbroken to leave his friend behind, but there was nothing he could do. As a ward of the state, Bill was unable to leave Minnesota without the approval of the mental competency board. In the fall of 1974, the Morrows packed up, said a tearful good-bye, and relocated to a farm in Kalona, not far from the university.

然而，这位年轻的电影制作人很快就被迫为养家糊口而奔波。当他收到爱荷华大学社会工作学院多媒体专家的职位邀请时，他决定接受。他心碎地离开了朋友，却无能为力。作为州政府的监护对象，比尔未经精神能力委员会的批准无法离开明尼苏达州。1974 年秋天，莫罗一家收拾行装，含泪告别，搬到了卡洛纳附近的一个农场，那里离大学不远。



A few months later, Morrow’s phone rang again. A social worker was calling because Bill had been found by the side of the road, passed out from the pain of his ulcerous leg, which he had neglected to care for after the Morrows left town. Feeling abandoned, he had reverted to his old ways, vegetating alone in his room while dreaming of Jeannie in reruns. His leg would likely have to be amputated, and the social worker asked for Morrow’s help in preparing him for the operation. On the long drive back to Minneapolis, he rehearsed the speech he would deliver when he got there—about how he felt bad about Bill’s leg, but after all, he had brought this suffering on himself by not doing the simple things that the doctors had told him to do to keep himself healthy. He had to lose the leg or he’d lose his life.

几个月后，莫罗的电话再次响起。一位社工打来电话，因为比尔被发现在路边昏迷，溃疡的腿疼痛难忍——莫罗一家离开小镇后，他疏于照顾自己的腿。感到被抛弃的他重拾旧习，独自在房间里萎靡度日，靠重播《杰尼的梦想》度日。他的腿很可能需要截肢，社工请求莫罗帮忙为他做好手术前的心理准备。在驱车返回明尼阿波利斯的漫长路上，莫罗反复演练着到达后要说的话——关于他对比尔的腿感到难过，但归根结底，是他自己没按医嘱做那些简单的保健措施才招致这般痛苦。他必须截肢，否则就会丧命。



Upon arriving at the hospital, Morrow consulted with the medical team about Bill’s postoperative options, which he assumed would include being fitted with a prosthetic leg and entering a rehab program so he could return to work. Instead, Morrow was informed that, given the patient’s mental competency status, he would not be eligible for a prosthesis or rehab and would almost certainly be sent back to Faribault to live out the rest of his days as a bedridden invalid. When Morrow walked into Bill’s room, instead of delivering his prepared speech, he said, “We’ve got to get you out of here, buddy. Do you want to come and live with us in Iowa?” Bill was overjoyed. Together, the two men headed south.

到达医院后，莫罗与医疗团队商讨了比尔术后的康复方案——他原以为会包括安装假肢和进入康复计划，以便比尔能重返工作岗位。然而院方告知，鉴于患者的认知能力状况，他既不符合安装假肢的条件，也无法接受康复治疗，几乎注定要被送回法里博，在卧床不起中度过余生。当莫罗走进比尔病房时，他放弃了事先准备好的说辞，直接说道："伙计，我们得带你离开这里。你愿意来爱荷华州和我们一起生活吗？"比尔欣喜若狂。两人随即一同南下。



Morrow helped Bill nurse his leg back to health, found him a room in a local boardinghouse, and arranged with a sympathetic advisor at the university, Thomas Walz, to hire Bill as a developmental disabilities consultant and brainstorm about productive things for him to do. But there was an unexpected legal wrinkle. From the point of view of the Minnesota authorities, Morrow had broken the law by taking Bill out of state; he could have been charged with kidnapping. The two men would have to return to Minneapolis and face the mental competency board to make a compelling case for Morrow becoming Bill’s legal conservator despite the fact that he was less than half his age.

莫罗帮助比尔照料他的腿伤直至康复，在当地的寄宿公寓为他找到一间房，并与大学里一位富有同情心的顾问托马斯·瓦尔兹商议，聘请比尔担任发展障碍顾问，并集思广益为他寻找有意义的工作。但一个意想不到的法律难题出现了。从明尼苏达州当局的角度来看，莫罗将比尔带出州界已属违法；他本可能被指控绑架。两人不得不返回明尼阿波利斯，面对精神能力评估委员会，竭力证明莫罗成为比尔法定监护人的合理性——尽管他的年龄还不到比尔的一半。



On the day of the hearing, Morrow tied his long blond hair back into a ponytail and tucked it under his collar. He also put on a sport coat and brought along a briefcase (which was empty) to complete the picture of a supremely competent conservator. To avoid unexpected outbursts during the hearing, he instructed Bill to stay mum: “These people are tricky, buddy, so just let me do all the talking.”

听证会当天，莫罗将金色长发扎成马尾辫塞进衣领，穿上运动外套，还带了一个空公文包，以此塑造出绝对称职监护人的形象。为避免听证会上出现意外状况，他叮嘱比尔保持沉默：“伙计，这些人不好对付，让我来说话就行。”



Even with Morrow’s makeover, however, the hearing did not go well. Sitting around a long table, the members of the board started grilling him with questions that he hadn’t prepared for, and he found himself lapsing into legal doublespeak that sounded absurd even to him. The men who would decide Bill’s fate didn’t seem to be buying any of it. Basically, their questions focused on only one thing: Why was this twentysomething trying to become the legal guardian of this older, blatantly retarded man in ill health?

然而，即便莫罗对听证会做了精心准备，进展仍不顺利。委员会成员围坐在长桌旁，开始用他毫无准备的问题轮番质询，他发现自己不自觉地陷入法律术语的套话中，连自己都觉得荒谬。这些将决定比尔命运的人似乎并不买账。他们的疑问始终聚焦于一点：这个二十出头的年轻人，为何要成为这位年长且明显智力障碍、健康状况不佳的男子的法定监护人？



Suddenly, Bill interrupted the somber proceeding and took matters into his own hands. “Let us pray!” he declared. Instinctively, the members of the board bowed their heads respectfully. “Our Father, who art in Heaven, hollow be thy knee,” he began—staying with the cadence of the Lord’s Prayer, but substituting his own life story. “And thank you, dear Lord, for bringing me my buddy, Mister Barry, he takes good care o’ me. I got a bird named Chubby, I got a good life now, and I don’t want to ever go back to that hellhole—you know that, Lord.” He continued on in that vein until the concluding “amen.”

突然，比尔打断了这场严肃的听证会，自己掌控了局面。“让我们祈祷吧！”他宣布道。委员会成员们本能地恭敬低头。“我们在天上的父，愿人都尊你的名为圣，”他开口念道——遵循主祷文的韵律，却穿插着自己的生平故事。“感谢你，亲爱的主，让我遇见我的好兄弟巴里先生，他把我照顾得很好。我养了只叫胖胖的鸟，现在过着好日子，再也不想回到那个地狱般的地方——主啊，你知道的。”他继续这样诉说着，直到最后那句“阿门”。



After a brief silence, the man at the head of the table cleared his throat and said, “Well, I think that says it all.” He signed an official form and slid it down to the end of the table. Bill christened the form his “on-my-own papers.” He was officially a free man.

短暂的沉默后，坐在桌首的男人清了清嗓子说道：“好吧，我想这就说明一切了。”他在一份正式文件上签了字，然后将其滑到桌子的另一端。比尔把这份文件戏称为他的“独立文书”。从此，他正式成为了一个自由人。



III



With Morrow’s and Walz’s help, Bill became the proprietor of his own café at the University of Iowa, Wild Bill’s Coffeeshop, which remains open to this day, employing adults with developmental disabilities. He never really figured out how to work the cash register—sometimes a mug of java cost 25 cents and sometimes it cost $250—but it all worked out, and he became a treasured member of the community. In 1978, Bill was named the Handicapped Iowan of the Year, and President Jimmy Carter invited him to the White House. Letters of congratulations poured in from all over, including one from the owner of a local salon who offered to make him a new, stylish salt-and-pepper wig, which he wore proudly for the rest of his life.

在莫罗和瓦尔茨的帮助下，比尔在爱荷华大学拥有了自己的咖啡馆——“狂野比尔的咖啡店”，这家店至今仍在营业，并雇佣着患有发育障碍的成年人。他始终没真正学会如何使用收银机——有时一杯咖啡只卖 25 美分，有时却要 250 美元——但一切都很顺利，他成了社区里备受珍视的一员。1978 年，比尔被评为“爱荷华年度残障人士”，吉米·卡特总统还邀请他前往白宫。祝贺信从四面八方涌来，其中一封来自当地一家沙龙店主，主动提出为他制作一顶时髦的花白假发，这顶假发后来被他骄傲地戴了一辈子。



Morrow had started making videos for the university on topics like aging and child abuse, and it occurred to him that Bill’s story would make a compelling documentary. He started shopping the idea around to funding agencies, but no one was interested in funding a film about the life of an intellectually disabled man. In 1980, however, Morrow was invited to present his show reel to a representative of the Mobil Oil corporation in New York City. Several NBC executives also attended the screening and told him that they were interested in producing a made-for-TV drama based on the story of Bill’s journey to independence.

莫罗开始为大学制作关于老龄化与儿童虐待等主题的视频，他意识到比尔的故事可以拍成一部引人入胜的纪录片。于是他开始向各资助机构推销这个想法，但没有人愿意资助一部关于智力障碍者生活的影片。然而在 1980 年，莫罗受邀前往纽约市向美孚石油公司的代表展示他的作品样片。几位 NBC 高管也出席了放映会，并表示他们有兴趣根据比尔争取独立的故事制作一部电视电影。



Bill, starring Mickey Rooney, with a handsome unknown named Dennis Quaid playing Morrow, aired in 1981—the year of Reds, On Golden Pond, and Chariots of Fire. The film went on to win an Emmy award, a Peabody, and two Golden Globes. Rooney turned in a masterful performance that captured Bill’s distinctive mixture of childlike wonder and poignant gravitas, shooting scenes during the day while appearing in Sugar Babies on Broadway at night. At the Golden Globes ceremony, Bill was invited to accept the Best Actor award in Rooney’s stead, preemptively stripped of his harmonica. At the last moment, though, he again took matters into his own hands and whipped a backup mini-harmonica out of his pocket. Jane Fonda started clapping along, and the usually slick event was interrupted by a spontaneous outburst of authenticity.

1981 年，由米基·鲁尼主演、英俊的新人丹尼斯·奎德饰演莫罗的电视电影《比尔》播出——同年还有《赤色分子》、《金色池塘》和《烈火战车》。该片最终斩获艾美奖、皮博迪奖和两项金球奖。鲁尼贡献了精湛演技，捕捉到比尔兼具孩童般天真与深刻庄重的独特气质——他白天拍戏，晚上还要在百老汇演出《甜心宝贝》。金球奖颁奖礼上，比尔受邀代鲁尼领取最佳男主角奖，事先被没收了口琴。但最后一刻，他再次自作主张，从口袋里掏出一把备用迷你口琴。简·方达带头鼓掌，这场向来浮华的盛会被突如其来的真情流露打断。



Two years later, Morrow wrote a sequel, Bill: On His Own. By then, he’d moved to Hollywood to try his luck as a screenwriter. “I want to stay here,” Bill told him before he left Iowa City. “It’s my home.” Though he had been written off as incapable of learning at age seven, he developed dramatically in his fifties and sixties, prompted by the respect of those who had made a place for him in their lives. On the morning of June 16, 1983, Bill’s landlady found him slumped in his favorite chair, freshly showered and dressed, with his lunch box at his side, ready to take his usual bus to the café. Bill had died peacefully of old age. He was buried with his harmonica and his on-my-own papers in his pocket.

两年后，莫罗创作了续集《比尔：独自前行》。那时他已搬到好莱坞，尝试以编剧身份闯荡。"我想留在这里，"比尔在爱荷华城临别前告诉他，"这是我的家。"尽管七岁时曾被判定为无法学习，但在那些为他留出生活空间的人的尊重下，他在五六十岁时实现了惊人的成长。1983 年 6 月 16 日清晨，比尔的房东发现他瘫坐在心爱的扶手椅上，刚沐浴更衣完毕，身旁放着午餐盒，准备像往常一样乘公交去咖啡馆。比尔因年迈安详离世，口袋里揣着他的口琴和那份"独立宣言"。



“What Bill taught me,” Morrow says, “is that not only do people like Bill need society, society needs people like Bill.”

"比尔教会我的，"莫罗说，"不仅是像他这样的人需要社会，社会也需要像他这样的人。"



—



IN THE YEARS AFTER Bill’s death, Morrow was unable to get this lesson off his mind. As he pursued his career in Hollywood, he became active in advocacy organizations like the Arc, the network of parents and disabled adults that had fought for the reforms that led to Bill’s liberation from Faribault.

比尔去世后的岁月里，莫罗始终无法释怀这个领悟。在好莱坞打拼事业的同时，他积极投身于 Arc 等倡导组织——正是这个由家长和残障成年人组成的网络，曾为改革而战，最终让比尔从法里博的禁锢中获得解放。



One night in 1984, at an Arc conference in Arlington, Texas, Morrow met a man who had one of the most unusual minds on earth. The bones of Kim Peek’s cranium had failed to fuse properly in the womb, so at birth, part of his cortical tissue protruded through a baseball-sized blister at the back of his head. His brain also lacked a corpus callosum, the thick bundle of white matter that usually coordinates communication between the left and right hemispheres. When he was nine months old, a neurologist rushing off to a golf game told his parents that Peek was hopelessly retarded, would never amount to anything, and belonged in an institution. But his father and mother, Fran and Jeanne, refused to abandon him, vowing to care for him at home as best they could.

1984 年某个夜晚，在德克萨斯州阿灵顿的一场 Arc 会议上，莫罗遇见了一个拥有世上最非凡头脑的人。金·皮克颅骨的骨缝在母体内未能正常愈合，出生时部分大脑皮层组织从后脑勺一个棒球大小的水疱状凸起中突出。他的大脑还缺少胼胝体——这束通常协调左右脑半球沟通的厚实白质纤维。九个月大时，一位急着赶去高尔夫球赛的神经科医生告诉他的父母，皮克患有不可逆转的智力障碍，将来一事无成，应该被送进收容机构。但他的父亲弗兰和母亲珍妮拒绝放弃他，发誓要尽己所能在家照顾他。



As an infant, Peek began developing cognitive capacities so extraordinary that they can only be described as uncanny. By eighteen months, he was memorizing every book his parents read to him, word for word, and turning them over on the shelf so they wouldn’t waste his time by reading them again. At three, he was able to look up words in the dictionary and sound them out phonetically. He was equally adept with numbers. He would read telephone books for fun and total up the numbers on passing license plates. He was eventually able to read two pages of a book simultaneously—one with his right eye and one with his left—even if they were held upside down or reflected in a mirror.

婴儿时期，皮克便开始展现出超凡的认知能力，其惊人程度只能用"不可思议"来形容。十八个月大时，他已能逐字记住父母为他读过的每一本书，并将它们倒扣在书架上，以免父母重复朗读浪费他的时间。三岁时，他就能查阅字典并拼读单词。数字同样难不倒他——他会把电话簿当消遣读物，还能心算过往车辆的车牌数字之和。最终他练就了同时阅读两页书的绝技：左右眼各读一页，即便书页倒置或通过镜面反射也毫无障碍。



Permanently excluded from school for being disruptive, he mastered the standard high school curriculum with the help of tutors by the time he was fourteen, though the local school board declined to award him with an equivalency certificate. Taking a job in a sheltered workshop for disabled people, he performed complex payroll calculations without benefit of an adding machine; one of his nicknames was “the Kimputer.” Yet he was unable to dress himself or attend to many of his basic needs without help. When he finally learned to shave, he would close his eyes in front of the mirror because he couldn’t stand seeing the sides of his face reversed.

因扰乱课堂秩序被学校永久开除后，他在十四岁时通过家教辅导掌握了标准高中课程，但当地教育委员会拒绝授予他同等学力证书。他在一家为残障人士设立的庇护工场工作，无需计算器就能完成复杂的薪资核算，因此得了个"金普特"的绰号。然而他无法自行穿衣，许多基本生活需求都需要他人协助。当他终于学会刮胡子时，总会在镜子前闭上眼睛——他无法忍受看到自己左右颠倒的脸颊。



Peek was a savant: a modern version of the prodigiously gifted “idiots” described by nineteeth-century clinicians like Édouard Séguin and John Langdon Down, the superintendent of the Royal Earlswood Asylum in Surrey. One of Langdon Down’s patients was an intellectually disabled boy who had memorized The Rise and Fall of the Roman Empire after reading it once and could recite it word for word—albeit in rote, mechanical fashion. (Having skipped a line in his original reading, he went back and corrected himself, but then every time he reached that passage in his memory, he went through the same cumbersome process.) Another boy could recall the address of every confectioner’s shop where he’d eaten sweets in London, along with the dates of all his visits. A third boy was able to instantly multiply two three-digit numbers in his head even before the doctor could jot them down—but was rarely able to recall Down’s name, despite the fact that he talked to him nearly every day. Langdon Down also recalled seeing “many examples of children who had spoken well and with understanding, but who lost speech at the period of the second dentition, and had also suspension of mental growth”—a description that anticipated modern parents’ accounts of their autistic children’s abrupt loss of skills by a century.

皮克是一位奇才：他是 19 世纪临床医生如爱德华·塞甘和萨里郡皇家厄尔斯伍德精神病院院长约翰·朗顿·唐所描述的那种天赋异禀的“白痴天才”的现代版本。朗顿·唐的一名患者是位智力障碍的男孩，他仅读一遍就记住了《罗马帝国衰亡史》，并能逐字背诵——尽管是以机械刻板的方式。（由于最初阅读时跳过了一行，他回头纠正了自己，但此后每次在记忆中念到那段文字时，他都会重复同样繁琐的修正过程。）另一个男孩能回忆起他在伦敦所有吃过糖果的糖果店地址，以及每次光顾的日期。第三个男孩能在医生还没记下数字之前，就瞬间心算出两个三位数的乘积——但他却很少能记住唐的名字，尽管他几乎每天都和唐交谈。 朗登·唐还回忆起"见过许多孩子，他们原本说话清晰且理解力正常，但在换牙期突然丧失语言能力，同时智力发展也停滞不前"——这一描述比现代父母对孤独谱系孩子突然丧失技能的叙述早了一个世纪。



Unlike the savants in Earlswood Asylum, however, Peek’s special abilities were not restricted to one or two narrow domains. He could also recall classical music scores note for note, would advise conductors about mistakes that the orchestra had made, and once stood up in the middle of a production of Shakespeare yelling, “Stop the play!” When one of the actors asked him what was wrong, Peek informed him that he had omitted some words from a previous line. When the actor remarked that he didn’t think anyone would notice or care, Peek countered, “Shakespeare would have cared!”

然而，与厄尔斯伍德精神病院的学者症候群患者不同，皮克的特异才能并不局限于一两个狭窄领域。他能逐音符记下古典音乐总谱，会提醒指挥家管弦乐队演奏中的错误，还曾在莎士比亚戏剧演出中途站起来大喊："停演！"当演员问他出了什么问题时，皮克指出他漏掉了前一句台词中的几个词。演员表示不认为会有人注意到或在意时，皮克反驳道："莎士比亚会在意！"



After seeing the Bill films, Peek’s father, who was the communications director for the Arc, invited Morrow to Arlington to enlist him in raising public awareness of intellectual disability. Peek introduced himself with the dramatic statement, “Think about yourself, Barry Morrow.” Fran explained that when his son got excited, he would lose track of his pronouns; what he had really meant to say was “I think about you, Barry Morrow.” The screenwriter couldn’t fathom why Peek had been thinking about someone he’d never met, but that became clear when he reeled off the closing credits from Bill verbatim. As they went over mailing lists, Peek began correcting erroneous zip codes on the fly and was able to recite step-by-step driving directions between any two points in the United States and Canada. He was also an inexhaustible font of sports trivia. To his family and a small circle of friends, Peek was an eccentric marvel who spent most of his time alone in his room. To Morrow, though, he seemed like an extraordinary protagonist in search of a plot. On the plane back to Los Angeles, he started jotting down ideas for his next film.

看完《比尔》这部电影后，皮克的父亲——阿卡组织的通讯主管——邀请莫罗前往阿灵顿，希望借助他的力量提高公众对智力障碍的认识。皮克用一句戏剧性的话自我介绍：“想想你自己，巴里·莫罗。”弗兰解释说，儿子一激动就会搞混人称代词；他真正想说的是“我想着你，巴里·莫罗。”这位编剧百思不得其解，为什么皮克会惦记一个素未谋面的人，但当皮克逐字逐句背出《比尔》片尾字幕时，一切豁然开朗。在核对邮寄名单时，皮克能当场纠正错误的邮政编码，还能背诵美国和加拿大任意两点之间的详细行车路线。他更是一座取之不尽的体育冷知识宝库。在家人和少数朋友眼中，皮克是个古怪的天才，大部分时间都独自待在房间里。但在莫罗看来，他仿佛是一个等待情节的非凡主角。在飞回洛杉矶的航班上，莫罗开始为下一部电影记下灵感。



Morrow’s agent warned him to steer clear of any more projects involving disability, but he couldn’t stop thinking about his meeting with Peek—“a man with more information in his brain than the encyclopedias I used to sell,” as he puts it. The notion of a Hollywood movie with a “retarded” lead character was unusual, to say the least, but it had been tried successfully once before: Cliff Robertson won an Academy Award in 1969 for his sensitive portrayal of an intellectually disabled baker in Charly, an adaptation of Daniel Keyes’s heartbreaking novella Flowers for Algernon.

莫罗的经纪人警告他不要再参与任何涉及残障题材的项目，但他始终无法忘记与皮克的会面——用他自己的话说，"这个人的大脑里储存的信息比我当年卖过的百科全书还多"。一部以"智障"角色为主角的好莱坞电影在当时堪称异类，但此前已有成功先例：1969 年，克里夫·罗伯逊凭借在《查理》中细腻演绎一位智力障碍的面包师而获得奥斯卡奖，该片改编自丹尼尔·凯斯令人心碎的中篇小说《献给阿尔吉侬的花束》。



That film’s broad appeal had benefited from its Pygmalionesque science fiction twist—an experimental operation that temporarily turned the shambling Charlie Gordon into a genius. Only after the operation did his character become fully human, capable of love, lust, ambition, sorrow, and rage. In Peek’s case, Nature had already performed the operation that made him a genius, but would moviegoing audiences accept a permanently impaired protagonist as human?

这部电影之所以广受欢迎，得益于其皮格马利翁式的科幻设定——一场实验性手术让笨拙的查理·戈登暂时变成了天才。只有在手术后，他的角色才变得完整，拥有了爱、欲望、野心、悲伤和愤怒的能力。而在皮克身上，大自然早已完成了让他成为天才的"手术"，但电影观众会接受一个永久性残障的主角作为"人"吗？



Morrow’s original conception for the character he would call Raymond Babbitt was part Peek and part Bill—a man with savant abilities who was “kidnapped” from an institution that was the only world he knew. To ratchet up the dramatic tension, Morrow designed Raymond’s younger brother Charlie as his own opposite. Rather than being a naïve and well-intentioned midwesterner, Charlie was an abrasive, egotistical gray-market dealer in luxury sports cars who befriended this awkward brother he didn’t know he had to gain control of a $3 million trust fund.

莫罗最初构思他称之为雷蒙德·巴比特的角色时，融合了皮克和比尔两人的特质——一个拥有学者症候群能力的人，被从他所知的唯一世界——一家疗养院中“绑架”出来。为了增强戏剧张力，莫罗将雷蒙德的弟弟查理设计成他的对立面。查理并非中西部那种天真善良的人，而是一个粗鲁、自私的灰色市场豪华跑车经销商，他主动结识这位素未谋面的笨拙哥哥，目的是为了掌控一笔 300 万美元的信托基金。



In a scene calculated to thrill mainstream audiences, Morrow had Charlie exploit his brother’s savant abilities by bringing him to a casino in Vegas, where Raymond beat the blackjack dealer by counting cards. (Ironically, when the screenwriter brought Peek to Reno to see if this was truly plausible, he declined to go along with the scheme, saying, “This is not fair, Barry Morrow.”)

在一个旨在震撼主流观众的场景中，莫罗让查理利用哥哥的学者能力，带他来到拉斯维加斯的赌场，雷蒙德通过算牌击败了二十一点庄家。（讽刺的是，当编剧带皮克去里诺验证这一情节是否真实可行时，皮克拒绝配合这个计划，说道：“这不公平，巴里·莫罗。”）



Morrow also flipped the Pygmalion theme on its head. Rather than Raymond becoming human by being cured of his disability, Charlie would learn what was truly important in life by interacting with him—as Morrow himself had learned from Bill. He also put the unlikely pair of brothers on the road for a series of perilous adventures involving loan sharks and survivalists in the desert. At the end of the script, the two brothers decided to live together happily ever after.

莫罗还颠覆了皮格马利翁主题。雷蒙德并非通过治愈残障而变得人性化，查理反而在与他的互动中领悟了人生真谛——正如莫罗本人从比尔身上学到的那样。他还让这对不寻常的兄弟踏上旅途，经历了一系列惊险冒险，涉及沙漠中的高利贷者和生存主义者。剧本结尾，两兄弟决定永远幸福地生活在一起。



Though the Bill films had been well received, Morrow still had so little confidence in his ability that he listed his occupation on tax forms as “typist.” But in the fall of 1986, he got enormously encouraging feedback from United Artists. “This script is a beautifully written, extremely moving tragi-comedy that depicts a personality type seldom, if ever, explored in a feature film format,” a UA production assistant wrote. “This is a remarkable first draft offering two meaty roles that should appeal to a number of big name acting duos . . . this is the kind of gripping, original and emotional script that could evolve into a film classic.”

尽管比尔系列电影反响不错，莫罗对自己的能力仍缺乏信心，以至于在纳税申报单上将自己的职业填写为"打字员"。但在 1986 年秋天，他从联美公司获得了极大的鼓励。"这个剧本是一部文笔优美、感人至深的悲喜剧，描绘了一种在故事片中鲜少被探索的人格类型，"联美公司的一位制片助理写道。"这是一部出色的初稿，提供了两个分量十足的角色，应该能吸引众多大牌演员搭档……这是一部扣人心弦、原创且充满情感的剧本，有望成为电影经典。"



Anticipating a lighthearted, action-packed, buddy comedy appropriate for a Christmas release, UA optioned the script, prevailing upon Morrow to add a “ring of fire” sequence in which the brothers were trapped by survivalists in a barn surrounded by a moat filled with flaming gasoline. To escape, Raymond employed his savant superpowers to assemble a motorcycle from parts stored in a hayrack.

UA 公司看中了一部轻松幽默、动作场面丰富、适合圣诞档期的兄弟喜剧剧本，于是买下版权，并说服莫罗加入一段"火圈"戏份：兄弟俩被生存主义者困在谷仓里，四周环绕着燃烧汽油的护城河。为逃出生天，雷蒙德运用其天才超能力，用干草架上的零件组装出一辆摩托车。



The production assistant’s enthusiasm turned out to be prophetic, but not before the script had passed through the hands of several A-list directors, including Martin Brest (Beverly Hills Cop), Sydney Pollack (The Way We Were), and Steven Spielberg (E.T.). Luckily for Morrow, his draft landed on the desk of superagent Michael Ovitz, who forwarded it to one of the hottest marquee names in the business: Dustin Hoffman, who was coming off his tour de force performance in Tootsie as a male actor who transformed himself into an actress to earn a sought-after role. Ovitz’s idea was to have Hoffman play Charlie opposite Bill Murray as Raymond.

这位制片助理的热情后来被证明具有先见之明，不过在此之前，剧本已辗转经过多位一线导演之手，包括马丁·布莱斯特（《比佛利山警探》）、西德尼·波拉克（《往日情怀》）和史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格（《E.T.外星人》）。幸运的是，莫罗的剧本最终落到了超级经纪人迈克尔·奥维茨的案头，他将其转交给当时业内最炙手可热的巨星之一——达斯汀·霍夫曼。这位刚在《窈窕淑男》中完成震撼表演的男演员，在片中饰演一位为争取角色而男扮女装的演员。奥维茨的构想是让霍夫曼饰演查理，与饰演雷蒙德的比尔·默瑞搭档。



Hoffman loved the script. But he didn’t want to play Raymond’s callow younger brother—he wanted to play Raymond. A few years earlier, he’d seen a 60 Minutes broadcast profiling three savants: an intellectually disabled black sculptor named Alonzo Clemons who crafted astonishingly lifelike representations of horses with no artistic training; a blind musical savant with cerebral palsy named Leslie Lemke, who spontaneously developed the ability to play complex compositions on the piano after hearing them once; and George Finn, one of the calculating twins that Oliver Sacks met at the Bronx Psychiatric Center and described in his 1985 bestseller, The Man Who Mistook His Wife for a Hat. When Morley Safer asked Finn what the weather had been like in his hometown on November 3, 1958, he replied correctly without hesitation, “It was a cloudy day, it was on a Monday. Snow flurries that morning, very cold. Little bits of raindrops too.” In a flash, he determined that June 6 in the year 91,360 will fall on a Friday. Yet he was unable to multiply seven and five.

霍夫曼非常喜欢这个剧本。但他不想演雷蒙那个稚嫩的弟弟——他想演雷蒙本人。几年前，他看过一期《60 分钟》节目，报道了三位学者症候群患者：一位是智力障碍的黑人雕塑家阿隆佐·克莱蒙斯，他未经任何艺术训练却能创作出栩栩如生的马匹雕塑；一位是患有脑瘫的盲人音乐天才莱斯利·莱姆克，他只要听过一次复杂的钢琴曲就能自发演奏出来；还有乔治·芬恩，他是奥利弗·萨克斯在布朗克斯精神病中心遇到的那对计算天才双胞胎之一，萨克斯在 1985 年的畅销书《错把妻子当帽子》中描述过他们。当莫利·塞弗问芬恩 1958 年 11 月 3 日他家乡的天气如何时，他毫不犹豫地准确回答："那是个阴天，星期一。那天早晨有零星小雪，非常冷。还下了点小雨。"他瞬间就能算出 91360 年 6 月 6 日是星期五，却连七乘以五都算不出来。



Though Murray could have pulled off the tricky role of Raymond with panache, Hoffman had already proven himself to be an actor of inimitable range, having memorably played an anxious intellectual wooed by an older married woman (The Graduate), a jaded Times Square con man (Midnight Cowboy), and a driven ad executive (Kramer vs. Kramer). What Ovitz didn’t know was that long before his breakthrough role in The Graduate, Hoffman had been polishing the skills he would need to play the first member of Asperger’s forgotten tribe that most people in the world would ever see.

尽管默里本可以凭借魅力驾驭雷蒙德这一棘手角色，但霍夫曼早已证明自己是位戏路无可比拟的演员——他曾令人难忘地饰演过被已婚年长女性追求的知识分子（《毕业生》）、时代广场玩世不恭的骗子（《午夜牛郎》），以及野心勃勃的广告公司高管（《克莱默夫妇》）。奥维茨不知道的是，早在凭借《毕业生》取得突破性角色之前，霍夫曼就已开始打磨日后饰演这位全球观众首次见到的阿斯伯格症候群边缘人所需的演技。



IV



Shortly after arriving in New York City from Los Angeles in 1958, Hoffman moved into a sixth-floor walkup at West 109th Street and Broadway with another actor who would rise to the stratosphere of their profession: Robert Duvall. They teamed up with the equally talented Gene Hackman, and the men became an inseparable trio. They applied themselves to the perfection of their craft with the intensity of religious fanatics. (Their goal, as Duvall once described it, was to be able to “live truthfully in an imaginary set of circumstances . . . in a somewhat effortless way.”) Between cold readings into pitch-black, echoing rooms at cattle-call auditions, they took any job they could scrounge up, mining the speech and behavior of the people around them for rhythms and gestures they could use to flesh out their roles onstage. Hoffman checked coats on Broadway, threaded orchids on wires for a company that sold Hawaiian leis, dressed up as Paul Revere to shout out headlines in Times Square, and became a typist for the Yellow Pages. To cultivate his French accent, he waited tables in a bistro, where he passed himself off as a native speaker. (If a customer happened to actually be a native speaker, he would explain that he needed to practice his English.)

1958 年从洛杉矶抵达纽约市后不久，霍夫曼搬进了西 109 街与百老汇交汇处一栋无电梯公寓的六楼，同住的还有另一位日后将跻身行业巅峰的演员：罗伯特·杜瓦尔。他们与同样才华横溢的吉恩·哈克曼结盟，三人形影不离。他们以宗教狂热分子般的专注力打磨演技。（正如杜瓦尔曾描述的那样，他们的目标是能够"在虚构情境中真实地生活……以一种毫不费力的方式"。）在参加人山人海的试镜时，他们对着漆黑回响的房间进行冷读，同时抓住任何能糊口的工作，从周围人的言语举止中挖掘节奏和姿态，用以充实舞台角色。霍夫曼在百老汇寄存外套，为一家销售夏威夷花环的公司将兰花穿在铁丝上，装扮成保罗·里维尔在时代广场高喊头条新闻，还当过黄页打字员。为了培养法语口音，他在一家小酒馆当服务员，谎称自己是法语母语者。 （如果顾客恰好是英语母语者，他会解释自己需要练习英语。）)



But the job that opened up the richest vein of material for him was being a nurse’s aide at the New York Psychiatric Institute (NYPI), a short hop on the A train from his apartment. Starting each morning at 6:30 a.m., he worked an eight-hour shift that consisted of playing Ping-Pong, Scrabble, and other games with the patients, accompanying them to hydrotherapy sessions, laundering their soiled bedclothes, and holding them down for shock treatments. (The technique was introduced to America in 1939 with a public demonstration at NYPI on a boy diagnosed with childhood schizophrenia.) “All my life I had wanted to get inside a prison or a mental hospital, like most kids want to go to a zoo,” Hoffman recalled. “I wanted to get inside where behavior, human behavior, was so exposed. All the things the rest of us were feeling and stopping up were coming out of these people, as if through their pores.”

但为他提供最丰富素材的工作，是在纽约精神病学研究所（NYPI）担任护士助理——从公寓搭乘 A 线地铁仅需短途即可抵达。每天清晨 6:30 开始，他工作八小时，内容包括与患者打乒乓球、拼字游戏和其他活动，陪同他们进行水疗，清洗他们弄脏的床单，并在电击治疗时按住他们。（这项技术于 1939 年通过 NYPI 对一名被诊断为儿童精神分裂症的男孩进行公开演示后引入美国。）"我一生都想进入监狱或精神病院，就像大多数孩子想去动物园一样，"霍夫曼回忆道，"我想进入行为——人类行为——如此赤裸暴露的地方。我们其他人感受并压抑的一切，都从这些人身上涌出，仿佛透过他们的毛孔。"



The patient who made the deepest impression on him was an older man known simply as “the Doctor.” He had once been a brilliant pathologist at NYPI but then suffered a series of strokes that left him nearly immobile. His devoted wife, also a doctor, would visit him every day at lunch. By that point, he could speak only in gibberish; the young actor would speak gibberish back to him. As Oliver Sacks was doing at Bronx Psychiatric, Hoffman would play piano to entertain the patients, and the Doctor particularly loved it when he sang “Goodnight, Irene.” One day, he began singing along when his wife walked in. Suddenly the Doctor stood up, met his wife in the middle of the room, and began to sob. “What is it?” she asked, adding tenderly, “We’ll have lunch, we’ll talk.” A moment of stark lucidity crossed his face. “I can’t, I caaan’t!” he moaned.

给他留下最深印象的病人是一位被称为“医生”的老人。他曾是纽约精神病理学研究所（NYPI）杰出的病理学家，但接连几次中风后几乎无法动弹。他忠实的妻子也是一位医生，每天午餐时间都会来探望他。那时，老人只能含糊不清地说话，而这位年轻演员也会用同样的方式回应他。就像奥利弗·萨克斯在布朗克斯精神病院所做的那样，霍夫曼会弹钢琴为病人解闷，而这位“医生”尤其喜欢听他唱《晚安，艾琳》。有一天，当他的妻子走进来时，老人开始跟着唱。突然，他站了起来，在房间中央迎向妻子，开始抽泣。“怎么了？”她问道，温柔地补充说，“我们吃午饭，我们聊聊。”他脸上闪过一丝清晰的意识。“我不能，我不能啊！”他呻吟道。



Hoffman broke down crying too, and he quit the institution shortly after that. When he read Morrow’s script for Rain Man, memories of that moment came flooding back to him.

霍夫曼也忍不住哭了起来，不久后便离开了那家机构。当他读到莫罗为《雨人》写的剧本时，那一刻的记忆如潮水般涌回。



A meeting was arranged in Hollywood between Hoffman, Peek, Peek’s father, Morrow, and Brest, who was still attached to the project. Accompanying the actor was his longtime friend Murray Schisgal, an award-winning playwright and co-author of Tootsie, who acted as his consigliere. Jazzed up to meet a famous Hollywood actor, Peek had added knowledge of cinematic history to his memory banks. As he perambulated around the room, flapping his hands excitedly, Hoffman fell in behind him. “I can vividly recall Dustin walking behind Kim, mimicking his walk, his body language, his hand movements, and his head tilt—as if he was trying Kim on like a coat,” Morrow says. “I thought everything was going well—how could you not be fascinated meeting a person like Kim? But then Murray sidled up to me and said something like, ‘This isn’t going to work. Dustin’s not going to do Kim. He’s too complicated and weird.’”

在好莱坞安排了一次会面，与会者包括霍夫曼、皮克、皮克的父亲、莫罗，以及仍参与该项目的布莱斯特。陪同这位演员的是他的老友默里·希斯盖尔——一位获奖剧作家、《窈窕淑男》的联合编剧，他充当着霍夫曼的军师。皮克因即将见到一位好莱坞著名影星而兴奋不已，特意将电影史知识存入记忆库。他在房间里来回踱步，激动地拍打着双手，霍夫曼紧随其后。“我清晰地记得达斯汀跟在金身后，模仿他的步态、肢体语言、手势和歪头的动作——仿佛在像试穿外套一样体验金这个角色，”莫罗回忆道，“我当时觉得一切进展顺利——见到金这样的人怎么可能不为之着迷？但默里悄悄凑过来对我说：‘这行不通。达斯汀不会演金。他太复杂、太古怪了。’”



This unpromising meeting was the first of a long series of setbacks for the project that nearly scuttled it altogether. In the coming years, Hoffman would emerge as Rain Man’s most tireless champion, steering it through troubled waters that would have sunk many other films. His unwavering commitment to the project also attracted the interest of a handsome young former seminarian named Tom Cruise, then rising rapidly through the ranks buoyed by lead roles in Top Gun and The Color of Money. He idolized Hoffman and jumped at the chance to play his smarmy younger brother.

这场看似毫无希望的会面，只是该项目漫长挫折史的开端，几乎令其彻底夭折。在接下来的岁月里，霍夫曼成为了《雨人》最不知疲倦的捍卫者，引领它穿越足以击沉无数影片的惊涛骇浪。他对项目的坚定投入，还吸引了一位英俊的前神学院学生——汤姆·克鲁斯的关注。彼时，克鲁斯凭借《壮志凌云》和《金钱本色》中的主角光环迅速崛起，他崇拜霍夫曼，并欣然接受了饰演其油滑弟弟的机会。



Morrow had never even heard the word autism when he wrote the first draft of Rain Man. Hoffman was instrumental in making the character of Raymond specifically autistic rather than just intellectually disabled. If it weren’t for a chance conversation between Hoffman’s associate producer, Gail Mutrux, and a psychotherapist named Bruce Gainsley, the movie that introduced the concept of autistic adults to the world might never have touched on the subject at all.

莫罗在撰写《雨人》初稿时，甚至从未听说过“孤独症”这个词。霍夫曼在将雷蒙德这一角色塑造为特定孤独谱系患者（而非单纯智力障碍）的过程中起到了关键作用。若非霍夫曼的联合制片人盖尔·穆特鲁克斯与心理治疗师布鲁斯·盖恩斯利的一次偶然交谈，这部向世界介绍成年孤独谱系个体概念的影片，或许根本不会触及这一主题。



One day, Mutrux happened to mention to Gainsley that she needed to find out more about savant syndrome. He referred her to two psychologists who agreed to read Morrow’s script and offer feedback. One was Peter Tanguay, an NIMH-funded researcher on social communication at UCLA. And the other was Bernie Rimland. The notion of making his son’s condition the subject of a Hollywood blockbuster was the golden opportunity that Rimland had been waiting for.

一天，穆特鲁克斯偶然向盖恩斯利提起，她需要了解更多关于学者综合征的信息。盖恩斯利便推荐了两位心理学家，他们同意阅读莫罗的剧本并提供反馈。一位是彼得·坦圭，他是美国国家心理健康研究所资助的加州大学洛杉矶分校社会沟通研究员。另一位是伯尼·里姆兰。将儿子的病症作为好莱坞大片主题的想法，正是里姆兰等待已久的黄金机会。



Both Tanguay and Rimland came to the same conclusion: the chances of finding a real-life “idiot savant” who could beat a blackjack dealer in Vegas were statistically slim. But the possibility of an autistic savant being capable of such a feat was much more likely. In the files in his office, Rimland had the names of half a dozen young men who could fill the bill. He also felt that the eccentricities of autism (such as the difficulty in expressing emotion) would make the film far more interesting. Tanguay agreed: “I told Gail, this guy’s autistic.”

坦圭和里姆兰得出了相同的结论：从统计学上看，在现实中找到一位能在拉斯维加斯击败二十一点庄家的“白痴天才”概率微乎其微。但一位孤独谱系天才能够完成这一壮举的可能性则大得多。里姆兰办公室的档案中记录着六位符合要求的年轻男性。他还认为，孤独谱系的特质（例如表达情感的困难）会让这部电影更加引人入胜。坦圭也表示赞同：“我告诉盖尔，这家伙是孤独谱系。”



For Hoffman, the idea of taking on a role that would deny him the usual ways of connecting with his fellow actors and the audience was an irresistible challenge. But the notion of playing Raymond that way threatened to take the film far out of the realm of feel-good holiday fare. By that point, Brest had brought on screenwriter Ron Bass to rework Morrow’s script. Hoffman told Brest and Bass that the essence of the film was a love story between the estranged brothers. “Maybe it’s too easy to love this guy, because he’s so sweet,” he added. “What if the guy was, like, autistic or something, and a real pain in the ass?” Brest changed the subject, telling Bass privately that he would set Hoffman straight about this misguided idea. That conversation evidently didn’t go as planned and Brest bailed on the project over “creative differences” shortly thereafter, effectively shelving the film.

对霍夫曼而言，饰演一个无法像往常那样与同剧演员及观众建立联系的角色，是个难以抗拒的挑战。但让雷蒙德以这种方式出场，却可能让这部电影彻底偏离温馨假日片的范畴。彼时，布雷斯特已请来编剧罗恩·巴斯重写莫罗的剧本。霍夫曼告诉布雷斯特和巴斯，这部电影的核心是讲述一对疏离兄弟间的爱情故事。"也许让观众爱上这个角色太容易了，因为他实在太讨喜，"他补充道，"但如果这个角色患有孤独谱系障碍之类的病症，而且还是个让人头疼的家伙呢？"布雷斯特转移了话题，私下对巴斯说他会纠正霍夫曼这个离谱的想法。显然那次谈话并未按计划进行——布雷斯特不久后便以"创作理念分歧"为由退出项目，这部电影也因此被搁置。



But then, a few months later, Ovitz called Bass with good news: Steven Spielberg, fresh off the success of The Color Purple, had decided to resurrect Rain Man. The first time the director spoke with Bass, he told him bluntly that he was in favor of playing Raymond as autistic: “Dustin Hoffman is right and you’re wrong,” Spielberg said. “Do you know why?” Bass was already ahead of him: “I know why. The love story is only as good as its obstacle—and it’s a much greater obstacle if the guy’s autistic. I think it’s a cool idea, so let’s give it a try.” Another reason Bass supported the idea was that his sister worked with autistic people at UCLA. After a few months of brainstorming, though, Spielberg dropped the project to direct Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade.

然而，几个月后，奥维茨给巴斯带来了好消息：凭借《紫色》大获成功的史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格决定重启《雨人》。这位导演首次与巴斯交谈时，直截了当地表示支持将雷蒙德塑造成孤独谱系角色："达斯汀·霍夫曼是对的，你错了，"斯皮尔伯格说，"你知道为什么吗？"巴斯早已心领神会："我知道。爱情故事的精彩程度取决于障碍的大小——如果主角是孤独谱系，这个障碍会大得多。我觉得这个主意很棒，我们试试看吧。"巴斯支持这个想法的另一个原因是，他的姐姐在加州大学洛杉矶分校与孤独谱系群体共事。然而经过几个月的头脑风暴后，斯皮尔伯格为了执导《夺宝奇兵 3：圣战奇兵》而搁置了这个项目。



When it looked like Rain Man was going to be scrapped for good, Barry Levinson, on a roll after Diner, The Natural, and Good Morning, Vietnam, stepped up and agreed to direct the film. He believed that treating the subject of developmental disability in a lighthearted fashion, instead of the somber tone of an after-school special, would give the audience “greater empathy for it in the end.” Finally, all the stars were aligned for autism to make its big-screen debut.

当《雨人》眼看就要彻底搁浅时，巴里·莱文森——在《餐馆》、《天生好手》和《早安越南》之后正春风得意——挺身而出，同意执导这部电影。他认为，以轻松诙谐的方式处理发展障碍这一主题，而非像课后特别节目那样采用阴郁基调，最终能让观众“对其产生更深的共情”。至此，所有条件都已成熟，孤独谱系即将在大银幕上首次亮相。



V



In 1986, Bass and Mutrux paid a visit to Rimland’s office in Kensington, carting out armfuls of books and papers. Hoffman read Temple Grandin’s Emergence and sought out the author, who told him that the one thing she wanted more than anything else in life was for someone to hug her—but the moment that anyone did, she couldn’t bear it. “That sentence just destroyed me,” Hoffman said.

1986 年，巴斯和穆特鲁克斯拜访了里姆兰位于肯辛顿的办公室，搬走了成堆的书籍和文件。霍夫曼阅读了坦普尔·葛兰汀的《浮现》一书，并找到了这位作者。葛兰汀告诉他，她一生中最渴望的事情就是有人能拥抱她——但每当有人真的拥抱她时，她又无法忍受。“那句话彻底击垮了我，”霍夫曼说。



He also made a pilgrimage to Oliver Sacks’s house on City Island, a New England–style hamlet on an island in the Bronx. After visiting one of Sacks’s patients in the hospital, they headed to the New York Botanical Garden, where Hoffman trailed a few yards behind as the neurologist chatted with a member of the actor’s entourage. “Suddenly I thought I heard my patient,” Sacks recalled. “I was extremely startled, and turned round—and saw it was Dustin thinking to himself, but thinking with his body, thinking enactively, thinking of the young autistic man he had just seen.”

他还专程前往城市岛，拜访了奥利弗·萨克斯的住所——那是一座位于布朗克斯区岛屿上的新英格兰风格小村庄。在医院探望了萨克斯的一位病人后，他们前往纽约植物园。当这位神经学家与演员随行人员交谈时，霍夫曼跟在几码之外。"突然我好像听到了我的病人说话，"萨克斯回忆道，"我大吃一惊，转过身——发现那是达斯汀在自言自语，但他用身体在思考，用行动在思考，思考着他刚刚见过的那个年轻孤独谱系患者。"



Rimland also put Mutrux in touch with several parents in his network, including Ruth Christ Sullivan, whose son Joe was featured in Portrait of an Autistic Young Man. When Ruth and her daughter arrived in California for a much-needed vacation, Mutrux sent a car to bring them to the studio. Sullivan felt a heavy weight of responsibility going into the meeting, as if she had to speak for the mothers of autistic children all over the world. But Hoffman, in jeans and tennis shoes, made her feel at ease while asking her to tell story after story about her son.

里姆兰还让穆特鲁克斯联系了他网络中的几位家长，其中包括露丝·克里斯特·沙利文，她的儿子乔曾出现在《一位孤独谱系青年的肖像》中。当露丝和女儿抵达加州享受难得的假期时，穆特鲁克斯派车将她们接到片场。沙利文在会面时感到责任重大，仿佛她必须代表全世界孤独谱系孩子的母亲发声。但穿着牛仔裤和运动鞋的霍夫曼让她感到轻松自在，同时请她一遍又一遍地讲述关于她儿子的故事。



An hour into the meeting, the actor seemed to abruptly withdraw from the conversation. He shifted slightly in his chair as a serious expression came over his face. “Tuh-raaaagedy,” he said—drawing out the vowel, perfectly capturing Joe’s mischievous way of saying one of his favorite words. Sullivan was profoundly moved that the actor had studied his behavior so closely. The scenes of Raymond instantly multiplying large numbers, compulsively lining up salt and pepper shakers, and snapping photographs with a little camera in the car were all based on Joe.

会议进行了一个小时，这位演员似乎突然从对话中抽离出来。他在椅子上微微调整坐姿，脸上浮现出严肃的神情。"悲——剧——"他说道——拖长了元音，完美复刻了乔说起自己最爱词汇时那种顽皮的口吻。萨利文深受触动，这位演员竟如此细致地研究过他的举止。雷蒙德瞬间心算大数字、强迫症般排列调味瓶、在车里用小相机不停拍照的场景，全都源自乔的真实生活。



Peek went down in history as “the real Rain Man” (the title of a book written by his father), but that was a benign white lie that enabled the filmmakers to keep the identity of a second family in Rimland’s network secret. In truth, Raymond was a composite of Joe Sullivan and a young man in New Jersey named Peter Guthrie, whose distinctive shuffling gait, bemused tilt of the head, and verbal tics (“Uh-oh,” “Definitely,” and “Of course”) became central to Hoffman’s conception of the character. While Peek reveled in all the attention he got after the film came out, Peter had no interest in becoming a celebrity. When Mutrux contacted his family, he told his parents, “I don’t want my name becoming a household word. I definitely don’t want my name in USA Today.” But he agreed to be part of Hoffman’s research, and Mutrux lent his brother Kevin a movie camera so he could film Peter at home.

皮克被载入史册，被称为“真正的雨人”（这是他父亲所著一本书的书名），但这其实是一个善意的谎言，让电影制作人得以对里姆兰家族网络中另一个家庭的身份保密。事实上，雷蒙德这个角色融合了乔·沙利文和新泽西州一位名叫彼得·格思里的年轻人，后者独特的拖曳步伐、困惑的歪头动作以及口头禅（“哎呀”、“当然”、“没错”）成为霍夫曼塑造角色的核心。虽然皮克在电影上映后陶醉于所有关注，但彼得对成为名人毫无兴趣。当穆特鲁克斯联系他的家人时，他对父母说：“我不想让自己的名字家喻户晓。我绝对不想让自己的名字出现在《今日美国》上。”但他同意参与霍夫曼的研究，穆特鲁克斯借给他兄弟凯文一台电影摄像机，让他可以在家里拍摄彼得。



Robert and Becky Guthrie fit Kanner’s descriptions of gifted and highly accomplished parents to a T, minus the lack of affection for their children. Robert was a four-star general who served as the Army’s project officer for the launching of the first American satellite, Explorer 1, in 1958. He went on to oversee the development of the Black Hawk helicopter and the Patriot missile. Becky was a first-generation autism “mother warrior”: as president of the northern Virginia chapter of NSAC in the 1970s, she fought for the right of autistic children to a public education at a time when they were ineligible for admittance to mainstream schools. Kevin, a few years younger than Peter, was a college football star who bore more than a passing resemblance to Tom Cruise. After Rain Man, he went on to launch JSTOR, a digital archive of journals and other research materials that now services eight thousand institutions in more than 160 countries.

罗伯特和贝基·格思里完全符合坎纳对天赋异禀且成就卓越父母的描述，唯独缺少对子女的冷漠。罗伯特是四星上将，1958 年曾担任美国首颗卫星"探险者 1 号"发射项目的陆军军官，后来还负责黑鹰直升机和爱国者导弹的研发工作。贝基则是第一代孤独症"母亲战士"：20 世纪 70 年代担任 NSAC 北弗吉尼亚分会主席期间，她为孤独谱系儿童争取公立教育权利而奋斗——当时这些孩子还没有资格进入主流学校就读。比彼得小几岁的凯文曾是大学橄榄球明星，长相与汤姆·克鲁斯颇为相似。在《雨人》之后，他创办了 JSTOR——这个收录期刊及其他研究资料的数字档案库，如今已为 160 多个国家的八千家机构提供服务。



The Guthries suspected that Peter was different from their other kids when he was just a few months old. When he glanced at his mother, she felt that he was looking straight through her. Several doctors diagnosed him as severely retarded, but just before he turned two, as his brothers and sisters unwrapped their gifts on Christmas morning, Peter reached for a magnetic letter board and spelled out Esso, Grecian Bread, and Smirnoff Vodka. Soon he was assembling jigsaw puzzles upside down, drawing maps of the United States to scale freehand, and cutting letters of identical width out of construction paper without a ruler. He communicated with his parents by spelling out words rather than saying them, like “C-h-e-e-r-i-o-s.” (For two years, he ate nothing but Cheerios.) A child psychiatrist at Walter Reed Army Medical Center finally diagnosed him with autism.

格思里夫妇在彼得仅几个月大时就察觉到他与其他孩子不同。当他看向母亲时，她感觉他的目光仿佛穿透了她。几位医生诊断他为重度智力障碍，但就在他快两岁前的圣诞节早晨，当兄弟姐妹们拆开礼物时，彼得伸手拿起磁性字母板，拼出了"埃索"、"希腊面包"和"斯米诺夫伏特加"。不久后，他能倒着拼装拼图，徒手按比例绘制美国地图，还能不用尺子从手工纸上剪出等宽的字母。他与父母交流时更倾向于拼写单词而非说出来，比如"C-h-e-e-r-i-o-s"（整整两年，他只吃家乐氏麦片）。最终，沃尔特·里德陆军医疗中心的儿童精神病学家诊断他患有孤独症。



Instead of treating Peter’s passions for letters, numbers, and order as pathological, Becky encouraged them. By age ten, he had taught himself Cyrillic using a pocket dictionary; he was eventually able to read, write, and speak French, Arabic, Hebrew, Spanish, and Old English. When his father was stationed in Tokyo, Peter became fascinated with the statistics of sumo wrestling. For years after the family returned to the States, he kept track of matches in Japanese newspapers, copying the results into the archive of spiral notebooks and manila folders that filled his bedroom. In addition to being a walking database of sports history and an impressive calendar calculator, he memorized Billboard’s record-sales charts going back to the 1950s. A decade before most people thought about buying a personal computer, he was using one to cut down the clutter in his room.

贝基没有将彼得对字母、数字和秩序的痴迷视为病态，反而鼓励他发展这些兴趣。十岁时，他借助袖珍词典自学了西里尔字母；最终能读、写、说法语、阿拉伯语、希伯来语、西班牙语和古英语。当父亲驻守东京时，彼得迷上了相扑运动的统计数据。全家返回美国后的数年里，他仍持续追踪日本报纸上的相扑赛事，将结果抄录在堆满卧室的螺旋笔记本和马尼拉文件夹中。除了堪称体育史活数据库和惊人的日历计算器外，他还熟记了可追溯至 20 世纪 50 年代的《公告牌》唱片销量排行榜。在大多数人考虑购买个人电脑的十年前，他就已开始用电脑整理房间的杂物。



Hoffman and Cruise met the Guthrie brothers at the Carlisle Hotel in Manhattan on Valentine’s Day, 1987. Though Peter, like Peek, prepped himself for meeting the actors by memorizing every detail of the actors’ filmographies, he wasn’t good at recognizing faces. When Cruise stuck out his hand, Peter asked him, “What’s your first name?” Then he said the same thing to Hoffman. After a stiff two-hour meeting, Kevin said to the actors, “You know, if you really want to see Peter relaxed, you have to go bowling with him. He loves to bowl.”

1987 年情人节，霍夫曼和克鲁斯在曼哈顿的卡莱尔酒店见到了格思里兄弟。虽然彼得和皮克一样，通过背诵两位演员所有电影作品的细节来为会面做准备，但他并不擅长认脸。当克鲁斯伸出手时，彼得问他："你叫什么名字？"接着又对霍夫曼问了同样的话。经过两小时拘谨的会面后，凯文对两位演员说："要知道，如果你们真想看到彼得放松的样子，就该和他一起去打保龄球。他特别爱打保龄球。"



A few days later, the brothers met up with Hoffman and Cruise at Bowlmor Lanes in Union Square. As the actors engaged in a heady discussion of dramatic strategy for their roles, Peter worked the lanes. When it was Cruise’s turn, he would shout, “Top Gun, Top Gun, you’re up!”

几天后，兄弟俩与霍夫曼、克鲁斯在联合广场的鲍尔莫保龄球馆碰面。当两位演员热烈讨论各自角色的戏剧表现策略时，彼得专注地打着球道。每当轮到克鲁斯投球时，他就会喊道："《壮志凌云》，《壮志凌云》，该你上场了！"



The process of helping Hoffman develop the character, and generally being regarded with respect by people other than his family, had a beneficial effect on Peter. “People began treating him more seriously—as more than just this bizarre guy,” Kevin recalls. “He became more willing to be social. I saw him reach inside himself and pull out emotional responses that I didn’t know he was capable of. He enjoyed showing off what he could do.”

帮助霍夫曼塑造角色的过程，以及除了家人之外普遍受到他人尊重，对彼得产生了积极影响。“人们开始更认真地对待他——不再仅仅把他当作一个古怪的人，”凯文回忆道，“他变得更愿意社交。我看到他深入内心，挖掘出连我都不知道他具备的情感反应。他很享受展示自己的能力。”



To ensure that the dialogue in the film rang true, Hoffman called Kevin regularly to read him the day’s scenes and ask him what his brother would say. Mutrux also kept Ruth Sullivan on speed dial.
