The climactic scene in which Raymond is freaked out by a blaring smoke detector—the pivotal moment when Charlie realizes that his brother would have to go back to the institution—was based on her description of Joe’s reaction to a fire in a wastebasket. “All of that stuff came from me making calls ten minutes before we started shooting,” Mutrux recalls.

为了确保电影中的对话真实可信，霍夫曼经常打电话给凯文，朗读当天的场景，并询问他哥哥会说什么。穆特鲁克斯还把露丝·沙利文的号码设为快捷拨号。高潮场景中，雷蒙德被刺耳的烟雾报警器吓到——这是查理意识到哥哥必须回到疗养院的关键转折点——正是基于她对乔在垃圾桶着火时反应的描述。“所有这些内容都来自我们在开拍前十分钟打的电话，”穆特鲁克斯回忆道。



The one major way that the film departed from real life was that Joe Sullivan and Peter Guthrie—like Bill Sackter and Kim Peek—were fully capable of living outside of institutions with the help of their families. In fact, it’s highly unlikely that any of them would have developed their impressive skills and abilities had they been condemned to a place like Wallbrook, the institution depicted in the film. Peter lived in his own apartment in Princeton with a roommate, shopped and cooked for himself, managed a bank account, and regularly took the train to see his parents in Virginia. For the past four decades, he has quietly worked as a reference librarian at the university. Joe had never lived in an institution, because his parents fought hard to make a space for him in the community.

电影与现实生活最大的不同在于，乔·沙利文和彼得·格思里——就像比尔·萨克特和金·匹克一样——在家人帮助下完全有能力在机构外生活。事实上，如果他们被囚禁在电影中描绘的沃尔布鲁克那样的机构，他们几乎不可能发展出令人惊叹的技艺与能力。彼得与室友合住在普林斯顿的公寓里，自己购物做饭、管理银行账户，还定期乘火车去弗吉尼亚探望父母。过去四十年间，他一直在大学图书馆默默担任参考咨询馆员。乔从未在机构生活过，因为他的父母竭尽全力为他在社区中争取了一席之地。



But Mutrux’s experts were adamant that few autistic people would be able to survive outside institutions. “The ‘happy ending’ in the original script is simply not realistic,” Wisconsin psychiatrist Darold Treffert, the world’s leading expert on savant syndrome, wrote in his book Islands of Genius. “There is no six-day cross-country cure for autism.” Though Rimland never considered putting his son, Mark, in an institution, he insisted that state homes like Wallbrook were the only appropriate places to house people like Raymond Babbitt.

但穆特鲁克斯的专家们坚持认为，只有极少数孤独谱系群体能够在机构之外生存。“原剧本中的‘幸福结局’根本不现实，”威斯康星州精神病学家、世界顶尖学者达罗德·特雷弗特在其著作《天才岛屿》中写道，“孤独症无法通过六天的跨州旅行治愈。”尽管里姆兰从未考虑过将儿子马克送入机构，但他坚持认为像沃尔布鲁克这样的州立机构是安置雷蒙德·巴比特这类人的唯一合适场所。



Ironically, while Raymond was widely referred to in the press as “high-functioning” and “one of the lucky ones” when the film came out, he was portrayed as less capable of living independently than any of the real-life models on which his character was based. Levinson—who made an uncredited appearance in the film as Raymond’s psychiatrist—insisted that the poignancy of him going back to Wallbrook would be more dramatically satisfying for the audience. Though Morrow made sure that Sackter never had to go back to Faribault, he has made peace with the ending of Rain Man. “I felt betrayed politically, but artistically, it was a triumph,” he says.

讽刺的是，尽管电影上映时媒体普遍称雷蒙德为“高功能患者”和“幸运儿之一”，但他被描绘成比其角色原型中任何真实人物都更缺乏独立生活能力的人。莱文森——他在片中客串出演雷蒙德的精神科医生（未署名）——坚持认为雷蒙德回到沃尔布鲁克的辛酸结局更能满足观众的戏剧期待。尽管莫罗确保萨克特再也不用回到法里博，但他已与《雨人》的结局达成和解。“从政治立场上我感到被背叛，但从艺术角度而言，这无疑是一次胜利，”他说。



—



THE FILM’S SUCCESS was not at all assured in the weeks leading up to its release. Responses at test screenings were mixed because audiences were so unfamiliar with autism. (“Why doesn’t the little guy just snap out of it?” one viewer wrote.) Ruth Sullivan talked Hoffman into giving Rain Man a sneak preview in Huntington as a benefit for the Autism Services Center (ASC) there two nights before its official New York premiere. This gala event took place in a grand old vaudeville showcase called the Keith-Albee Theater, and tickets sold out far in advance, enabling the ASC to buy its first piece of property—Pelican House, the group home where her son still resides. Hoffman introduced the movie by saying:

电影上映前的几周，其成功前景并不明朗。试映反响褒贬不一，因为观众对孤独症极为陌生。（一位观众写道：“那小家伙怎么就不能振作起来呢？”）露丝·沙利文说服霍夫曼在亨廷顿为《雨人》举办一场预映，作为当地孤独症服务中心的筹款活动，时间定在纽约首映前两晚。这场盛会在名为基思-阿尔比剧院的古老杂耍剧场举行，门票早早售罄，使孤独症服务中心得以购置其第一处房产——鹈鹕之家，即她儿子至今仍居住的集体之家。霍夫曼在介绍电影时说：



We just made a film that will play for a month or two, or whatever, in cities around the world, and be put out on cassette and put on shelves and seen once. But you people have Joe in your community for the rest of your life, and I would take that any day of the week . . . When I first looked at that footage [of Joe in Portrait of an Autistic Young Man] I said, “I love that man.” And I love you for making him a part of your community.

我们刚拍了一部电影，它会在世界各地的城市上映一两个月，然后被制成录像带，放在架子上，被人看一次。但你们这些人却要终生与乔这样的孤独谱系人士共处社区，我宁愿选择后者……当我第一次看到（《一位孤独谱系青年的肖像》中）乔的影像时，我说：“我爱那个人。”我也爱你们，因为你们让他成为社区的一部分。



Then he took a seat behind Joe to observe his reactions. Ruth recalls, “Joe told me that he especially liked the parts about him, like the scene where Raymond eats cheese puffs with toothpicks.” Only on her second viewing did Ruth notice that Charlie ate his cheese puffs with toothpicks too—a subtle tribute to the ways that the families of autistic children learn to adapt to their behavior.

随后他在乔身后坐下，观察他的反应。露丝回忆道：“乔告诉我，他特别喜欢电影里关于他的部分，比如雷蒙德用牙签吃奶酪泡芙的场景。”直到第二次观看时，露丝才注意到查理也用牙签吃奶酪泡芙——这是对孤独谱系儿童家庭如何学会适应其行为方式的微妙致敬。



Leading critics took issue with the film in ways that said more about prevailing views of autism than they did about Rain Man. Richard Schickel of Time compared it favorably to the usual “disease-of-the-month TV movie” while admiring Hoffman’s portrayal of a “truly hopeless case” who had only two options: being committed to an institution or becoming “a kind of living pull toy for his brother, flapping and clacking in his wake.” Pauline Kael was equally scathing in the New Yorker, writing that she left the theater feeling “stupefied.”

主流影评人对这部电影的批评，更多反映了当时对孤独症的普遍看法，而非《雨人》本身的问题。《时代》杂志的理查德·希克尔将其与通常的“每月疾病电视电影”相提并论，同时赞赏霍夫曼演绎了一个“真正无望的病例”——这个角色只有两种选择：被送进精神病院，或成为“他弟弟身边一个活生生的牵线玩偶，在他身后扑腾作响”。宝琳·凯尔在《纽约客》上的批评同样尖锐，她写道自己离开影院时感到“目瞪口呆”。



But audiences embraced the film, which went on to gross nearly $355 million worldwide, making it one of the most financially successful Hollywood releases of all time. In addition to winning Oscars for Best Picture, Best Actor in a Leading Role, Best Director, and Best Screenplay, Rain Man earned a slew of other honors and distinctions, including two Golden Globes and a People’s Choice award. It even spawned its own fandom. Handmade posters sprang up on walls all over Tokyo when the movie opened there, and when the oak trees in front of the Kentucky convent that served as the Wallbrook exterior set were cut down in 2007, devotees gathered to reenact Raymond’s liberation from the hospital.

但观众们却对这部电影青睐有加，其全球票房最终接近 3.55 亿美元，成为好莱坞历史上最成功的商业片之一。除了斩获奥斯卡最佳影片、最佳男主角、最佳导演和最佳剧本奖外，《雨人》还赢得了包括两项金球奖和一项人民选择奖在内的众多荣誉与殊荣。它甚至催生了专属粉丝群体。电影在东京上映时，手工海报贴满了全城的墙壁；2007 年，肯塔基修道院前作为沃布鲁克外景地的橡树被砍伐时，粉丝们聚集在那里，重演雷蒙德从医院获释的场景。



Morrow got his first glimpse of the phenomenon he had wrought by reading a letter from a mother shortly after the film opened. She explained that taking her son shopping was an ordeal because he almost inevitably had a meltdown, and other mothers would chastise her for having such an out-of-control child. Recently, however, when a woman skewered her with a withering look at the market, she asked her: “Did you see Rain Man?”

莫罗首次意识到自己引发的现象，是在电影上映后不久读到一位母亲的来信。她写道，带儿子购物对她而言是一场煎熬，因为孩子几乎总会情绪崩溃，而其他母亲会责备她管教无方。然而最近，当一位女士在超市用严厉的目光瞪她时，她问道：“你看过《雨人》吗？”



“Oh yes,” the woman replied, “I loved that movie.”

“哦，是的，”那位女士回答，“我很喜欢那部电影。”



“Well, my son Johnnie is like Raymond Babbitt,” the mother said.

“嗯，我儿子约翰尼就像雷蒙德·巴比特一样，”那位母亲说道。



The other woman’s face softened. “Oh, Johnnie,” she said. “Do you have autism? I understand.”

另一位女性的表情柔和下来。“哦，约翰尼，”她说，“他有孤独谱系障碍吗？我理解。”



Phyllis Terri Gold, one of NSAC’s co-founders, told Hoffman that her mother had refused to even acknowledge the existence of her son to her friends until she saw the film. The parents of another boy described in a letter how on the way home from the theater their son, who rarely spoke, declared proudly, “I’m autistic!” By putting one autistic person on the screen, the filmmakers had made innumerable others visible—to their loved ones, to their neighbors, to their teachers and doctors, and to themselves.

NSAC 的联合创始人之一菲利斯·泰瑞·戈尔德告诉霍夫曼，她的母亲在看过这部电影之前，甚至拒绝向朋友们承认自己儿子的存在。另一个男孩的父母在信中描述道，在从电影院回家的路上，他们那个很少说话的儿子骄傲地宣布：“我是孤独谱系人士！”通过将一位孤独谱系人士呈现在银幕上，电影制作者让无数其他孤独谱系人士变得可见——对他们的亲人、邻居、老师、医生，以及他们自己而言。



Rimland’s phone began ringing off the hook. One call came from a man in his forties named Jerry Newport who had spent his life wondering why he never felt at home among other people. As a little boy, he discovered that he could add columns of four-digit numbers and factor square roots in his head. At first, he used these skills to impress his classmates, but he ended up feeling like a sideshow freak. Unable to find a job after college, he drove a cab for twenty years, eventually becoming so depressed that he tried to commit suicide. Then he saw Rain Man and immediately recognized himself on the screen. Rimland referred him to UCLA for a diagnosis.

里姆兰德的电话开始响个不停。其中一通电话来自一位四十多岁的男子杰里·纽波特，他一生都在困惑为何自己从未在人群中感到自在。童年时，他发现自己能心算四位数数列的加法，还能分解平方根。起初他用这些技能博取同学的惊叹，最终却感觉自己像个马戏团怪胎。大学毕业后找不到工作，他开了二十年出租车，最终抑郁到试图自杀。直到他看了《雨人》，立刻在银幕上认出了自己。里兰德将他推荐到加州大学洛杉矶分校进行诊断。



The character of Raymond Babbitt made autism recognizable and familiar even to those who had no personal connection to the subject. In his promotional tour, Hoffman made it a point to portray the condition in universal human terms. At a press conference in New York City, the actor broke down crying, saying that the film “touches something in us that I can’t explain. We all go through life not hugging quite as much as we’d like to. Something cuts us off . . . We’re always keeping a lid on our own autism.” Soon, Ruth Sullivan was fielding calls from parents in England, France, Japan, Italy, Sweden, and Australia, seeking practical strategies for getting groups like NSAC off the ground.

雷蒙德·巴比特这个角色让孤独症变得可识别且熟悉，即使是对此毫无亲身经历的人也不例外。在宣传巡演中，霍夫曼刻意用普世的人类语言来诠释这种状况。在纽约的一场新闻发布会上，这位演员当场落泪，称这部电影"触动了我们内心某种难以言说的东西。我们一生都在渴望更多拥抱，却总是不够尽兴。某种东西将我们隔绝……我们始终压抑着自己的孤独谱系。"很快，露丝·沙利文就接到了来自英国、法国、日本、意大利、瑞典和澳大利亚的家长电话，他们寻求如何像 NSAC 这样的组织从零起步的实际策略。



An unprecedented surge of interest in autism rippled outward through mainstream media. “It seems that Rain Man has stimulated almost every newspaper and magazine in the country to run an article” on the subject, Rimland observed. He was only slightly exaggerating. In the year before the film came out, fewer than a hundred stories on autism had been published in major newspapers in the United States. The following year, that number quadrupled. It would never decline again. After Hoffman thanked Peter Guthrie at the Academy Awards ceremony, the Washingtonian ran an in-depth story on him called “Dustin and Me.” (He had warmed to the idea of his name becoming a household word.) Inevitably, Peter was presented as the exception to the rule, the rarest of the rare, one of the lucky ones who was able to live a “fairly normal life,” which was “virtually unheard of among autistic people.”

主流媒体对孤独症的关注空前高涨。里姆兰观察到："《雨人》似乎刺激了全国几乎每家报纸杂志都刊登相关文章。"他这话只是略微夸张。电影上映前一年，美国主流报纸刊登的孤独症相关报道不足百篇。次年这个数字翻了四倍，此后从未回落。当霍夫曼在奥斯卡颁奖典礼上感谢彼得·格思里时，《华盛顿人》杂志对他进行了深度报道，题为《达斯汀与我》（他已欣然接受自己成为家喻户晓的名字）。不出所料，彼得被塑造成特例中的特例——极少数能过上"相当正常生活"的幸运儿，这种案例"在孤独谱系群体中几乎闻所未闻"。



People ran a spread on Joe Sullivan that described his mother’s fight for his education. The handsome, soft-spoken young man went on to make appearances on Oprah and The Larry King Show, performing feats of lightning calculation for the wide-eyed hosts. In 1993, the Disney Company added a feature on Joe to the multimedia presentation “Frontiers of Medicine” at the Epcot Center, seen by more than a million people a year.

人们曾为乔·沙利文撰写专题报道，描述他母亲为争取其教育权利所做的斗争。这位英俊温和的年轻人后来登上奥普拉和拉里·金秀的舞台，在主持人惊叹的目光中展现闪电心算的绝技。1993 年，迪士尼公司在艾波卡特中心的"医学前沿"多媒体展示中加入了乔的专题介绍，每年有超过百万人观看。



Soon other autistic characters began to infiltrate the popular imagination. Within months of Rain Man’s release, Ann Martin published Kristy and the Secret of Susan, the thirty-second installment of The Baby-Sitters Club, one of the biggest-selling series of young adult books in history. Susan’s “secret” was autism, and while she wasn’t much of a character—doing little to advance the plot but flap her hands and make life difficult for her mother—the book was notable for portraying autism in terms that even a twelve-year-old could understand.

不久后，其他孤独谱系角色开始渗透进大众想象。《雨人》上映仅数月，安·马丁便出版了《克里斯蒂与苏珊的秘密》——这是史上最畅销的青少年系列小说《保姆俱乐部》的第三十二部。苏珊的"秘密"正是孤独谱系，虽然这个角色塑造得并不丰满——除了拍手和给母亲制造麻烦外，对剧情推进毫无贡献——但这本书的独特之处在于，它用连十二岁孩子都能理解的方式描绘了孤独谱系。



—



A FEW MONTHS AFTER the film opened, Ruth Sullivan attended a family wedding in Pittsburgh. Only aunts and uncles of the bride and groom were invited to the rehearsal dinner, so Joe was on his own for supper in a strange city, which normally would have been a cause of great anxiety for her.

电影上映几个月后，露丝·沙利文在匹兹堡参加了一场家庭婚礼。只有新郎新娘的叔伯姑姨受邀参加彩排晚宴，所以乔得独自在陌生城市解决晚餐——这通常会让露丝极度焦虑。



Ruth asked the hotel doorman if he could help her son find a place to eat close by, adding that because Joe was autistic, he might not seem to be listening to his directions. The doorman’s eyes lit up. “Like Rain Man!” he said. She watched as the two men crossed the street into a world that had been transformed in a very short time. “One film did that. One film did more for autism than all of us working together worldwide had been able to do in twenty-five years,” she says.

露丝请酒店门童帮儿子在附近找个吃饭的地方，并补充说因为乔患有孤独谱系，他可能看起来没在听指示。门童眼睛一亮："就像《雨人》！"她看着两个男人穿过街道，走进一个在极短时间内被改变的世界。"一部电影做到了。一部电影为孤独谱系所做的贡献，比我们全球所有人在二十五年里共同努力做到的还要多。"她说。



But Rain Man was just the beginning.

但《雨人》仅仅是个开始。







---


NeuroTribes








Ten

十



PANDORA’S BOX

潘多拉魔盒



It’s a question of diagnosis.

这是一个关于诊断的问题。



—LORNA WING

——洛娜·温



While autism was rapidly assimilating into mainstream awareness in the wake of Rain Man, a strategic series of revisions to the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, prompted by Lorna Wing and her colleagues in London, were chipping away at Kanner’s monolithic edifice from the inside.

在《雨人》上映后，孤独症迅速进入主流视野，而与此同时，由伦敦的洛娜·温及其同事推动的《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》一系列战略性修订，正从内部逐步瓦解坎纳那坚不可摧的理论大厦。



It was only because of these revisions that children like Tyler Bell were able to get their initial diagnoses of PDD-NOS, one of several new flavors of autism spectrum disorder added to the manual in 1994, along with Asperger’s syndrome. This was, of course, precisely what Lorna had in mind when she undertook her campaign to recast the DSM criteria: to make it possible for children who would have been excluded from support services before to get them. By the end of the decade, however, the startling rise in diagnoses—and the alarms in the media that autism had become an epidemic—took even Lorna by surprise. “Since the publication of my paper on Asperger’s work,” she admitted, “I have felt like Pandora after she opened the box.”

正是因为这些修订，像泰勒·贝尔这样的孩子才得以首次被诊断为 PDD-NOS——这是 1994 年手册新增的几种孤独谱系障碍亚型之一，与阿斯伯格综合征并列。这当然正是洛娜发起 DSM 标准修订运动时的初衷：让此前被排除在支持服务之外的儿童能够获得这些服务。然而到 90 年代末，诊断数量的惊人增长——以及媒体关于孤独症已成为流行病的警报——连洛娜本人都始料未及。"自从我发表关于阿斯伯格研究的论文以来，"她坦言，"我一直感觉自己就像打开潘多拉魔盒后的潘多拉。"



—



AUTISM MADE ITS DEBUT in the first edition of the bible of psychiatry, the DSM-I, in 1952, as “schizophrenic reaction, childhood type.” This condition was unhelpfully defined only by what it was not: “The clinical picture may differ from schizophrenic reactions occurring in other age periods because of the immaturity and plasticity of the patient at the time of onset of the reaction.” What this clinical picture looked like in human terms was left to the clinician’s imagination.

孤独症首次亮相于 1952 年出版的《精神疾病诊断与统计手册》第一版（DSM-I），被归类为“精神分裂症反应，儿童型”。这种病症的定义毫无助益，仅通过否定形式描述：“由于患者在发病时的发育不成熟和可塑性，其临床表现可能与其他年龄阶段的精神分裂症反应有所不同。”至于这种临床表现具体呈现为何种人类行为，则完全留给临床医生自行想象。



The original impetus for creating a standardized guide of diagnostic nomenclature for psychiatrists was war. Before the 1940s, the only such guide was the Statistical Manual for the Use of Hospitals for Mental Diseases, designed for use by the staff of large institutions to aid in the collection of clinical data. But Veterans Administration (VA) psychiatrists found this guide of little help in diagnosing and treating the problems of the young men returning from Europe and Asia traumatized by what they had seen. Veterans unable to shake off memories of bombed-out cities and starved bodies in concentration camps ended up being diagnosed as having “psychopathic” or “psychoneurotic” personalities, because they were the only labels available.

为精神科医生制定一套标准化诊断术语指南的最初动力源于战争。在 20 世纪 40 年代之前，唯一的此类指南是《精神病院统计手册》，供大型机构工作人员使用，以协助收集临床数据。但退伍军人管理局的精神科医生发现，这本指南在诊断和治疗从欧洲和亚洲返回的、因所见所闻而受到创伤的年轻男性时几乎毫无帮助。那些无法摆脱被炸毁的城市和集中营中饥饿尸体记忆的退伍军人，最终被诊断为具有“精神病态”或“精神神经症”人格，因为这是当时唯一可用的标签。



The DSM-I added a couple of alternate categories to the list—“gross stress reaction” and “adult situational reaction”—so these young men could receive VA benefits without being branded psychopaths for life. The notion that the successors to this modest 132-page document (crammed with terms like vagabondage, urge to say words, and homosexual panic, acute) would someday be employed to determine a child’s access to an education, behavioral therapy, insurance reimbursement, and other essential services would have seemed unlikely.

DSM-I 在清单中增加了几个备选类别——“严重应激反应”和“成人情境反应”——这样这些年轻人就能获得退伍军人事务部的福利，而无需终身背负精神变态的标签。这本仅有 132 页的朴素手册（其中充斥着流浪癖、说话冲动、急性同性恋恐慌等术语）的后续版本，有朝一日竟会被用来决定一个孩子能否获得教育、行为治疗、保险报销及其他基本服务，这在当时看来似乎不太可能。



The description of “schizophrenia, childhood type” in the DSM-II, published in 1968, when Bettelheim was the rage, was more specific, but in the wrong direction, citing “autistic, atypical and withdrawn behavior” and “general unevenness” as evidence of a “failure to develop identity separate from the mother’s.” If this description was still vague, and the theory behind it was nonsense, the DSM’s impact was still limited. Few copies of the slim second volume, about the same length as its predecessor, found their way outside the walls of asylums, where it was used to provide convenient labels for patients by superintendents and ward supervisors who—in increasing numbers—often lacked a medical degree themselves.

1968 年出版的《精神疾病诊断与统计手册》第二版（DSM-II）中，对"儿童型精神分裂症"的描述更为具体，却走向了错误的方向。该手册将"孤独谱系、非典型及退缩行为"和"整体发展不均衡"视为"未能建立与母亲分离的独立自我"的证据。如果说这种描述仍显模糊，其背后的理论更是无稽之谈，那么 DSM 的影响力在当时依然有限。这本薄薄的第二卷手册篇幅与前身相当，鲜少流传到精神病院围墙之外。在医院里，越来越多的主管和病房监督员——他们中许多人甚至没有医学学位——用它来为患者贴上便利的标签。



By contrast, the DSM-III, published in 1980, was designed by the APA’s Robert Spitzer with a more sweeping mission in mind: saving psychiatry itself from extinction. By that point, the forces amassed against the profession were powerful and various, including disgruntled academic researchers, well-connected insurance lobbyists, and a rising phalanx of “anti-psychiatry” groups like the Insane Liberation Front. (A significant boost to this movement was provided in 1975 by another Hollywood blockbuster: One Flew over the Cuckoo’s Nest.) The pressures exerted by these groups—and the idiosyncratic mind of Spitzer himself—reframed the DSM in a way that reinvented psychiatry as the front end of the pharmaceutical industry rather than the arcane art of soul healing, akin to shamanism, that it had been.

相比之下，1980 年出版的《精神障碍诊断与统计手册（第三版）》由美国精神病学协会的罗伯特·斯皮策主导设计，其使命更为宏大：拯救精神病学免于消亡。彼时，反对该学科的势力已集结成强大而多元的阵营，包括心怀不满的学术研究者、人脉广泛的保险业说客，以及如"精神解放阵线"般日益壮大的"反精神病学"团体。（1975 年另一部好莱坞大片《飞越疯人院》为这场运动注入了强劲动力。）这些团体施加的压力——加之斯皮策本人特立独行的思维——重塑了《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》，使精神病学从类似萨满教般晦涩的灵魂疗愈技艺，转变为制药工业的前沿阵地。



The key word in Spitzer’s mind as he undertook the revision in 1974 was reliability—the ability to produce consistent, replicable results. It was an open secret that two patients presenting with the same complaints in two psychiatrists’ offices might end up diagnosed with different disorders. This flexibility, so to speak, was built into the system, reflecting the enduring influence of Kanner’s mentor, Adolf Meyer. For Meyerians, atypical behavior was merely the superficial manifestation of an underlying “reaction” caused by the patient’s struggle to adapt to a particular life situation. It was the psychiatrist’s job to arrive at an understanding of this situation by interpreting symptoms and probing into the patient’s background, using the tools of whatever theoretical school the therapist subscribed to. The first two editions of the DSM were designed to sit unobtrusively beside the monumental tomes of Freud, Otto Rank, Alfred Adler, and other master cartographers of the psyche.

斯皮策在 1974 年着手修订时，脑海中浮现的关键词是可靠性——即产生一致、可重复结果的能力。两个患者带着相同症状走进不同精神科医生的诊室，最终可能被诊断为不同疾病，这已是公开的秘密。这种所谓的灵活性被刻意植入系统，折射出坎纳的导师阿道夫·迈耶的持久影响。在迈耶学派看来，异常行为不过是患者为适应特定生活情境而挣扎时，其潜在"反应"的表层显现。精神科医生的职责在于通过解读症状、探究患者背景，运用其所属理论流派的工具来理解这种情境。DSM 前两版的设计初衷，正是要谦逊地安放在弗洛伊德、奥托·兰克、阿尔弗雷德·阿德勒等心灵地图绘制大师的煌煌巨著之侧。



But Spitzer was less tolerant than his predecessors of approaches to therapy that promised much and delivered little in the way of practical improvement in patients’ lives. As a resident at the Columbia Center for Psychoanalytic Training and Research, Spitzer had an unimpressive experience attempting to treat patients with psychoanalysis. “I was always unsure that I was being helpful,” he said. “I don’t think I was uncomfortable listening and empathizing—I just didn’t know what the hell to do.” He became obsessed with the problem of diagnostic unreliability and developed a software program for computer-assisted diagnosis called DIAGNO in 1965, when few psychiatrists had even seen a computer.

但斯皮策对于那种承诺很多、却对患者生活实际改善甚微的治疗方法，容忍度远低于他的前任。在哥伦比亚精神分析培训与研究中心担任住院医师期间，斯皮策尝试用精神分析法治疗患者，经历并不理想。“我始终不确定自己是否真的帮到了他们，”他说，“倾听和共情对我来说并不难——我只是根本不知道该做什么。”他对诊断不可靠的问题愈发执着，并于 1965 年开发了一款名为 DIAGNO 的计算机辅助诊断软件程序，而当时几乎没有精神科医生见过计算机。



By the 1970s, his frustration was widely shared, and much of the blame was put on the DSM. Researchers were frustrated by descriptions of conditions like “inadequate personality,” “social maladjustment,” and “other neurosis” (with symptoms that included writer’s cramp) that were so ill-defined and context-dependent that there was little hope of uncovering empirical proof that they even existed. Powerful drugs like chlorpromazine were proving more effective than talk therapy in pacifying “difficult” and “agitated” patients, but pharmaceutical companies saw few blockbuster opportunities in targeting afflictions like “hysterical neurosis” and “adjustment reaction of adolescence” (described as “irritability and depression associated with school failure and manifested by temper outbursts, brooding and discouragement”).

到了 20 世纪 70 年代，他的挫败感已成为普遍现象，而《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》（DSM）则成了众矢之的。研究人员对诸如"人格缺陷"、"社会适应不良"和"其他神经症"（症状包括书写痉挛）等病症的描述感到沮丧——这些定义模糊且依赖具体情境，以至于几乎不可能找到实证证据证明它们确实存在。像氯丙嗪这样的强效药物在安抚"难缠"和"躁动"的患者方面，比谈话疗法更有效，但制药公司发现，针对"歇斯底里性神经症"和"青少年适应反应"（被描述为"与学业失败相关的易怒和抑郁，表现为脾气爆发、沉思和沮丧"）等疾病，几乎没有什么重磅药物的机会。



Payment for psychotherapy was increasingly becoming the responsibility of insurance companies and the federal Medicaid program, and decision makers were understandably wary of pouring stockholders’ and taxpayers’ money into fishing expeditions. Time on the couch wasn’t easily translatable into spreadsheet terms for cost-benefit analysis; even the traditional bond of confidentiality between client and therapist was seen as a barrier to accountability, the buzzword du jour in discussions of mental health care on Capitol Hill. “Compared to other types of [medical] services there is less clarity and uniformity of terminology concerning mental diagnoses, treatment modalities, and types of facilities providing care,” the vice president of Blue Cross, Robert J. Laur, said in 1975. “One dimension of this problem arises from the latent or private nature of many services; only the patient and the therapist have direct knowledge of what services were provided and why.” Senator Jacob Javits agreed: “Unfortunately, I share a congressional consensus that our existing mental health care delivery system does not provide clear lines of clinical accountability.”

心理治疗的支付责任正逐渐转移到保险公司和联邦医疗补助计划上，决策者们对将股东和纳税人的钱投入无果的探索持谨慎态度，这完全可以理解。躺在沙发上的治疗时间很难转化为电子表格中的成本效益分析数据；就连患者与治疗师之间传统的保密关系，也被视为问责制的障碍——这是当时国会山讨论心理健康护理时最流行的术语。蓝十字副总裁罗伯特·J·劳尔在 1975 年表示："与其他类型的医疗服务相比，心理诊断、治疗方式和护理设施类型的术语缺乏明确性和统一性。这个问题的一个方面源于许多服务的隐蔽性或私密性；只有患者和治疗师直接了解提供了什么服务以及为什么提供。"参议员雅各布·贾维茨对此表示赞同："不幸的是，我与国会多数人的共识一致，即我们现有的心理健康护理服务体系未能提供清晰的临床问责制。"



For decades, psychoanalytic pundits like Bettelheim had occupied an exalted place in American culture akin to secular priests, but psychologists and social workers were making significant inroads into the APA’s client base. What value did a medical degree add if psychiatry wasn’t really medicine?

几十年来，像贝特尔海姆这样的精神分析权威在美国文化中占据着类似世俗神父的崇高地位，但心理学家和社会工作者正在显著蚕食美国心理学会的客户基础。如果精神病学并非真正的医学，那么医学学位又能增添什么价值呢？



Meanwhile, the very raison d’être of the DSM was under attack by apostates and heretics like Thomas Szasz, psychiatrist-author of such popular books as The Manufacture of Madness, who declared that mental illness was a myth, brutally employed to police the bounds of socially acceptable behavior. “Our adversaries are not demons, witches, fate, or mental illness,” he wrote in 1960. “We have no enemy whom we can fight, exorcise, or dispel by ‘cure.’ What we do have are problems in living—whether these be biologic, economic, political, or sociopsychological.” These critics found an unexpectedly sympathetic ear in Spitzer, who had written about the stigmatizing effect of labels in reference to schizophrenia and played a key role in eroding psychiatry’s aura of infallibility by leading the task force behind the abrupt “delisting” of homosexuality as a mental illness in the DSM-II’s seventh printing in 1974.

与此同时，DSM 存在的根本理由正受到托马斯·萨斯等异端分子的攻击。这位精神病学家兼《疯狂制造》等畅销书作者宣称，精神疾病是一个神话，被残酷地用来约束社会可接受行为的边界。"我们的对手不是恶魔、女巫、命运或精神疾病，"他在 1960 年写道，"我们没有可以通过'治愈'来对抗、驱除或消除的敌人。我们真正面对的是生活中的问题——无论是生物学、经济学、政治学还是社会心理学层面的。"这些批评者在斯皮策那里意外获得了共鸣。斯皮策曾撰文论述标签对精神分裂症患者的污名化影响，并在 1974 年领导工作组将同性恋从 DSM-II 第七版中突然"除名"，从而削弱了精神病学不容置疑的权威光环。



Spitzer’s strategy was to ground his revamped field guide to mental illness in as much empirical research as possible. He formed twenty-five committees to develop detailed descriptions of disorders in each category, favoring psychiatrists who saw themselves primarily as scientists rather than clinicians. These committee members came to be known as DOPs: “data-oriented people.” Clinicians with nonmedical backgrounds were included only after the basic framework had been established. (An APA oversight committee had to step in and insist that he engage more psychoanalysts in the process.) Spitzer’s overall goal was to finally “operationalize” the DSM criteria—to make them mission-critical for clinical practitioners and researchers while aligning them with the standards of the International Classification of Diseases (ICD), the diagnostic manual used in most of the world.

斯皮策的策略是尽可能以实证研究为基础，重塑他修订的精神疾病诊断指南。他组建了二十五个委员会，为每个类别的疾病制定详细描述，并优先选择那些自视为科学家而非临床医生的精神科医生。这些委员会成员后来被称为 DOP（"数据导向人士"）。非医学背景的临床医生只有在基本框架确立后才被纳入。（美国精神病学协会的一个监督委员会不得不介入，坚持要求他在过程中吸纳更多精神分析师。）斯皮策的总体目标是最终将《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》的标准"操作化"——使其对临床从业者和研究人员具有关键指导意义，同时与全球多数地区使用的诊断手册《国际疾病分类》的标准保持一致。



Not surprisingly, Spitzer was a confirmed DOP himself. He labored over the DSM-III for six years, often working seventy or eighty hours a week in relative isolation even when he was sitting in a crowded conference room.

不出所料，斯皮策本人也是一名坚定的 DOP。他耗时六年编纂《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》第三版，即便身处拥挤的会议室，也常常每周独自工作七十到八十个小时。



His uncanny ability to glide above the fray was associated with a certain personal remoteness. Around Columbia, where Spitzer was a professor of psychiatry, he became infamous for never saying hello to anyone, for failing to recognize colleagues’ faces, for sometimes not even acknowledging the presence of those speaking directly to him, and for striding down busy corridors paying no heed to anyone. For a man who spearheaded the creation of the most detailed map of psychological states ever created, he didn’t seem to pick up on other people’s internal states very well. He found it difficult to adopt their perspectives, even in relatively trivial matters like buying a gift for a colleague.

他那种超然于纷争之上的奇特能力，与他个人的疏离感息息相关。在斯皮策担任精神病学教授的哥伦比亚大学，他因从不与人打招呼、认不出同事的面孔、有时甚至无视直接与他交谈的人的存在，以及大步流星地穿过拥挤的走廊却对任何人视而不见而声名狼藉。作为开创有史以来最详细心理状态图谱的领军人物，他似乎并不擅长察觉他人的内心状态。即便是在为同事挑选礼物这类相对琐碎的事情上，他也难以站在别人的角度思考。



His primary criterion for signing off on the adoption of a new diagnosis was that it worked in the context of the whole: “Whether it fit in. The main thing was that it had to make sense. It had to be logical.” This Spock-like approach won Spitzer few friends but enabled him to unburden psychiatry of baggage it had been lugging around since turn-of-the-century Vienna.

他批准采纳新诊断的主要标准是，它必须在整体语境中行之有效：“是否契合整体。关键在于它必须合理，必须符合逻辑。”这种斯波克式的作风让斯皮策朋友寥寥，却使他得以卸下精神病学自世纪之交的维也纳以来一直背负的重担。



In short, while Spitzer’s eccentricities may have fallen short of meeting the criteria for Asperger’s syndrome, the DSM-III was the product of a mind that exhibited many classic qualities of autistic intelligence. These traits enabled Spitzer to get the job done with a minimum of fretting about offending various sectors of the profession. Calling him an “idiot savant of diagnosis,” Spitzer’s colleague Allen Frances, who went on to chair the task force that created the DSM-IV, observed, “He doesn’t understand people’s emotions. He knows he doesn’t. But that’s actually helpful in labeling symptoms. It provides less noise.”

简而言之，尽管斯皮策的古怪行为可能未达到阿斯伯格综合征的诊断标准，但 DSM-III 的诞生恰恰源于一种展现出许多典型孤独谱系智慧特质的心智。这些特质使斯皮策能够以最低程度的顾虑完成工作，不必担心冒犯业内不同群体。斯皮策的同事艾伦·弗朗西斯——后来担任 DSM-IV 编制工作组主席——称他为"诊断领域的白痴天才"，并评论道："他不理解他人的情感。他自己也清楚这一点。但这反而有助于症状分类，因为干扰因素更少。"



—



THE INCLUSION OF “INFANTILE AUTISM” in the DSM-III, published in 1980, marked Kanner’s moment of triumph. At last, his “unique syndrome” was lifted out of the swamp of schizophrenia, establishing it as the core of a new category of “pervasive developmental disorders.” Autism was framed narrowly in terms of his two cardinal signs: “pervasive lack of responsiveness to other people” coupled with “resistance to change.” The age of onset was specified as “before 30 months,” in keeping with his theory that his syndrome was present from the start, which ruled out virtually all the kids who would later be diagnosed with Asperger’s syndrome.

1980 年出版的《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》第三版（DSM-III）将“婴儿孤独症”纳入其中，标志着坎纳的胜利时刻。终于，他提出的“独特综合征”从精神分裂症的泥沼中脱离出来，成为“广泛性发育障碍”这一新类别的核心。孤独症被严格界定为他的两个核心特征：“对他人普遍缺乏反应”以及“抗拒变化”。发病年龄被限定为“30 个月之前”，这符合他关于该综合征自出生即存在的理论，从而几乎排除了所有后来被诊断为阿斯伯格综合征的儿童。



Most importantly, the checklist of clinical features that had to be present before making the diagnosis—including “gross deficits of language development” and “bizarre responses to the environment”—was nonnegotiable. Every single feature was required, as Kanner would have demanded. (In technical terms, the checklist was monothetic, describing a class alleged to be identical in every salient aspect.) The description of autism also noted that it was “apparently more common in the upper socioeconomic classes”—an accurate description of the families in Kanner’s referral network, if nothing else.

最重要的是，在做出诊断前必须满足的临床特征清单——包括“语言发展的严重缺陷”和“对环境的怪异反应”——是不可商量的。每一项特征都必须具备，正如坎纳所要求的那样。（用专业术语来说，这份清单是单维的，描述了一个据称在所有显著方面都相同的类别。）对孤独症的描述还指出，它“显然更常见于社会经济地位较高的阶层”——这至少准确描述了坎纳转诊网络中的家庭情况。



Each of these qualifications increased the likelihood that autism would forever remain as Kanner had described it: a rare disorder. Furthermore, the word infantile guaranteed that it would continue to be viewed primarily as a condition of early childhood. For the mature Temple Grandins of the world, the only diagnosis on offer was “Infantile Autism, Residual State”—an awkward kluge invented to describe people who met the criteria for the full syndrome in infancy and still manifested “oddities of communication and social awkwardness.”

这些限定条件中的每一项都增加了孤独症永远如坎纳所描述的那样成为一种罕见障碍的可能性。此外，“婴儿期”这个词确保了它将继续主要被视为一种幼儿期的状况。对于世界上那些成熟的坦普尔·格兰丁们来说，唯一可用的诊断是“婴儿期孤独症，残留状态”——这是一个为描述那些在婴儿期符合完整综合征标准、但仍表现出“沟通异常和社交尴尬”的人而发明的笨拙拼凑。



To accommodate kids who suffered a loss of skills after thirty months, there was “Childhood Onset Pervasive Developmental Disorder” (COPDD) marked by “lack of appropriate social responsivity” (which was vague enough), “inappropriate clinging” (along with “asociality”—clearly a mixed bag), “hyper- or hypo-sensitivity to sensory stimuli” (covering all the bases), and “insistence on doing things in the same manner every time.” COPDD was described as being even rarer than autism, which was not surprising, considering that it was also characterized by “bizarre” fantasizing and preoccupation with “morbid” thoughts and interests. (Can calendar dates, multiplication, chemistry, and the weather be considered morbid?) In actual practice, few clinicians bothered with the ill-conceived diagnosis. One clinic reported only a single child meeting the criteria for COPDD in five years.

为了容纳那些在三十个月后丧失技能的儿童，出现了“儿童期起病广泛性发育障碍”（COPDD），其特征包括“缺乏适当的社会回应能力”（这定义足够模糊）、“不恰当的依恋行为”（以及“社交回避”——显然是个混杂概念）、“对感官刺激过度或过低敏感”（涵盖所有可能性），以及“坚持每次以相同方式行事”。COPDD 被描述为比孤独症更为罕见，这并不令人意外，因为它还以“怪异”的幻想和对“病态”想法与兴趣的沉迷为特征。（日历日期、乘法、化学和天气能算作病态吗？）在实际操作中，很少有临床医生会费心采用这个构思欠妥的诊断。一家诊所报告称，五年内仅有一名儿童符合 COPDD 的诊断标准。



On the whole, however, Spitzer’s reinvention of the DSM was a hit that succeeded far beyond the APA’s expectations. Compared to its svelte, spiral-bound predecessors, it was a gargantuan tome, with descriptions of 265 mental disorders (as opposed to DSM-II’s 182) sprawling across 494 pages—nearly four times longer than the previous edition. Its very heft communicated authority. DSM-III “looks very scientific,” Spitzer recalled. “If you open it up, it looks like they know something.”

总体而言，斯皮策对《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》的革新大获成功，其影响力远超美国精神病学会的预期。与先前轻便的螺旋装订版本相比，这部巨著以 494 页的篇幅描述了 265 种精神障碍（而 DSM-II 仅收录 182 种），篇幅几乎是前一版的四倍。其厚重的体量本身就传递着权威性。DSM-III"看起来非常科学"，斯皮策回忆道，"翻开它，你会觉得他们确实掌握着真知灼见。"



Soon, everyone would know something. The readership of the new edition went far beyond the usual crew of hospital superintendents and nosological data wonks. Psychiatrists who never thought about giving the DSM a second glance became very interested, glimpsing in it a road map to their own economically viable futures (which led straight to Big Pharma). It became de rigueur reading for psychologists, educators, social workers, prison administrators, drug developers, judges, insurance underwriters, government officials, service providers, and virtually everyone involved in health care and research.

很快，所有人都将有所了解。新版手册的读者群远不止医院主管和疾病分类学专家。那些从未对 DSM 多看一眼的精神科医生开始产生浓厚兴趣，从中窥见了通往自身经济前景的路线图（这条道路直指大型制药公司）。它成为心理学家、教育工作者、社会工作者、监狱管理者、药物研发人员、法官、保险承保人、政府官员、医疗服务提供者，以及几乎所有医疗保健和研究领域从业者的必读书目。



Spitzer had done more than revise a manual. He had elevated psychiatry to new prominence in the national conversation, academia, and the research enterprise. The DSM-III became an international best seller, making “an unbelievable amount of money for the APA,” said Spitzer. In the coming years, sales of the supersized DSM—and a whole cottage industry of related merchandise, including “pocket guides”—would become a cash cow for the formerly struggling organization.

斯皮策所做的远不止修订一本手册。他将精神病学提升到了全国对话、学术界和研究领域的新高度。《精神障碍诊断与统计手册（第三版）》成为国际畅销书，为美国精神病学协会带来了"难以置信的巨额收入"，斯皮策说道。在随后的几年里，这本超大篇幅的 DSM 手册——以及包括"袖珍指南"在内的相关衍生品产业——成为了这个曾经举步维艰的组织的摇钱树。



—



THOUGH FEW PEOPLE OUTSIDE the APA knew it at the time, the DSM-III had a dark secret. For a document created by DOPs, much of the data behind it was sketchy and provisional. Allen Frances later admitted that “there was very little scientific evidence available to guide” the decision making of Spitzer’s committees. Nowhere was that more evident than in the description of the pervasive developmental disorders, with its weird hodgepodge of vagueness (“music of all kinds may hold a special interest for the child”) and overspecificity (the arbitrary cutoff point between infantile autism and COPDD).

尽管当时美国精神病学协会之外很少有人知道，但《精神障碍诊断与统计手册（第三版）》隐藏着一个黑暗的秘密。作为由精神科医生编写的文件，其背后的大量数据都粗糙且具有临时性。艾伦·弗朗西斯后来承认，"几乎没有科学证据可以指导"斯皮策各委员会做出决策。这一点在广泛性发育障碍的描述中表现得最为明显——其中混杂着模糊不清的表述（"各种音乐都可能引起孩子的特殊兴趣"）和过度具体化的内容（婴儿孤独症与儿童期全面发育障碍之间武断的划分界限）。



The popularity of the DSM-III—particularly with regard to autism—was brief and distinguished by a chorus of complaints from clinicians who found the criteria difficult to apply in practice. To prepare for the next revision, Spitzer appointed three of the smartest clinician-scientists in the field to review the literature and draft an improved set of criteria: Lorna Wing and two American psychologists, Lynn Waterhouse and Bryna Siegel. A task force was formed to refine their drafts and conduct field tests. The fruits of this labor were published in 1987, in the next major revision of the manual, the DSM-III-R.

DSM-III 的流行——尤其是在孤独症领域——十分短暂，其特点是临床医生们纷纷抱怨标准难以实际应用。为准备下一次修订，斯皮策任命了该领域三位最聪明的临床科学家来审阅文献并起草改进版标准：洛娜·温以及两位美国心理学家林恩·沃特豪斯和布莱娜·西格尔。一个工作组随之成立，负责完善草案并开展实地测试。这项工作的成果于 1987 年发表，即该手册的下一次重大修订版 DSM-III-R。



This edition was even longer and more ambitious than its predecessor, adding twenty-seven new disorders and seventy-three pages of description to its taxonomy of misery. The changes in the criteria for the pervasive developmental disorders were bold and comprehensive, reflecting the depth of cognitive research that had been going on in London while Bettelheim blathered on about Nazi mothers on American talk shows.

这一版本比前作篇幅更长、野心更大，在其痛苦分类学中新增了 27 种障碍和 73 页描述。对广泛性发育障碍标准的修改大胆而全面，反映了伦敦正在进行深度认知研究——而此时贝特尔海姆还在美国脱口秀上喋喋不休地谈论纳粹母亲。



The word infantile was finally gone for good, and Kanner’s syndrome was rechristened “autistic disorder,” which was understood to persist from the cradle (or shortly thereafter) to the grave. The age-of-onset cutoff was modified to a suggestion that the clinician take note of when the signs first appeared, while the notion of a “residual state” was dispensed with entirely. The COPDD diagnosis was also dropped.

“婴儿期”一词终于彻底消失，坎纳综合征被重新命名为“孤独症障碍”，人们认为这种障碍从摇篮（或稍晚些）持续到坟墓。发病年龄的截止标准被修改为建议临床医生注意症状首次出现的时间，而“残留状态”的概念则被完全摒弃。COPDD 诊断也被取消了。



Crucially, the nonnegotiable checklist had been replaced by a veritable banquet of options for the diagnostician to pick and choose from: “At least eight of the following sixteen items are present, these to include at least two items from A, one from B, and one from C.” This ensured that fewer children would slip through the diagnostic net because they failed to exhibit one behavior or another on evaluation day. The descriptions of these behaviors were also made less absolute. In the A list, for example, Kanner’s “pervasive lack of responsiveness to other people” became Wing’s “qualitative impairment in reciprocal social interaction.” It was left to the clinician to decide whether the degree of impairment was sufficient to make the diagnosis. Items on the B list encompassed a similarly vast expanse of terrain, from having “no mode of communication” whatsoever (including an utter absence of facial expression and gesturing) to making “frequent irrelevant remarks (for example, starts talking about train schedules during a conversation about ports).” Likewise, C-list items—describing a “restricted repertoire of activities”—ranged from “hand flicking or twisting, spinning, [or] head-banging” to “amassing facts about meteorology.” It’s hard to imagine another disorder composed of such seemingly oxymoronic extremes.

关键在于，那份不可妥协的核对清单已被替换为诊断者可以自由选择的丰盛选项菜单：“以下十六项中至少出现八项，且必须包含 A 类至少两项、B 类至少一项、C 类至少一项。”这确保了更少的孩子会因为在评估日未能表现出某种行为而漏诊。这些行为的描述也不再那么绝对。例如，在 A 类清单中，坎纳提出的“普遍缺乏对他人的回应”变成了温格的“互惠社交互动中的质性损伤”。临床医生需自行判断损伤程度是否足以做出诊断。B 类清单中的项目同样涵盖了广阔的范围，从完全没有沟通方式（包括完全缺乏面部表情和手势）到“频繁发表无关言论（例如，在讨论港口的对话中突然开始谈论火车时刻表）”。同样，C 类项目——描述“受限的兴趣活动模式”——从“手部拍打或扭动、旋转、撞头”到“收集气象学事实”不等。很难想象还有哪种障碍会包含如此看似矛盾的两极。



One thing that is immediately obvious is that the new criteria could be applied to a much larger and more diverse population than the criteria in the DSM-III. A nonspeaking boy of six who rocked in a corner all day would fill the bill, as would a woman in her late twenties who reflexively averted her eyes when speaking and calmed herself by knitting while inwardly fancying herself the real-life equivalent of Sarah Jane Smith on Doctor Who. The potential for the DSM-III-R triggering a significant rise in diagnoses was not lost on Wing and her colleagues. Indeed, their field trials had already shown this to be the case. Later studies confirmed that the revised criteria were better at picking up cases of autism at every level of ability, including children who would have been diagnosed only with “mental retardation” in previous generations. Wing and company had done their job well.

一个显而易见的事实是，新标准比 DSM-III 的标准适用范围更广、覆盖人群更多样。一个整天在角落里摇晃的六岁非口语男孩符合标准，一个二十多岁、说话时下意识移开目光、通过编织来平复情绪、内心幻想自己是《神秘博士》中莎拉·简·史密斯现实翻版的女性也同样符合。DSM-III-R 可能引发诊断率显著上升的潜在影响并未被温及其同事忽视。事实上，他们的实地试验已经证明了这一点。后续研究证实，修订后的标准能更好地识别各个能力水平的孤独谱系案例，包括在上一代只会被诊断为“智力障碍”的儿童。温及其团队的工作卓有成效。



But there was a sleeper in the new criteria that refused to behave the way they anticipated: “Pervasive Developmental Disorder—Not Otherwise Specified.” Basically, PDD-NOS was subthreshold autism, but with the rituals, intense focus, and repetitive behavior à la carte. (“Some people with this diagnosis,” the DSM advised, “will exhibit a markedly restricted repertoire of activities and interests, but others will not.”) Based on their field trials and additional research, the task force made the reasonable assumption that PDD-NOS would remain a humble footnote to the primary label. Instead, it turned out to be wildly popular, quickly eclipsing autistic disorder to become the most commonly used PDD diagnosis. Like Asperger’s syndrome, it was an autism diagnosis that didn’t contain the word autism and thus was more readily accepted by parents and health care workers.

但在新标准中，有一个诊断类别出乎意料地不受控制，那就是"未特定的广泛性发育障碍"。简单来说，PDD-NOS 属于阈下孤独症，但伴有可选的仪式化行为、高度专注和重复性特征。（《诊断手册》指出："部分被诊断为该症的患者会表现出明显的活动与兴趣范围受限，但另一些患者则不会。"）基于实地试验和补充研究，工作组合理推测 PDD-NOS 只会作为主要诊断标签的次要补充。然而事实恰恰相反，这个诊断类别迅速走红，很快超越孤独症障碍成为最常用的 PDD 诊断。与阿斯伯格综合征类似，它虽属孤独谱系诊断却不含"孤独症"字眼，因此更容易被家长和医护人员接受。



On the front lines, clinicians played fast and loose with the labels anyway. Judy Rapoport, former chief of child psychiatry at the NIMH, told anthropologist Roy Richard Grinker, “I am incredibly disciplined in the diagnostic classifications in my research, but in my private practice, I’ll call a kid a zebra if it will get him the educational services I think he needs.”

在前线，临床医生们对诊断标签的使用相当随意。美国国家精神卫生研究所（NIMH）前儿童精神病学主任朱迪·拉波波特告诉人类学家罗伊·理查德·格林克：“我在研究中严格遵循诊断分类标准，但在私人执业时，如果称一个孩子为‘斑马’能让他获得我认为他需要的教育服务，我就会这么做。”



The DSM-III-R was an even bigger hit than the previous edition. Over the course of six years and eighteen printings, half a million copies of DSM-III were sold—an unheard-of number by DSM standards—but the DSM-III-R sold 280,000 copies in its first two years alone.

《精神障碍诊断与统计手册（第三版修订版）》（DSM-III-R）比前一版更受欢迎。在六年内，《DSM-III》经过十八次印刷，售出五十万册——按 DSM 的标准来看，这是闻所未闻的数字——而《DSM-III-R》仅在前两年就售出了二十八万册。



There was some hemming and hawing at the APA about the “fuzzy boundaries” of Wing’s criteria, but they were clearly an improvement over the last batch, so these concerns were deferred until the next edition. By the end of the process, autism had been transformed into something that Kanner would have barely recognized. And Wing wasn’t finished yet.

美国精神病学协会（APA）内部对温氏标准“模糊的边界”有些犹豫不决，但这些标准显然比上一版有所改进，因此这些担忧被推迟到下一版再处理。到这一过程结束时，孤独症已被改造成坎纳几乎认不出的模样。而温的工作尚未结束。



II



Estimates of autism prevalence began to increase worldwide after the publication of the DSM-III and DSM-III-R. To Wing and her Swedish colleague Christopher Gillberg, this was no surprise: awareness of autism among professionals was dramatically increasing at the same time that the boundaries of the condition were expanded. The new numbers reflected the estimates realigning themselves with the reality of the spectrum.

在 DSM-III 和 DSM-III-R 出版后，全球范围内的孤独谱系障碍发生率估算开始上升。对于温及其瑞典同事克里斯托弗·吉尔伯格而言，这并不意外：专业人士对孤独症的认识急剧提升，同时该病症的界定范围也在扩大。新的数据反映了估算结果正与孤独谱系的实际状况重新对齐。



Support for this theory was emerging in a handful of studies undertaken in the wake of Wing and Gould’s survey in Camberwell. The resulting estimates varied widely, depending on the scope of the survey, but the overall trend was clear: the more recent the criteria employed, the higher the estimate turned out to be. “Autism spectrum disorders (i.e., autism and autistic-like conditions) might be as prevalent as 1 in 100 children,” Wing and Gillberg ventured. “Autism should no longer be conceptualized as an extremely rare disorder . . . The higher prevalence rate needs to be communicated to administrators, service providers and boards of research funds so that appropriate resources may be allocated.” But many medical professionals and childcare specialists didn’t get the memo.

支持这一理论的证据，在温与古尔德于坎伯韦尔进行调查后开展的少数研究中逐渐浮现。根据调查范围的不同，得出的估算结果差异很大，但总体趋势是明确的：采用的诊断标准越新，估算的患病率就越高。温与吉尔伯格大胆提出："孤独症谱系障碍（即孤独症及类似孤独症的状况）的患病率可能高达每 100 名儿童中就有 1 例。孤独症不应再被视为极其罕见的障碍……需要将更高的患病率告知行政管理者、服务提供方和研究基金委员会，以便分配适当的资源。"但许多医疗专业人士和儿童保育专家并未接收到这一信息。



One of the first clinicians in the world to raise the alarm about the rising numbers was Martin Bax, an unusually colorful pediatrician in London who founded a magazine of avant-garde art, poetry, and erotica called Ambit. (J. G. Ballard, Ralph Steadman, and David Hockney were regular contributors.) In the 1970s he had written a dystopian novel called The Hospital Ship about a global outbreak of psychosis that resulted in scores of children becoming autistic. By 1994, he became fearful that his apocalyptic vision was coming true.

最早对孤独症病例数量上升发出警报的临床医生之一，是伦敦一位风格特立独行的儿科医生马丁·巴克斯。他创办了一本名为《Ambit》的前卫艺术、诗歌与情色杂志（J.G.巴拉德、拉尔夫·斯特德曼和大卫·霍克尼都是其常驻撰稿人）。1970 年代，他创作了一部反乌托邦小说《医院船》，讲述了一场全球性精神病爆发导致大量儿童患上孤独症的故事。到 1994 年，他开始担忧自己笔下的末日预言正在成真。



“Rates of autism appear to be rising in the Western world,” Bax alerted the readers of Developmental Medicine and Child Neurology. How did he know this? Because he had been “wandering around both in Europe and in North America asking colleagues whether they are seeing more cases; anecdotally the answer has always been ‘yes.’” As further evidence of a frightening increase, Bax noted that registration of cases of autism with the Family Fund—a provider of grants to low-income families raising disabled children in the United Kingdom—“have recently gone up year by year.”

"西方世界的孤独症发病率似乎正在上升，"巴克斯在《发育医学与儿童神经学》期刊上向读者发出警示。他是如何得知这一情况的？因为他"在欧洲和北美各地走访时询问同行是否发现更多病例；根据传闻，答案始终是'是的'。"作为发病率惊人增长的另一佐证，巴克斯指出，英国为抚养残疾儿童的低收入家庭提供补助的"家庭基金"中登记的孤独症病例"近年来逐年递增"。



On that point, Bax was entirely correct. Between 1990 and 2000, cases of autism in the Family Fund database went up by an astonishing 22 percent on average each year. By the end of the decade, autism-related conditions accounted for a quarter of the disabilities among families of children age sixteen and younger receiving grants—up from a mere 5 percent in 1990. What the devil was going on? Referring to himself as an “outsider” to autism research, Bax didn’t get into subtle issues of nosology and epidemiology in his editorial, turning his attention instead to a colleague’s theory that, in some cases, autism is “wholly or partly the expression of early-life onset” of bipolar disorder, another condition allegedly on the rise.

在这一点上，巴克斯完全正确。1990 年至 2000 年间，家庭基金数据库中记录的孤独症病例数量以每年平均 22%的惊人速度增长。到十年末，与孤独症相关的疾病已占 16 岁及以下儿童家庭所获补助金中残疾案例的四分之一——而 1990 年这一比例仅为 5%。这究竟是怎么回事？巴克斯自称是孤独症研究的“局外人”，在社论中并未深入探讨诊断分类与流行病学的微妙问题，而是将注意力转向一位同事的理论：在某些案例中，孤独症“完全或部分表现为双相情感障碍的早发性症状”——而双相情感障碍据称也在呈上升趋势。



In fact, what was going on was precisely what Wing and Gillberg said was going on, concluded PricewaterhouseCoopers, the auditing giant that undertook a comprehensive analysis of the Family Fund database for the U.K. Department of Education and Skills. The apparent increase in autism and related conditions among U.K. schoolchildren receiving grants was almost certainly the “result of better recognition,” they said, while “improved diagnosis and recognition have resulted in increased numbers of children reporting specific disabilities.”

事实上，正如温与吉尔伯格所言，普华永道这家审计巨头为英国教育与技能部对家庭基金数据库进行全面分析后得出结论：英国学龄儿童中接受补助的孤独症及相关病症患者数量明显增加，几乎可以确定是"认知提升的结果"，同时"诊断与识别手段的改进，导致报告患有特定残疾的儿童数量增加"。



A major change in referral patterns was also under way in England that was guaranteed to produce a spike in autism diagnoses that would never level off again. Before the 1970s, most kids with learning disabilities were admitted to special schools, vocational training centers, and institutions without being referred to a specialist for a specific diagnosis. By the 1990s, however, referral to a specialist before applying for services had become the rule rather than the exception. Contrary to Bax’s framing of his anecdata as “depressing,” the uptick in the numbers at the Family Fund was a sign that the system was finally working.

英格兰的转诊模式也发生了重大变化，这必然导致孤独症诊断数量激增且再也不会回落。20 世纪 70 年代前，大多数有学习障碍的儿童被送入特殊学校、职业培训中心或收容机构，无需经专科医生确诊。然而到 90 年代，申请服务前转诊至专科医生已成为常规操作。巴克斯将这种个人观察描述为"令人沮丧"，但家庭基金数据的增长恰恰表明，这套体系终于开始发挥作用了。



A similar evolution was taking place in the United States, prompted by a set of amendments to the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act—the new name for the Education for All Handicapped Children Act that Ruth Sullivan, Becky Guthrie, and other NSAC parents had fought so hard for fifteen years earlier. In 1991, autism was included in IDEA as its own category of disability for the first time, which enabled children with a diagnosis to gain access to individualized instruction and other services. The effects of this change rippled outward nationally, motivating clinicians to apply the diagnosis more readily and increasing awareness of autism among schoolteachers and staff. The new IDEA rules also required schools to comply by reporting an annual count of the number of children being served to the Department of Education. Autism was finally coming out of the statistical shadows at the federal level.

美国也发生了类似的演变，这源于《残障个体教育法案》的一系列修正案——该法案是十五年前露丝·沙利文、贝基·古思里等 NSAC 家长奋力争取的《全体残障儿童教育法案》的新名称。1991 年，孤独症首次被纳入该法案，成为独立的残障类别，这使得确诊儿童能够获得个性化教学及其他服务。这一变革的影响在全国范围内扩散，促使临床医生更积极地使用该诊断，并提高了学校教师及工作人员对孤独症的认识。新法案还要求学校遵守规定，每年向教育部报告接受服务的儿童人数。孤独症终于从联邦层面的统计阴影中走了出来。



In tandem with IDEA’s promise of a “free and appropriate public education” for all, state legislators passed laws making public funds available to families for early-intervention therapy, under pressure from parents encouraged by Lovaas’s claims that forty hours of ABA a week could prompt full recovery. Only the wealthiest families could afford forty hours a week of one-on-one intervention without financial assistance, and widespread fears that parents could “miss the window” in their child’s development when behavioral training would be effective suggested that there was no time to waste. In an era when the standard prognosis for autism was life in an institution, clinicians felt an ethical obligation to provide a diagnosis as early in their young patients’ lives as possible.

与 IDEA 承诺为所有人提供“免费且适当的公共教育”同步，各州立法者在家长的压力下通过了法律，使公共资金可用于家庭的早期干预治疗。这些家长受到洛瓦斯主张的鼓舞——他认为每周四十小时的 ABA 疗法可促使完全康复。没有经济援助，只有最富裕的家庭才能负担每周四十小时的一对一干预，而普遍存在的“家长可能错过孩子行为训练有效窗口期”的担忧，暗示着时间不容浪费。在那个孤独症的标准预后是终身住进收容机构的时代，临床医生感到有道德义务，在年轻患者生命早期尽可能早地提供诊断。



Simultaneously, the first standardized clinical instruments to screen for autism were becoming widely available. Before the 1980s, autistic kids were generally considered “untestable” in America. Psychiatrists diagnosed them on the basis of whatever concepts were in vogue in their school of psychiatry. The same child might be diagnosed with early infantile autism by one clinician, by another with schizophrenia, and by a third with minimal brain damage. (And children who were black or poor were likely to end up classified as mentally retarded.) This was precisely the problem Spitzer set out to solve by “operationalizing” the DSM, but without a set of tools for diagnosis and assessment, the revised criteria provided just another outline of behavior framed in terms of deficits and impairments.

与此同时，首批用于筛查孤独症的标准化临床工具开始广泛普及。在 20 世纪 80 年代之前，美国的孤独症儿童通常被视为"无法测试"。精神科医生根据各自学派流行的概念对他们进行诊断。同一个孩子可能被一位临床医生诊断为早期婴儿孤独症，被另一位诊断为精神分裂症，又被第三位诊断为轻微脑损伤。（而黑人儿童或贫困儿童则很可能被归类为智力障碍。）这正是斯皮策试图通过"操作化"《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》来解决的问题，但若缺乏一套诊断与评估工具，修订后的标准不过是另一套以缺陷和损伤为框架的行为描述。



The first attempt to develop and popularize such a tool was Rimland’s E-1 behavioral checklist and its successor, the E-2. While his lists were effective in encouraging parents that their son’s or daughter’s condition could finally be understood by a compassionate clinician, they had serious methodological flaws in practice. They were entirely dependent on parental recall rather than direct clinical observation, and a child’s score could vary widely depending on which parent filled in the checklist. Independent analyses of the validity of the data produced highly uneven results. This work was hampered by the fact that Rimland refused to publish his scoring key; as far as other researchers were concerned, his algorithm was a black box.

首次尝试开发并推广此类工具的是里姆兰德的 E-1 行为检查表及其后续版本 E-2。虽然他的检查表有效鼓励了家长——让他们相信富有同理心的临床医生终于能理解子女的状况，但在实际应用中存在严重的方法论缺陷。这些检查表完全依赖家长回忆而非直接临床观察，且孩子的得分会因填写检查表的家长不同而产生巨大差异。对数据有效性的独立分析得出了极不均衡的结果。由于里姆兰德拒绝公开评分标准，这项研究受到阻碍；对其他研究者而言，他的算法就像个黑箱。



There were several attempts over the years to come up with assessment tools that were more reliable and versatile than Rimland’s checklists, but the big breakthrough didn’t come until 1980, when Eric Schopler and his TEACCH colleagues introduced the Child Autism Rating Scale (CARS), which was particularly good at distinguishing autism from other forms of developmental delay, such as intellectual disability. After observing the child engage in a structured interaction through a one-way mirror, the rater scored the child on a seven-point continuum along several dimensions, including verbal and nonverbal communication, interaction with people and objects, sensory responsiveness, intellectual functioning, bodily movement, adaptation to change, and so on. By rating behaviors along a scale of severity, CARS anticipated the spectrum model of autism in the DSM-III-R. Independent analyses demonstrated that the scale was highly reliable and consistent, and that its scores matched well with assessment by other means. Best of all, new raters could be trained in a single one-hour session.

多年来，人们多次尝试开发比里姆兰检查表更可靠、更通用的评估工具，但直到 1980 年才取得重大突破。当时埃里克·肖普勒及其 TEACCH 团队推出了儿童孤独症评定量表（CARS），该量表在区分孤独症与其他发育迟缓类型（如智力障碍）方面表现尤为出色。评估者通过单向镜观察儿童参与结构化互动后，从七个维度对儿童进行七级评分，包括言语与非言语沟通、与人及物品的互动、感官反应、智力功能、身体动作、适应变化能力等。通过按严重程度对行为进行分级评估，CARS 预见了 DSM-III-R 中孤独症谱系模型的雏形。独立分析表明，该量表具有高度可靠性和一致性，其评分结果与其他评估方法高度吻合。最理想的是，新评估者仅需一小时的培训即可掌握使用方法。



CARS also provided an accurate picture of the child’s strengths, which was crucial for developing an appropriate plan for his or her education. Schopler believed that an approach to autism that took into account strong rote-memory capabilities and enhanced visual-processing skills would result in not only more effective teaching but more accurate neurological research. In 1988, Schopler and his colleagues issued a second edition of CARS that was even easier to use. After reading the manual and watching a thirty-minute video, medical students, speech-language pathologists, and special-education teachers could produce ratings that were nearly as accurate as those of seasoned clinical observers. Additionally, the new version of CARS could be employed to diagnose teenagers and adults. As a result, it became wildly popular, far beyond what Schopler and his colleagues expected.

CARS 还准确描绘了孩子的优势能力，这对制定合适的教育计划至关重要。肖普勒认为，若针对孤独症采用兼顾强大机械记忆能力与增强视觉处理能力的干预方法，不仅能提升教学效果，还能推动更精准的神经科学研究。1988 年，肖普勒与同事发布了更易操作的 CARS 第二版。医学生、言语病理学家和特教教师只需阅读手册并观看三十分钟视频，就能给出与资深临床观察者几乎同样准确的评分。此外，新版 CARS 还可用于诊断青少年和成人患者。因此，该量表迅速风靡，其普及程度远超肖普勒及其同事的预期。



Diagnosing autism was no longer the exclusive domain of a small, elite network of specialists. At the historical moment that autism was poised to enter mainstream awareness, reliable tools to screen for it—and to distinguish it from other forms of disability—were made available on a mass scale. The demand for diagnoses and the clinical means of meeting that demand were perfectly calibrated.

诊断孤独症不再只是少数精英专家网络的专属领域。在孤独症即将进入主流视野的历史时刻，可靠的筛查工具——以及将其与其他形式的残障区分开来的工具——被大规模推广使用。对诊断的需求与满足这一需求的临床手段达到了完美契合。



Then, six months after Rain Man opened, an international team of researchers led by Catherine Lord and Michael Rutter introduced a comprehensive tool for assessing problems with communication, social interaction, and play in children between ages five and twelve called the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule (ADOS). Based on the criteria that would appear in the upcoming DSM-IV—which stretched the spectrum even more broadly—the ADOS and a companion tool called the Autism Diagnostic Interview were instantly embraced as the long-awaited gold standard of autism assessment. A series of revisions to both were introduced shortly thereafter to extend their reach to infants, teenagers, and adults. As word got around, parents began showing up for their appointments carrying voluminous notebooks of observations—the equivalent of Beaman Triplett’s thirty-three-page letter to Kanner about Donald. But this time, clinicians welcomed parents’ input; collaboration was now seen as essential to the process.

随后，《雨人》上映六个月后，由凯瑟琳·洛德和迈克尔·鲁特领导的国际研究团队推出了一套综合评估工具，用于评估 5 至 12 岁儿童在沟通、社交互动和游戏方面的问题，名为《孤独症诊断观察量表》（ADOS）。基于即将出版的《精神障碍诊断与统计手册（第四版）》中更广泛扩展的谱系标准，ADOS 及其配套工具《孤独症诊断访谈》迅速被奉为期待已久的孤独症评估黄金标准。此后不久，这两项工具经过一系列修订，适用范围扩展至婴幼儿、青少年和成人。随着消息传开，家长们开始带着厚厚的观察笔记前来就诊——这堪比比曼·特里普利特写给坎纳关于唐纳德的那封 33 页长信。但这一次，临床医生欢迎家长的参与；协作如今被视为评估过程中不可或缺的一环。



The clinical population was changing enormously, but clinicians’ estimations of what their young patients would be capable of in the future had barely changed at all. “Fifty percent of the autistic population are mute and remain that way all of their lives,” one author declared in a 1994 anthology for professionals called Autism in Children and Adults. “Even high-IQ autistic adolescents sustain only rudimentary social relationships and seem to retain the characteristic lack of empathy and the shallow affect,” another author claimed.

临床人群正在发生巨大变化，但临床医生对年轻患者未来能力的评估却几乎毫无改变。“孤独谱系群体中有 50%的人终生缄默不语，”一位作者在 1994 年为专业人士编写的文集《儿童与成人中的孤独症》中宣称。“即使是高智商的孤独谱系青少年，也只能维持最基本的社会关系，似乎始终保留着典型的缺乏共情和情感淡漠的特征，”另一位作者声称。



The clinical definition of autism was mutating, ramifying, spreading out into a rainbow of a million colors. But the outlook on the lives and potential of autistic people remained relentlessly monochrome.

孤独症的临床定义正在变异、分化，扩散成百万种色彩的彩虹。但对孤独谱系群体生活与潜能的看法，却始终顽固地停留在单调的黑白之中。



III



The head of the APA subcommittee charged with developing a new set of criteria for the DSM-IV was an affable, rumpled man with a Captain Kangaroo mustache named Fred Volkmar, chairman of the autism research program at the Yale Child Study Center. Among the items on his to-do list was considering Wing’s proposal to include Asperger’s syndrome as a separate diagnosis in the next revision. Her successful lobbying for its inclusion in the tenth edition of the ICD, published by the World Health Organization (WHO) in 1990, made it nearly inevitable that the diagnosis would also appear in the DSM. But the study of the condition was still in its infancy. The first international conference on Asperger’s wasn’t held until 1988, once the revision process was already under way, and the first draft of a set of criteria to define it didn’t emerge for another year.

负责为 DSM-IV 制定新诊断标准的 APA 小组委员会主席，是一位和蔼可亲、衣着随和、留着"袋鼠船长"式胡须的弗雷德·沃尔克马尔，他同时担任耶鲁儿童研究中心孤独症研究项目主任。他的待办事项之一，是考虑温提出的将阿斯伯格综合征作为独立诊断纳入下一版修订的建议。温成功游说世界卫生组织（WHO）在 1990 年出版的 ICD 第十版中纳入该诊断，这使得该诊断几乎必然也会出现在 DSM 中。但当时对这种状况的研究仍处于起步阶段。首届阿斯伯格综合征国际会议直到 1988 年才召开——此时修订工作已在进行中——而界定该诊断的首套标准初稿又过了一年才问世。



The fact that the syndrome shaded into subclinical eccentricity raised a question that cut to the core of the entire psychiatric enterprise: Was Asperger’s syndrome truly a mental disorder or a common personality type in its most extreme form? Asperger’s 1944 description suggested a more holistic view: it was a personality type that could become profoundly disabling in the absence of adequate adaptation by the patient and the people in his or her environment. Volkmar cautioned his colleagues, “Odd and unusual behaviors do not, in and of themselves, constitute a ‘disorder’ unless they are related to a manifestation of serious dysfunction within the individual.” Yet, even in Volkmar’s clinic at Yale, the nature of what constituted “serious dysfunction” was much more open to interpretation than an elevated level on a blood test or an anomalous waveform on an EEG.

该综合征与亚临床怪癖之间的界限模糊，引发了一个直指整个精神病学核心的问题：阿斯伯格综合征究竟是一种真正的精神障碍，还是一种常见人格类型的极端表现形式？阿斯伯格在 1944 年的描述提出了一种更全面的观点：这是一种人格类型，若患者及其所处环境的人未能进行充分适应，则可能导致严重失能。沃尔克马告诫同行："怪异反常的行为本身并不构成'障碍'，除非这些行为与个体内部严重功能障碍的表现相关。"然而，即便在沃尔克马执掌的耶鲁诊所中，所谓"严重功能障碍"的界定标准，远比血液检测指标升高或脑电图波形异常更易引发争议。



Consider Robert Edwards, an eleven-year-old boy profiled by Volkmar and his colleague Ami Klin as a “relatively classic” case of Asperger’s disorder. Robert said his first words by his first birthday and breezed through C. S. Lewis’s seven-volume fantasy epic The Chronicles of Narnia while still in kindergarten. Despite his prodigious verbal abilities, by the time he was three, he had become “a major source of concern” to his parents—who were both doctors—because he didn’t seem to be making friends in preschool.

来看看罗伯特·爱德华兹，一个十一岁的男孩，沃尔克马和他的同事阿米·克林将其描述为阿斯伯格综合征的“相对典型”案例。罗伯特在一岁生日前说出了第一个词，还在幼儿园时就轻松读完了 C.S.刘易斯的七卷奇幻史诗《纳尼亚传奇》。尽管他语言能力超群，但到了三岁时，他已经成为父母（两人都是医生）的“主要担忧来源”，因为他在幼儿园似乎交不到朋友。



Klin and Volkmar attributed Robert’s “social problems” to his precocious fascination with astronomy. “He would pursue this interest at any opportunity,” they reported. “The interest intruded on essentially all aspects of his life. For example, in any conversation with peers, he inevitably brought the conversation or play around to stars and planets or time and its measurement.” His “eccentric” interests also included “computer games—their rules, programmers, and the companies that produce them.” (Within a few years, such interests on the part of an eleven-year-old boy wouldn’t be considered odd or eccentric at all.)

克林和沃尔克马将罗伯特的“社交问题”归因于他对天文学的早熟迷恋。“他会抓住任何机会追求这个兴趣，”他们报告说。“这种兴趣几乎侵入了他生活的方方面面。例如，在与同龄人的任何对话中，他总会不可避免地让谈话或游戏转向星星和行星，或者时间及其测量。”他的“古怪”兴趣还包括“电脑游戏——它们的规则、程序员以及生产它们的公司。”（几年后，一个十一岁男孩的这些兴趣就不会再被视为古怪或异常了。）



By the time Klin and Volkmar saw Robert, the boy had already spent nearly all of his life under the clinical gaze. When he was five, his parents had him evaluated by an occupational therapist for his “low motor tone.” Three years later, they sent him to a psychiatrist, who diagnosed him with an anxiety disorder. At ten, he was put through a battery of tests because of his poor handwriting and “social isolation.” Once his teacher “started to make some accommodations” for him (never described by Klin and Volkmar), Robert was accepted into an accelerated math program. But he was still regarded as profoundly ill.

当克林和沃尔克玛见到罗伯特时，这个男孩几乎一生都活在临床诊断的审视之下。五岁时，父母因他"肌张力低下"带他接受作业治疗师评估；三年后，他被送到精神科医生处，确诊为焦虑障碍；十岁时，因书写潦草和"社交孤立"接受了一系列测试。当老师开始为他"提供特殊照顾"（克林和沃尔克玛从未具体说明是何措施）后，罗伯特得以进入数学加速班。但他仍被视为患有严重疾病。



Klin and Volkmar were disturbed by his “rather formal and pedantic communication style.” When they asked Robert to provide another word for call, he said “beckon,” which might not have sounded out of place in the world of The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe. Asked to produce a synonym for thin, he replied “dimensionally challenged,” but this witticism was lost on his examiners. They noted that Robert’s friendships appeared to be “based almost exclusively on their common interest in computers,” as if the idea of friendships based on shared interests was clinically suspicious. Klin and Volkmar were equally unimpressed by an autobiographical statement that they asked Robert to bring to their office, which they cited as another example of his special interests “intruding” into other areas of his life.

克林和沃尔克马对他“相当正式且刻板的沟通风格”感到不安。当他们要求罗伯特提供“call”的同义词时，他回答“beckon”，这个词放在《狮子、女巫与魔衣橱》的世界里或许并不突兀。当被要求给出“thin”的同义词时，他回答“维度挑战者”，但这个俏皮话并未被考官理解。他们指出，罗伯特的友谊似乎“几乎完全建立在共同对计算机的兴趣上”，仿佛基于共同兴趣的友谊在临床上值得怀疑。克林和沃尔克马同样对罗伯特应要求带到办公室的自述材料不以为然，他们将其视为他的特殊兴趣“侵入”生活其他领域的又一例证。



My name is Robert Edwards. I am an intelligent, unsociable, but adaptable person. I would like to dispel any untrue rumors about me. I am not edible. I cannot fly. I cannot use telekinesis. My brain is not large enough to destroy the entire world when unfolded. I did not teach my long-haired guinea pig Chronos to eat everything in sight (that is the nature of the long-haired guinea pig).

我叫罗伯特·爱德华兹。我是个聪明、不善交际但适应力强的人。我想澄清一些关于我的不实传言：我不可食用，不会飞行，不会使用念力，我的大脑展开后也不足以毁灭整个世界。我也没有教我的长毛豚鼠克洛诺斯见什么吃什么——那本就是长毛豚鼠的天性。



Absent the context of a psychiatric case history, the story of a boy who reads the Narnia books in kindergarten, cracks jokes about being “dimensionally challenged,” and spends his grade school years hanging out with his fellow geeks could be the biography of practically anyone destined to become a successful entrepreneur in Silicon Valley. Thus, clinical accounts of Asperger’s syndrome tended to reframe neutral or even positive aspects of behavior as manifestations of deficit and impairment. Intense curiosity became perseveration. Precociously articulate speech became hyperlexia. An average score on a test became a relative deficit—evidence of an uneven cognitive profile.

若抛开精神病史的背景，一个在幼儿园就读《纳尼亚传奇》、自嘲"维度受限"、整个小学阶段都与同好厮混的男孩故事，几乎可以成为任何硅谷成功企业家的传记。因此，阿斯伯格综合征的临床描述往往倾向于将中性甚至积极的行为特征重新定义为缺陷与损伤的表现：强烈的好奇心被视作刻板重复，早慧的语言能力被诊断为阅读早慧症，测试中的平均分则成了相对缺陷——认知能力不均衡的证据。



If Robert represented a classic case of Asperger’s syndrome, it was clearly a disorder of degree, and gauging the degree of social impairment is highly subject to social context. “As I explain to parents, the cure for Asperger’s syndrome is very simple—it is not surgery, medication or intensive therapy,” says Tony Attwood, one of the world’s leading authorities on the subject. “It is taking your son or daughter to their bedroom, leaving the bedroom, and closing the door. You cannot have a social deficit when you are alone. You cannot have a communication problem when you are alone. Your repetitive behavior does not annoy anyone when you are alone. All the diagnostic criteria dissolve in solitude. That’s why teenagers with Asperger’s are reluctant to leave their bedroom for school: the signs of autism, and the degrees of stress and withdrawal, are proportional to the number of people present.”

如果罗伯特代表了阿斯伯格综合征的典型病例，那么这显然是一种程度上的障碍，而衡量社交损伤的程度在很大程度上取决于社会环境。“正如我向家长解释的那样，阿斯伯格综合征的疗法非常简单——不是手术、药物或强化治疗，”全球该领域的权威专家之一托尼·阿特伍德表示，“只需带你的儿子或女儿到他们的卧室，离开房间，然后关上门。当你独处时，不会有社交缺陷；当你独处时，不会有沟通问题；当你独处时，重复行为不会惹恼任何人。所有诊断标准在独处中都会消失。这就是为什么患有阿斯伯格综合征的青少年不愿离开卧室去上学：孤独谱系的迹象，以及压力和退缩的程度，与在场的人数成正比。”



Left to his own devices, Robert might not have experienced himself as mentally ill at all, though he certainly could have developed an anxiety disorder from being perpetually grilled by men with clipboards. Given a technology that enabled him to communicate with other like-minded young people, he might have encouraged them to feel that their problems originated not in themselves, but in the system that had branded them diseased and inferior.

若任其自然发展，罗伯特或许根本不会认为自己有精神疾病——尽管被手持写字板的人反复盘问确实可能让他患上焦虑症。倘若有一种技术能让他与其他志同道合的年轻人交流，他或许会鼓励他们相信：问题的根源不在自身，而在于那个将他们标记为病态与低劣的体制。



Considerations like this in psychiatry are usually left to sociologists, but they would come back to haunt the editors of the DSM-IV once the criteria for Asperger’s syndrome were set loose in the wild. Few members of Volkmar’s subcommittee could have predicted that the term Aspie would become a badge of honor and defiant pride within a decade, even for those without an official diagnosis. The genie of autistic intelligence was poised to escape the bottle in which it had been trapped for fifty years.

精神病学中这类思考通常留给社会学家，但当阿斯伯格综合征的诊断标准被投入现实世界后，它们终将萦绕在《精神障碍诊断与统计手册（第四版）》编委会的心头。沃尔克马小组的成员们几乎无人能预见，在十年之内，"阿斯伯格人"这个术语会成为荣誉徽章与叛逆骄傲的象征——即便对那些未获正式诊断者而言也是如此。孤独谱系智慧这盏神灯，即将挣脱禁锢它半个世纪的魔瓶。



—



THE CHAIR OF THE DSM-IV task force, Allen Frances, was wary of the rampant proliferation of labels and disturbed by his colleagues’ apparent willingness to pathologize eccentricity. But seeing his job as that of being a “consensus scholar,” he deferred on the subject of autism to the expertise of Volkmar and his colleagues, who reassured him that the changes planned for the fourth edition would not result in any major upheaval. In the end, Wing’s pragmatic argument that the addition of the diagnosis would result in more families gaining access to services won the day. Of ninety-four new diagnoses proposed for the fourth edition of the manual, only two—Asperger’s and type II bipolar disorder—would make the cut.

DSM-IV 工作组主席艾伦·弗朗西斯对标签的泛滥蔓延保持警惕，并对同事们似乎乐于将古怪行为病理化的倾向感到不安。但当他将自身角色定位为"共识学者"时，便在孤独症议题上遵从了沃尔克马及其同僚的专业意见——后者向他保证，第四版修订计划不会引发重大动荡。最终，洛娜·温提出的实用主义论点占据了上风：增加该诊断类别将使更多家庭获得服务资源。在第四版手册拟新增的 94 种诊断类别中，最终仅有两项——阿斯伯格综合征与 II 型双相情感障碍——得以入选。



There was just one bit of unfinished business to attend to: those rumors that Asperger, who had died in 1980, was a Nazi. “It was a crazy problem. It took me weeks to figure it out,” Volkmar confessed. Finally, he decided to phone up Wing and bluntly ask her if there was any truth to the rumors. She came up with the perfect answer—one that was utterly irrelevant but virtually guaranteed to persuade Volkmar to sign off on the new diagnosis. “Oh, dear no,” she reassured him from London. “Asperger was a deeply religious man.”

只剩下一件未了之事需要处理：那些关于 1980 年去世的阿斯伯格是纳粹的传言。“这是个棘手的问题。我花了好几周才想明白，”沃尔克马坦言。最终，他决定打电话给温格，直截了当地问她这些传言是否属实。温格给出了一个完美的回答——这个回答虽然完全不相关，但几乎肯定能说服沃尔克马同意新的诊断。“哦，亲爱的，绝对不是，”她在伦敦安慰道，“阿斯伯格是个非常虔诚的人。”



—



IF THE DSM-III TURNED Spitzer and his data geeks into “rock stars” (as his wife, Janet Williams, put it), the fourth edition was Michael Jackson’s Thriller. DSM-IV was an international smash that earned $18 million in its first ten months in print alone and $100 million altogether while launching a thriving industry of branded tie-ins and lucrative subsidiaries. DSM-IV casebooks, study guides, videotapes, and software poured onto the market, and readers interested in the making-of backstory could browse through a four-volume DSM-IV Sourcebook. Spotting the signs of autism—once the arcane skill of the initiated few—became the job of nearly everyone involved in pediatric medicine, psychology, and education.

如果《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》第三版让斯皮策和他的数据极客们变成了“摇滚明星”（正如他的妻子珍妮特·威廉姆斯所说），那么第四版就是迈克尔·杰克逊的《颤栗》。《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》第四版在全球大获成功，仅出版后的头十个月就赚了 1800 万美元，总收入达到 1 亿美元，同时还催生了一个蓬勃发展的品牌周边产业和利润丰厚的衍生业务。第四版的案例手册、学习指南、录像带和软件如潮水般涌入市场，而对其幕后制作过程感兴趣的读者，还可以翻阅四卷本的《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》第四版资料集。识别孤独症的迹象——曾经只是少数入门者的神秘技能——如今几乎成了儿科医学、心理学和教育领域每个人的工作。



In fourteen years and a handful of revisions, the DSM had gone from a slim volume that sat unread on dusty shelves in institutions to a nine-hundred-page behemoth that found its way into classrooms, courtrooms, community clinics, research labs, congressional hearings, pharma stockholders’ meetings, social service agencies, and guidance counselors’ offices. The entire clinical infrastructure of autism had been transformed from a channel for optional reporting of isolated cases to a network for active surveillance of the general population. Inevitably, the more that clinicians and educators looked for a condition, the more they found it. The upward trend that began in the wake of the DSM-III-R began to snowball after the publication of the DSM-IV.

在十四年间历经数次修订，DSM 从一本尘封于机构书架上无人问津的薄册，蜕变为一本九百页的巨著，渗透进教室、法庭、社区诊所、研究实验室、国会听证会、制药公司股东大会、社会服务机构以及辅导咨询办公室。孤独症的全部临床基础设施，已从一条可选择性报告孤立病例的渠道，转变为一张主动监测普通人群的网络。不可避免地，临床医生和教育工作者越是寻找某种状况，就越会发现它。始于 DSM-III-R 之后的上升趋势，在 DSM-IV 出版后开始如滚雪球般加速发展。



In fact, the numbers were rising a little too steeply, because the DSM-IV editors had made a small but crucial error in the final run-up to publication. Instead of requiring that a child display impairments in social interaction, communication, and behavior before getting a diagnosis of PDD-NOS, the criteria substituted the word or for and. (In other words, a clinician could deliver the whole banquet by choosing one from column A.) This fateful typo went uncorrected for six years and was unacknowledged in the literature until the editor of the DSM-IV Text Revision, Michael First, finally copped to it in a notably understated article in an obscure journal in 2002.

事实上，这些数字的攀升速度有些过快，因为 DSM-IV 的编辑们在最终出版前的冲刺阶段犯了一个微小但关键的失误。在诊断 PDD-NOS（广泛性发育障碍未特定型）时，标准本应要求儿童在社交互动、沟通和行为方面均存在障碍，但实际标准却将"和"误写为"或"。（换言之，临床医生只需从 A 列中选择一项，就能开具整套诊断。）这个致命的笔误在六年内未被纠正，文献中也无人提及，直到 DSM-IV 文本修订版编辑迈克尔·弗斯特在 2002 年一篇发表于小众期刊、措辞极为低调的文章中才最终承认此事。



This certainly didn’t mean that every child diagnosed with PDD-NOS in the years between 1994 and 2000 was misdiagnosed, but the impact of the botched language was potentially significant. By reanalyzing the field-test data using the erroneous wording, Volkmar found that “about 75 percent of children identified by clinicians as not having the disorder (true negatives) were incorrectly identified as having it according to DSM-IV.” For epidemiologists gauging the DSM-IV’s impact in the crucial period that would go down in history as the years a mysterious “autism epidemic” took hold, it was a statistical nightmare. Yet, until author Roy Richard Grinker called attention to the typo in his 2008 book Unstrange Minds, hardly anyone outside the usual tiny circle of experts was aware of it.

这当然并不意味着在 1994 年至 2000 年间被诊断为 PDD-NOS 的每个孩子都被误诊了，但措辞失误的影响可能相当显著。沃尔克玛通过重新分析使用错误措辞的现场测试数据发现，"根据 DSM-IV 标准，约 75%被临床医生判定为未患病（真阴性）的儿童被错误地认定为患病。"对于流行病学家而言，在评估 DSM-IV 对那段被历史铭记为神秘"孤独症流行"爆发关键时期的影响时，这堪称统计学的噩梦。然而，直到作家罗伊·理查德·格林克在其 2008 年著作《不寻常的心灵》中指出这一笔误前，除了少数专家圈子外，几乎无人知晓此事。



IV



Far from the APA subcommittees debating issues of nosology over pastrami sandwiches and cream soda, an explanation for the rising numbers was taking shape that had nothing to do with diagnostic criteria, screening instruments, or the rise of medicalized psychiatry. Instead, it was a terrifying story of the poisoning of innocent children by heartless corporations.

远离 APA 各小组委员会就熏牛肉三明治和奶油苏打水争论疾病分类学的场景，另一种解释正在成形——它与诊断标准、筛查工具或医学化精神医学的兴起毫无关联。相反，这是一个关于无情企业毒害无辜儿童的恐怖故事。



Nestled among apple orchards forty-five miles northwest of Boston, Leominster is a classic New England factory town, with austere white steeples and sprawling strip malls juxtaposed around a central common. Celebrated as the birthplace of Johnny Appleseed, Leominster earned another distinction in the 1940s, when one in five residents was working for plastics manufacturers like Foster Grant, the company that turned sunglasses—a product formerly associated with invalids—into a fashion accessory for strolling on the Atlantic City boardwalk. To manufacture its stylish frames, Foster Grant built a giant plastic-injection plant along the Nashua River. The proud town fathers posted signs along the highway christening Leominster “the Plastic City.”

位于波士顿西北 45 英里处，莱明斯特这座新英格兰经典工业小镇被苹果园环绕，肃穆的白色尖塔与绵延的购物中心在中央广场周围形成鲜明对比。作为约翰尼·苹果籽的诞生地而闻名，莱明斯特在 1940 年代又获得了一项殊荣——当时五分之一的居民为福斯特·格兰特等塑料制造商工作，这家公司成功将太阳镜（曾被视为病弱者专属产品）转变为大西洋城海滨步道上的时尚配饰。为生产时尚镜框，福斯特·格兰特在纳舒厄河畔建造了巨型注塑工厂。自豪的市政官员在高速公路旁竖起标牌，将莱明斯特命名为"塑料之城"。



Soon it became the Polluted City. A green haze hung in the air that smelled alternately like rotten eggs and paint thinner. The locals used to say that you could tell which shade of sunglasses was being made that day depending on the color of the clouds belching out of the plant’s smokestacks. The waters of the Nashua flowed red, white, and blue. Local gardeners got used to PVC particles frosting their vegetable beds like sugar, and housewives sucked on Vicks cough drops to numb the burning sensation in their throats. Then an international conglomerate acquired Foster Grant and outsourced its frame manufacturing to Mexico. The defunct plant was declared a hazardous-waste site by state authorities.

不久，这里便成了污染之城。空气中弥漫着绿色的雾霾，气味时而像臭鸡蛋，时而像油漆稀释剂。当地人常说，只要看工厂烟囱喷出的云朵颜色，就能知道那天在生产哪种色调的太阳镜。纳舒亚河的水流呈现出红、白、蓝三色。当地园丁早已习惯 PVC 颗粒像糖霜般覆盖在菜畦上，家庭主妇们则靠含服维克斯止咳糖来缓解喉咙的灼烧感。后来，一家跨国集团收购了福斯特格兰特公司，并将镜框生产线转移到了墨西哥。这座废弃的工厂被州政府列为危险废物处理场。



A couple of years after the plant closed, a couple in Leominster named Lori and Larry Altobelli had their second child, Joshua. It soon became apparent that he had profound developmental delays. His parents were unable to toilet-train him, and he learned to speak only a handful of words. He would spin endlessly in circles and run laps around the living room while maintaining a tight grip on his favorite toy. When he was three, he was diagnosed with PDD-NOS, which his mother dubbed “junior autism.” His younger brother, Jay, was also eventually diagnosed with PDD-NOS.

工厂关闭几年后，莱明斯特的一对夫妇洛莉和拉里·阿尔托贝利迎来了他们的第二个孩子约书亚。很快人们就发现他存在严重的发育迟缓问题。父母无法教会他如厕，他仅学会了寥寥几个单词。他会不停地原地转圈，紧握最心爱的玩具绕着客厅跑圈。三岁时，他被诊断出患有待分类的广泛性发育障碍（PDD-NOS），母亲称之为"轻度孤独症"。他的弟弟杰伊后来也被诊断出患有同样的病症。



Joshua’s speech therapist asked Lori and Larry if they would be willing to offer tips about local services and resources to another couple whose son had recently been diagnosed with PDD-NOS, Melanie and Ralph Palotta. As the couples swapped stories, Ralph couldn’t shake the feeling that Larry looked familiar; then he remembered seeing his face on the school bus in the morning when they were in fifth grade together. A few months later, at a meeting of the Association for Retarded Citizens, Ralph met Rich Frenette, the father of another newly diagnosed boy on the spectrum. Ralph recalled that they had played on the same Little League team and lived one block apart. The fact that all three men were from the same neighborhood seemed too suggestive to be merely a coincidence, as did their shared memories of growing up in the poisonous penumbra of Foster Grant.

约书亚的语言治疗师问洛莉和拉里，是否愿意为另一对夫妇——梅兰妮和拉尔夫·帕洛塔——提供一些关于本地服务和资源的建议，他们的儿子最近被诊断出患有 PDD-NOS。随着两对夫妇分享彼此的故事，拉尔夫总觉得拉里看起来很眼熟；后来他想起，五年级时他们曾一起坐校车，早上经常看到他的脸。几个月后，在一次智力障碍者协会的会议上，拉尔夫遇到了里奇·弗雷内特，他是另一个刚被诊断出自闭症谱系障碍的男孩的父亲。拉尔夫回忆说，他们曾参加过同一个少年棒球队，而且住在仅隔一个街区的地方。这三个男人都来自同一个社区，这一事实似乎太过巧合，而他们共同拥有的在福斯特·格兰特有毒阴影下成长的记忆，也同样令人难以忽视。



The Altobellis were haunted by a similar chain of events that had unfolded a decade earlier forty miles up the highway, in a working-class town called Woburn. Jimmy Anderson was just four years old when he was diagnosed with a rare form of cancer called acute lymphocytic leukemia. As his mother, Anne, glanced around a waiting room at Massachusetts General in Boston, she recognized the faces of women that she’d seen at her neighborhood supermarket. Then she began to hear about other neighborhood kids who had also contracted this rare disease. What was going on?

十年前，在高速公路向北四十英里处一个名为沃本的工薪阶层小镇上，曾发生过一连串相似的事件，令阿尔托贝利家族深受困扰。吉米·安德森年仅四岁时，就被诊断出患有急性淋巴细胞白血病这种罕见的癌症。当他的母亲安妮在波士顿麻省总医院的候诊室里环顾四周时，她认出了几张曾在社区超市见过的女性面孔。随后，她陆续听说社区里其他孩子也患上了这种罕见疾病。这究竟是怎么回事？



Anne had a flash of mother’s intuition. It was the water—the stinky, acrid-tasting, perpetually discolored Woburn water. But doctors and city officials pooh-poohed her concerns, and even her friends thought she was a little crazy. No one could deny, however, that the residents of East Woburn seemed increasingly unwell, with headaches, blurred vision, and awful rashes that wouldn’t go away. Was it really normal for this many young women to have miscarriages? Eventually, Anne didn’t seem so crazy. She organized the neighborhood parents into a united front to demand answers from city officials. A local newspaper reporter uncovered the fact that barrels of industrial chemicals known to be carcinogenic and neurotoxic had been buried near two of East Woburn’s wells. Anne’s investigation became the basis for a best-selling book by Jonathan Harr called A Civil Action, which was turned into an Oscar-winning film.

安妮突然涌起一股母亲的直觉。问题出在水——沃本那股发臭、带着刺鼻气味、永远浑浊不清的水。但医生和市政官员对她的担忧嗤之以鼻，连朋友们都觉得她有点疯癫。然而，没人能否认东沃本居民的身体状况每况愈下：头痛、视力模糊、还有久治不愈的可怕皮疹。这么多年轻女性流产，真的正常吗？最终，安妮看起来没那么疯狂了。她组织社区家长结成统一战线，要求市政官员给出解释。当地报社记者发现，已知具有致癌性和神经毒性的工业化学品桶被埋在东沃本两口井附近。安妮的调查成为乔纳森·哈尔畅销书《民事诉讼》的素材，该书后被改编成奥斯卡获奖影片。



Lori, who had a master’s degree in health care administration, started asking parents at autism support group meetings if they had ever lived in her husband’s old neighborhood.
