She was shocked by how many said yes. She pinned a map of the neighborhood up on her wall, marking with an X the places where the mother or father of an autistic child had lived. Soon dozens of X’s had accumulated on the map.

拥有医疗管理硕士学位的洛莉，开始在孤独症互助小组会议上询问家长们是否曾在她丈夫的旧社区居住过。她惊讶地发现，竟有如此多的人给出了肯定的回答。她在墙上钉了一张该社区的地图，用 X 标记出每位孤独症儿童母亲或父亲曾居住过的地方。很快，地图上便布满了密密麻麻的 X 标记。



On March 25, 1990, Lori sent a letter to the CDC headquarters in Atlanta demanding an investigation. She also sent a copy to the mayor. CDC officials forwarded her letter to the Massachusetts Department of Public Health (MDPH), and a couple of months later an epidemiologist arrived in town to begin collecting data. To avert mass panic, MDPH officials asked Lori to keep the investigation a secret until a definitive connection to the plant was found. She was willing to play along until she heard that the city was planning to build a playground adjacent to the location of the old factory. Furious, Lori called up the mayor, Steve Perla, and said, “You can’t build a kids’ playground two hundred feet from a toxic waste dump!”

1990 年 3 月 25 日，洛莉致信亚特兰大的疾控中心总部，要求展开调查，同时将副本寄给了市长。疾控中心官员将她的信转交给了马萨诸塞州公共卫生部，几个月后，一名流行病学家抵达该镇开始收集数据。为避免引发大规模恐慌，州公共卫生部官员要求洛莉在找到与工厂的确切关联之前对调查保密。她原本愿意配合，直到听说市政府计划在旧工厂原址附近修建一座游乐场。洛莉勃然大怒，打电话给市长史蒂夫·佩拉说：“你们不能在距离有毒废物堆两百英尺的地方建儿童游乐场！”



Perla postponed the opening of the playground by releasing a phony story about a missing bolt in a swing set, but an anonymous caller tipped off local reporters. The Altobellis and an environmental activist named Matt Wilson responded by holding a press conference in the shadow of the old plant and launching an organization called Leominster Citizens for a Safe Environment. The Altobellis were inundated with phone calls from frightened parents. The link to the Foster Grant plant seemed undeniable; at least one study in the literature suggested that a disproportionate number of the parents of autistic children—one in four—had suffered occupational exposure to toxic chemicals. (It was Mary Coleman’s study conducted in 1974 with the help of Rimland and other NSAC parents.) Exposure to thalidomide, an over-the-counter drug used in the 1960s to relieve morning sickness in pregnant women that resulted in ten thousand cases of babies being born with serious malformation of the limbs, had been linked to autism in numerous studies over the years.

佩尔拉推迟了游乐场的开放，散布了一个关于秋千架少了一颗螺栓的虚假消息，但一位匿名举报者向当地记者透露了实情。阿尔托贝利一家与一位名叫马特·威尔逊的环保活动家为此在老工厂的阴影下召开新闻发布会，并成立了一个名为"莱明斯特市民安全环境组织"的团体。阿尔托贝利家接到了无数惊慌失措的家长打来的电话。与福斯特·格兰特工厂的关联似乎无可辩驳；文献中至少有一项研究表明，孤独症儿童家长中，有异常高比例——四分之一——曾因职业原因接触过有毒化学物质。（这是玛丽·科尔曼 1974 年在里姆兰及其他 NSAC 家长协助下进行的研究。）沙利度胺是一种 20 世纪 60 年代用于缓解孕妇晨吐的非处方药，曾导致一万名婴儿出生时出现严重肢体畸形，多年来多项研究已将其与孤独谱系联系起来。



The Altobellis decided to go national. They contacted ABC News’s chief medical editor, Timothy Johnson, who had previously worked as a reporter for Channel 5 in Boston and was already familiar with the story. On March 13, 1992, veteran news anchor Hugh Downs prepared millions of viewers of the award-winning show 20/20 for a landmark broadcast. “We begin with a report we believe will surprise the medical world. Indeed, the information offered tonight is groundbreaking,” said Downs. “Along with you, many experts will hear it here for the first time.”

阿尔托贝利家族决定将业务拓展至全国。他们联系了 ABC 新闻的首席医学编辑蒂莫西·约翰逊，此人曾在波士顿第五频道担任记者，对该事件已有所了解。1992 年 3 月 13 日，资深新闻主播休·唐斯在获奖节目《20/20》中向数百万观众预告了一场里程碑式的直播。"我们将播出一则报道，相信它会震惊医学界。事实上，今晚呈现的信息具有开创性意义，"唐斯说道，"与各位观众一样，许多专家也将首次在此听到这些内容。"



The ABC segment, “The Street Where They Lived,” became a sensation. “Consider this,” Johnson said. “Only fifteen children in every ten thousand have symptoms of autism or PDD and yet, so far, Lori has connected forty-two cases of autism and PDD to the small neighborhood of about six hundred homes which circle the Foster Grant plant.” Firm evidence of the role of toxicity, he acknowledged, was frustratingly elusive: “Scientists don’t know what causes autism, although they have many theories, from head trauma to upbringing to heredity.” Barbara Walters praised Lori for embarking on a “lonely crusade” to expose the ignorance of the medical establishment.

ABC 频道的《他们居住的街道》节目引起了轰动。“想想看，”约翰逊说，“每一万个孩子中只有十五个表现出孤独症或广泛性发育障碍的症状，但到目前为止，洛莉已经在福斯特格兰特工厂周围约六百户的小社区里联系到了四十二个孤独症和广泛性发育障碍的病例。”他承认，毒性作用的明确证据难以捉摸，令人沮丧：“科学家们不知道孤独症的病因，尽管他们有很多理论，从头部创伤到养育方式再到遗传。”芭芭拉·沃尔特斯称赞洛莉发起了一场“孤独的十字军运动”，以揭露医学界的无知。



Then it was the parents’ turn to testify. “We were a completely normal family, doing completely normal things, and we were so looking forward to the baby,” one father said. “Our children look perfectly normal,” Lori added. “They all look perfect.” Another mother compared Leominster to the Twilight Zone.

接着轮到家长们作证。“我们曾是一个完全正常的家庭，做着完全正常的事情，我们非常期待这个孩子的到来，”一位父亲说。“我们的孩子看起来完全正常，”洛莉补充道，“他们看起来都很完美。”另一位母亲将莱明斯特比作《阴阳魔界》。



Larry reminisced about playing ice hockey as a boy on the river, which froze unevenly because of the sheer volume of industrial toxins that had been pumped into it. A former Foster Grant employee was brought on to admit to dumping “several thousands of gallons of styrene . . . where all the kids play down there.” Johnson painted a vivid picture of “twenty-seven smokestacks belching out a potent derivative of vinyl chloride, which is known to cause cancer and other serious problems.” Including autism? The show’s producers finessed the absence of any known link by turning their cameras onto a terrified mother who said she was convinced of the connection because the rates of autism were “blown out of the water” in Leominster.

拉里回忆起小时候在河上打冰球的情景，由于大量工业毒素排入河中，河面结冰很不均匀。一位前福斯特·格兰特员工被请来承认曾倾倒“数千加仑的苯乙烯……就在孩子们玩耍的地方。”约翰逊生动地描绘了“27 根烟囱喷涌着一种强效的氯乙烯衍生物，这种物质已知会致癌并引发其他严重问题。”包括孤独症吗？节目制作人巧妙回避了缺乏已知关联的事实，将镜头转向一位惊恐的母亲，她声称自己确信两者有关联，因为利奥明斯特的孤独症发病率“高得惊人”。



But anecdotes are not statistics, and 20/20’s producers didn’t mention that there were no statistics for the historical prevalence of autism in Leominster, because studies to establish the normal baseline rates had never been done. Indeed, the PDD-NOS diagnosis was too new to determine if the incidence was rising at all. ABC’s cameras cut back to Johnson, who made the chilling claim that “spontaneous abortions” and “certain cancers associated with environmental exposure” were rampant in Plastic City. He appended a halfhearted caveat—“It’s a long road from coincidence to proving causation, but it’s certainly a theory worth pursuing”—before moving on to the next story.

但轶事并非统计数据，20/20 的制片人并未提及莱明斯特地区孤独症历史患病率缺乏统计数据，因为从未进行过建立正常基线水平的研究。事实上，待分类的广泛性发育障碍（PDD-NOS）这一诊断标准过于新颖，尚无法判断发病率是否在上升。ABC 的镜头切回约翰逊，他令人不寒而栗地声称"塑料之城"中"自然流产"和"某些与环境暴露相关的癌症"十分猖獗。他敷衍地补充了一句免责声明——"从巧合到证明因果关系还有很长的路要走，但这无疑是一个值得探索的理论"——随后便转向下一个故事。



The Altobellis’ phone rang continuously in the wake of the broadcast, and the map on their wall grew dark with X’s. The story was picked up by the Sally Jessy Raphael show, and a week later 20/20 aired an even more sensationalistic follow-up. Johnson declared that parents all over the country had contacted the network, expressing “relief at finding out that they’re part of a larger picture, they’re not alone, that maybe they’re not responsible for what happened” (as if anyone had implied that they were responsible). Lori was defiant: “This morning, as Larry left for work, he said, ‘You really opened Pandora’s box, didn’t you?’ I said, ‘And now that it’s open enough, I’m not going to let anybody close it.’” She predicted that she and her fellow parents would “rewrite the book on autism.”

节目播出后，阿尔托贝利家的电话响个不停，墙上的地图被密密麻麻的 X 标记覆盖。这个故事被《莎莉·杰西·拉斐尔秀》选中，一周后《20/20》播出了更耸人听闻的后续报道。约翰逊宣称全国各地的家长都联系了电视台，表示"得知自己并非孤例，不必为发生的事独自承担责任，感到如释重负"（仿佛有人暗示过他们该负责似的）。洛丽态度强硬："今早拉里出门上班时说'你真是打开了潘多拉魔盒'，我回答'既然盒子已经打开，我就绝不让任何人关上它'。"她预言自己与其他家长将"改写关于孤独症的教科书"。



A team of geneticists had arrived from Stanford to take blood samples and analyze them for potentially damaging mutations. A toxicology unit from MDPH tested the soil near the defunct plant for solvents, heavy metals, and other lingering contaminants. A graduate student named Martha Lang spent three years working with the Altobellis and the other families of Leominster for her graduate thesis at Brown University on the leadership of mothers in community struggles against powerful polluters.

来自斯坦福的一个遗传学家团队已抵达，采集血样并分析其中可能存在的有害突变。马萨诸塞州公共卫生部的一个毒理学小组对废弃工厂附近的土壤进行了检测，寻找溶剂、重金属及其他残留污染物。一位名叫玛莎·朗的研究生花了三年时间，与阿尔托贝利家族及莱明斯特的其他家庭合作，为她在布朗大学的毕业论文研究母亲们在社区对抗强大污染源斗争中的领导作用。



Lang’s research was hampered by another lingering contaminant: the social stigma of having a child with autism. Many families declined to participate in her data-gathering process. But an examination of the medical records of the kids in Lori’s files indicated that the number of confirmed cases of autism in town was lower than she had been led to believe. Of the twenty-four kids whose records were analyzed by the MDPH, six “quite clearly did not meet the criteria” for either autism or PDD-NOS, while the data for seven more proved inconclusive. In several cases, the parents’ proximity to the Foster Grant plant was tenuous at best; indeed, some parents in Lori’s files had never lived in Leominster at all. After failing to find evidence of chromosomal abnormalities in the community, the Stanford team suggested that “secular changes in the definition of autism” rather than a true increase in prevalence were driving the rising numbers. Lang concluded that the tale of the “Leominster autism cluster” was much less clear-cut than she thought. But the media circus had long ago moved on.

朗的研究还受到另一个挥之不去的污染源的影响：拥有孤独症孩子的社会污名。许多家庭拒绝参与她的数据收集过程。但通过对洛里档案中儿童医疗记录的审查发现，该镇确诊的孤独症病例数量比她被告知的要少。在马萨诸塞州公共卫生局分析的 24 名儿童记录中，有 6 名"显然不符合"孤独症或广泛性发育障碍未分类型的诊断标准，另有 7 名儿童的数据无法得出明确结论。在几个案例中，家长与福斯特格兰特工厂的邻近关系最多只能算牵强；事实上，洛里档案中有些家长从未在莱明斯特居住过。在未能发现该社区存在染色体异常的证据后，斯坦福团队提出，推动病例数量上升的是"孤独症定义随时代变迁"，而非患病率的真实增长。朗得出结论，"莱明斯特孤独症聚集"的故事远没有她想象的那么清晰。但媒体的喧嚣早已转向别处。



One of the reporters who broke the story for a local newspaper, David Ropeik, is now a consultant on the science of risk perception. After watching the events in Plastic City unfold firsthand, he says he understands why so many people there—and in the viewing audience of 20/20—were drawn to the explanation that their children had been poisoned by the town’s toxic legacy.

率先报道此事的当地报纸记者之一大卫·罗佩克，如今已成为风险认知科学领域的顾问。在亲眼目睹塑料城事件的全过程后，他表示理解为何当地居民以及《20/20》节目的观众，都倾向于相信孩子被城镇有毒遗留物毒害的解释。



“As a parent, your one job in life is to take care of your kid. When you feel powerless over a serious risk, you have a deep emotional need to find answers,” he says. “Your mind is open to suggestion—could it be the plastics?—because in those suggestions, there is a kind of hope. Lori, who is a very reasonable person, grudgingly accepted the fact that the real story was more complicated. But you could also see that it hurt her to do that, because she was desperate for some sense of control over her children’s plight.”

“作为父母，人生唯一职责就是照顾孩子。当面对严重风险却感到无能为力时，内心会产生寻找答案的强烈情感需求，”他说道，“你的思维会向暗示敞开——会不会是塑料的问题？——因为这些暗示中蕴含着某种希望。洛丽是个非常理性的人，她勉强接受了事实真相更为复杂的现实。但你能看出这样做让她很痛苦，因为她迫切渴望能对孩子的困境有所掌控。”



V



In the aftermath of the Leominster scandal, other “autism clusters” started popping up all over the country—notably one in Brick Township, New Jersey, where sixty-three million gallons of septic waste had been dumped into a landfill between 1969 and 1979. No one was tracking these events more closely than Bernard Rimland, who started covering the Leominster story in his newsletter two years before the 20/20 broadcasts.

莱明斯特丑闻之后，全国范围内开始陆续出现其他"孤独谱系聚集区"——其中最引人注目的是新泽西州布里克镇，该地在 1969 年至 1979 年间曾有 6300 万加仑化粪池废物被倾倒入垃圾填埋场。伯纳德·里姆兰对这类事件的追踪最为密切，早在《20/20》节目播出两年前，他就已在自家通讯中报道了莱明斯特事件。



At first, he seemed resistant to the idea that the rates of diagnosis were changing rapidly on his watch. When the DSM-IV was published in 1994, Rimland said bluntly, “It is not reasonable to believe that the population of [autistic] children has changed much” in the years between the 1920s and the 1990s. But soon his position changed dramatically, and his position as the most trusted authority in the autism parents’ community gave him enormous influence.

起初，他似乎抗拒承认在其任职期间诊断率正在急剧变化。1994 年《精神障碍诊断与统计手册（第四版）》出版时，里姆兰直言不讳地表示："没有合理依据认为，从 20 世纪 20 年代到 90 年代期间，（孤独谱系）儿童群体发生了显著变化。"但不久后他的立场发生了戏剧性转变，而作为孤独症家长群体中最受信赖的权威，他的观点转变产生了巨大影响力。



At the same time, his alienation from mainstream medicine was increasing. The 1990s were a frustrating time for Rimland as the center of gravity in autism research tilted away from his storefront in Kensington and toward Wing and the London group. He barely addressed the potentially enormous impact of Wing’s DSM revisions in his newsletter, focusing instead on an inside-baseball controversy over the APA’s use of the word pervasive. (He called the PDD-NOS label “pseudoscientific” and predicted that it was too cumbersome to catch on.) ARI was also struggling financially. In a bizarre turn of events, a $75,000 check intended for Rimland from the producers of Rain Man ended up going to the Autism Society of America; even a letter from Dustin Hoffman and a lawsuit by Rimland failed to convince the organization to yield.

与此同时，他与主流医学界的隔阂日益加深。对里姆兰而言，1990 年代是令人沮丧的时期——孤独症研究的重心正从他在肯辛顿的店面转向温格和伦敦团队。他在简报中几乎未提及温格对《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》修订可能产生的巨大影响，反而聚焦于美国心理学会使用"广泛性"一词引发的业内争议（他将"广泛性发育障碍未特定型"标签称为"伪科学"，并预言这个过于冗长的术语难以普及）。孤独症研究所也陷入财务困境。更离奇的是，《雨人》制片方原本开给里姆兰的 7.5 万美元支票最终落入美国孤独症协会囊中；即便达斯汀·霍夫曼亲自致函、里姆兰提起诉讼，该组织仍拒绝归还这笔款项。



In 1995—after a torrent of inquiries from parents—Rimland ran a banner headline in his newsletter, “Is There an Autism Epidemic?” His answer was yes: “I believe that the increase is real, and not merely an increase in awareness.” To prove his point, he provided a chart showing that, in the years from 1965 to 1969, only 1 percent of the parents in his network sought a diagnosis for a child younger than three. In the 1980s (following the publication of the DSM-III and DSM-III-R), that number increased to 5 percent. After the release of the DSM-IV, it spiked to 17 percent. But instead of focusing on the changes in the diagnostic criteria, he raised the terrifying possibility that pollution, antibiotics, and vaccines were triggering a tsunami of new cases, citing the Leominster “cluster” as a dramatic example. Rimland’s version of the events in the town took hold permanently in the autism parents’ community, becoming part of the growing lore of the epidemic.

1995 年，在收到大量家长咨询后，里姆兰在其通讯中刊登了醒目标题《是否存在孤独症流行？》。他的回答是肯定的："我认为增长是真实的，而不仅仅是认知度的提升。"为证明观点，他提供了一张图表：1965 至 1969 年间，其联络网中仅有 1%的家长为三岁以下子女寻求诊断；1980 年代（DSM-III 及 DSM-III-R 出版后），该比例升至 5%；DSM-IV 发布后，更飙升至 17%。但他并未聚焦诊断标准的变化，反而提出污染、抗生素和疫苗正引发新病例浪潮的可怕可能性，并以莱明斯特"聚集性案例"作为戏剧性例证。里姆兰对事件的解读在孤独症家长群体中根深蒂固，成为日益壮大的流行病传说的一部分。



Considering the potential impact of environmental factors on autism was nothing new for Rimland. Back in 1967, after receiving a number of reports from parents that their children had been adversely affected by the diphtheria-pertussis-tetanus vaccine, he added a question about it to ARI’s evaluation forms. He had good reason to: in the annals of public health, the original form of the DPT vaccine—the only inoculation based on killed whole bacterial cells widely distributed in the United States—was the most “reactogenic” vaccine in the country’s history. Adverse events like seizures, fainting, fevers, swelling, shock, and high-pitched crying for hours were not common. The whole-cell DPT was also bedeviled by quality control failures, unreliable potency, and other serious problems, includings individual batches (known as “hot lots”) that triggered higher-than-usual incidences of side effects before being removed from distribution by the CDC. The whole-cell DPT was scrapped in the United States in favor of the much safer “acellular” form of the inoculation in 1971.

对里姆兰来说，考虑环境因素对孤独症的潜在影响并非新鲜事。早在 1967 年，在收到大量家长报告称孩子因接种白喉-百日咳-破伤风疫苗而出现不良反应后，他就在孤独症研究所的评估表中增加了相关问题。他这么做有充分理由：在公共卫生史上，原始形式的百白破疫苗——美国唯一广泛使用的基于灭活全菌细胞的疫苗——是该国历史上"反应原性"最强的疫苗。癫痫发作、昏厥、发热、肿胀、休克以及持续数小时的高声哭闹等不良反应并不罕见。全细胞百白破疫苗还饱受质量控制失效、效力不稳定及其他严重问题的困扰，包括个别批次（即"热批次"）在疾控中心停止分发前会引发高于正常水平的副作用发生率。1971 年，美国淘汰了全细胞百白破疫苗，转而采用更安全的"无细胞"型疫苗。



One of the key factors in Rimland’s turnabout on the question of the rising rates was a book called DPT: A Shot in the Dark, written by Harris Coulter and Barbara Loe Fisher. In the early 1980s, Fisher was working as a PR consultant in Virginia when her two-and-a-half-year-old son, Christian, received his fourth DPT shot and oral polio inoculation. Until that day, according to Fisher, he was a cheerful and sociable child who spoke in full sentences, read avidly, and could count up to twenty. After his third DPT injection, however, a hard, red lump had appeared on his arm, which a nurse attributed to a “bad lot” of vaccine.

边缘地带在疫苗接种率上升问题上态度转变的关键因素之一，是一本名为《白喉-百日咳-破伤风疫苗：暗夜一针》的著作，作者是哈里斯·库尔特和芭芭拉·洛·费舍尔。20 世纪 80 年代初，费舍尔在弗吉尼亚州担任公关顾问时，她两岁半的儿子克里斯蒂安接种了第四剂白喉-百日咳-破伤风疫苗和口服脊髓灰质炎疫苗。据费舍尔回忆，在那之前，克里斯蒂安是个开朗合群的孩子，能说完整句子，酷爱阅读，还能数到二十。然而，在接种第三剂白喉-百日咳-破伤风疫苗后，他的手臂上出现了一个硬邦邦的红肿块，护士将其归因于"批次不佳"的疫苗。



Within hours of Christian getting his fourth shot, according to Fisher, she found him in a chair staring vacantly, his face pale and his lips blue. When she called out his name, his eyes fluttered and rolled back, and he seemed to fall asleep. She carried him to bed, where he slept for six hours. Fisher woke him, but he was disoriented, and he fell asleep again for another half a day. She would later describe these events as a classic vaccine reaction. In the days and weeks that followed, “Chris became a totally different child,” she testified to a congressional committee in 1999. He never smiled, seemed to have trouble focusing, lost interest in his beloved books, and suffered a series of debilitating infections. He was eventually diagnosed with multiple learning disabilities, including ADHD.

据费舍尔称，克里斯蒂安接种第四针疫苗后数小时内，她发现他瘫坐在椅子上目光呆滞，面色苍白嘴唇发紫。当她呼唤他的名字时，他的眼睛颤动后翻，似乎陷入沉睡。她将他抱到床上，他连续睡了六个小时。费舍尔唤醒他时，他意识模糊，随后又昏睡了整整半天。她后来将这些症状描述为典型的疫苗反应。在随后的数日乃至数周里，"克里斯完全变了一个孩子"，1999 年她在国会委员会作证时说道。他不再微笑，注意力难以集中，对心爱的书籍失去兴趣，并接连遭受一系列消耗体力的感染。最终他被诊断出包括多动症在内的多种学习障碍。



Two years later, Fisher saw an NBC special called “DPT: Vaccine Roulette,” and the pieces fell into place. The broadcast featured a parade of experts who played down the risks of pertussis (which had killed 7,500 children in 1934 alone, out of 265,000 cases) while highlighting the risks of the vaccine, intercut with wrenching footage of brain-damaged children being cared for by their parents. Gordon Stewart, identified as a member of the United Kingdom’s Committee on the Safety of Medicines, described the vaccine as a “crude brew, literally, of all the bacteria and their gross products.” Bobby Young, billed as a former FDA vaccine researcher, warned that DPT shots were capable of turning a healthy child into “a vegetable,” while producer Lea Thompson hinted that the plummeting death rates from diseases like pertussis after the widespread adoption of vaccines was a coincidence caused by better sanitation. Thompson also suggested that savvy British mothers, armed with the knowledge that “the vaccine is worse than the disease,” were opting not to immunize their children. As the daughter of a nurse, Fisher, watching the broadcast, “felt betrayed by a medical profession I had revered all my life.”

两年后，费舍尔看到了 NBC 的一档特别节目《DPT：疫苗轮盘赌》，所有线索终于串联起来。节目中轮番出场的专家们轻描淡写地淡化百日咳的风险（1934 年 26.5 万病例中仅造成 7500 名儿童死亡），却着重强调疫苗的危险性，其间穿插着脑损伤儿童由父母照料的揪心画面。被标注为英国药品安全委员会成员的戈登·斯图尔特将疫苗形容为"字面意义上用所有细菌及其粗制产物熬成的汤药"。自称前 FDA 疫苗研究员的鲍比·杨警告说，DPT 疫苗能让健康儿童变成"植物人"，而制作人莉亚·汤普森则暗示，疫苗普及后百日咳等疾病死亡率骤降，不过是卫生条件改善带来的巧合。汤普森还暗示，深谙"疫苗比疾病更危险"之道的英国母亲们，正选择不为孩子接种疫苗。 作为护士的女儿，费舍尔在观看直播时，"感到被自己一生敬仰的医学界背叛了。""



In truth, Thompson, who was awarded an Emmy for “DPT: Vaccine Roulette,” had consistently exaggerated or distorted the credentials of her cherry-picked “experts.” Young, for example, had never researched bacterial vaccines for the FDA, while Stewart had only provided data to the committee and was well-known in the United Kingdom as a prominent antivaccine activist.

事实上，凭借《DPT：疫苗轮盘赌》获得艾美奖的汤普森，一贯夸大或歪曲她精心挑选的"专家"资质。例如，杨从未为 FDA 研究过细菌疫苗，而斯图尔特仅向委员会提供过数据，且在英国是众所周知的著名反疫苗活动人士。



After airing on WRC-TV in Washington, “DPT: Vaccine Roulette” was rebroadcast on local affiliates coast-to-coast and excerpted at length on the Today show. Pediatricians reported levels of fear among parents unseen since the polio scare of the 1950s, and members of Congress were besieged by constituents demanding immediate changes in national vaccine policy. WRC-TV capitalized on the growing scandal by providing viewers who called the station with the numbers of other callers. Fisher was one of the parents who phoned in, and another was Kathi Williams, whose son also experienced a reaction to a DPT shot. With another parent, Jeff Schwartz, they founded a group called Dissatisfied Parents Together, later renamed the National Vaccine Information Center (NVIC), which became the organizational powerhouse of the movement. (The NVIC is careful not to identify itself as “antivaccine” in its PR materials, instead calling itself “the oldest and largest consumer led organization advocating for the institution of vaccine safety and informed consent protections in the public health system.”)

在华盛顿 WRC-TV 播出后，《DPT：疫苗轮盘赌》在全美各地的本地电视台重播，并在《今日秀》节目中大篇幅摘录。儿科医生报告称，父母们的恐惧程度堪比 1950 年代脊髓灰质炎恐慌时期，国会议员们被选民团团围住，要求立即改变国家疫苗政策。WRC-TV 利用这场日益发酵的丑闻，向致电电视台的观众提供其他来电者的电话号码。费舍尔是打来电话的父母之一，另一位是凯西·威廉姆斯，她的儿子也对 DPT 疫苗注射产生了反应。他们与另一位家长杰夫·施瓦茨共同创立了一个名为"不满父母联盟"的组织，后更名为国家疫苗信息中心（NVIC），该组织成为这场运动的组织核心。（NVIC 在其公关材料中谨慎地不将自己标识为"反疫苗"，而是自称"公共卫生系统中倡导疫苗安全和知情同意保护的最古老、最大的消费者主导组织"。）



To write DPT: A Shot in the Dark, Fisher teamed up with a man named Harris Coulter, a longtime foe of government-mandated vaccination programs with a complex past. Though he is often described on antivaccine websites as a Yale-educated medical historian, he did not study medicine at Yale. In fact, he never took a single course in biology, physiology, or chemistry, and he had no intention of becoming a historian of the field as an undergraduate. Instead, his focus was Russian studies.

为了撰写《DPT：黑暗中的一枪》，费舍尔与一位名叫哈里斯·库尔特的人合作，此人长期反对政府强制疫苗接种计划，背景复杂。尽管反疫苗网站常称其为耶鲁毕业的医学史学家，但他并未在耶鲁学习医学。事实上，他从未修过任何生物学、生理学或化学课程，本科期间也无意成为该领域的史学家。他的专业方向其实是俄罗斯研究。



At the height of the cold war in the early 1960s, Coulter was working in Moscow, translating the Kremlin’s official pronouncements for the U.S. State Department. (During the Warren Commission hearings into the assassination of John F. Kennedy, he served as the official interpreter for Marina Oswald, wife of accused assassin Lee Harvey Oswald.) While vacationing in Paris, Coulter’s wife, Catherine, suffered an allergy attack. Instead of going to see a doctor, they decided to visit a homeopath, because traditional doctoring had never done her much good. The homeopath gave her a remedy that “worked like magic,” Coulter recalled. After a single dose, she was able to eat fish, which had always provoked hives in her before.

20 世纪 60 年代初冷战正酣之际，库尔特在莫斯科为美国国务院翻译克里姆林宫的官方声明。（在沃伦委员会调查约翰·F·肯尼迪遇刺案期间，他曾担任被告李·哈维·奥斯瓦尔德之妻玛丽娜·奥斯瓦尔德的官方翻译。）在巴黎度假时，库尔特的妻子凯瑟琳突发过敏反应。他们没有去看医生，而是决定拜访一位顺势疗法医师，因为传统疗法对她一直收效甚微。这位顺势疗法医师给她开了一剂药，库尔特回忆说"效果立竿见影"。仅服用一次后，她就能吃鱼了——而此前吃鱼总会让她起荨麻疹。



Coulter returned to the United States fascinated by homeopathy. When his thesis on Russian studies at Columbia fell through, he persuaded his graduate advisor to let him write a dissertation on the history of homeopathy instead. He became convinced that homeopathy was superior to mainstream (“allopathic”) medicine but had lost out as the dominant paradigm in the nineteenth century because of widespread corruption in the American Medical Association. His dissertation became the third volume of a series of books called Divided Legacy, which he self-published.

库尔特回到美国后，对顺势疗法深深着迷。当他在哥伦比亚大学关于俄罗斯研究的论文计划搁浅时，他说服研究生导师允许他转而撰写一部关于顺势疗法历史的学位论文。他坚信顺势疗法优于主流（"对抗疗法"）医学，但由于 19 世纪美国医学会普遍存在的腐败现象，顺势疗法未能成为主导范式。他的论文最终成为他自行出版的《分裂的遗产》系列丛书的第三卷。



While researching DPT: A Shot in the Dark, Fisher interviewed families while Coulter filled in the historical background. It’s a terrifying book, depicting pediatric medicine as a horror show of heedless doctors, craven vaccine manufacturers, opportunistic researchers, sleazy government officials, grieving parents, and desperately sick kids who have allegedly been rendered mute, incontinent, and permanently disabled by the inoculations that were supposed to keep them safe. Throughout the book, infants recoil from their doctors’ overeager needles, shrieking in primal terror as the shots wreak havoc in their brains.

在撰写《白喉-百日咳-破伤风疫苗：暗箭难防》的研究过程中，费希尔负责采访家庭，而库尔特则负责补充历史背景。这是一本令人不寒而栗的著作，将儿科医学描绘成一场恐怖秀：鲁莽的医生、懦弱的疫苗制造商、投机取巧的研究人员、卑鄙的政府官员、悲痛欲绝的父母，以及那些据称因本应保护他们的疫苗而变得失语、失禁、永久残疾的垂死儿童。全书随处可见婴儿在医生急不可耐的针头前退缩，在疫苗对大脑造成破坏时发出原始恐惧的尖叫。



DPT: A Shot in the Dark was much more than an exposé of the risks of a vaccine—it was a scathing critique of the whole apparatus of mainstream medicine, including the process of peer review and the use of placebo-controlled trials in drug testing. Clearly, for Coulter, the book was homeopathy’s long-overdue revenge against the AMA and a call to arms against an inhumane society that puts the good of the many over the fate of the vulnerable few who suffer violent reactions to vaccines. “I do know that God gave me a perfect child. I was so happy when he was born,” says one mother. “He was so beautiful, with ten toes and ten fingers. God gave me a perfect child, and man, with his own ways, damaged God’s perfect work.”

《DPT：暗夜一枪》远不止是揭露疫苗风险的调查报告——它是对整个主流医学体系的尖锐批判，包括同行评审机制和药物试验中安慰剂对照组的应用。显然，在库尔特看来，这本书是顺势疗法对美国医学会迟来的复仇，更是向非人道社会发出的战斗檄文——这个社会将多数人的利益置于因疫苗产生剧烈反应的少数脆弱者命运之上。"我确信上帝赐予我一个完美的孩子，"一位母亲说道，"他出生时我多么幸福，十指十趾完美无瑕。上帝赐予我完美的杰作，而人类却用自以为是的方式，损毁了上帝完美的造物。"



The notion that children with learning disabilities are damaged goods runs through the book; the authors refer to them as “vaccine-injured” instead of as dyslexic or autistic and portray them as helplessly entombed inside their own bodies. “She understands everything, but she can’t get it out,” another mother says. “You can see it in her little eyes. It’s all there, but she can’t get it out the way she wants to. Sometimes her little voice quavers because she tries so hard. You can see she’s got it but it’s trapped.”

书中贯穿了一种观念，即学习障碍儿童是“受损的次品”；作者称他们为“疫苗受害者”，而非阅读障碍或孤独谱系儿童，并将他们描绘成无助地困在自己身体里的状态。“她什么都懂，但就是表达不出来，”另一位母亲说道，“你能从她的小眼睛里看出来。一切都在那里，但她无法按自己想要的方式表达出来。有时她的小声音会颤抖，因为她太努力了。你能看出她明白，但那些想法被困住了。”



The book’s publication inspired a public uproar that culminated in a series of congressional hearings and a wave of reforms. The National Childhood Vaccine Injury Act, passed in 1986, set up a federal vaccine injury compensation program and created a federal Vaccine Adverse Events Reporting System (VAERS) to enable public-health authorities and consumers to track emerging problems with immunization programs nationwide. In that sense, the book accomplished what it had set out to do. Coulter, however, was not nearly through with his life’s work. In the years following the publication of A Shot in the Dark, his views became even more extreme.

该书的出版引发了公众的强烈反响，最终导致一系列国会听证会和改革浪潮。1986 年通过的《国家儿童疫苗伤害法案》设立了联邦疫苗伤害赔偿计划，并创建了联邦疫苗不良事件报告系统（VAERS），使公共卫生当局和消费者能够追踪全国免疫计划中出现的各种问题。从这个意义上说，这本书实现了其初衷。然而，库尔特远未完成他毕生的事业。在《暗夜一针》出版后的几年里，他的观点变得更加极端。



At the height of the AIDS epidemic, Coulter theorized that human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) was not responsible for the illness, which he believed was a form of syphilis that specifically targets drug users and people living “the gay lifestyle . . . which involves very heavy consumption of medications.” Though autism was barely mentioned in A Shot in the Dark, it was the central subject of his next book, Vaccination, Social Violence, and Criminality, in which he proposed that rising rates of autism, homosexuality, obesity, dyslexia, ADHD, drug abuse, epilepsy, juvenile delinquency, and spree killings were all expressions of an epidemic of encephalitis caused by mandatory vaccine programs. Furthermore, he claimed that these facts were well-known in the medical community but were being covered up by a vast conspiracy. Indeed, he claimed that this conspiracy was so vast that “it is not easy to discern the outlines of the incubus which the vaccination program has loosed upon us.”

在艾滋病疫情最严重时期，库尔特提出理论称人类免疫缺陷病毒并非致病元凶，他认为这种疾病是一种专门针对吸毒者及"沉溺于大量用药的同性恋生活方式"人群的梅毒变种。尽管《暗箭伤人》中鲜少提及孤独症，但这正是他下一部著作《疫苗接种、社会暴力与犯罪》的核心议题。他在书中提出，孤独症、同性恋、肥胖症、阅读障碍、多动症、药物滥用、癫痫、青少年犯罪及连环杀人案发病率的攀升，都是强制性疫苗计划引发的脑炎疫情的表现形式。此外，他声称这些事实在医学界广为人知，却被庞大的阴谋集团掩盖。他甚至宣称这个阴谋规模之大，以至于"我们难以看清疫苗接种计划释放的梦魇轮廓"。



One night in February 1995, Rimland was at home watching a talk show about the risks of vaccination when he noticed that several of the mothers interviewed for the broadcast referred to Coulter and his work. After looking into it, he came to believe that Coulter had found the elusive solution to the puzzle of the rising autism rates. That fall, he published his full-page editorial declaring his belief in an autism epidemic.

1995 年 2 月的一个夜晚，里姆兰在家观看一档关于疫苗接种风险的脱口秀节目时，注意到节目中受访的几位母亲都提到了库尔特及其研究成果。经过深入调查，他开始相信库尔特找到了孤独症发病率持续攀升这一谜题的答案。同年秋天，他发表整版社论，公开宣称自己相信孤独症正在流行。



Rimland’s endorsement gave Coulter’s fringe theories about autism, encephalitis, and vaccines a reach they would never have had otherwise while effectively laundering them of their more unsavory aspects, such as his association of autism and criminal behavior. By then, Coulter had already moved on to the next stage of his career, which was helping a Russian immunologist to develop a vaccine derived from human placenta that would treat cancer. The outcome of the experimental trials, conducted on patients in Moscow and the Bahamas, was decidedly mixed—several of the patients died anyway. But Coulter was undaunted, seeing in the patients’ responses to the vaccine (including fevers, headaches, and increased pain at sites of previous surgical operations) vivid demonstrations of homeopathic principles.

里姆兰德的背书让库尔特关于孤独症、脑炎和疫苗的边缘理论获得了前所未有的影响力，同时有效掩盖了其更令人不快的方面，例如他将孤独症与犯罪行为联系起来。到那时，库尔特已进入职业生涯的下一个阶段——协助一位俄罗斯免疫学家开发一种源自人类胎盘的癌症治疗疫苗。在莫斯科和巴哈马患者身上进行的试验结果好坏参半——多名患者最终仍不幸去世。但库尔特并未气馁，他将患者对疫苗的反应（包括发烧、头痛以及既往手术部位疼痛加剧）视为顺势疗法原理的生动例证。



VI



While Rimland was instrumental in spreading Coulter’s ideas within the autism parents’ community, a young gastroenterologist in England named Andrew Wakefield was responsible for introducing them into the mainstream by claiming to have discovered a potential mechanism by which the combination measles-mumps-rubella (MMR) vaccine causes brain injury.

当里姆兰德在孤独症家长群体中传播库尔特观点时，英国一位名叫安德鲁·韦克菲尔德的年轻胃肠病学家则通过声称发现麻疹-腮腺炎-风疹三联疫苗（MMR）可能导致脑损伤的潜在机制，将这些理论引入主流视野。



On February 28, 1998, Wakefield held a press conference at Royal Free Hospital in Hampstead, North London. The occasion was the publication of his new case series in the Lancet, one of Britain’s most prestigious medical journals. To ensure that the press conference would be a major event, the hospital’s public relations staff had taken the unusual step of sending out a twenty-minute promotional video to journalists beforehand, featuring graphic footage of children who were obviously in agony. The video was accompanied by a press release, which read in part, “Researchers at the Royal Free Hospital School of Medicine may have discovered a new syndrome involving a new inflammatory bowel disease and autism.” As a result, the room was packed with reporters.

1998 年 2 月 28 日，韦克菲尔德在伦敦北部汉普斯特德的皇家自由医院召开了一场新闻发布会。这场发布会是为了庆祝他的新病例系列研究在英国最负盛名的医学期刊之一《柳叶刀》上发表。为确保发布会能引起轰动，医院公关人员采取了非同寻常的举措——提前向记者发送了一段二十分钟的宣传视频，其中包含明显处于痛苦中的儿童的特写画面。视频附有一份新闻稿，部分内容写道："皇家自由医院医学院的研究人员可能发现了一种涉及新型炎症性肠病和孤独症的新综合征。"因此，发布会现场挤满了记者。



Wakefield seemed well positioned to make a breakthrough in pediatric gastroenterology. In 1987, he took a post as the head of the Royal Free Hospital’s Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) Study Group, where the major focus of his work became investigating links between viruses and Crohn’s disease. In a series of studies published in the 1990s, Wakefield and his colleagues zeroed in on the measles virus as potentially contributing to Crohn’s and IBD. These studies were considered groundbreaking when they were first published and attracted media attention to the Royal Free Hospital’s medical school, which was trying to shed its image as a backwater of inconsequential research. But they eventually came under fire as other researchers either failed to replicate Wakefield’s results or directly refuted them.

韦克菲尔德似乎在儿科胃肠病学领域占据有利地位，有望取得突破。1987 年，他担任皇家自由医院炎症性肠病研究小组负责人，主要研究方向是病毒与克罗恩病之间的关联。在 20 世纪 90 年代发表的一系列研究中，韦克菲尔德及其同事将麻疹病毒锁定为可能诱发克罗恩病和炎症性肠病的潜在因素。这些研究最初发表时被视为开创性成果，为当时正试图摆脱"无关紧要的研究荒漠"形象的皇家自由医院医学院吸引了媒体关注。但随着其他研究者要么无法复现韦克菲尔德的研究结果，要么直接驳斥其结论，这些研究最终遭到猛烈抨击。



Undaunted, Wakefield continued to search for a measles-Crohn’s connection, eventually publishing a study in the Lancet that seemed to validate his theory. After reviewing twenty-five thousand patient records taken at the University Hospital in Uppsala, Sweden, in the 1940s, Wakefield and co-author Anders Ekbom found three cases of children born of measles-infected mothers who developed Crohn’s later in life. On the basis of these cases, Wakefield and Ekbom drew a sweeping and dramatic conclusion: “Our study suggests that exposure to measles virus in utero is a major risk factor for the development of Crohn’s disease later in life; such early exposure appears to incur a risk of extensive, aggressive disease.”

韦克菲尔德并未气馁，继续寻找麻疹与克罗恩病的关联，最终在《柳叶刀》上发表了一项看似证实其理论的研究。通过查阅 20 世纪 40 年代瑞典乌普萨拉大学医院保存的 2.5 万份病历，韦克菲尔德与合著者安德斯·埃克博姆发现三例母亲孕期感染麻疹、子女成年后罹患克罗恩病的病例。基于这些案例，两人得出一个笼统而惊人的结论："我们的研究表明，子宫内接触麻疹病毒是日后罹患克罗恩病的主要风险因素；这种早期暴露似乎会导致广泛且侵袭性强的疾病风险。"



Wakefield then turned his focus of his investigations toward the MMR vaccine, which is formulated with live viruses in a weakened state to activate the body’s immune response. It was at this point that his work began to attract pointed criticism from British public-health officials who were well aware of the potentially catastrophic danger of shaking public confidence in the safety of a vaccine that prevents millions of deaths worldwide every year. (The World Health Organization estimates that, in 2000 alone, thirty to forty million people developed measles, resulting in 777,000 deaths, most of them in sub-Saharan Africa, where rates of inoculation and standards of medical care are lower.) Eventually, Wakefield’s dean at the medical school, Arie Zuckerman, privately expressed concern about the “unwelcome controversy” gathering around Wakefield’s work on Crohn’s and the MMR to the United Kingdom’s chief medical officer, Kenneth Calman. In retrospect, that controversy was a teapot-tempest compared to the storm on the horizon.

韦克菲尔德随后将调查重点转向 MMR 疫苗，这种疫苗采用减毒活病毒配制，旨在激活人体免疫反应。正是在这个节点上，他的研究开始招致英国公共卫生官员的尖锐批评——这些官员深知，动摇公众对这款每年挽救全球数百万人生命的疫苗安全性的信心，可能带来灾难性后果。（世界卫生组织估计，仅 2000 年就有 3000 万至 4000 万人感染麻疹，导致 77.7 万人死亡，其中多数发生在疫苗接种率和医疗水平较低的撒哈拉以南非洲地区。）最终，韦克菲尔德所在医学院的院长阿里·祖克曼私下向英国首席医疗官肯尼思·卡尔曼表达了担忧，称围绕韦克菲尔德关于克罗恩病与 MMR 疫苗的研究所引发的"不受欢迎的争议"正在发酵。事后看来，这场争议与即将到来的风暴相比，不过是茶杯里的风波。



In 1995, Wakefield got a call from the mother of an autistic boy that left him profoundly distressed. At first, he didn’t know why she was calling him, a gastroenterologist. “I didn’t know anything about autism,” he later admitted. According to Wakefield, the mother explained that her son had serious bowel problems, including diarrhea and incontinence up to twelve times a day. He also seemed to be in pain and was violent and self-injurious. She said that he had been developing “perfectly normally” until he received an MMR vaccine. Shortly after getting the shot, the mother said, he suffered a high fever, after which point he deteriorated rapidly and lost the ability to speak. Wakefield would later claim that he was compelled to undertake further research on autism after receiving “five calls in two days” like this. It turns out that his controversial work on Crohn’s disease had already made him a respected figure among antivaccine activists, and the mothers who called him were members of the same antivaccine network.

1995 年，韦克菲尔德接到一位孤独症男孩母亲的电话，这通电话让他深感痛苦。起初，他并不明白这位母亲为何会联系自己这位胃肠病专家。"我当时对孤独症一无所知，"他后来承认道。据韦克菲尔德描述，这位母亲解释说，她的儿子存在严重的肠道问题，包括每天多达十二次的腹泻和失禁。孩子似乎还承受着疼痛，并表现出暴力和自残行为。她说，儿子在接种 MMR 疫苗前一直"发育得完全正常"。这位母亲表示，接种疫苗后不久，孩子就发起了高烧，此后病情迅速恶化，并丧失了语言能力。韦克菲尔德后来声称，在"两天内接到五个类似电话"后，他被迫对孤独症展开进一步研究。事实证明，他关于克罗恩病的争议性研究早已使他成为反疫苗活动人士中备受尊敬的人物，而打电话给他的母亲们正是同一反疫苗网络的成员。



Like the Uppsala study, Wakefield’s 1998 case series hinged on a small sample of patients; in this case, a dozen children. In cautious and qualified language, his team reported that the onset of the “behavioral symptoms” of autism had been “associated by the parents” with administration of the MMR vaccine in eight of the twelve cases. The researchers claimed that all of the children showed evidence of intestinal abnormalities, ranging from “patchy chronic inflammation” to “ulceration.” In most of these cases, they reported, “after a period of apparent normality,” the children had dramatically regressed in the wake of receiving the vaccine.

与乌普萨拉研究类似，韦克菲尔德 1998 年的病例系列研究同样基于少量患者样本——这次是 12 名儿童。研究团队以谨慎克制的措辞报告称，在 12 例病例中有 8 例的孤独症"行为症状"发作被"家长认为"与接种 MMR 疫苗存在关联。研究人员声称所有儿童均出现肠道异常迹象，从"斑片状慢性炎症"到"溃疡"不等。他们指出，多数病例中，儿童在经历"一段看似正常的时期后"，接种疫苗后出现了显著的功能退化。



Building on Wakefield’s earlier investigations of viruses, vaccines, and intestinal inflammation, as well as the work of other scientists, the researchers postulated that partially digested proteins from grains and dairy products were leaking into the children’s bloodstreams through the walls of their damaged intestines. Once there, they theorized, these proteins—known as opioid peptides—were carried to the developing brain, where they caused disruption of neural regulation and growth, causing a sudden and dramatic loss of skills. Wakefield eventually christened this syndrome “autistic enterocolitis.”

基于韦克菲尔德早期对病毒、疫苗和肠道炎症的研究，以及其他科学家的成果，研究人员推测，来自谷物和乳制品中未完全消化的蛋白质正通过受损的肠壁渗入儿童的血液。他们提出理论认为，这些被称为阿片肽的蛋白质一旦进入血液，就会被输送到发育中的大脑，在那里干扰神经调节和生长，导致技能的突然且显著丧失。韦克菲尔德最终将这种综合征命名为“孤独谱系肠道炎”。



The concept of opioid peptides disrupting brain development was not new, particularly among the clinicians and science-savvy parents in Rimland’s network, who had dubbed the same phenomenon “leaky gut syndrome.” They had been frustrated for years that their observations of their children’s gastrointestinal distress and fussy food preferences were generally dismissed by doctors as just another inexplicable aspect of a mysterious condition. Many parents, following the advice in Karyn Seroussi’s book, had found that removing grains and milk (sources of gluten and casein respectively) from the child’s diet not only relieved cramping, diarrhea, and bloating but also seemed to improve the child’s level of social engagement. It is perhaps not surprising that children who would be happier to persist in eating only a single dish at every meal (like Henry Cavendish’s leg of mutton, Joseph Sullivan’s cheese curls, and Leo Rosa’s naan) would eventually develop GI issues.

阿片肽干扰大脑发育的概念并非新观点，尤其在里姆兰网络中的临床医生和具备科学素养的家长群体中，他们早已将类似现象称为"肠漏综合征"。多年来，这些家长一直感到沮丧：他们观察到孩子出现胃肠道不适和挑食偏食的症状，但医生通常将这些表现视为某种神秘疾病中又一个难以解释的特征。许多家长遵循卡琳·塞鲁西著作中的建议，从孩子饮食中剔除谷物和牛奶（分别含有麸质和酪蛋白），不仅缓解了痉挛、腹泻和腹胀症状，还似乎改善了孩子的社交参与度。那些宁愿每餐只吃单一食物（如亨利·卡文迪什的羊腿、约瑟夫·沙利文的芝士卷、利奥·罗莎的馕饼）的孩子最终出现胃肠道问题，这或许并不令人意外。



In addition, episodes of fever, rashes, convulsions, and other usually transitory reactions to vaccines (understandably terrifying to parents) are well documented in the annals of immunology, as Coulter and Fisher had documented extensively in their book. Very rarely, these negative reactions are neither mild nor transitory, resulting in lifelong incapacity or death. The fact that modern medicine is built on trade-offs of socially acceptable risks (most lifesaving drugs have serious side effects, and every major surgery or anesthesia is potentially fatal) is precisely why Coulter favored homeopathy. While it may never cure, it also never kills directly. The most novel aspect of Wakefield’s paper was the supreme confidence with which he turned this confluence of disparate phenomena into a theory of autism causation.

此外，发烧、皮疹、惊厥及其他通常短暂的疫苗反应（对父母而言，这无疑是令人恐惧的）在免疫学史上早有详尽记载，正如库尔特和费希尔在其著作中广泛记录的那样。极少数情况下，这些不良反应既非轻微也非短暂，可能导致终身残疾或死亡。现代医学建立在社会可接受风险权衡的基础之上（大多数救命药物都有严重副作用，每次重大手术或麻醉都可能致命），这正是库尔特推崇顺势疗法的原因。顺势疗法或许永远无法治愈疾病，但也绝不会直接导致死亡。韦克菲尔德论文中最具创新之处，在于他以极度自信将这一系列互不相关的现象整合成一套关于孤独症成因的理论。



After peer reviewers of an early draft expressed concerns about the study’s language and potential impact, the Lancet’s editor requested a rewrite and slapped an “Early Report” slug on the article in print, emphasizing its speculative nature. The researchers took care to note in the discussion section, “We did not prove an association between measles, mumps, and rubella vaccine and the syndrome described. Virological studies are under way that may help to resolve this issue. If there is a causal link between measles, mumps, and rubella vaccine and this syndrome, a rising incidence might be anticipated after the introduction of this vaccine in the UK in 1988. Published evidence is inadequate to show whether there is a change in incidence or a link with measles, mumps, and rubella vaccine.”

在早期草稿的同行评审人对该研究的语言表述及潜在影响表示担忧后，《柳叶刀》编辑要求重写稿件，并在印刷版文章上标注了“早期报告”标签，强调其推测性质。研究人员在讨论部分谨慎注明：“我们并未证明麻疹、腮腺炎、风疹疫苗与所述综合征之间存在关联。目前正在进行病毒学研究，可能有助于解决这一问题。若麻疹、腮腺炎、风疹疫苗与该综合征存在因果关系，则自 1988 年该疫苗在英国引入后，其发病率可能上升。现有已发表的证据不足以说明发病率是否发生变化，或是否与麻疹、腮腺炎、风疹疫苗存在关联。”



In the promotional video and the press conference itself, however, Wakefield was not nearly so tentative or cautious. Instead, he suggested that his study was just the latest evidence challenging the safety of the MMR. A formidably built man with deep-set blue eyes and a crisp, no-nonsense manner, Wakefield carried himself like a man who was above the fray. At the press conference, he used every ounce of this gravitas to cast a pall of doubt over the MMR vaccine, venturing much further into the realm of conjecture than the wording of his paper suggested.

然而，在宣传视频和新闻发布会现场，韦克菲尔德的表现远非如此犹豫或谨慎。相反，他暗示自己的研究只是质疑 MMR 疫苗安全性的最新证据。这位身材魁梧、蓝眼睛深陷、作风干脆利落的学者，举手投足间俨然一副超脱纷争的姿态。在新闻发布会上，他竭尽全力利用这份庄重感，为 MMR 疫苗蒙上怀疑的阴影，其推测范围远超论文措辞所暗示的程度。



“This is a moral issue for me,” Wakefield intoned gravely. “With the debate over MMR that has started, I cannot support the continued use of the three vaccines given together.” He struck a similarly ominous tone in the promotional video, insisting that the study “certainly raises a question mark” over the vaccine while acknowledging that “there is no proven link as such,” but then adding, “It is our suspicion that there may well be . . . I have to say that there is sufficient anxiety in my own mind of the safety.”

“这对我来说是个道德问题，”韦克菲尔德严肃地宣称，“随着关于 MMR 的辩论开始，我无法支持继续使用这三种联合疫苗。”他在宣传视频中也同样语带不祥，坚称这项研究“无疑为疫苗打上了问号”，同时承认“目前尚无确凿证据表明两者存在关联”，但随即补充道：“我们怀疑很可能存在……我必须说，我内心对安全性已产生足够的不安。”



Predictably, most of the reporters assembled in the room downplayed or ignored the caveats of Wakefield’s colleagues and kicked into horror-movie mode. “Scientists’ Warning Prompts Fears over Measles Vaccine,” blared the headline on the London Evening Standard. “Doctors Warn of a New Child Vaccine Danger,” screamed the Independent. “Undetected Bowel Illness Led to Baby’s Misery,” wailed the Guardian. “Measles Jab Turned My Son into an Autistic Child,” howled the Daily Record. The dependably hyperbolic Daily Mail—which had been sowing the seeds of mass panic for months with headlines like “Both of My Little Boys Are Autistic and My Wonderful Marriage Is in Tatters, Our Lives Have Been Ruined by a Vaccine”—ran with “Ban Three-in-One Jab Urge Doctors after New Fears,” as if the Royal College of Paediatrics had issued an emergency alert.

不出所料，房间里聚集的大多数记者要么淡化或忽略韦克菲尔德同事提出的警告，要么直接切换到恐怖片模式。《伦敦标准晚报》的标题赫然写着"科学家警告引发麻疹疫苗恐慌"，《独立报》惊呼"医生警告儿童疫苗新危险"，《卫报》哀叹"未发现的肠道疾病导致婴儿痛苦"，《每日纪事报》咆哮"麻疹疫苗让我儿子变成孤独症儿童"。一贯夸张的《每日邮报》——几个月来一直用"我的两个小男孩都患有孤独症，我美好的婚姻支离破碎，疫苗毁了我们的人生"这样的标题播下大规模恐慌的种子——这次推出了"医生因新担忧呼吁禁止三联疫苗"，仿佛皇家儿科医学院发布了紧急警报。



This press coverage sent shock waves through the autism parents’ community and far beyond. For Rimland, the Wakefield study was the smoking gun he’d been waiting for. In the coming years, many members of his network would become convinced that autism was the product of multiple insults to a child’s developing brain from vaccines, vaccine preservatives, or both. Activists like Fisher set their sights on eliminating the use of a specific vaccine preservative, thimerosal, which became the subject of a raging worldwide debate. As public health authorities rushed to reassure terrified parents that the vaccine was safe, Rimland thundered in a press release, “It is ludicrous to claim that the link between many cases of autism and vaccination is just coincidental. Dr. Wakefield’s group has greatly expanded our understanding of one possible mechanism.”

这篇新闻报道在孤独症家长群体及更广泛的社会中引起了轩然大波。对于里姆兰而言，韦克菲尔德的研究正是他等待已久的铁证。在接下来的几年里，他网络中的许多成员逐渐相信，孤独症是疫苗、疫苗防腐剂或两者共同对儿童发育中的大脑造成多重伤害的结果。像费舍尔这样的活动家将目标锁定在消除一种特定疫苗防腐剂——硫柳汞的使用上，这引发了全球范围内的激烈争论。当公共卫生部门急于安抚恐慌的家长，宣称疫苗安全时，里姆兰在一份新闻稿中怒斥道：“声称许多孤独症病例与疫苗接种之间的联系纯属巧合，这简直荒谬至极。韦克菲尔德博士的研究团队极大地拓展了我们对其中一种可能机制的理解。”



After an outcry from organizations like Fisher’s National Vaccine Information Center, the Centers for Disease Control in Atlanta and the American Academy of Pediatrics asked vaccine manufacturers to remove thimerosal from their products, and the preservative was quickly phased out of most vaccines in the United States and Europe. While later studies would show that this had no impact on rising rates of autism diagnosis, this precautionary step had the unintended effect of appearing to provide an official imprimatur to parental anxieties about mercury. News of the link between autism and vaccines spread through the parent-run e-mail lists and websites proliferating across the Internet, stoked by an endless stream of “balanced” stories in major media outlets by journalists who found the David-and-Goliath angle—a visionary doctor backed by an army of warrior moms going up against a conspiracy between Big Pharma and government officials—irresistible. In November 2000, Wakefield appeared on 60 Minutes to blame the MMR for triggering an epidemic of autism, framed by frightening before-and-after footage of a boy who he claimed was made autistic by a vaccine.

在费舍尔国家疫苗信息中心等组织的强烈抗议下，亚特兰大疾病控制中心和美国儿科学会要求疫苗生产商从产品中去除硫柳汞，这种防腐剂很快在美国和欧洲的大多数疫苗中被淘汰。尽管后来的研究表明，这对孤独症诊断率的上升没有影响，但这一预防措施却产生了意想不到的效果——似乎为家长对汞的焦虑提供了官方背书。孤独症与疫苗关联的消息通过家长运营的电子邮件列表和网站迅速在互联网上蔓延，主流媒体记者们不断炮制"平衡"报道，他们发现这个大卫与歌利亚式的故事——一位富有远见的医生得到一群战士妈妈的支持，对抗大药厂与政府官员的阴谋——令人难以抗拒。2000 年 11 月，韦克菲尔德出现在《60 分钟》节目中，指责麻腮风三联疫苗引发了孤独症疫情，节目还播放了一个男孩令人震惊的前后对比视频，他声称这个孩子因疫苗而患上孤独症。



Rates of immunization for measles, mumps, and pertussis began to fall worldwide. For parents in countries where these communicable diseases were rare, nursing a kid through a week of the measles seemed like a small price to pay for dodging the bullet of a lifelong developmental disorder. Self-published books started popping up like Melanie’s Marvelous Measles, described by its author, Stephanie Messenger, as a story that “takes children aged 4–10 years on a journey of discovering about the ineffectiveness of vaccinations, while teaching them to embrace childhood disease.” Similarly, some parents began hosting “pox parties”—promoted in members-only online networks—where their children were intentionally exposed to diseases like chicken pox.

全球麻疹、腮腺炎和百日咳的疫苗接种率开始下降。在一些这些传染病已十分罕见的国家，家长们认为，让孩子经历一周的麻疹，比起终身发育障碍的风险，似乎是个更小的代价。自助出版的书籍开始涌现，比如《梅兰妮的神奇麻疹》，作者斯蒂芬妮·梅辛杰将其描述为一个“带领 4-10 岁儿童探索疫苗接种无效性，同时教导他们接纳童年疾病”的故事。同样，一些家长开始举办“痘疹派对”——在会员制网络社群中推广——故意让自家孩子接触水痘等疾病。



—



WAKEFIELD’S CASE SERIES BECAME one of the most influential journal articles in the history of public health—a considerable accomplishment for someone who admits that he knew nothing about autism before he undertook the study. But it would also become one of the most widely and thoroughly refuted. Investigations and inquiries launched in the years following its publication by journalist Brian Deer, the General Medical Council, the British Medical Journal, and other watchdogs uncovered numerous problems with its methodology, ethics, and reporting.

韦克菲尔德的病例系列研究成为公共卫生史上最具影响力的期刊文章之一——对于一个承认在研究之前对孤独症一无所知的人来说，这堪称一项重大成就。但这项研究也成为了被最广泛、最彻底驳斥的研究之一。在其发表后的几年里，记者布莱恩·迪尔、英国综合医学委员会、英国医学杂志及其他监督机构发起的调查和质询，揭示了其方法论、伦理规范及报告撰写方面存在的诸多问题。



Children described in the study as “normal” before receiving the vaccine had actually been flagged for developmental issues such as hand flapping and language delay. Two children who were reported to have suffered from autistic enterocolitis after the MMR had never been diagnosed with autism at all. Wakefield had also been creative in calculating the time between administration of the vaccine and the onset of regression, making it appear as if the children had suffered symptoms within days of receiving the MMR, when his own records showed that weeks or months had elapsed. The father of one boy in the study told Deer, “If my son really is Patient 11, then the Lancet article is simply an outright fabrication.”

研究中被描述为接种疫苗前“正常”的儿童，实际上早已被标记出存在手部拍打、语言发育迟缓等发育问题。两名据称在接种 MMR 疫苗后患上孤独症相关性小肠结肠炎的儿童，从未被确诊过孤独谱系。韦克菲尔德在计算疫苗接种与症状出现的时间间隔时也进行了"创造性处理"，使儿童看起来在接种 MMR 后数日内就出现症状，而他的原始记录显示实际间隔了数周甚至数月。研究中一名男童的父亲告诉迪尔："如果我的儿子真的是第 11 号患者，那么《柳叶刀》的那篇文章纯粹就是彻头彻尾的捏造。"



Even more damningly, Deer discovered that Wakefield had failed to disclose to the Lancet editors a substantial financial agreement with lawyers planning to mount a class-action suit against vaccine manufacturers. As these and other irregularities came to light, ten of the study’s co-authors took their names off the paper, and the study itself was finally retracted by the Lancet in 2004. Wakefield was stripped of his medical license in England by the General Medical Council in 2010, and the editors of the British Medical Journal denounced his study as “an elaborate fraud” in 2011.

更令人震惊的是，迪尔发现韦克菲尔德未向《柳叶刀》编辑披露一项重大财务协议——他与计划对疫苗制造商提起集体诉讼的律师达成了经济合作。随着这些及其他违规行为曝光，该研究的十位共同作者从论文中撤名，该研究最终于 2004 年被《柳叶刀》撤回。2010 年，英国综合医学委员会吊销了韦克菲尔德的医师执照，2011 年《英国医学杂志》编辑更将其研究斥为"精心策划的骗局"。



Multiple attempts by independent researchers to confirm a link between autism and the MMR vaccine have failed. In 2003, researchers writing for the Archives of Pediatrics and Adolescent Medicine performed a systematic meta-analysis of a dozen epidemiological studies and concluded, “The current literature does not suggest an association between ASD and the MMR vaccine. While the risk of autism from MMR remains theoretical, the consequences of not vaccinating are real.”

独立研究人员多次尝试证实孤独症与麻腮风三联疫苗之间的关联均告失败。2003 年，为《儿科学与青少年医学档案》撰稿的研究人员对十余项流行病学研究进行了系统性荟萃分析，结论指出："现有文献并未表明孤独谱系障碍与麻腮风疫苗存在关联。虽然疫苗导致孤独症的风险仍停留在理论层面，但拒绝接种的后果却是真实存在的。"



—



FROM THEIR OFFICE AT the Lorna Wing Centre for Autism a few miles outside London, Lorna and Judith regarded the vaccine controversy with a sense of tragic inevitability. There was no question in their minds that the changes they wrought to the DSM criteria were the primary factor responsible for rising numbers. Chatting over tea with the two senior researchers in 2011, overlooking the quiet garden they had planted for the benefit of the children at the center, was like sitting in the calm eye of a hurricane that was blowing all over the world.

在距离伦敦几英里外的洛娜·温孤独症中心办公室里，洛娜和朱迪丝带着一种宿命般的悲剧感看待这场疫苗争议。她们心中毫无疑虑：正是她们对《精神障碍诊断与统计手册》诊断标准的修改，成为孤独谱系障碍人数上升的首要原因。2011 年，与这两位资深研究员喝茶聊天时，俯瞰着她们为中心儿童精心栽种的静谧花园，仿佛置身于席卷全球的飓风眼中。



“It’s a question of diagnosis,” Lorna said firmly. By expanding Kanner’s narrow definition of his syndrome to include more mildly impaired children and adults, she had expected estimates of autism prevalence to rise. That was precisely the point: making the diagnosis available to more people, so that they and their families wouldn’t have to struggle along without help as they had in the 1960s. “These people have always existed,” she said.

“这是诊断标准的问题，”洛娜坚定地说。通过将坎纳对孤独症综合征的狭隘定义扩展至包含更多轻度障碍的儿童和成人，她早已预料到孤独谱系障碍发生率的估算会上升。这正是关键所在：让更多人能够获得诊断，使他们及其家人不必像 20 世纪 60 年代那样在无助中挣扎。“这些人一直存在，”她说。



Judith agreed. “We were not surprised when people started saying it was an epidemic,” she said. “Obviously, by broadening the spectrum, you’re going to get higher numbers. We’ve been saying this all along, but people were just pooh-poohing us.”

朱迪丝表示赞同。“当人们开始说这是一种流行病时，我们并不感到惊讶，”她说，“显然，通过扩大谱系范围，数据自然会上升。我们一直这么说，但人们总是不以为然。”



Lorna suggested that blurring the boundaries between autism and eccentricity has also inevitably contributed to the widespread perception that the condition is on the rise. After their development of the concept of Asperger’s syndrome, Lorna and Judith began to see traits of the syndrome as common in the people around them, particularly in the families of children brought to the center for evaluation and workers in technical fields. “It’s very difficult to draw the lines, certainly between Kanner’s and Asperger’s,” Lorna said, “but also between Asperger’s and normality.”

洛娜认为，模糊孤独症与怪癖之间的界限，也必然加剧了人们普遍认为该病症正在蔓延的看法。在她们提出阿斯伯格综合征概念后，洛娜和朱迪丝开始发现，周围许多人身上都带有该综合征的特征，尤其是那些被带到中心进行评估的儿童家属以及技术领域工作者。“要划清界限非常困难——不仅是在坎纳综合征与阿斯伯格综合征之间，”洛娜说，“在阿斯伯格综合征与正常状态之间也是如此。”



Another reason that autistic people have become more visible, Judith proposed, is that gender roles have become more fluid in recent decades. “In traditional British life, men worked, and were cared for by their wives, who didn’t work. They were the caregivers and men were the breadwinners,” she said. “I see many, many men who refer themselves here for diagnosis who would never have even thought they had a problem in times gone by because they were protected by the family and society.” Lorna added that if a “dash of autism” is essential for success in science and art, as Asperger suggested, perhaps the advent of the Internet has accelerated “an evolutionary tendency in that direction.”

朱迪丝提出，孤独谱系群体变得更加可见的另一个原因是，近几十年来性别角色变得更加流动。她说："在传统的英国生活中，男性工作，由不工作的妻子照顾。她们是照顾者，男性是养家糊口的人。我看到许多许多男性来这里寻求诊断，在过去他们甚至不会认为自己有问题，因为他们受到家庭和社会的保护。"洛娜补充说，如果正如阿斯伯格所暗示的那样，'一丝孤独特质'是科学和艺术成功的关键，那么互联网的出现可能加速了'朝着这个方向的进化趋势'。"



But she had no illusions about how disabling the core features of autism can be, even with the best kinds of care. By the time I spoke with her, Lorna and her husband, John, had buried their beloved Susie, who died in 2005 at age forty-nine after the hormonal storms of menopause gave her a compulsion to drink excessive quantities of water. She finally died of a heart attack. John died of Alzheimer’s disease five years later, after Lorna nursed him faithfully at home through the final stages of his illness. She would pass away herself in 2014 at age eighty-five.

但她对孤独症核心症状可能带来的严重障碍并不抱幻想，即便是在最优质的照护条件下也是如此。在我与她交谈时，洛娜和丈夫约翰已安葬了他们深爱的苏西——苏西于 2005 年因更年期激素紊乱导致强迫性过量饮水，最终死于心脏病发作，享年 49 岁。五年后，约翰在阿尔茨海默病的折磨下去世，此前洛娜一直在家中忠实地照料他直至生命终章。而洛娜本人于 2014 年离世，享年 85 岁。



The day I visited her clinic, Lorna seemed remarkably youthful and cheerful in a bright floral-print dress, reminiscing about having tea with Asperger one afternoon in the Maudsley Hospital cafeteria just before he died in 1980. (She described him as “charming, polite, and a man who listened well.”) With the help of his paper, she and the other parents who launched the National Autistic Society in the 1960s had changed the world to make it a better place for their children.

我去她诊所拜访那天，洛娜身着明艳的碎花连衣裙，显得格外年轻而神采奕奕。她回忆着 1980 年阿斯伯格去世前，两人在莫兹利医院咖啡厅共饮下午茶的往事（她形容他"风度翩翩、彬彬有礼，且善于倾听"）。凭借阿斯伯格的论文，她与 20 世纪 60 年代共同创立英国国家孤独症协会的其他家长们，已改变了世界，为孩子们创造了更美好的生存环境。



—



THE MOST INSIDIOUS EFFECT of Wakefield’s case study and the firestorm of controversy that followed it was hijacking the movement created by parents like Lorna and Ruth Sullivan, diverting it from its original mission of demanding services and accommodations in education into a rancorous debate about vaccines. In the heat of the Autism Wars, virtually every other issue—such as the pressing need for programs to help autistic teenagers prepare for employment—was swept off the table.

韦克菲尔德案例研究及其引发的激烈争议，其最阴险的影响在于劫持了由洛娜和露丝·沙利文这样的家长发起的运动，将其从原本要求教育服务与支持的核心使命，转向了一场充满敌意的疫苗辩论。在孤独症战争的激烈交锋中，几乎所有其他议题——比如帮助孤独谱系青少年做好就业准备的迫切需求——都被扫下了谈判桌。



Fears of an epidemic have also skewed the direction of autism research. Most studies backed by the NIMH and other federal agencies and private organizations like Autism Speaks are committed to an endless search for potential causes and risk factors, while projects devoted to improving the quality of autistic people’s lives are perpetually underfunded.

对孤独症流行的恐惧也扭曲了研究的方向。美国国家心理健康研究所及其他联邦机构、像"孤独症之声"这样的私人组织资助的大多数研究，都执着于无休止地寻找潜在病因和风险因素，而致力于改善孤独谱系群体生活质量的科研项目却始终资金不足。



But now that’s starting to change. By leveraging the technology passed down to them by their predecessors in previous generations, autistic people are taking control of their own destinies, with the help of parents who no longer believe that what their children need most is a cure.

但如今，情况开始改变。借助前几代先驱传承下来的技术，孤独谱系群体正在掌控自己的命运，而他们的父母也不再相信孩子最需要的是"治愈"。







---


NeuroTribes








Eleven

十一



IN AUTISTIC SPACE

在孤独谱系的空间中



All our lives we’ve been alone in a world of alien men. To find kindred at last is a special joy.

我们一生都在一个充满异类男人的世界里孤独前行。终于找到同类，是一种特别的喜悦。



—A. E. VAN VOGT, SLAN

——A·E·范·沃格特，《斯兰》



In May 1989, a lanky, sandy-haired industrial designer sporting a Western suit and Texas tie stepped up to the podium at a conference of autism professionals and educators in Chapel Hill, North Carolina. The focus of the gathering, prompted by the release of Rain Man five months earlier, was “high-functioning individuals with autism,” and the featured speaker certainly filled the bill. “I am a 44-year-old autistic woman who has a successful international career designing livestock equipment,” she began. “I completed my PhD in Animal Science at the University of Illinois in Urbana and I am now an Assistant Professor of Animal Science at Colorado State University.” Her name was Temple Grandin, and she was not yet widely known outside autism circles.

1989 年 5 月，一位身着西装配德州领带、身形瘦长、沙色头发的工业设计师，在北卡罗来纳州教堂山的一场孤独症专业人士与教育者会议上走上讲台。这场因五个月前《雨人》上映而举办的会议，聚焦于"高功能孤独谱系个体"，而这位特邀演讲者无疑完全符合这一描述。"我是一名 44 岁的孤独谱系女性，在畜牧设备设计领域拥有成功的国际职业生涯，"她开场说道，"我在伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳分校获得了动物科学博士学位，现任科罗拉多州立大学动物科学助理教授。"她的名字叫坦普尔·葛兰汀，当时在孤独症圈外尚不广为人知。



A few years earlier, Ruth Sullivan had spotted Grandin in a terminal at the St. Louis Airport en route to a conference, eavesdropping from the sidelines as parents swapped stories about raising their children. Though NSAC was two decades old by that point, Sullivan had never seen a mature woman who called herself autistic; the thought didn’t even cross her mind as they rode on the bus to their hotel. “If I’d known then what I know now, I would have seen it,” Sullivan recalls. “But Temple was just kind of shy, nicely dressed, and spoke very well. After she went to her room, all of a sudden it hit me.” By inviting the industrial designer to moderate a roundtable discussion, she effectively launched Grandin’s career as a public speaker.

几年前，露丝·沙利文在圣路易斯机场的一个航站楼里遇到了坦普尔·葛兰汀，当时她们正要去参加一场会议。沙利文在一旁偷听，家长们互相分享着抚养孩子的故事。尽管那时全国孤独症协会已经成立二十年，沙利文却从未见过一个自称是孤独症谱系人士的成年女性；在她们乘车前往酒店的路上，她甚至从未有过这个念头。“如果当时我知道现在所了解的，我本应能看出来，”沙利文回忆道，“但坦普尔只是有点害羞，穿着得体，说话也很流利。等她回到房间后，我突然恍然大悟。”通过邀请这位工业设计师主持一场圆桌讨论，沙利文实际上开启了葛兰汀作为公共演讲者的职业生涯。



“There were about twenty roundtables going on in this big banquet hall, but when I started talking, suddenly the whole room got quiet,” Grandin says. “Everybody at the other tables started turning around so they could listen to me.” (Two decades later, the scene was re-created for the Emmy-winning HBO biopic Temple Grandin starring Clare Danes.) “You could ask Temple questions, and it was like speaking to all of our kids,” Sullivan recalls. “None of them could tell us what she could tell us.”

“这个大型宴会厅里大约有二十场圆桌讨论在进行，但当我开始说话时，整个房间突然安静了下来，”格兰丁说。“其他桌的每个人都转过身来听我说话。”（二十年后，这一幕被重现于 HBO 获奖传记电影《自闭历程》中，由克莱尔·丹尼斯主演。）“你可以向坦普尔提问，那感觉就像是在对我们所有的孩子说话，”沙利文回忆道。“没有人能像她那样告诉我们那些事。”



Recounting the story of her life for the audience of professionals in Chapel Hill in her inimitably gruff and blunt-spoken way, Grandin cast more light on the day-to-day reality of autism than decades of clinical observation and speculation had managed to produce. The fact that this accomplished and articulate industrial designer was unable to speak until age three and struggled with severe behavioral issues through her teens suggested that labels like high-functioning and low-functioning were too simplistic. She had managed to avoid being institutionalized only because the neurologist who initially examined her diagnosed her with brain damage rather than autism.

在教堂山为专业听众讲述自己的人生故事时，格兰丁以她标志性的粗哑直率风格，比数十年的临床观察和推测更清晰地揭示了孤独症日常生活的真实面貌。这位成就卓著、表达清晰的工业设计师直到三岁才能说话，青少年时期还饱受严重行为问题的困扰——这一事实表明，像“高功能”和“低功能”这样的标签过于简单化了。她之所以能避免被送入收容机构，仅仅是因为最初为她检查的神经科医生将她诊断为脑损伤而非孤独症。



First Grandin characterized descriptions of nonverbal children as willfully oblivious to the people around them as terribly misguided. “If adults spoke directly to me I could understand everything they said, but I could not get my words out,” she said. “My mother and teachers wondered why I screamed. Screaming was the only way I could communicate.” Then she pointed out the inadequacy of existing empirical methods for capturing the sensory sensitivities at the core of autistic experience. The auditory tests she was given as a child revealed nothing unusual about her hearing, but Grandin described being bombarded with certain sounds as like “having a hearing aid stuck on ‘super loud.’” The reason that she misbehaved in church so often as a little girl, she explained, was that the unfamiliar petticoats, skirts, and stockings she was forced to wear on Sundays felt scratchy against her skin.

首先，格兰丁指出，将非语言儿童描述为故意无视周围人的说法是极其错误的。“如果大人直接对我说话，我能理解他们说的每一句话，但我无法说出自己的话，”她说，“我的母亲和老师都奇怪我为什么尖叫。尖叫是我唯一能沟通的方式。”接着，她指出现有实证方法在捕捉孤独症核心的感官敏感性方面的不足。她小时候接受的听觉测试并未显示她的听力有任何异常，但格兰丁描述某些声音对她的冲击就像“助听器被调到了‘超响’模式”。她解释说，自己小时候在教堂里经常行为不当，是因为周日被迫穿的不熟悉的衬裙、裙子和长袜让她感觉皮肤刺痒。



She pointedly referred to her autism as a “handicap” rather than a mental illness, invoking the humanizing language of disability over the stigmatizing lexicon of psychiatry. But in addition to shedding light on the challenges faced by people with autism, she also described the ways that the visual nature of her thought processes and memory had given her practical advantages in her career. “If somebody says the word cat,” she said, “my images are of individualized cats I have known or read about. I do not think of a generalized cat. My career as a designer of livestock facilities maximizes my talent areas and minimizes my deficits . . . Visual thinking is an asset for an equipment designer. I am able to ‘see’ how all the parts of a project fit together and see potential problems.” She cited the infamous case of a catwalk that collapsed in the lobby of a Hyatt Regency in 1981, killing more than a hundred people, as a catastrophe that could have been averted by having a visual thinker like her on the design team.

她特意将自己的孤独症称为“障碍”而非精神疾病，用残障的人性化语言取代了精神病学带有污名化的术语。但除了揭示孤独谱系群体面临的挑战外，她还描述了其思维过程和记忆的视觉特性如何为她的职业生涯带来实际优势。“如果有人提到‘猫’这个词，”她说，“我脑海中浮现的是我认识或读到过的具体猫咪形象，而非笼统的猫。作为畜牧设施设计师，我的职业最大化发挥了天赋领域，同时弥补了缺陷……视觉思维对设备设计师而言是种优势。我能‘看见’项目各部件如何契合，并预见潜在问题。”她援引 1981 年凯悦酒店大堂天桥坍塌导致百余人死亡的著名案例，指出若有像她这样的视觉思维者参与设计团队，这场灾难本可避免。



Then she traced the roots of her creative gifts through the branches of her family tree, describing her paternal great-grandfather as a maverick who launched the biggest corporate wheat farm in the world and her maternal grandfather as a shy engineer who helped invent the automatic pilot for airplanes. She pointed out that all three of her siblings think visually and that one of her sisters, a gifted interior designer, is dyslexic. Her emphasis on the virtues of atypical minds marked a significant departure from the view of most psychologists, who framed the areas of strength in their patients’ cognitive profiles as mere “splinter skills”—islands of conserved ability in seas of general incompetence. Instead, Grandin proposed that people with autism, dyslexia, and other cognitive differences could make contributions to society that so-called normal people are incapable of making.

接着，她追溯了自己创作天赋在家族谱系中的根源，将曾祖父描述为一位特立独行者——他创办了全球最大的企业化小麦农场；而外祖父则是一位腼腆的工程师，曾参与发明飞机自动驾驶仪。她指出，三个兄弟姐妹都具备视觉思维特质，其中一位天赋异禀的室内设计师姐姐患有阅读障碍。她对非典型思维优势的强调，与多数心理学家的观点形成显著分野——后者常将患者认知图谱中的优势领域仅视为"碎片化技能"，即普遍能力缺陷海洋中残存的孤岛能力。而格兰丁提出，孤独谱系群体、阅读障碍者及其他认知差异人群，能够为那些所谓"正常人"无法企及的社会领域做出独特贡献。



She ended her talk by paying tribute to her mentors, starting with her mother, Eustacia Cutler, who never lost faith in her potential and fought many battles to ensure that Temple got an education. She also thanked William Carlock, the high school science teacher who channeled her teenage fascination with cows into a career in animal science. She explained that the turning point in her young life occurred one summer at her aunt Ann’s ranch, when she noticed that fearful calves calmed down when they were herded into a device called a squeeze chute that held them securely in place. Like many autistic people, Grandin struggles with chronic anxiety, and she wondered if she, too, might gain peace of mind if she placed herself in the chute. With her aunt’s help, she did just that, and the feeling of deep pressure from the device pressing against her sides alleviated her “nerve attacks.” Later, with Carlock’s encouragement, she devised a similar apparatus for herself out of scrap wood.

她以向导师们致敬作为演讲的结尾，首先感谢母亲尤斯塔西娅·卡特勒——这位母亲从未对女儿的天赋失去信心，并为确保坦普尔能接受教育而历经无数抗争。她还感谢高中科学教师威廉·卡洛克，正是他将这位少女对牛的痴迷引导至动物科学领域的职业生涯。她解释道，自己人生的转折点发生在安妮姨妈牧场的一个夏天：当时她注意到受惊的小牛被赶进一种名为"挤压栏"的装置后，因身体被牢牢固定而平静下来。和许多孤独谱系群体成员一样，格兰丁长期受慢性焦虑困扰，她不禁思考：若将自己置于挤压栏中，是否也能获得内心的安宁？在姨妈的帮助下，她真的这样做了——装置两侧施加的深层压力感缓解了她的"神经性发作"。后来在卡洛克的鼓励下，她用废木料为自己制作了类似的装置。



Predictably, the school psychologist wasn’t pleased with her invention, telling her that he couldn’t decide if it was “a prototype of a womb or a casket.” (“We don’t think we’re a cow or something, do we?” he asked Grandin, who shot back, “Do you think you’re a cow?”) School officials tried to persuade Eustacia that her daughter’s device represented a “sick” fixation that should be taken away from her. But Grandin sensed that she was on the right track. Safely ensconced in the arms of her squeeze machine, she not only became less anxious, she felt more emotionally connected to the people around her. “For the first time in my life,” she said, “I felt a purpose for learning.”

不出所料，学校心理医生对她的发明并不满意，表示自己无法判断这究竟是"子宫原型还是棺材雏形"。（"我们不会觉得自己是头牛之类的吧？"他问葛兰汀，后者反呛道："那你觉得自己是头牛吗？"）校方试图说服尤斯塔西娅，认为她女儿的设备代表一种"病态"执念，应当予以没收。但葛兰汀直觉自己走在正确的道路上。当安全地蜷缩在挤压机的怀抱中时，她不仅焦虑感减轻了，更感受到与周围人情感上的联结。"有生以来第一次，"她说，"我找到了学习的意义。"



—



AS ONE OF THE first adults to publicly identify as autistic, Grandin helped break down decades of shame and stigma. One nearly forgotten aspect of her “coming out,” though, shows how quickly the ground was shifting under her feet. To most clinicians at the time, the notion of an autistic adult with a doctorate and a successful career seemed implausible at best. So Grandin presented herself in Chapel Hill as someone who had “recovered” from autism, encouraged by Rimland, who introduced her 1986 memoir, Emergence, as “the first book written by a recovered autistic individual.”

作为最早公开承认自己是孤独谱系人士的成年人之一，格兰丁帮助打破了数十年的羞耻与污名。然而，她"出柜"过程中一个几乎被遗忘的侧面，恰恰揭示了当时社会观念转变之迅速。对当时大多数临床医生而言，一个拥有博士学位且事业成功的孤独谱系成年人，即便不是天方夜谭，也令人难以置信。因此，在里姆兰德的鼓励下，格兰丁在教堂山将自己描述为从孤独症中"康复"的人——里姆兰德在为她 1986 年出版的回忆录《破茧而出》作序时，称其为"第一本由康复的孤独谱系人士撰写的著作"。



It soon became obvious to her, however, that she had not recovered but had learned, with great effort, to adapt to the social norms of the people around her. “When I said that early stuff, I didn’t realize how different my thinking was,” Grandin says. “I was doing a lot of construction projects in the early nineties, and I could draw something and test-run that piece of equipment in my mind. I started asking other designers to describe how they think, and they told me they could draw the layout for a meat-cutting line but couldn’t make the conveyors move. I could make the conveyors move.”

但她很快意识到，自己并非康复，而是通过巨大努力学会了适应周围人的社会规范。"当初说那些话时，我并未意识到自己的思维方式如此与众不同，"格兰丁表示，"九十年代初我参与了许多建筑项目，能在脑海中绘制图纸并预演设备运行。当我开始询问其他设计师的思考方式时，他们告诉我能画出肉类切割线的布局图，却无法让传送带在想象中运转——而我可以做到。"



She had a similar revelation when she asked a speech therapist what came into her mind after hearing the phrase church steeple. “I was shocked when she said ‘vague pointy thing,’” Grandin recalls. “I saw pictures of specific steeples.” She began to think of herself as having a powerful digital workstation in her head, capable of running instantaneous searches through a massive library of stored images and generating 3-D videos from the sketches on her drafting table.

她曾向一位语言治疗师询问，听到“教堂尖顶”这个词时脑海中浮现什么，结果有了类似的顿悟。“当她说‘模糊的尖东西’时，我震惊了，”格兰丁回忆道，“而我看到的是具体尖顶的画面。”她开始意识到自己大脑中仿佛装着一台强大的数字工作站，能瞬间从海量图像库中检索信息，并将绘图桌上的草图转化为三维动态影像。



Grandin also noticed how many parents at autism conferences were gifted in technical fields. “Early on I met a family with two severely autistic nonverbal kids. Dad was a computer programmer and Mom was a chemist. Both super-smart,” she says. “I saw lots and lots of cases like this. I started to think of autistic traits as being on a continuum. The more traits you had on both sides, the more you concentrated the genetics. Having a little bit of the traits gave you an advantage, but if you had too much, you ended up with very severe autism.” She warned that efforts to eradicate autism from the gene pool could put humankind’s future at risk by purging the same qualities that had advanced culture, science, and technological innovation for millennia. The maker of the first stone spear, she observed, was likely a lone autistic at the back of the cave, perseverating over the subtle differences between various types of rocks—not one of the “yakkity yaks” chattering away in the firelight.

格兰丁还注意到，在孤独症会议上，许多家长在技术领域都极具天赋。“早期我遇到一个家庭，有两个重度孤独症且不会说话的孩子。父亲是电脑程序员，母亲是化学家。两人都超级聪明，”她说，“我见过很多很多这样的案例。我开始认为孤独谱系特质是一个连续谱。你在两端拥有的特质越多，基因就越集中。拥有少量特质能带来优势，但如果太多，就会导致重度孤独症。”她警告说，试图从基因库中根除孤独症的努力，可能会危及人类的未来，因为这会清除那些数千年来推动文化、科学和技术创新的特质。她指出，第一个石制长矛的制造者，很可能是一个孤独的孤独谱系人士，躲在洞穴深处，执着地研究着不同岩石之间的细微差别——而不是那些在火光旁喋喋不休的“话匣子”。



Aware adults with autism and their parents are often angry about autism. They may ask why nature or God created such horrible conditions as autism, manic depression, and schizophrenia. However, if the genes that caused these conditions were eliminated there might be a terrible price to pay. It is possible that persons with bits of these traits are more creative, or possibly even geniuses. If science eliminated these genes, maybe the whole world would be taken over by accountants.

有孤独症的成年患者及其父母常对孤独症感到愤怒。他们或许会质问，为何自然或上帝要创造出孤独症、躁郁症和精神分裂症这般可怕的病症。然而，若将这些致病基因彻底消除，人类或将付出惨痛代价。那些带有这些特质的人，或许更具创造力，甚至可能是天才。倘若科学消灭了这些基因，也许整个世界都会被会计师们所主宰。



—



TWO YEARS LATER, a distinguished visitor arrived in Grandin’s office in the animal sciences department at Colorado State University: Oliver Sacks, who had flown in from New York City while researching a profile of the young savant artist Stephen Wiltshire. His own views of autism were evolving swiftly, informed by the insights of Lorna Wing, Uta Frith, and the others in the London group. When he first read Emergence, he suspected that Grandin’s co-author, Margaret Scariano, must have ghostwritten it. “The autistic mind, it was supposed at that time, was incapable of self-understanding and understanding others and therefore of authentic introspection and retrospection,” he explained. “How could an autistic person write an autobiography? It seemed a contradiction in terms.” After reading dozens of her papers, however, he found that Grandin’s distinctive persona—that of an irrepressibly curious observer of society from the outside, an “anthropologist on Mars,” as she put it—was consistent throughout. She was clearly writing in her own voice.

两年后，一位尊贵的访客来到科罗拉多州立大学动物科学系格兰丁的办公室：奥利弗·萨克斯，他从纽约飞来，当时正在为撰写关于年轻学者型艺术家斯蒂芬·威尔特希尔的专题报道做调研。受伦敦小组中洛娜·温、乌塔·弗里思等人的见解影响，他对孤独症的看法正在迅速演变。当他初次读到《破茧而出》时，曾怀疑格兰丁的合著者玛格丽特·斯卡里亚诺是代笔人。“当时人们认为，孤独症患者无法理解自我与他人，因此不可能进行真正的内省与反思，”他解释道，“一个孤独症患者怎能写出自传？这听起来自相矛盾。”然而，在阅读了她数十篇论文后，他发现格兰丁独特的个性——一个从外部观察社会、永不停歇的好奇者，用她自己的话说，一位“火星上的人类学家”——始终如一。显然，她是在用自己的声音写作。



After decades in the shadows, Asperger’s forgotten tribe was finally making its way toward the light. Before meeting Grandin, Sacks had spent the summer visiting camps for autistic kids and acquainting himself with a California couple he called the B.’s, who had turned their home into a haven for expat extraterrestrials. Upon meeting in college, Mr. and Mrs. B. felt like they had known each other for a million years. As fellow Star Trek fans, they liked to say that they had beamed down on the transporter together. Both of their sons turned out to be autistic—one nonverbal and one with Asperger’s syndrome—so they put up a trampoline in their backyard where the whole family could jump and flap to their hearts’ content. Their walls were emblazoned with surrealistic cartoons, their bookshelves were laden with science fiction, and notes posted in the kitchen offered meticulously explicit directions for cooking and setting the table. Sacks initially assumed that these detailed directives were an expression of the B.’s need for order and routine, but he eventually realized they were a family in-joke at the expense of those who thought that autistic people were incapable of “getting” humor.

在隐匿数十年后，阿斯伯格综合征中被遗忘的群体终于走向光明。在拜访格兰丁之前，萨克斯曾花了一个夏天探访自闭症儿童营地，并结识了一对被他称为 B 夫妇的加州夫妻——他们将自家住宅打造成外星流亡者的避风港。大学初遇时，B 夫妇便觉彼此已相识百万年。作为《星际迷航》同好，他们总说两人是一起用传送器降落到地球的。他们的两个儿子都被确诊为自闭症——一个无语言能力，另一个患有阿斯伯格综合征——于是他们在后院架起蹦床，让全家人都能尽情跳跃、挥舞手臂。墙上贴满超现实主义漫画，书架塞满科幻小说，厨房里张贴着烹饪与摆桌的详尽说明。萨克斯最初以为这些细致指令是 B对秩序和常规的需求，但他最终意识到，这其实是家族内部的一个玩笑，意在调侃那些认为孤独谱系群体无法“理解”幽默的人。



The B.’s were well aware that the protocols and conventions of nonautistic society were opaque to them, and that they were required to “ape human behavior” at work, as Mr. B. put it, to avoid alarming their professional colleagues. But Sacks reported that they had come to feel that their autism, “while it may be seen as a medical condition, and pathologized as a syndrome, must also be seen as a whole mode of being, a deeply different mode or identity, one that needs to be conscious (and proud) of itself.” At home with other members of their tribe, in an environment designed for their comfort, they didn’t feel disabled; they just felt different from their neighbors.

B 夫妇很清楚，非孤独谱系社会的规则和惯例对他们而言是难以理解的，正如 B 先生所说，他们在工作中必须“模仿人类行为”，以免引起同事的警觉。但萨克斯指出，他们逐渐意识到，自己的孤独谱系特质“虽然可能被视为一种医学状况，并被病理化为一种综合征，但也必须被看作是一种完整的生存方式，一种深层次的差异或身份认同，它需要被自我认知（并为之自豪）。”在与其他同类成员共处的家中，在一个为他们舒适度身定制的环境里，他们并不觉得自己有障碍；他们只是感到与邻居不同。



Eager to observe Grandin in her native element, Sacks spent several days touring cattle farms and meatpacking plants that she helped design, sharing a meal of ribs and beer with her in a cowboy-themed restaurant, and visiting her at home, where he gamely climbed into her squeeze machine to try it out himself, finding a “sweet, calming” feeling in its mechanical embrace. They also took hikes together in the mountains, where he was impressed by her knowledge of the names of the local birds, plants, and rock formations, even if she seemed unimpressed by the feelings of sublimity and awe that they evoked in him. In turn, Grandin was amused to discover that the eminent neurologist was nearly as eccentric as she was. “He was like a kindly absentminded professor who zoned out a lot,” she recalls. “When he had to pee in the woods, he announced that he was going to ‘fertilize the ground.’ He had me stop the car so he could go swimming in a lake, but he didn’t notice that the current would have carried him right over a dam. I probably saved his life.”

为了观察格兰丁在她熟悉的环境中的状态，萨克斯花了好几天时间参观她参与设计的养牛场和肉类加工厂，与她在牛仔主题餐厅共享肋排和啤酒，还去她家拜访。在那里，他兴致勃勃地爬进她的挤压机亲自体验，发现这种机械拥抱带来一种"甜美而平静"的感觉。他们还一起在山间徒步，她对当地鸟类、植物和岩层名称的熟悉程度令他印象深刻，尽管她似乎对他所感受到的崇高与敬畏之情无动于衷。反过来，格兰丁也饶有兴致地发现这位著名神经学家几乎和她一样古怪。"他就像个和蔼可亲的心不在焉的教授，经常走神，"她回忆道，"当他在树林里需要小便时，会宣布要去'给土地施肥'。他还让我停车，好让他去湖里游泳，却完全没注意到水流会把他直接冲过大坝。我大概救了他一命。"



Their interactions made such a profound impression on Sacks that he turned his planned footnote to the Wiltshire story into an in-depth profile that became the centerpiece of his next bestseller, An Anthropologist on Mars. After fifty years of case reports describing autistic people in terms befitting robots or “imbeciles,” Sacks presented Grandin in the full breadth of her humanity—capable of joy, whimsy, tenderness, passion about her work, exuberance, longing, philosophical musing on her legacy, and sly subterfuge (she smuggled him into a plant by giving him a hard hat and telling him to act like a sanitary engineer). He acknowledged the prevailing theory that autism is “foremost a disorder of affect, of empathy,” but also explored her deep sense of kinship with other disabled people and with animals, whose fates she saw as intertwined in a society that views them both as less than human.

他们的互动给萨克斯留下了如此深刻的印象，以至于他将原本计划为威尔特郡故事添加的脚注，扩展成了一篇深度人物特写，并成为他下一本畅销书《火星上的人类学家》的核心篇章。在长达半个世纪的病例报告中，人们一直用适合描述机器人或"低能儿"的词汇来形容孤独谱系群体，而萨克斯则展现了格兰丁完整的人性维度——她能够感受快乐、充满奇思妙想、温柔体贴、对工作满怀热忱、活力四射、心怀渴望、对自己的遗产进行哲学思考，甚至还会耍些小聪明（她给他戴上一顶安全帽，让他假装成卫生工程师，偷偷把他带进工厂）。他承认当时的主流理论认为孤独症"首先是情感和共情能力的障碍"，但也探讨了她与其他残障人士及动物之间深厚的亲缘感——在一个将他们都视为次等生命的社会里，她认为他们的命运是相互交织的。



In one study for the university, Grandin analyzed the social and environmental factors that influence the attitudes of livestock industry employees toward cattle in feedlots, auction markets, and slaughterhouses. In badly designed facilities where animals routinely slipped on slick floors or were trapped by slamming gates, she observed, workers tended to become desensitized to their plight and used whips and electric prods on them with abandon. She noted that in states where cattle are routinely treated badly, disabled people face high levels of abuse and discrimination.

在一项为大学进行的研究中，格兰丁分析了影响畜牧业员工对饲养场、拍卖市场和屠宰场中牛只态度的社会与环境因素。她观察到，在设施设计不当、动物经常在湿滑地板上摔倒或被猛然关闭的门困住的地方，工人们往往会对动物的困境变得麻木，并肆意使用鞭子和电棍对待它们。她指出，在那些牛只经常遭受虐待的州，残疾人也面临着严重的虐待和歧视。



She came to see her profound emotional connection with animals as essentially autistic, and crucial for her work. “If I could snap my fingers and be non-autistic, I would not, because then I wouldn’t be me,” she told Sacks. “Autism is part of who I am.” By then, she was on her way to becoming the most widely recognized autistic person on earth. After the publication of An Anthropologist on Mars, Sacks’s office was deluged with letters from readers who saw aspects of themselves, their relatives, or their coworkers in his descriptions of a mature person on the spectrum with a complex inner life. “It was overwhelming, like opening a floodgate,” recalls Kate Edgar, the neurologist’s longtime assistant and editor. “There was such a pent-up desire to have a name for this cluster of traits in older people and to hear someone talk about autism from the perspective of acceptance.”

她将自己与动物之间深厚的情感联系视为典型的孤独谱系特质，并认为这对她的工作至关重要。“如果我能打个响指就变得非孤独谱系，我不会这么做，因为那样我就不再是我了，”她告诉萨克斯。“孤独谱系是我的一部分。”那时，她正逐渐成为全球最广为人知的孤独谱系人士。在《火星上的人类学家》出版后，萨克斯的办公室收到了大量读者来信，他们在书中对一位拥有复杂内心世界的成年谱系人士的描述中，看到了自己、亲属或同事的影子。“那感觉就像打开了闸门，势不可挡，”这位神经学家的长期助手兼编辑凯特·埃德加回忆道。“人们迫切希望为老年人身上这些特质组合找到一个名称，也渴望听到有人从接纳的角度谈论孤独谱系。”



The chances of Grandin’s perspective taking root among autism professionals or traditional advocacy organizations, however, were slim. The notion that an autism diagnosis was a fate worse than death proved hard to dispel even in sectors of the medical profession. As late as 2001, one of the most respected figures in modern epidemiology, Walter Spitzer of McGill University, described autism as “a terminal illness . . . a dead soul in a live body.”

然而，格兰丁的观点在孤独症专业人士或传统倡导组织中扎根的可能性微乎其微。即使在医学界的某些领域，"孤独症诊断比死亡更糟糕"这一观念也难以消除。直到 2001 年，现代流行病学领域最受尊敬的人物之一、麦吉尔大学的沃尔特·斯皮策仍将孤独症描述为"一种绝症……活着的躯体里住着死去的灵魂。"



In fact, the expansion of the diagnosis inspired the creation of a whole new set of dehumanizing stereotypes in the media. The first mention of Asperger’s syndrome in an English-language newspaper, in the Toronto Star in 1989, described “strange” and “clumsy” nerds who read books compulsively without understanding them, were incapable of friendship, and burst into tears and laughter for no reason “like stroke patients who have suffered brain damage.” The second mention, in the Sydney Morning Herald, led off with the sentence, “It is the plague of those unable to feel.”
