We have to organize.” His friend looked at him and said, “Ari, you don’t even know, you’re so unusual. There’s no way you have anything in common with other autistic people.” The diversity of the spectrum made organizing difficult by opening up wedge issues that drove various factions of the community apart. Some “high-functioning” people went out of their way to distance themselves from “low-functioning” people, and didn’t want anything to do with the word disability. But Ne’eman rejected this approach, as Jim Sinclair had done before him, because all autistic people would benefit from destigmatizing the condition and improving access to services and education.

对于阿斯伯格综合征患者而言，“圈内”与“圈外”的复杂性远超他的想象。高中某天，尼曼对朋友说：“我在考虑参与社会运动，因为孤独谱系群体作为少数群体正遭受歧视，我们必须采取行动，必须组织起来。”朋友看着他回应：“阿里，你根本不知道，你太特别了。你和其他孤独谱系群体不可能有任何共同点。”谱系的多样性通过制造分裂议题使组织工作举步维艰，这些议题将社群中的不同派别割裂开来。部分“高功能”患者刻意与“低功能”患者划清界限，甚至拒绝与“残障”一词产生任何关联。但尼曼如同前辈吉姆·辛克莱一样拒绝这种立场——因为消除病症污名化、改善服务与教育可及性，将使所有孤独谱系群体受益。



Ne’eman managed to get himself readmitted to the mainstream school in his neighborhood for one period a day in the afternoon, which opened up a world of extracurricular activities to him. He signed up for anything that had to do with policy and politics: Model UN, Model Congress, Debate Club, Mock Trial, and the Future Business Leaders of America. By the summer of 2006, he had started attending autism conferences. He was disappointed in how little interest there seemed to be in issues of public policy among the celebrities in the autism world, whom he started thinking of as “professional autistics.” Discussions of policy at these events rarely went beyond talk of launching online petitions and sending e-mails to Congress. Sitting in an atrium in Manhattan one day, he found himself reading two letters back-to-back. One was from New Jersey governor Jon Corzine, appointing him as the student representative to the state’s Special Education Commission, and the other was from the University of Medicine and Dentistry, inviting Ne’eman to help plan an autism program for adults. It occurred to him, “When I go to these things, I shouldn’t just be Ari. I should be representing a larger group of people and acting as a conduit for their access.”

尼曼成功争取到每天下午回社区主流学校上一节课的机会，这为他打开了课外活动的新世界。他报名参加了所有与政策政治相关的活动：模拟联合国、模拟国会、辩论社、模拟法庭，以及美国未来商业领袖。到 2006 年夏天，他开始参加孤独症会议。令他失望的是，孤独症领域的知名人士似乎对公共政策议题兴趣寥寥——他开始称这些人为"职业孤独症人士"。这些会议的政策讨论往往止步于发起网络请愿和给国会发邮件。某天坐在曼哈顿的中庭里，他接连读了两封信：一封是新泽西州长乔恩·科尔津任命他为州特殊教育委员会学生代表的委任状，另一封是医学与牙科大学邀请他参与规划成人孤独症项目的邀请函。他突然意识到："参加这些活动时，我不该只是阿里。" 我应当代表更广泛的群体，并成为他们获取资源的渠道。”



He decided that he would help found an organization that would represent autistics in discussions of public policy. One of the first people he called for help was Scott Robertson, a graduate student he’d met a few months earlier. “I brought the politics and the policy and Scott brought the research. We made a great team. We both talked about our personal experience, but we used it as punctuation on the talk about the issues. I really respected that. He was someone who was clearly an autistic professional, not a professional autistic.” ASAN was founded in 2006 and soon attracted other members, including Paula Durbin-Westby, who began sitting in on meetings of the Interagency Autism Coordinating Committee in Washington, which coordinates policy within the Department of Health and Human Services and helps set the federal research agenda.

他决定协助创立一个组织，在公共政策讨论中代表孤独谱系群体。他最早求助的人之一是几个月前结识的研究生斯科特·罗伯逊。“我负责政治与政策层面，斯科特负责研究领域。我们组成了出色的团队。我们都会谈及个人经历，但仅将其作为议题讨论中的点缀。我对此深表敬意。他显然是一位孤独谱系专业人士，而非职业化的孤独谱系人士。”孤独谱系自我倡导网络（ASAN）于 2006 年成立，很快吸引了包括保拉·德宾-韦斯特比在内的成员。她开始列席华盛顿的跨部门孤独症协调委员会会议——该委员会负责协调卫生与公众服务部的政策，并协助制定联邦研究议程。



The ransom notes campaign was ASAN’s collective initiation: proof that the people formerly known as “patients” could redefine the terms of a public discussion of autism by a powerful institution.

勒索信运动是 ASAN 的集体首秀：证明曾被称作“患者”的群体，能够重新定义某个权威机构对孤独症的公共讨论话语。



On December 6, the day after the Child Study Center’s ad campaign made its debut at a gala dinner for eight hundred people featuring appearances by Hillary Clinton and CBS Evening News anchor Katie Couric, messages started pouring into ASAN’s inbox demanding a response. Ne’eman e-mailed the Child Study Center politely expressing his concerns and left phone messages, but got no reply. Two days later, ASAN blasted out an action alert with contact numbers and e-mail addresses for the Child Study Center, the director of the NYU Medical Center, BBDO, and the two donors who provided funding for the university’s Asperger Institute.

12 月 6 日，儿童研究中心广告宣传活动在八百人晚宴上首次亮相，希拉里·克林顿与 CBS 晚间新闻主播凯蒂·库里克出席助阵。次日，ASAN 的收件箱便涌入大量要求回应的信息。尼曼通过邮件礼貌地向儿童研究中心表达关切，并留下语音留言，但未获回复。两天后，ASAN 发布了行动警报，附上儿童研究中心、纽约大学医学中心主任、BBDO 广告公司以及为该校阿斯伯格研究所提供资助的两名捐赠者的联系电话和邮箱。



Parents offended by the ads were starting to light up the blogosphere. After reading the ad signed “Autism,” a blogger who called herself MOM-NOS replied:

被广告激怒的家长们开始在博客圈掀起热议。一位自称 MOM-NOS 的博主在读到署名"孤独症"的广告后回应道：



Dear Autism,

亲爱的孤独症：



You don’t have my son; I do.

你并未拥有我的儿子——我才是。



I will make sure that he is never defined by his autism alone, and I will help him to recognize that, although his autism makes some things incredibly challenging, it also brings with it remarkable gifts. I will make sure that we work on his challenges. I will make sure that we celebrate his gifts.

我会确保他绝不仅仅被孤独谱系所定义，并帮助他认识到，尽管孤独谱系让某些事情变得异常艰难，但也带来了非凡的天赋。我会确保我们共同应对他的挑战，也会确保我们庆祝他的天赋。



This is only the beginning.

这仅仅是个开始。



Kristina Chew, the former classics professor whose acceptance of her son Charlie helped Shannon Rosa come to terms with Leo’s autism, wrote on her blog Autism Vox:

前古典学教授克里斯蒂娜·邱曾在博客《孤独症之声》中写道，她接纳儿子查理的过程帮助香农·罗莎接受了利奥的孤独谱系：



This is a “public awareness” campaign that makes the public aware only of one very dark aspect of being an autistic person and of raising an autistic child. Spend a day in our household and, while you will witness more than a few moments of anxiety, fretfulness, and a bit of noise, I hope you might most of all sense my son’s limitless desire to do his best, to struggle through his worries, and to smile and speak in half-echoed snatches of phrases—his patience and his constant efforts to try and try harder. It is not a household that Charlie, or Jim or I, feel at all in need of being rescued from.

这是一场"公众认知"运动，却只让公众看到孤独谱系人士和抚养孤独谱系儿童过程中极其阴暗的一面。若你在我家待上一天，虽然会目睹不少焦虑、烦躁和些许喧闹的时刻，但我更希望你感受到的是：我儿子永无止境的努力意愿——他奋力克服忧虑、用断断续续的回声般短语微笑说话——他的耐心，以及他不断尝试、再尝试的坚持。这个家，无论是查理、吉姆还是我，都丝毫不需要被"拯救"。



Ne’eman spent all twenty-four hours of his twentieth birthday, on December 10, coordinating ASAN’s response. The group’s chances of success seemed slim. NYU was powerful, and BBDO’s campaign represented hundreds of thousands of dollars in pro bono work. By contrast, ASAN hadn’t yet rented an office or opened a bank account. To persuade other disability advocacy groups to sign a letter of protest, Ne’eman cold-called Bob Kafka, the national director of ADAPT, one of the leading disability rights organizations in the country, with chapters in thirty states. Kafka agreed to sign the letter immediately, and thirteen other organizations quickly added their names to the list. Since Ne’eman had not yet gotten a response from NYU, an ASAN representative hand-delivered it to the receptionist at the Child Study Center, who looked haggard as phones rang in the background. By then, the Wall Street Journal, the New York Times, the Daily News, and other major media outlets were running stories on the controversy. After receiving thousands of calls, letters, and e-mails from across the country, the Child Study Center finally agreed to pull the ads.

12 月 10 日，尼曼在二十岁生日那天整整二十四小时都在协调 ASAN 的应对工作。这个组织成功的希望似乎十分渺茫。纽约大学实力雄厚，而 BBDO 的广告活动代表着数十万美元的公益投入。相比之下，ASAN 连办公室都没租，银行账户也没开。为了说服其他残障倡导组织签署抗议信，尼曼主动联系了鲍勃·卡夫卡——ADAPT 的全国主任，ADAPT 是美国领先的残障权利组织之一，在三十个州设有分支机构。卡夫卡立即同意签署这封信，随后另外十三个组织也迅速加入。由于尼曼尚未收到纽约大学的回复，ASAN 的一名代表亲自将信送到了儿童研究中心的接待员手中，当时电话铃声此起彼伏，接待员显得疲惫不堪。此时，《华尔街日报》、《纽约时报》、《每日新闻》及其他主要媒体已开始报道这场争议。在收到来自全国各地的数千通电话、信件和电子邮件后，儿童研究中心最终同意撤下这些广告。



It was the first of many victories for ASAN. In 2010, after being nominated by President Obama, Ne’eman took a seat on the National Council on Disability (NCD). In recent years, ASAN has played a significant role in influencing the formulation of federal disability policy. In 2014, when Obama issued an executive order raising the minimum wage for federal contractors, the Secretary of Labor announced that tens of thousands of workers with disabilities making subminimum wages—sometimes as low as a few cents an hour—would be exempt from the new higher wage. In response, ASAN assembled a diverse coalition of organizations—including the American Civil Liberties Union, the AFL-CIO, and the National Association for the Deaf—that persuaded the White House to reverse its decision and include disabled workers in the order, a bridge-building effort that paid off with a historical achievement at the highest level of policymaking.

这是 ASAN 众多胜利中的第一个。2010 年，在奥巴马总统提名后，尼曼进入国家残障委员会（NCD）任职。近年来，ASAN 在影响联邦残障政策制定方面发挥了重要作用。2014 年，奥巴马发布行政命令提高联邦承包商最低工资时，劳工部长宣布，数万名领取低于最低工资（有时低至每小时几美分）的残障工人将被排除在新工资标准之外。对此，ASAN 组建了一个多元化的组织联盟——包括美国公民自由联盟、美国劳工联合会-产业工会联合会以及全国聋人协会——成功说服白宫撤销这一决定，将残障工人纳入该命令的覆盖范围。这一搭建桥梁的努力最终在最高政策制定层面取得了历史性成就。



When the APA drafted its new set of criteria for the DSM-5, ASAN staffers worked with the subcommittee to ensure that the coping skills employed by autistic teenagers and adults to fit in would not be used to exclude them from a diagnosis, while other revisions they suggested highlighted the needs of historically underdiagnosed populations, including women and people of color. Nearly eighty years after the discovery of the autistic continuum by Asperger’s team at the Heilpädagogik Station, its full breadth was finally reflected in the APA’s criteria.

当美国精神病学会（APA）为《精神障碍诊断与统计手册（第五版）》（DSM-5）起草新标准时，孤独谱系自倡导网络（ASAN）的工作人员与小组委员会合作，确保孤独谱系青少年和成年人为融入社会而采用的应对技巧不会被用作排除诊断的依据；同时，他们提出的其他修订意见也强调了历史上被低估群体的需求，包括女性和有色人种。在阿斯伯格团队于海勒教育站发现孤独谱系连续谱近八十年后，其完整广度终于在美国精神病学会的标准中得到体现。



Ironically, the syndrome that made Asperger’s name a household word in the 1990s after decades of obscurity also disappeared from the same edition of the manual, folded into the umbrella of autism spectrum disorder. But the fact that the APA included autistic people in the decision-making process was a fitting tribute to a man who collaborated with “uneducable” students like Harro to develop his innovative teaching methods.

讽刺的是，让阿斯伯格的名字在沉寂数十年后于 20 世纪 90 年代家喻户晓的综合征，也在同一版手册中消失了——它被归入孤独症谱系障碍的范畴。但美国精神病学会将孤独谱系群体纳入决策过程，这本身就是对这位曾与哈罗等"不可教育"学生合作、开创创新教学法的学者最恰当的致敬。



As the concept of neurodiversity takes root on college campuses, ASAN has developed into an incubator for the next generation of disability rights activists, many of whom are women. Lydia Brown, an alum of the organization’s summer leadership training program, provided testimony to the United Nations Special Rapporteur on Torture that increased public pressure on the Judge Rotenberg Educational Center’s continued use of electric shock on autistic children. Brown was named a White House Champion for change in 2013. Another alum, Kris Guin, went on to launch an organization called Queerability to explore the intersection between disability and LGBT issues.

随着神经多样性理念在大学校园中扎根，ASAN 已发展为培养下一代残障权利活动家的孵化器，其中许多成员为女性。该组织暑期领导力培训项目的毕业生莉迪亚·布朗，曾向联合国酷刑问题特别报告员提供证词，促使公众对罗滕伯格法官教育中心持续对孤独谱系儿童使用电击疗法施加更大压力。布朗于 2013 年被授予白宫变革先锋称号。另一位毕业生克里斯·吉恩则创立了名为"酷儿能力"的组织，致力于探索残障与 LGBT 议题的交叉领域。



ASAN’s Julia Bascom published a groundbreaking anthology of essays by people on the spectrum called Loud Hands, which offered a broad range of autistic perspectives on such issues as being labeled “low-functioning” and the harm inflicted by organizations like Autism Speaks that frame autistic people as a tragedy and a burden to society. “One of the cruelest tricks our culture plays on autistic people is that it makes us strangers to ourselves,” Bascom wrote, adding that autistics are no longer willing to be “spectators in our own stories.”

ASAN 的朱莉娅·巴斯康出版了一部开创性的文集《响亮的手》，收录了孤独谱系群体的多篇随笔，从广泛的自闭症视角探讨了被贴上"低功能"标签等问题，以及像"自闭症之声"这类组织将孤独谱系群体描绘成社会悲剧和负担所造成的伤害。"我们的文化对孤独谱系群体施加的最残酷的伎俩之一，就是让我们成为自己的陌生人，"巴斯康写道，并补充说，孤独谱系群体不再愿意"成为自己故事的旁观者"。



VI



For parents like Craig and Shannon Rosa, the emergence of the neurodiversity movement has offered ways of fighting for a better future for their children that don’t depend on hopes of recovery. It has also made something available to young people on the spectrum that previous generations of autistic people never had: role models of happy, creative, and socially engaged autistic lives.

对于像克雷格和香农·罗莎这样的父母来说，神经多样性运动的兴起为他们提供了为孩子争取更美好未来的途径，这些途径不再依赖于康复的希望。它还为年轻一代的孤独谱系群体提供了前几代自闭症人士从未拥有过的东西：快乐、富有创造力且积极参与社会生活的孤独谱系生活榜样。



At a conference in May 2011, Shannon met a professor from Adelphi University named Stephen Shore. When Shore was eighteen months old, he suddenly lost the ability to speak. A year later, he was diagnosed with “atypical development with strong autistic tendencies,” and his doctors recommended institutionalization. His parents refused to give up on him and instead created a comprehensive intervention program for him emphasizing music, movement, and techniques to help him integrate the confusing barrage of information from his senses. At four, his spoken language returned, and he went on to earn a PhD in special education at Boston University.

在 2011 年 5 月的一次会议上，香农结识了阿德尔菲大学的教授斯蒂芬·肖尔。肖尔在 18 个月大时突然丧失语言能力，一年后被诊断为"非典型发育伴随强烈孤独谱系倾向"，医生建议将其送入收容机构。但他的父母没有放弃，反而为他制定了一套综合干预方案，重点通过音乐、运动及整合感官信息的技术，帮助他处理纷繁混乱的感知信息。四岁时，他的口语能力恢复，最终在波士顿大学获得特殊教育博士学位。



At the conference, Shannon told him that she wished she could find a music teacher for Leo. That October, he arrived at the Rosas’ house to give Leo his first lesson. Now in his fifties, Shore is a witty and affable man with a neatly trimmed beard who revels in his autistic eccentricities. (He praises things that he likes by calling them “very stimmy.”) At first, Leo didn’t seem to notice that Shore had entered the room, but he quickly warmed up to him. For kids with limited expressive language like Leo, Shore has found that music can serve as a more natural medium of communication than speech.

会议上，香农告诉他自己希望能为利奥找一位音乐老师。那年十月，肖尔来到罗萨斯家，为利奥上了第一堂课。年过五旬的肖尔是个机智随和的人，留着修剪整齐的胡须，对自己的孤独谱系特质颇为自得。（他称赞喜欢的事物时会说"非常刺激"。）起初利奥似乎没注意到肖尔进了房间，但很快就对他热络起来。肖尔发现，对于像利奥这样语言表达能力有限的孩子，音乐比言语更能成为自然的沟通媒介。



First, Shore sat down at a table with a pen, a piece of paper, a ruler, and a stack of Post-its, and indicated that Leo was welcome to sit with him. He didn’t become impatient as Leo orbited around the room, gradually coming to rest in the chair next to Shore. Then Shore asked Leo to draw a straight line on the paper, using the ruler, and then three more perpendicular lines, creating a grid. Shore asked Leo to name the first letter of the alphabet and he said “A!” He prompted Leo to draw an A in the first box on the grid, which he did successfully. Eventually, Leo had filled in the grid with the first seven letters of the alphabet, corresponding to the range of keys on his sister Zelly’s electronic keyboard. Then Shore took a stack of Post-its that had been labeled with these letters and began laying them across the tops of the appropriate keys. Leo quickly picked up on the pattern and completed it himself, earning praise from Shore.

首先，肖尔在一张桌子前坐下，桌上放着笔、纸、尺子和一叠便利贴，并示意利奥可以坐在他旁边。利奥在房间里转悠，慢慢坐到肖尔旁边的椅子上时，他并没有表现出不耐烦。接着，肖尔让利奥用尺子在纸上画一条直线，再画三条垂直线，形成一个网格。肖尔问利奥字母表的第一个字母是什么，他回答：“A！”肖尔引导他在网格的第一个格子里画一个 A，他成功完成了。最终，利奥在网格中填满了字母表的前七个字母，对应他姐姐泽莉电子琴上的音域。然后，肖尔拿出一叠标有这些字母的便利贴，开始将它们贴在相应琴键的上方。利奥很快领会了规律，自己完成了剩下的部分，赢得了肖尔的称赞。



At no point did Shore compel Leo to do something he didn’t want to do. If Leo jumped up and hopped up and down for a minute because he got excited, that was okay. Shore acted like there was all the time in the world. The little tasks he gave Leo quickly became self-rewarding, because they played to a classic autistic strength: pattern recognition. Together, they turned the eighty-eight keys on the keyboard into a map that Leo could then explore by playing the notes. Shannon had never seen anyone “get” Leo so quickly. (Shore confesses that he finds neurotypical kids harder to teach, because he doesn’t understand how their minds work.) By the end of the hourlong lesson, Leo could play a simple, pleasing sequence of notes—and he had also learned that he could be good at doing something he had never tried before.

肖尔从未强迫利奥做任何他不愿意做的事。如果利奥因为兴奋而跳起来蹦跶一分钟，那也没关系。肖尔表现得仿佛时间永远充裕。他交给利奥的小任务很快就变成了自我奖励，因为这些任务利用了孤独谱系的一个经典优势：模式识别。他们一起将键盘上的八十八个琴键变成了一张地图，利奥可以通过弹奏音符来探索它。香农从未见过有人能如此迅速地“理解”利奥。（肖尔坦言，他觉得教神经典型的孩子更难，因为他无法理解他们的思维方式。）到一小时的课程结束时，利奥已经能弹奏出一段简单悦耳的音符序列——他也明白了，自己可以擅长一件从未尝试过的事情。



One of the most important lessons that Shannon and Craig have learned on their journey with Leo is patience. Instead of comparing his arc of development to an idealized set of milestones, they have come to accept that he is unfolding at his own pace. Two steps forward and three steps back—and then, one day, a hurtling leap into his own future, as if he’d been saving it up.

香农和克雷格在与利奥的旅程中学到的最重要一课就是耐心。他们不再将他的成长轨迹与理想化的里程碑相比较，而是逐渐接受他以自己的节奏成长。两步前进，三步后退——然后，有一天，他会突然飞跃进自己的未来，仿佛这一切都是他积蓄已久的力量。



So that parents just starting out on the journey with their own children don’t have to go through the ordeal that the Rosas did, Shannon and her circle of friends launched a website called Thinking Person’s Guide to Autism. The range of subjects covered on the site is broad (from “Outings, Travel, and Autism” to “When Is Medication the Right Choice for Your Child?”) and there’s no whitewashing or promotion of dubious treatments. Just the facts, from people a little further down the road.

为了让那些刚刚踏上育儿之路的父母们不必经历罗莎一家所遭遇的磨难，香农和她的朋友们创建了一个名为《孤独症思考者指南》的网站。该网站涵盖的主题范围广泛（从“外出、旅行与孤独症”到“何时为孩子选择药物才是正确的？”），并且没有粉饰或推广可疑疗法。只有来自那些更有经验的人提供的真实信息。



Some of the site’s regular contributors are on the spectrum themselves, and some are autistic parents of autistic children, like Carol Greenberg. I visited Carol; her husband, John Ordover; and their son Arren at their home in Brooklyn, which was decorated with artifacts reflecting their shared love of science fiction, including a replica of a brain in a bubbling aquarium. They met at a Star Trek convention as teenagers, married years later, and eventually became the editors of the Star Trek book franchise.

该网站的一些常驻撰稿人本身就在孤独谱系上，还有一些是孤独谱系孩子的孤独谱系父母，比如卡罗尔·格林伯格。我拜访了卡罗尔、她的丈夫约翰·奥多弗以及他们的儿子阿伦，他们住在布鲁克林，家中装饰着反映他们共同热爱科幻小说的物品，包括一个在冒泡水族箱中的大脑复制品。他们十几岁时在一次《星际迷航》大会上相识，多年后结婚，并最终成为了《星际迷航》系列图书的编辑。



As a young girl, Carol was drawn to Star Trek because she saw a metaphor for an inclusive society in the multiracial, multispecies crew of the Enterprise. “There was no one who was left out in the Star Trek universe, no one who was ostracized, no one who was too weird. In fact, the weirder you were, the cooler you were, because you had more to bring to the table,” she says. “That was a lifesaving message for a kid who got bullied for being different. Look at Geordi, who had a visual impairment but had access to technology that enabled him to turn it into a gift and made it possible for him to see things that other people couldn’t see. I wanted to live on the Enterprise.”

小时候，卡罗尔迷上《星际迷航》，是因为她从企业号上由多物种、多民族组成的船员身上，看到了一个包容社会的隐喻。“在《星际迷航》的宇宙里，没有人被排除在外，没有人被排斥，也没有人因为太古怪而被嫌弃。事实上，你越古怪就越酷，因为你能为团队带来更多价值，”她说，“对一个因与众不同而遭受欺凌的孩子来说，这简直是救命稻草。看看乔迪，他虽然有视觉损伤，但借助科技将缺陷转化为天赋，能看到别人看不见的东西。我真想生活在企业号上。”



Predictably, the member of the crew that Carol related to most was Mr. Spock, who seemed much cooler than the conniving and chronically intemperate humans around him. (When her peers ridiculed her in school, she would ask herself, “What would Spock do?”) When Arren was diagnosed, she began researching autism online and kept seeing messages for parents like “Prepare to have to enter a totally different world to understand your child.” But reading the descriptions she kept thinking, “Different how?”

不出所料，卡罗尔最认同的船员是斯波克先生，他比身边那些诡计多端、脾气暴躁的人类酷多了。（当同学在学校嘲笑她时，她会问自己：“斯波克会怎么做？”）阿伦被确诊后，她开始在网上搜索关于孤独症的信息，总能看到对家长说这样的话：“做好准备，你需要进入一个完全不同的世界才能理解你的孩子。”但读着那些描述，她一直在想：“到底哪里不同？”



Carol was diagnosed two years later, at age forty-four. Now she works as a special-education advocate for families, helping them through the process of having their children evaluated, developing an individualized education program, and accompanying them to IEP meetings.

卡罗尔在两年后被诊断出患有此症，当时她 44 岁。如今，她作为一名特殊教育倡导者，帮助家庭完成孩子评估、制定个性化教育计划，并陪同他们参加 IEP 会议。



As we wrapped up our interview, night was falling in Brooklyn. Arren came downstairs and said, “Light candles, light candles.” When he first started doing this, Carol and John became concerned about his sudden interest in fire. But then they heard him whisper “Baruch,” the first word of the Hebrew blessing for the Sabbath. Though it wasn’t actually the Sabbath, Carol and Arren went into the kitchen, lit candles at the stove, and sang the old prayers together anyway. “When I look at my son,” she says, “I think, ‘He’s not broken. He’s just neurologically outnumbered, like me.’”

我们的采访结束时，布鲁克林夜幕降临。阿伦下楼说：“点蜡烛，点蜡烛。”起初他这样做时，卡罗尔和约翰曾担心他突然对火产生兴趣。但随后他们听到他低语“Baruch”——这是希伯来语安息日祝福的第一个词。尽管那天并非真正的安息日，卡罗尔和阿伦还是走进厨房，在炉边点燃蜡烛，一起唱起古老的祷文。“当我看着儿子时，”她说，“我想，‘他没有残缺，只是在神经学上像少数派一样，和我一样。’”



—



ON A DRIZZLY, windswept afternoon in 2012—a typical spring day in San Francisco—Shannon and Leo visited the California Academy of Sciences with Julia Bascom and Zoe Gross of ASAN. The Cal Academy, located in Golden Gate Park, is one of Leo’s favorite places to go, because it boasts an aquarium that wraps around overhead (allowing Leo to lie flat on a bench looking up through schools of glittering fish) and a planetarium (“I want to go to space!” he says).

2012 年一个细雨绵绵、凉风习习的下午——旧金山典型的春日——香农与利奥，以及 ASAN 的朱莉娅·巴斯康姆和佐伊·格罗斯，一同参观了加州科学院。这座位于金门公园内的科学院是利奥最爱的去处之一，因为这里既有环绕头顶的水族馆（利奥可以平躺在长椅上，仰望成群的银鳞游鱼），又有天文馆（“我要去太空！”他喊道）。



Friends like Julia and Zoe are able to translate Leo’s world into terms that Shannon can understand. After she posted a video of her son furiously pacing in a circle at the top level of an elaborate jungle gym in a playground, Zoe commented:

像朱莉娅和佐伊这样的朋友，能够将利奥的世界翻译成香农能理解的语言。当香农上传了一段视频——儿子在游乐场一座精巧的攀爬架顶层疯狂绕圈踱步——佐伊评论道：



Oh wow, what a great video. That pacing looks delicious (delicious is a food word, but acceptable here because I refer to a pleasurable and nutritious component of one’s sensory diet). There’s a certain size of circle you can pace—the one Leo’s doing here looks about perfect for his height—that gives you a sensation of pressure in your body, when you find yourself leaning inward to stay on course.

哦哇，这段视频真棒。那种踱步看起来好“美味”（“美味”本是形容食物的词，但这里可以接受，因为我指的是感官饮食中令人愉悦且富有营养的部分）。踱步时绕的圈有特定大小——利奥现在绕的圈似乎与他的身高完美契合——当你身体向内倾斜以保持轨迹时，会感受到体内产生一种压力感。



Leo also clearly enjoys being around people who are on the same wavelength. When Zoe sits down in the wraparound aquarium, Leo sits down too. (She’s wearing headphones to limit the confusing barrage of noise in public spaces.) He gently slides his fingertips down her forearm while twiddling one of his ever-present straws between his lips, and she brushes his forearm in return. Then he lies down on the bench in a comfortable tilde shape to stare up at the fish while resting his head in the softness of her lap, and she’s okay with that. After a while, he flips over on his belly and looks at the floor through the narrow slats in the bench, enjoying a quiet moment to himself. She’s okay with that too. Finally he gathers himself up so that he’s facing Zoe and gently interlaces his fingers with hers and commences rocking back and forth, making a kind of human seesaw. Only when a loud group of children comes bustling through their little sanctuary do they decide it’s time to move on.

利奥显然也很享受与志同道合的人相处。当佐伊在环形水族馆坐下时，利奥也坐了下来。（她戴着耳机，以隔绝公共场所嘈杂的噪音。）他一边用嘴唇拨弄着那根从不离手的吸管，一边用指尖轻轻滑过她的前臂，而她也轻抚他的前臂作为回应。随后他躺在长椅上，身体舒适地蜷成波浪形，仰头望着游鱼，将头枕在她柔软的大腿上——她并不介意。过了一会儿，他翻身趴着，透过长椅狭窄的缝隙凝视地板，享受片刻独处的宁静。她依然默许。最后他直起身子面对佐伊，温柔地将手指与她交缠，开始前后摇晃，仿佛组成了一架人形跷跷板。直到一群喧闹的孩子冲进他们的小天地，两人才决定该离开了。



Leo gets a little upset when his mother decides that there’s not enough time to “go to space” today, but soon he’s blissed out in front of the main window of the aquarium, pacing back and forth along its full length, gently resting himself or pressing against the glass periodically, as if this invisible boundary is somehow reassuring. (Later, as Leo, Zoe, and Julia walk down a hall, they each nonchalantly tap the same spot on the wall as they pass, as if it provides a useful coordinate for situating their bodies in space.)

当妈妈决定今天没有足够时间“去太空”时，里奥有点不高兴，但很快他就沉浸在 aquarium 主窗前，沿着整个玻璃墙来回踱步，时不时轻轻靠上去或贴着玻璃，仿佛这道无形的屏障能带来某种安慰。（后来，当里奥、佐伊和朱莉娅走过走廊时，他们各自漫不经心地拍了拍墙上同一个位置，仿佛这能为他们在空间中的身体定位提供有用的坐标。）



On our way out of the museum, Leo spots a big, white puffy dinosaur wearing a striped orange scarf, which exerts an irresistible attraction upon him. He puts his face right up to the face of this improbable creature (really a guide in a dinosaur suit), gazing at it intently from a couple of inches away. The employees at the Cal Academy are well aware of the fact that kids like Leo love dinosaurs, and no one bats an eye. As we walk out into the soft rain, Shannon bends her head down, kisses the crown of her son’s head, and says, “You did good, friend.”

离开博物馆时，里奥发现了一只戴着条纹橙色围巾的白色蓬松恐龙，这对他产生了不可抗拒的吸引力。他把脸凑到这只不可思议的生物（其实是穿着恐龙服装的引导员）面前，从几英寸的距离专注地凝视着它。加州科学院的员工们很清楚像里奥这样的孩子喜欢恐龙，所以没人对此大惊小怪。当我们走进绵绵细雨时，香农低下头，亲吻了儿子的头顶，说道：“你做得很好，朋友。”







---


NeuroTribes








Twelve

十二



BUILDING THE ENTERPRISE: DESIGNS FOR A NEURODIVERSE WORLD

构建企业：为神经多元世界设计



We need all hands on deck to right the ship of humanity.

我们需要全员齐心协力，才能纠正人类这艘航船的方向。



—ZOSIA ZAKS

——佐西亚·扎克斯



What is autism?

什么是孤独症？



Eight decades after Gottfried’s grandmother brought him to the door of Asperger’s clinic seeking to understand his behavior, many aspects of this question are still open. But there are a few points on which clinicians, parents, and neurodiversity advocates agree.

在戈特弗里德的祖母带他来到阿斯伯格诊所门前、试图理解他行为的八十年后，这个问题的许多方面仍悬而未决。但临床医生、家长和神经多样性倡导者在几个关键点上达成了共识。



Most researchers now believe that autism is not a single unified entity but a cluster of underlying conditions. These conditions produce a distinctive constellation of behavior and needs that manifests in different ways at various stages of an individual’s development. Adequately addressing these needs requires a lifetime of support from parents, educators, and the community, as Asperger predicted back in 1938. He was equally prescient in insisting that the traits of autism are “not at all rare.” In fact, given current estimates of prevalence, autistic people constitute one of the largest minorities in the world. There are roughly as many people on the spectrum in America as there are Jews.

如今大多数研究者认为，孤独症并非单一统一的病症，而是一组潜在状况的集合。这些状况会产生独特的行为模式与需求组合，在个体发展的不同阶段以不同方式呈现。要妥善满足这些需求，需要父母、教育者和社区终其一生的支持——正如阿斯伯格早在 1938 年所预言的那样。他同样具有先见之明地断言，孤独症的特征"绝非罕见"。事实上，根据当前的患病率估算，孤独谱系群体已成为世界上最大的少数群体之一。在美国，谱系人群的数量与犹太人口大致相当。



A thorough review of history also vindicates Asperger’s notion that autistic people have always been part of the human community, though they have often been relegated to the margins of society. For most of the twentieth century, they were hidden behind a welter of competing labels—Sukhareva’s “schizoid personality disorder,” Despert and Bender’s “childhood schizophrenia,” Robinson and Vitale’s “children with circumscribed interests,” Grandin’s initial diagnosis of “minimal brain damage,” and many other labels not mentioned in this book, such as “multiplex personality disorder,” which have fallen out of use. In the wake of the vaccine controversy, however, society continues to insist on framing autism as a contemporary aberration—the unique disorder of our uniquely disordered times—caused by some tragic convergence of genetic predisposition and risk factors hidden somewhere in the toxic modern world, such as air pollution, an overdose of video games, and highly processed foods.

对历史的全面审视也证实了阿斯伯格的观点：孤独谱系群体始终是人类社会的一部分，尽管他们常被边缘化。二十世纪的大部分时间里，他们被淹没在相互竞争的各类诊断标签中——苏哈列娃的"分裂样人格障碍"、德斯珀特和本德的"童年精神分裂症"、罗宾逊和维塔莱的"兴趣狭窄儿童"、格兰丁最初被诊断为"轻微脑损伤"，以及本书未提及的许多其他标签，如已不再使用的"多重人格障碍"。然而，在疫苗争议之后，社会仍坚持将孤独谱系视为当代的异常现象——这个独特混乱时代特有的紊乱——由遗传易感性与隐藏在现代有毒环境中的风险因素（如空气污染、过度沉迷电子游戏和高度加工食品）的悲剧性交汇所致。



Our DNA tells a different story. In recent years, researchers have determined that most cases of autism are not rooted in rare de novo mutations but in very old genes that are shared widely in the general population while being concentrated more in certain families than others. Whatever autism is, it is not a unique product of modern civilization. It is a strange gift from our deep past, passed down through millions of years of evolution.

我们的 DNA 讲述着不同的故事。近年来，研究人员已确定大多数孤独症案例并非源于罕见的基因新发突变，而是源自非常古老的基因——这些基因在普通人群中广泛存在，但在某些家族中更为集中。无论孤独症的本质为何，它绝非现代文明的独特产物。它是来自远古时代的奇特馈赠，经由数百万年的进化传承至今。



Neurodiversity advocates propose that instead of viewing this gift as an error of nature—a puzzle to be solved and eliminated with techniques like prenatal testing and selective abortion—society should regard it as a valuable part of humanity’s genetic legacy while ameliorating the aspects of autism that can be profoundly disabling without adequate forms of support. They suggest that, instead of investing millions of dollars a year to uncover the causes of autism in the future, we should be helping autistic people and their families live happier, healthier, more productive, and more secure lives in the present.

神经多样性倡导者提出，社会不应将这份馈赠视为自然的错误——一个需要通过产前检测和选择性流产等技术来破解和消除的谜题——而应将其视为人类基因遗产中宝贵的一部分，同时改善那些因缺乏足够支持而可能导致严重障碍的孤独症特征。他们建议，与其每年投入数百万美元去探寻孤独症的成因，不如在当下帮助孤独谱系群体及其家人过上更幸福、更健康、更富有成效、更有保障的生活。



This process has barely begun. Imagine if society had put off the issue of civil rights until the genetics of race were sorted out, or denied wheelchair users access to public buildings while insisting that someday, with the help of science, everyone will be able to walk. Viewed as a form of disability that is relatively common rather than as a baffling enigma, autism is not so baffling after all. Designing appropriate forms of support and accommodation is not beyond our capabilities as a society, as the history of the disability rights movement proves. But first we have to learn to think more intelligently about people who think differently.

这一进程才刚刚开始。试想，如果社会将民权问题搁置到种族基因被彻底厘清之后，或是在坚持"总有一天科学能让所有人都能行走"的同时，拒绝为轮椅使用者提供公共建筑的无障碍通道，那将是何等荒谬。将孤独谱系视为一种相对常见的残障形式，而非令人费解的谜团时，它其实并不那么难以理解。正如残障权利运动的历史所证明的，设计适当的支持与包容方案并非社会能力所不及。但首先，我们必须学会以更明智的方式去理解那些思维方式与众不同的人。



—



ONE WAY TO UNDERSTAND neurodiversity is to think in terms of human operating systems instead of diagnostic labels like dyslexia and ADHD. The brain is, above all, a marvelously adaptive organism, adept at maximizing its chances of success even in the face of daunting limitations.

理解神经多样性的一个途径，是将其视为人类操作系统的差异，而非贴上阅读障碍或注意力缺陷多动障碍这类诊断标签。大脑首先是一个具有惊人适应能力的器官，即便面对重重限制，也能巧妙地将成功概率最大化。



Just because a computer is not running Windows doesn’t mean that it’s broken. Not all the features of atypical human operating systems are bugs. By autistic standards, the “normal” brain is easily distractible, is obsessively social, and suffers from a deficit of attention to detail and routine. Thus people on the spectrum experience the neurotypical world as relentlessly unpredictable and chaotic, perpetually turned up too loud, and full of people who have little respect for personal space.

电脑没运行 Windows，不代表它坏了。非典型人类操作系统的所有特性并非都是缺陷。按照孤独谱系的标准，“正常”大脑容易分心、过度热衷社交，且缺乏对细节和常规的关注。因此，谱系人士眼中的神经典型世界总是难以预测、混乱不堪，音量永远调得过高，而且充斥着不尊重个人空间的人。



The main reason why the Internet was able to transform the world in a single generation is that it was specifically built to be “platform agnostic.” The Internet doesn’t care if your home computer or mobile device is running Windows, Linux, or the latest version of Apple’s iOS. Its protocols and standards were designed to work with them all to maximize the potential for innovation at the edges.

互联网之所以能在短短一代人的时间内改变世界，主要原因在于它被特意设计为“平台无关”。互联网不在乎你的家用电脑或移动设备运行的是 Windows、Linux 还是最新版苹果 iOS 系统。它的协议和标准旨在兼容所有这些系统，从而最大限度地激发边缘创新的潜力。



In recent years, a growing alliance of autistic self-advocates, parents, and educators who have embraced the concept of neurodiversity have suggested a number of innovations that could provide the foundation for an open world designed to work with a broad range of human operating systems.

近年来，越来越多的孤独谱系自我倡导者、家长和教育工作者接受了神经多样性的概念，他们提出了一系列创新方案，旨在为一个能够兼容多种人类操作系统的开放世界奠定基础。



The physical layout of such a world would offer a variety of sensory-friendly environments based on principles developed in autistic spaces like Autreat. An inclusive school, for example, would feature designated quiet areas where a student who felt temporarily overwhelmed could avoid a meltdown. In classrooms, distracting sensory input—such as the buzzing of fluorescent lights—would be kept to a minimum. Students would also be allowed to customize their personal sensory space by wearing noise-reducing headphones, sunglasses to avoid glare, and other easily affordable and minimally disruptive accommodations.

这样一个世界的物理布局将基于 Autreat 等孤独谱系友好空间所发展的原则，提供多种感官友好的环境。例如，一所包容性学校会设有指定的安静区域，让暂时感到不堪重负的学生能够避免情绪崩溃。在教室里，诸如荧光灯嗡嗡声等分散注意力的感官刺激将被控制在最低限度。学生还可以通过佩戴降噪耳机、防眩光太阳镜以及其他价格低廉且干扰性小的辅助设备，来定制自己的个人感官空间。



In 2011, a nonprofit corporation called the Theatre Development Fund in New York City launched an initiative to encourage Broadway producers to offer “autism-friendly” performances of hit shows like Mary Poppins and The Lion King. At these events, the use of strobe lights and pyrotechnics onstage was limited, quiet areas were set aside in the theater lobby, and social stories were made available to parents beforehand so that their children could know what to expect. These events were so successful that major cinema chains like AMC have begun offering sensory-friendly showings of movies like Disney’s Frozen in theaters all over the country. This is not only a humane idea, it’s smart marketing too, because the families of autistic children are often hesitant to bring them to movies and restaurants for fear of disrupting the experience of the other patrons. These special showings are invariably in high demand.

2011 年，纽约市一家名为"戏剧发展基金"的非营利组织发起了一项倡议，鼓励百老汇制作人为《欢乐满人间》和《狮子王》等热门剧目提供"孤独谱系友好"演出。在这些活动中，舞台上的频闪灯和烟火效果受到限制，剧院大厅设有安静区域，家长可提前获取社交故事资料，以便孩子了解演出内容。这些活动大获成功，以至于 AMC 等大型连锁影院开始在全美各地影院推出迪士尼《冰雪奇缘》等电影的感官友好场次。这不仅是人道主义举措，更是精明的营销策略——因为孤独谱系儿童的家长常因担心打扰其他观众而犹豫是否带孩子观影就餐。这些特殊场次总是供不应求。



The advent of digital technology has opened up new horizons in education for adapting teaching materials to suit learners with a diverse range of learning styles. Some students learn best by reading, while others benefit most from oral instruction; with tablet devices and customizable software, the same core curriculum can support both. The leader in this area has been the National Center on Universal Design for Learning, which offers free guidelines and resources to help teachers adapt their curricula for students with learning differences.

数字技术的出现为教育开辟了新的视野，使教学材料能够适应具有多样化学习风格的学习者。有些学生通过阅读学习效果最佳，而另一些则从口头指导中获益最多；借助平板设备和可定制软件，同一核心课程可以同时支持这两种学习方式。在这一领域，通用学习设计国家中心一直处于领先地位，该中心提供免费的指导方针和资源，帮助教师调整课程以适应有学习差异的学生。



Educators like Thomas Armstrong, author of Neurodiversity in the Classroom, suggest that more emphasis should be placed on early childhood education, when a child’s individual learning style first comes to light, because a child’s experiences in school can set him or her up for success or failure in later life. Armstrong points out that, too often, the process of negotiating an Individualized Education Program focuses exclusively on addressing a child’s deficits at the expense of focusing on strengths that teachers could employ to engage the child’s interests and help build confidence.

《课堂中的神经多样性》一书的作者托马斯·阿姆斯特朗等教育工作者建议，应更加重视幼儿教育，因为儿童的个人学习风格正是在这个阶段首次显现。儿童在学校的经历可能决定其未来人生的成败。阿姆斯特朗指出，在制定个性化教育计划的过程中，往往只关注弥补孩子的缺陷，而忽视了利用教师可以发挥的优势来激发孩子的兴趣并帮助其建立自信。



Many autistic people benefit from hands-on learning. The rise of the Maker movement—which hosts events called Maker Faires, where garage inventors of all ages are encouraged to show off their latest projects—has been a boon to young people on the spectrum. At the White House Science Fair in 2012, President Obama was featured shooting off an “Extreme Marshmallow Cannon,” which a fourteen-year-old autistic boy named Joey Hudy had designed and built himself.

许多孤独谱系群体受益于动手学习。创客运动的兴起——该运动举办名为"创客集市"的活动，鼓励各年龄段的车库发明家展示最新作品——对谱系青少年而言是一大福音。在 2012 年白宫科学展上，奥巴马总统亲自发射了一台"极限棉花糖大炮"，这台装置正是由 14 岁的孤独谱系少年乔伊·哈迪自行设计并制作的。



Neurodiversity is also being embraced in the workplace by companies like Specialisterne, founded in Denmark, which employs people on the spectrum to put their autistic intelligence to work in the technology industry. Specialisterne has been so successful that it has opened satellite offices in the United Kingdom and the United States and recently forged a strategic alliance with the German software company SAP to serve the needs of the rapidly growing technology industry in India. Instead of putting potential candidates through grueling face-to-face interviews, Specialisterne lets them cut loose with a table full of Lego Mindstorm Robots, little machines that can be programmed to perform simple tasks. Thus, candidates can just show off their skills rather than have to explain them.

神经多样性正被职场接纳，例如丹麦创立的 Specialisterne 公司，专门雇佣孤独谱系群体，让他们运用孤独谱系智慧投身科技行业。这家公司大获成功，已在英国和美国设立分支机构，近期还与德国软件公司 SAP 结成战略联盟，以满足印度快速发展的科技产业需求。Specialisterne 摒弃了让候选人经历严苛面对面面试的传统模式，转而让他们在一张摆满乐高头脑风暴机器人的桌前尽情发挥——这些小型机器可通过编程完成简单任务。如此一来，候选人只需展示技能，无需费力解释。



Neurodiversity activists have also pushed for more autistic representation in policy making, using the slogan “Nothing about us, without us.” Fund-raising organizations like Autism Speaks have been resistant to the input of autistic adults, who are arguably in the best position to decide what kinds of research would benefit autistic people and their families most.

神经多样性倡导者还推动政策制定中增加孤独谱系群体的代表权，他们高呼“没有我们的参与，不要做关于我们的决定”这一口号。像“孤独症之声”这样的筹款组织一直抗拒成年孤独谱系个体的意见，而这些人恰恰最有资格决定哪些研究能最大程度惠及孤独谱系群体及其家庭。



“Nothing about us, without us” also extends to the process of doing science itself. In recent years, a psychiatrist at the University of Montréal, Laurent Mottron, has produced a series of groundbreaking studies on autism with the help of his principal collaborator, an autistic researcher named Michelle Dawson. She fulfills a number of essential functions in the lab, including keeping Mottron up-to-date with the state of the research in the field (“She reads everything and forgets nothing,” he says), vetting experimental designs for errors and subtle forms of bias, and advocating for higher scientific standards in the field overall. “Many autistics, I believe, are suited for academic science,” Mottron wrote in Nature in 2011. “I believe that they contribute to science because of their autism, not in spite of it.”

“没有我们的参与，就不要做关于我们的决定”这一原则同样适用于科学研究本身。近年来，蒙特利尔大学的精神病学家洛朗·莫特龙在其主要合作者——一位名叫米歇尔·道森的孤独谱系研究者——的协助下，完成了一系列关于孤独症的开创性研究。道森在实验室中承担着多项关键职能，包括让莫特龙及时了解该领域的研究进展（莫特龙评价道：“她阅读所有文献且过目不忘”），审查实验设计中的错误和隐性偏见，并倡导提升整个领域的科学标准。2011 年，莫特龙在《自然》杂志上写道：“我相信许多孤独谱系人士适合从事学术科研。我认为他们之所以能为科学做出贡献，恰恰是因为他们的孤独谱系特质，而非克服了这种特质。”



A group called the Academic Autistic Spectrum Partnership in Research and Education (AASPIRE) is collaborating with self-advocates to set their agenda for research. In 2014, AASPIRE released a comprehensive toolkit designed to inform patients and providers of the unique needs of autistic people in the health care system. ASAN’s leadership training program has demonstrated the potential of peer mentoring for young people on the spectrum. Zoe Gross has recently completed a term of service as a disability policy staffer for the Senate Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee, and is now working in the HHS Administration on Community Living. Like Lydia Brown, she was named a White House Champion for Change in 2013. ASAN has also launched an internship program with the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation.

一个名为“学术孤独谱系研究与教育合作组织”（AASPIRE）的团体正与自我倡导者合作，制定其研究议程。2014 年，AASPIRE 发布了一套全面的工具包，旨在告知患者和医疗服务提供者孤独谱系群体在医疗保健系统中的特殊需求。ASAN 的领导力培训项目已展现出同伴指导对孤独谱系年轻人的潜力。佐伊·格罗斯近期刚完成在参议院健康、教育、劳工与养老金委员会担任残障政策工作人员的任期，目前正在卫生与公众服务部社区生活管理局工作。与莉迪亚·布朗一样，她于 2013 年被授予白宫变革先锋称号。ASAN 还与联邦住房贷款抵押公司合作启动了实习项目。



The process of building a world suited to the needs and special abilities of all kinds of minds is just starting, but unlike long-range projects like teasing out the genetics and environmental factors that contribute to complex conditions like autism, the returns for autistic people and their families are practical and immediate. These innovations are also often much less expensive than projects requiring millions of dollars in federal funding.

构建一个适合各类心智需求与特殊能力的世界的进程才刚刚开始，但与解析孤独症等复杂状况的遗传和环境因素这类长期项目不同，这项努力为孤独谱系群体及其家庭带来的回报是切实且立竿见影的。这些创新举措往往也比需要数百万美元联邦资金支持的项目成本低得多。



With the generation of autistic people diagnosed in the 1990s now coming of age, society can no longer afford to pretend that autism suddenly loomed up out of nowhere, like the black monolith in 2001: A Space Odyssey. There is much work to be done.

随着上世纪 90 年代确诊的孤独谱系群体一代人逐渐成年，社会再也不能假装孤独症像《2001 太空漫游》中的黑色巨石那样凭空降临。前路漫漫，任重道远。






---


NeuroTribes








Epilogue

后记



THE MAYOR OF KENSINGTON

肯辛顿市长



Bernie Rimland’s desk is much as he left it, buried under stacks of file folders and letters from parents all over the world. The room that once resonated with the sound of his voice instilling confidence in a mother or father who dialed a number in the middle of the night is eerily quiet, as if he had just stepped away for a moment. The headquarters of the Autism Research Institute feels a bit sleepy these days, with its autographed Rain Man memorabilia and racks of dusty brochures.

伯尼·里姆兰德的办公桌仍保持着他离开时的模样，堆满了文件夹和来自世界各地父母的信件。这个曾回荡着他声音的房间——那声音曾给深夜拨打电话的父母注入信心——如今异常安静，仿佛他只是暂时离开。孤独症研究所总部如今显得有些沉寂，墙上挂着签名的《雨人》纪念品，架子上摆满积灰的宣传册。



But suddenly the door swings open and a fit, boyishly handsome man with hair graying at the temples walks in and shakes my hand. It’s Mark, Bernie’s son, who has come down to the office to make the final preparations for his art opening in the gallery next door. Before he does that, we sit down to chat on his favorite bench in a sunny park. Even locals who have no idea of the roles that Mark and his father played in autism history smile and wave to the man they call the Mayor of Kensington.

但门突然被推开，一位身材匀称、鬓角花白却带着少年气质的男士走进来与我握手。他是伯尼的儿子马克，来办公室为隔壁画廊的个人画展做最后准备。在忙正事前，我们坐在阳光明媚的公园里他最爱的长椅上聊天。即便是不了解马克父子在孤独症领域贡献的当地居民，也会微笑着向这位被称为"肯辛顿市长"的人挥手致意。



Despite the dire prognostications about Mark’s future when he was a child, his life in middle age is creative and rewarding. Although his father was a vocal opponent of removing autistic children from institutions, Mark has never lived in one. He resides in a house near the office with his mother, Gloria; his younger brother, Paul; and his two beloved cats. On weekdays, he attends a day program for adults at St. Madeleine Sophie’s Center in El Cajon, founded in 1966 by nuns and families who rejected the conventional wisdom that developmentally disabled children were uneducable. When Mark was twenty-one, he surprised his mother one day by coming home from the center with a luminous watercolor of an eagle. “Where did you get it?” Gloria asked him. “I’m an artist now,” he replied.

尽管马克童年时外界对他的未来充满悲观预言，但他中年时期的生活却充满创造力且富有意义。虽然他的父亲曾公开反对将孤独谱系儿童从收容机构转移出来，但马克从未在机构中生活过。他与母亲格洛丽亚、弟弟保罗以及两只心爱的猫咪住在办公室附近的房子里。工作日期间，他参加位于埃尔卡洪的圣玛德琳·索菲中心为成年人开设的日间活动项目——该中心由修女和那些拒绝接受"发育障碍儿童不可教育"这一传统观念的家庭于 1966 年共同创办。马克 21 岁那年，某天从中心回家时，他带给母亲一幅熠熠生辉的水彩画，画中是一只雄鹰。"你从哪儿得到的？"格洛丽亚问道。"我现在是艺术家了。"他回答。



After seeing one of his paintings, his sister Helen was inspired to write a children’s book called The Secret Night World of Cats, about a tabby that slips out a bedroom window and has a series of adventures in the urban wilderness, trailed by her curious owner, a little girl named Amanda. Mark worked with his art teacher for a year to develop illustrations using traditional and digital media and to learn how to sprinkle salt on wet watercolors to simulate stars in the night sky. The figures in Mark’s art blaze with an otherworldly radiance, as if he has captured not just their forms and outlines but their inner life force.

妹妹海伦在看过他的一幅画后，受到启发创作了一本名为《猫咪的秘密夜世界》的童书。故事讲述一只虎斑猫溜出卧室窗户，在都市丛林中经历一系列冒险，身后跟着好奇的小主人阿曼达。马克与美术老师合作一年，运用传统与数字媒体创作插画，并学会了在湿水彩上撒盐来模拟夜空繁星。马克画作中的人物闪耀着超凡脱俗的光芒，仿佛他捕捉到的不仅是形体轮廓，更是内在的生命力。



In the pool at St. Madeleine Sophie’s, he learned to channel his inner jock, competing in five sports (swimming, skiing, basketball, volleyball, and floor hockey) and earning blue ribbons in the Special Olympics. He also got a job at the center’s gift shop and gallery, where people often drop by just to hang out with him. At night, he listens to music and reads about the bands he loves (mostly sixties groups like the Beatles, the Beach Boys, and the Doors) before heading down to the Kensington Café, where the waitresses pour his iced tea when they see him coming in the door. A young painter named Ryan Dean who met Mark at the café helps him manage his day-to-day affairs and keeps a notebook at hand to capture his observations of life, which are often amusingly profound—a sort of Tao According to Mark Rimland, with rules of thumb like “Create with laziness.”

在圣玛德琳·索菲中心的泳池里，他学会了释放内心的运动天赋，参加了五项运动（游泳、滑雪、篮球、排球和地板球），并在特奥会上赢得了蓝丝带奖。他还在中心的礼品店和画廊找到了一份工作，人们常常特意来店里和他聊天。晚上，他会听音乐，阅读他喜爱的乐队（大多是六十年代的乐队，比如披头士、海滩男孩和门户乐队）的相关资料，然后前往肯辛顿咖啡馆。那里的女服务员一看到他进门，就会为他倒好冰茶。一位在咖啡馆遇见马克的年轻画家瑞安·迪恩，帮助他处理日常事务，并随身携带笔记本记录他对生活的观察——这些观察往往既有趣又深刻，堪称《马克·瑞姆兰之道》，其中包含诸如“用懒惰去创造”这样的实用法则。



As we chat on the bench about his cat, Sierra (“She cries like a baby when we don’t pet her, because she doesn’t know there are other things to do besides petting”), his love of The Simpsons and the San Diego Zoo (“The zoo just gives me the feeling there aren’t as many spider monkeys as there used to be”), and his indelible memory of riding in an elevator in Beverly Hills to meet Dustin Hoffman (“It was on March 17, 1988, a Thursday”), it’s obvious that Mark is still profoundly autistic, but he is also at ease in his own skin. He knows precisely how many steps it will take to get from the door of his house to the café, and occasionally, when he’s not sure if you’re being as sincere as he is, he’ll say, “Oh, you’re teasing me”—as when I accidentally refer to Sleepless in Seattle as Sleepless in San Diego. When I ask him if he ever gets anxious before giving a presentation at a conference, he says, “I don’t ever let nervousness get in the way of my happiness.”

我们坐在长椅上聊起他的猫西拉（"如果我们不摸她，她会像婴儿一样哭，因为她不知道除了被摸之外还有其他事情可做"），他对《辛普森一家》和圣地亚哥动物园的热爱（"动物园给我的感觉是，蜘蛛猴的数量不如以前多了"），以及他对比弗利山庄乘电梯去见达斯汀·霍夫曼的难忘记忆（"那是 1988 年 3 月 17 日，星期四"）。很明显，马克仍然有严重的孤独谱系特征，但他也活得自在坦然。他清楚地知道从家门口到咖啡馆需要走多少步，偶尔当他不确定你是否像他一样真诚时，他会说："哦，你在逗我"——比如我不小心把《西雅图夜未眠》说成《圣地亚哥夜未眠》的时候。当我问他是否曾在会议演讲前感到焦虑时，他说："我从不允许紧张妨碍我的快乐。"



Exhausted by all the infighting in the autism community, his mother rarely grants interviews anymore, but Gloria agrees to meet me at the Kensington Café for breakfast with Steve Edelson. In her eighties, she is still spry and sharp, with sparkling blue eyes and a salty laugh. Over bagels and tea, I ask her if there’s anything that she wished she’d known when Mark was young.

因孤独症群体内部无休止的内耗而心力交瘁，他的母亲已极少接受采访。但格洛丽亚同意与史蒂夫·埃德尔森一起在肯辛顿咖啡馆与我共进早餐。年过八旬的她依然精神矍铄、思维敏锐，蓝眼睛炯炯有神，笑声爽朗。就着贝果和茶，我问她是否有什么事希望在马可年幼时就能明白。



“How well he’d turn out!” Gloria says proudly. “One of the most important things I learned from his teachers was to work with his strengths rather than trying to correct his deficits. Bernard and I were always so focused on what Mark couldn’t do—‘If only he could talk!’ Then he’d learn to talk and we’d move on to ‘If only he could read!’ But once he figured out that he loves art, everything else came along with it, because it feels good to do something you’re good at doing.”

“他后来能发展得这么好！”格洛丽亚自豪地说，“我从他老师那里学到最重要的一点，就是发挥他的长处，而不是试图纠正他的不足。伯纳德和我过去总盯着马可做不到的事——‘要是他能说话就好了！’等他学会说话，我们又念叨‘要是他能阅读就好了！’但当他发现自己热爱艺术后，其他一切便随之而来，因为做自己擅长的事会让人感觉良好。”



After helping Rimland launch Defeat Autism Now! in the 1990s, Edelson now advises parents to “run in the other direction” if a biomedical practitioner promises a cure. For years, he was married to Valerie Paradiz, who brought her son Elijah to the first Autreat. Now she designs curricula for teaching young people how to become effective self-advocates at school.

在 20 世纪 90 年代帮助里姆兰发起“战胜孤独症！”运动后，埃德尔森如今建议家长，如果生物医学从业者承诺能治愈孤独症，“就赶紧往反方向跑”。他曾与瓦莱丽·帕拉迪兹结婚多年，后者曾带着儿子以利亚参加首届 Autreat 活动。如今她设计课程，教导年轻人如何在校园中有效进行自我倡导。



Shortly before Rimland died in 2006, he told a reporter from a local newspaper that his fondest wish was to make his son “normal.” But he and Gloria had already given him something better than normal: a community that celebrates him for being exactly who he is. Midway through the journey of his life, Mark has the most precious and elusive thing that anyone can hope for. He is completely at home on earth.

2006 年里姆兰去世前不久，他曾对当地一家报社记者表示，自己最大的心愿是让儿子变得“正常”。但事实上，他和格洛丽亚已经给了儿子比“正常”更珍贵的东西：一个因他本来的样子而接纳他的社群。在人生旅途的中途，马克拥有了任何人都渴望却难以企及的最珍贵之物——他在这世间找到了完全的归属感。







---


NeuroTribes








Acknowledgments

致谢







This book would not have been written without the encouragement, support, and patience of my husband, Keith Karraker; my editor, Megan Newman; my agent, Beth Vesel; and my mother and my late father, Leslie and Donald Silberman. Special thanks to the autistic people and their loved ones and allies who allowed me deep glimpses into their lives, including Shannon, Craig, Leo, Zelly, and India Rosa; the staff and members of the Asperger/Autism Network; Alysia Abbott, Jeff Howe, and Finn; Julia Bascom; Peter, Liz, and Tyler Bell; Samantha Bodwell; Lydia Brown; Michael Buckholtz; Laura Butler; Matt Carey; Kerima Cevik; Kristina Chew; Rachel Cohen-Rottenberg; Liz Ditz; Todd, Erika, and Sam Drezner; Adam Feinstein; Lee Felsenstein; Chen Gershuni; Temple Grandin; Dan Grover; Carol Greenburg, John Ordover, and Arren; Zoe and Rob Gross; Scott Holman; Janet Lawson and Autistry Studios; Russell Lehmann; Karla McLaren; Colin Meloy, Carson Ellis, and Hank; Ari Ne’eman and the ASAN membership; Jennifer Byde Myers; Alex Plank; Barbara Platt; Dora Raymaker; Mark and Gloria Rimland; Scott Robertson; John Elder and Jack Robison; Marc Rosen; Jason Ross; Jim St. Leger; Ralph and DJ Savarese; Sola Shelly; Rudy Simone; Jim Sinclair; Lynne Soraya; Ruth Christ Sullivan; Nick Walker; Michael Forbes Wilcox; and Emily Willingham.

没有我的丈夫基思·卡拉克尔、编辑梅根·纽曼、经纪人贝丝·维塞尔，以及我的母亲和已故的父亲莱斯利与唐纳德·西尔伯曼的鼓励、支持与耐心，这本书便无法问世。特别感谢那些允许我深入窥见其生活的孤独谱系群体及其亲友与盟友，包括香农、克雷格、里奥、泽莉和印度·罗莎；阿斯伯格/孤独症网络的工作人员与成员；阿莉西亚·阿博特、杰夫·豪与芬恩；朱莉娅·巴斯科姆；彼得、莉兹与泰勒·贝尔；萨曼莎·博德韦尔；莉迪亚·布朗；迈克尔·巴克霍尔茨；劳拉·巴特勒；马特·凯里；克里玛·切维克；克里斯蒂娜·邱；蕾切尔·科恩-罗滕伯格；莉兹·迪茨；托德、埃里卡与萨姆·德雷兹纳；亚当·范斯坦；李·费尔森斯坦；陈格舒尼；坦普尔·葛兰汀；丹·格罗弗；卡罗尔·格林伯格、约翰·奥多弗与阿伦；佐伊与罗布·格罗斯；斯科特·霍尔曼；珍妮特·劳森与奥蒂斯特里工作室；拉塞尔·莱曼；卡拉·麦克拉伦；科林·梅洛伊、卡森·埃利斯与汉克；阿里·尼曼与 ASAN 成员；詹妮弗·拜德·迈尔斯；亚历克斯·普朗克；芭芭拉·普拉特；多拉·雷梅克；马克与格洛丽亚·里姆兰；斯科特·罗伯逊；约翰·埃尔德与杰克·罗比森；马克·罗森；杰森·罗斯；吉姆·圣 莱格、拉尔夫与 DJ·萨瓦雷斯、索拉·谢利、鲁迪·西蒙、吉姆·辛克莱、琳恩·索拉亚、露丝·克里斯·沙利文、尼克·沃克、迈克尔·福布斯·威尔科克斯；以及艾米丽·威林厄姆。



I’m also grateful to the clinicians, researchers, educators, writers, and professionals who generously shared their time and knowledge to inform this book, including Thomas Armstrong; Tony Attwood; Simon Baron-Cohen; Jon Brock; Geraldine Dawson; Steve Edelson and the Autism Research Institute; Brock and Fernette Eide; Graham Farmelo; Al Filreis; Uta Frith; Morton Ann Gernsbacher; Judith Gould; Roy Richard Grinker; Bennett Leventhal; Susan Moreno; Barry Morrow; Laurent Mottron; Christina Nicolaidis; Sally Ozonoff; Valerie Paradiz; Samantha Pierce; David Ropeik; Mark Roithmayr; Oliver Sacks; Marc Sirkin; Darold Treffert; Garret Westlake; the staff of the Willliams Syndrome Association and the campers at Whispering Trails; and Lorna Wing. Thanks also to those who provided assistance by transcribing interviews and translating texts, including Abby Royle and Eric Jarosinski.

我还要感谢那些慷慨分享时间和知识、为本书提供信息的临床医生、研究人员、教育工作者、作家和专业人士，包括托马斯·阿姆斯特朗、托尼·阿特伍德、西蒙·巴伦-科恩、乔恩·布洛克、杰拉尔丁·道森、史蒂夫·埃德尔森与孤独症研究所、布洛克与费内特·艾德、格雷厄姆·法梅洛、阿尔·菲尔雷斯、乌塔·弗里斯、莫顿·安·格恩斯巴赫、朱迪思·古尔德、罗伊·理查德·格林克、贝内特·莱文塔尔、苏珊·莫雷诺、巴里·莫罗、洛朗·莫特龙、克里斯蒂娜·尼古拉迪斯、萨莉·奥佐诺夫、瓦莱丽·帕拉迪兹、萨曼莎·皮尔斯、大卫·罗佩克、马克·罗伊特迈尔、奥利弗·萨克斯、马克·瑟金、达罗德·特雷弗特、加勒特·韦斯特莱克、威廉姆斯综合征协会的工作人员以及低语小径营地的营员们，还有洛娜·温。同时感谢艾比·罗伊尔和埃里克·雅罗辛斯基在转录访谈和翻译文本方面提供的帮助。



In memory of Lorna Wing (1928–2014).

谨以此纪念洛娜·温（1928–2014）。

she helped Vint Cerf develop the TCP/IP protocols: Closing the Innovation Gap: Reigniting the Spark of Creativity in a Global Economy, Judy Estrin. McGraw-Hill, 2008.

CHAPTER 1. THE WIZARD OF CLAPHAM COMMON

第一章 克拉珀姆公地的巫师



never hailing those who recognized him: The Life of the Hon. Henry Cavendish: Including Abstracts of His More Important Scientific Papers, and a Critical Inquiry into the Claims of All the Alleged Discoverers of the Composition of Water, George Wilson. Printed for the Cavendish Society, Jan. 1, 1851.

从未向认出他的人打招呼：《尊敬的亨利·卡文迪什生平：包括其重要科学论文摘要，以及对所有声称发现水组成成分者的批判性探究》，乔治·威尔逊著，卡文迪什学会印刷，1851 年 1 月 1 日。



making an undignified but effective escape: Sketches of the Royal Society and the Royal Society Club, Sir John Barrow. Murray, 1849.

进行了一次有失体面但有效的逃脱：《皇家学会及皇家学会俱乐部素描》，约翰·巴罗爵士著，默里出版社，1849 年。



A maid wielding a broom: Cavendish: The Experimental Life, Christa Jungnickel and Russell McCormmach. Bucknell, 2001.

一位手持扫帚的女仆：卡文迪许：实验人生，克里斯塔·容尼克尔与拉塞尔·麦科马克合著。巴克内尔大学出版社，2001 年。



a perfect simulation of the fish’s electrical organs: Draw the Lightning Down: Benjamin Franklin and Electrical Technology in the Age of Enlightenment, Michael Brian Schiffer. University of California Press, 2006, p. 119.

对鱼类发电器官的完美模拟：引雷而下：本杰明·富兰克林与启蒙时代的电气技术，迈克尔·布莱恩·希弗著。加州大学出版社，2006 年，第 119 页。



knocking one sixth of the city flat: On the Nature of Thunderstorms; and on the Means of Protecting Buildings and Shipping against the Destructive Effects of Lightning, W. S. Harris. Parker, 1743.

将城市六分之一的区域夷为平地：论雷暴的性质；以及保护建筑物和船舶免受闪电破坏性影响的方法，W.S.哈里斯著。帕克出版社，1743 年。



The lightning committee devised a crafty plan: The Electrical Researches of the Honourable Henry Cavendish, F.R.S.: Written between 1771 and 1781, Henry Cavendish. Ulan Press, repr. ed., 2011.

闪电委员会设计了一个巧妙计划：尊敬的亨利·卡文迪许（皇家学会会员）的电学研究：写于 1771 年至 1781 年间，亨利·卡文迪许著。乌兰出版社，再版，2011 年。



“the most rigid and satisfactory explanation”: Cavendish (Memoirs of the American Philosophical Society), Christa Jungnickel and Russell McCormmach. American Philosophical Society, Dec. 1996.

“最严谨且令人满意的解释”：卡文迪许（《美国哲学学会回忆录》），克里斯塔·容尼克尔与拉塞尔·麦科马克合著。美国哲学学会，1996 年 12 月。



his experimental methods made that revolution possible: “Henry Cavendish: The Catalyst for the Chemical Revolution,” Frederick Seitz. Notes and Records of the Royal Society, 2005, p. 59.

思考自己的孤独谱系特质：“理查德·博赫兹访谈”，西蒙·辛格著，《卫报》，1998 年 8 月 28 日。



“On a very drawn-out scale, I think I’m on the spectrum”: “Jerry Seinfeld to Brian Williams: ‘I Think I’m on the Spectrum.’” NBC News, Nov. 6, 2014. After a storm of media attention, Seinfeld would eventually walk those comments back, saying that he “related” to a dramatized account of the lives of people on the spectrum. http://www.nbcnews.com/nightly-news/jerry-seinfeld-brian-williams-i-think-im-spectrum-n242941

“在一个非常宽泛的尺度上，我认为自己处于孤独谱系中”：“杰瑞·宋飞对布莱恩·威廉姆斯说：‘我认为自己处于谱系中。’”NBC 新闻，2014 年 11 月 6 日。在媒体风暴之后，宋飞最终收回了这些言论，称他“共鸣”于对谱系人士生活的戏剧化描述。http://www.nbcnews.com/nightly-news/jerry-seinfeld-brian-williams-i-think-im-spectrum-n242941

第二章 爱绿吸管的男孩



On a good day: “Language Development,” Amanda C. Brandone et al. Children’s Needs III: Development, Prevention, and Intervention. National Association of School Psychologists, 2006.

divorce rates are no higher for families like the Rosas: “80 Percent Autism Divorce Rate Debunked in First-of-Its Kind Scientific Study,” Kennedy Krieger Institute, May 19, 2010.

像罗莎这样的家庭离婚率并不更高：“80%孤独症离婚率在同类首项科学研究中被推翻”，肯尼迪·克里格研究所，2010 年 5 月 19 日。

将他推入一系列临时工作：《压力驱使父亲杀子后自杀，家人称》，麦·特兰与迈克·安东，《洛杉矶时报》，2002 年 7 月 31 日。



“Her activities were becoming stranger”: Let Me Hear Your Voice, Catherine Maurice. Ballantine Books, repr. ed., 1994.

“她的行为变得越来越古怪”：《让我听见你的声音》，凯瑟琳·莫里斯，巴兰坦出版社，1994 年重印版。



based on ornithologist Nikolaas Tinbergen’s observations of birds: “Autistic” Children: New Hope for a Cure, N. Tinbergen and E. A. Tinbergen. Allen & Unwin, 1983, pp. 52–53.

基于鸟类学家尼古拉斯·廷伯根对鸟类的观察：《“孤独谱系”儿童：治愈的新希望》，N. 廷伯根与 E. A. 廷伯根，艾伦与昂温出版社，1983 年，第 52–53 页。



“We are not blaming these unfortunate parents”: “Ethology and Stress Diseases,” Nikolaas Tinbergen. Nobel lecture, Dec. 12, 1973.

“我们并非在指责这些不幸的父母”：引自尼古拉斯·廷伯根（Nikolaas Tinbergen）1973 年 12 月 12 日的诺贝尔奖演讲《行为学与应激性疾病》。



by force, if necessary: “Autistic” Children: New Hope for a Cure, p. 229.

必要时可采取强制手段：引自《“孤独谱系”儿童：治愈的新希望》第 229 页。



Tinbergen promoted as a “new hope”: “Autistic” Children: New Hope for a Cure.

廷伯根所倡导的“新希望”：引自《“孤独谱系”儿童：治愈的新希望》。

与同龄人难以区分：“孤独症儿童的行为治疗与正常智力及教育功能”，O. I. Lovaas 著，《咨询与临床心理学杂志》，第 55 卷，1987 年，第 3–9 页。

一种清除“不和谐”能量阻塞的方法：“BioSET 系统：三种基础疗法——器官特异性解毒、酶疗法与脱敏”，Ellen Cutler 著。http://www.drellencutler.com/pages/articles/?ArticleID=215

美国人现在咨询："美国的补充与替代医学"，托尼娅·帕萨雷利。凯斯西储大学，2008 年 4 月。



Up to three quarters of all autistic children in the United States: “Complementary and Alternative Medicine Treatments for Children with Autism Spectrum Disorders,” Susan E. Levy and Susan L. Hyman. Journal of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry, Vol. 17, No. 4, 2008.

美国多达四分之三的孤独谱系儿童："孤独谱系障碍儿童的补充与替代医学治疗"，苏珊·E·利维和苏珊·L·海曼。《儿童与青少年精神病学杂志》，第 17 卷，第 4 期，2008 年。

being able to dress and undress himself: Leo Rosa: Summary Progress Report, June 3, 2004.

能够自己穿脱衣服：利奥·罗莎：总结进度报告，2004 年 6 月 3 日。

有一天克里斯蒂娜突然想到：克里斯蒂娜·邱，与作者访谈，2014 年。



a “unique ‘syndrome,’ heretofore unreported”: “Autistic Disturbances of Affective Contact,” Leo Kanner. Nervous Child, Vol. 2, 1943, pp. 217–50.

一种“此前未曾报道过的独特‘综合征’”：《情感接触的孤独谱系障碍》，利奥·坎纳。《神经质儿童》，第 2 卷，1943 年，第 217–250 页。

“It’s all now”: “Jenny McCarthy on Healing Her Son’s Autism and Discovering Her Life’s Mission,” Allison Kugel. PR.com, Oct. 9, 2007. http://www.pr.com/article/1076

“一切就在当下”：艾莉森·库格尔，《珍妮·麦卡锡：治愈儿子孤独症，发现人生使命》，PR.com，2007 年 10 月 9 日。http://www.pr.com/article/1076



CHAPTER 3. WHAT SISTER VIKTORINE KNEW

第三章 维克托琳修女知道的事



“Once one has learnt to pay attention”: “Autistic Psychopathy in Childhood,” Hans Asperger. Translated by Uta Frith. Autism and Asperger Syndrome. Cambridge University Press, 1991, p. 39.

“一旦学会如何关注”：汉斯·阿斯伯格，《童年期的孤独谱系精神病态》，乌塔·弗里思译，《孤独症与阿斯伯格综合征》，剑桥大学出版社，1991 年，第 39 页。



“feeble-minded”: “Qualitative Intelligence Testing as a Means of Diagnosis in the Examination of Psychopathic Children,” Anni Weiss. American Journal of Orthopsychiatry, Vol. 5, No. 2, Apr. 1935, pp. 154–79.

“智力低下”：安妮·韦斯，《作为诊断手段的定性智力测试在精神障碍儿童检查中的应用》，《美国儿童精神病学杂志》，第 5 卷，第 2 期，1935 年 4 月，第 154–179 页。



He had been encouraged to do his postgraduate work: “Hans Asperger (1906–1980): His Life and Work,” Maria Asperger-Felder. Translated for the author by Kenneth Kronenberg.

他曾被鼓励从事研究生工作：“汉斯·阿斯伯格（1906–1980）：生平与事业”，玛丽亚·阿斯伯格-费尔德著，肯尼斯·克朗伯格为作者翻译。



Hamburger offered a popular course in children’s diseases: “News and Comment.” American Journal of the Diseases of Children, Vol. 52, No. 3, 1936, p. 674.

汉堡开设了一门广受欢迎的儿童疾病课程：“新闻与评论”。《美国儿童疾病杂志》，第 52 卷，第 3 期，1936 年，第 674 页。



Nicknamed “Red Vienna”: Red Vienna: Experiment in Working Class Culture, 1919–1934, Helmut Gruber. Oxford University Press, 1991.

绰号“红色维也纳”：《红色维也纳：1919–1934 年的工人阶级文化实验》，赫尔穆特·格鲁伯著，牛津大学出版社，1991 年。



dozens of ongoing salons: “I See Psychoanalysis, Art and Biology Coming Together: An Interview with Eric Kandel.” Der Spiegel, Oct. 11, 2012.

数十个持续进行的沙龙：“我预见精神分析、艺术与生物学将融合：埃里克·坎德尔访谈录”。《明镜周刊》，2012 年 10 月 11 日。



Gustav Mahler’s music: “Vienna: Trapped in a Golden Age,” Alexandra Starr. American Scholar, Winter 2008.

古斯塔夫·马勒的音乐：《维也纳：困于黄金时代》，亚历山德拉·斯塔尔著，《美国学者》2008 年冬季刊。



“His names for the various types were always quite incisive”: Erwin Lazar und sein Wirken, Valerie Bruck, George Frankl, Anni Weiss, and Viktorine Zak. Translated for the author by Eric Jarosinski. Springer, 1932.

“他对各类儿童的命名总是非常犀利”：瓦莱丽·布鲁克、乔治·弗兰克尔、安妮·魏斯与维克托琳·扎克合著《埃尔温·拉扎尔及其事业》，埃里克·亚罗辛斯基为作者翻译，施普林格出版社 1932 年版。



“It must give every child a chance”: “Mellansjö School-Home: Psychopathic Children Admitted 1928–1940, Their Social Adaptation over 30 Years: A Longitudinal Prospective Follow-up,” Ingegärd Fried. Acta Paediatrica, Vol. 84, Suppl. s408, 1995.

“必须给每个孩子机会”：《梅兰舍学校之家：1928-1940 年收治的精神病态儿童及其 30 年社会适应追踪研究》，英格加德·弗里德著，《儿科学报》第 84 卷增刊 s408，1995 年。



“It had never occurred to any of us to see [the children] as delinquents”: Wayward Youth, August Aichhorn. Penguin Books, repr. ed., 1965.

“我们从未有人想过[这些孩子]是少年犯”：奥古斯特·艾希霍恩著《迷途少年》，企鹅出版社 1965 年重印版。



Each morning, a procession: “Clemens Pirquet and His Work: Director of the Vienna University Kinder-Klinik, 1911–1929,” Harriette Chick. Lancet, Vol. 213, No. 5508, March 1929.

每天早晨，都会有一列队伍：“克莱门斯·皮尔奎特及其工作：维也纳大学儿童诊所主任，1911–1929”，哈里特·奇克。《柳叶刀》，第 213 卷，第 5508 期，1929 年 3 月。



Erwin Jekelius: See the reference to the 1936 paper by Jekelius in “Encopresis,” Acta Paediatrica, Vol. 55, Suppl. s169, 1966.

埃尔温·耶克利乌斯：参见耶克利乌斯 1936 年论文《遗粪症》的参考文献，《儿科学报》，第 55 卷，增刊 s169，1966 年。



the “true genius” of his clinic: “Asperger and His Syndrome,” Uta Frith. Autism and Asperger Syndrome. Cambridge University Press, 1991.

他诊所的“真正天才”：《阿斯伯格及其综合征》，乌塔·弗里思。《孤独症与阿斯伯格综合征》。剑桥大学出版社，1991 年。



started working at the clinic in 1927: George Frankl’s biographical file, Alan Mason Chesney Medical Archives, Johns Hopkins University.

Asperger would often just sit with the children: “Hans Asperger: His Life and Work.”

阿斯伯格常常只是静静地坐在孩子们中间：《汉斯·阿斯伯格：生平与著作》



a report published in 1991: “Asperger and His Syndrome.”

1991 年发表的一份报告：《阿斯伯格及其综合征》



an overlooked paper: “The Heilpedägogical Station of the Children’s Clinic at the University of Vienna,” Joseph J. Michaels. American Journal of Orthopsychiatry, 1935.

一篇被忽视的论文：《维也纳大学儿童诊所的教育治疗站》，约瑟夫·J·迈克尔斯著，《美国儿童精神病学杂志》，1935 年



“fixed in numbered seats”: Ibid.

“固定在编号座位上”：同上



“In this ‘age of technocracy’”: Ibid.

“在这个‘技术官僚时代’”：同上。



provided by Weiss in a paper: “Play Interviews with Nursery School Children,” Anni Weiss-Frankl. American Journal of Orthopsychiatry, Vol. 11, No. 1, Jan. 1941, pp. 33–39.

由韦斯在一篇论文中提供：“与幼儿园儿童的游戏访谈”，安妮·韦斯-弗兰克尔。《美国儿童精神病学杂志》，第 11 卷，第 1 期，1941 年 1 月，第 33–39 页。



one boy’s fascination: “The Importance of Symbol-Formation in the Development of the Ego,” Melanie Klein. International Journal of Psychoanalysis, Vol. 9, 1930, p. 5.

一个男孩的着迷：“自我发展过程中符号形成的重要性”，梅兰妮·克莱因。《国际精神分析杂志》，第 9 卷，1930 年，第 5 页。



“Some children frequently volunteered for play turns”: “Play Interviews with Nursery School Children,” Anni Weiss-Frankl. American Journal of Orthopsychiatry, Vol. 11, No. 1, Jan. 1941, p. 35.

“有些孩子经常自愿参与游戏环节”：“与幼儿园儿童的游戏访谈”，安妮·韦斯-弗兰克尔。《美国儿童精神病学杂志》，第 11 卷，第 1 期，1941 年 1 月，第 35 页。



Weiss’s play sessions: Ibid., p. 34.

Weiss 的游戏时段：同上，第 34 页。



“to determine the innate capacities of the child”: “The Heilpedägogical Station of the Children’s Clinic at the University of Vienna.”

“确定儿童先天能力”：“维也纳大学儿童诊所的 Heilpedägogical 站”。



Even the physical layout: “Asperger and His Syndrome.”

甚至物理布局：“阿斯伯格及其综合征”。



“We cannot be interested so much in the concrete result”: “Qualitative Intelligence Testing as a Means of Diagnosis in the Examination of Psychopathic Children,” p. 165.

“我们不能过分关注具体结果”：“作为检查精神障碍儿童诊断手段的定性智力测试”，第 165 页。



“G. is not able to escape from his logical attitude”: Ibid., p. 168.

“G. 无法摆脱他的逻辑态度”：同上，第 168 页。



more than two hundred children: “Autistic Psychopathy in Childhood,” p. 84.

超过两百名儿童：《儿童孤独谱系心理病理学》，第 84 页。



(“If something was only slightly different”): Ibid., p. 61.

（“如果某事物只有细微差别”）：同上，第 61 页。



a thousand matchboxes: Ibid., p. 82.

一千个火柴盒：同上，第 82 页。



“If one lays one’s head”: Ibid., p. 73.

“若有人枕首”：同上，第 73 页。



“a particularly interesting and highly recognizable type of child”: Ibid., p. 37.

“一种特别有趣且极易辨识的儿童类型”：同上，第 37 页。



these odd young ducks: “Die schizoiden Psychopathien im Kindesalter,” G. E. Sukhareva. Translated by Sula Wolff. Monatsschrift für Psychiatrie und Neurologie, 1926.

这些古怪的小家伙：《儿童期的分裂样精神病态》，G. E. 苏哈列娃著，苏拉·沃尔夫译，《精神病学与神经病学月刊》，1926 年。



“autistic thinking”: Dementia Praecox oder Gruppe der Schizophrenien, Eugen Bleuler. Deuticke, 1911.

“孤独谱系思维”：《早发性痴呆或精神分裂症群》，欧根·布洛伊勒著，多伊蒂克出版社，1911 年。



the hazy borderland between mental health and illness: “Mellansjö School-Home.”

心理健康与疾病之间的模糊边界地带：“梅兰舍学校之家”。



“all levels of ability”: “Autistic Psychopathy in Childhood,” p. 74.

“所有能力水平”：《童年孤独谱系精神变态》，第 74 页。



“we have never met a girl with the fully fledged picture of autism”: Ibid., p. 85.

“我们从未见过具有完全孤独谱系特征的女孩”：同上，第 85 页。



“an extreme variant of male intelligence”: Prenatal Testosterone in Mind: Amniotic Fluid Studies, Simon Baron-Cohen et al. MIT Press, 2004.

“男性智力的极端变体”：《思维中的产前睾酮：羊水研究》，西蒙·巴伦-科恩等，麻省理工学院出版社，2004 年。



underestimation of the prevalence of autism in women: “The Extreme Male Brain Theory of Autism and the Potential Adverse Effects for Boys and Girls with Autism,” Timothy M. Krahn, Andrew Fenton. Journal of Bioethical Inquiry, Vol. 9, No. 1, March 2012.

对女性孤独症患病率被低估的现象：《极端男性大脑理论下的孤独症及其对男女孤独症患者的潜在不良影响》，作者：Timothy M. Krahn、Andrew Fenton，发表于《生物伦理探究杂志》第 9 卷第 1 期，2012 年 3 月。



the Asperger FAQ: “Asperger Syndrome Fact Sheet,” National Institutes of Health. http://www.ninds.nih.gov/disorders/asperger/detail_asperger.htm

阿斯伯格综合征常见问题解答：《阿斯伯格综合征事实手册》，美国国立卫生研究院。http://www.ninds.nih.gov/disorders/asperger/detail_asperger.htm



Weiss’s in-depth case study of Gottfried: “Qualitative Intelligence Testing as a Means of Diagnosis in the Examination of Psychopathic Children.”

韦斯对戈特弗里德的深入个案研究：《定性智力测试作为精神障碍儿童诊断手段的探讨》。



“We know an autistic child”: “Autistic Psychopathy in Childhood,” p. 72.

“我们认识一个孤独谱系儿童”：《儿童期孤独谱系精神障碍》，第 72 页。



the designers of spaceships themselves were autistic: See Uta Frith’s footnote in “Autistic Psychopathy in Childhood,” p. 72.

飞船设计师本身具有孤独谱系特征：参见乌塔·弗里斯在《童年孤独症心理病理学》第 72 页的脚注。



“Autistic children have the ability”: “Autistic Psychopathy in Childhood,” p. 71.

“孤独谱系儿童具备这种能力”：引自《童年孤独症心理病理学》第 71 页。



“a dash of autism is essential”: “Problems of Infantile Autism,” Hans Asperger. Communication: Journal of the National Autistic Society, Vol. 13, 1979.

“具备一定程度的孤独谱系特质至关重要”：汉斯·阿斯伯格，《婴儿孤独症问题》。载于《沟通：国家孤独症协会期刊》第 13 卷，1979 年。



“the useless protrusion of a single faculty”: “New Facts and Remarks Concerning Idiocy: Being a Lecture Delivered Before the New York Medical Journal Association, October 15, 1869,” Édouard Séguin. American Journal of the Medical Sciences, Vol. 59, No. 120, 1870.

“单一功能的突兀凸显”：爱德华·塞甘，《关于白痴的新事实与评论：1869 年 10 月 15 日在纽约医学杂志协会的演讲》。载于《美国医学科学杂志》第 59 卷第 120 期，1870 年。



“In the case of learning difficulties, the question is”: “Qualitative Intelligence Testing as a Means of Diagnosis in the Examination of Psychopathic Children.”

“在学习困难的情况下，问题在于”：“定性智力测试作为检查精神障碍儿童的一种诊断手段”



“The teacher must at all costs be calm and collected”: “Autistic Psychopathy in Childhood,” p. 47.

“教师无论如何都必须保持冷静和镇定”：《儿童期孤独谱系精神病理学》，第 47 页。



“the teacher has to become somehow ‘autistic’”: Heilpädagogik, Hans Asperger. Excerpts translated for the author by Uta Frith. Springer, 1953.

“教师必须以某种方式变得‘孤独谱系’”：汉斯·阿斯伯格，《治疗教育学》。由乌塔·弗里斯为作者翻译的摘录。斯普林格出版社，1953 年。



Who was this man: “Hans Asperger (1906–1980): His Life and Work,” Maria Asperger-Felder. Translated for the author by Kenneth Kronenberg.

这个人是谁：“汉斯·阿斯伯格（1906–1980）：他的生平与工作”，玛丽亚·阿斯伯格-费尔德。由肯尼斯·克朗伯格为作者翻译。



“Reading is bound up with one’s fate and destiny”: Ibid.

“阅读与个人的命运和际遇紧密相连”：同上。



“one of the noblest flowerings of the German spirit”: Ibid.

“德国精神最高贵的绽放之一”：同上。

their replacements were bumbling fanatics: “A Leading Medical School Seriously Damaged: Vienna 1938,” Edzard Ernst. Annals of Internal Medicine, Vol. 122, No. 10, May 1995.

a gala weeklong event: War Against the Weak: Eugenics and America’s Campaign to Create a Master Race, Edwin Black. Four Walls Eight Windows, 2003.

一场为期一周的盛大活动：《对抗弱者：优生学与美国创造优等种族的运动》，埃德温·布莱克著，四墙八窗出版社，2003 年。



promoted in journals: Scientific Monthly, Vol. 13, No. 2, Aug. 1921.

在期刊上推广：《科学月刊》，第 13 卷，第 2 期，1921 年 8 月。

Papers were presented: “The Second International Congress of Eugenics,” C. C. Little. Carnegie Institution of Washington, 1921.

At one end of Eugenics Hall: Floor plan of the exhibit of the Second International Congress of Eugenics, from Cold Spring Harbor eugenics archive.

优生学大厅的一端：第二届国际优生学大会展品平面图，来自冷泉港优生学档案。



he refused to believe that any creature as noble as Homo sapiens: “Biographical Memoir of Henry Fairfield Osborn (1857–1935),” William K. Gregory. Presented to the autumn meeting of the National Academy of Sciences, 1937.

他拒绝相信像智人这样高贵的生物存在：“亨利·费尔菲尔德·奥斯本（1857–1935）传记回忆录”，威廉·K·格雷戈里著。提交至 1937 年美国国家科学院秋季会议。

it was the duty of his fellow scientists: “The Second International Congress of Eugenics Address of Welcome,” Henry Fairfield Osborn. Science, New Series, Vol. 54, No. 1397, Oct. 7, 1921.

他同行的科学家们有责任：“第二届国际优生学大会欢迎致辞”，亨利·费尔菲尔德·奥斯本著。《科学》新系列，第 54 卷，第 1397 期，1921 年 10 月 7 日。

Hundreds of families: Ibid.

数百个家庭：同上。

thousands of Austrians huddled: Eric Kandel’s biographical memoir, delivered on the occasion of receiving the Nobel Prize in medicine, 2000.

The final strains of the national anthem barely had faded from the airwaves: Fallen Bastions, G. E. R. Gedye. Faber & Faber, 1939.

the Vienna Psychiatric and Neurological Association issued a decree: Ibid.
